insights.title: Tooltip sideOffset default and shadow fix
insights.problem: TooltipContent did not provide a sensible default for sideOffset which led to inconsistent tooltip positioning; visual styling (shadow) was missing.
insights.root_cause: sideOffset prop default was set to 0 in the component signature and passed through directly, causing callers that omitted it to get an undesired offset. Styling omitted a shadow class and dark-mode adjustments were inconsistent.
insights.solution: Use sideOffset ?? 4 when rendering the TooltipPrimitive.Content and add shadow and dark-mode CSS classes to the tooltip's container className.
insights.lesson_learned: Optional numeric props should use nullish coalescing to provide sane defaults at usage sites. Visual components should centralize cross-theme styling (shadow, dark-mode) to avoid inconsistent appearance.

insights.title: Adjusted tooltip foreground/background for dark mode
insights.problem: Tooltip color styles were not consistent across light/dark themes.
insights.root_cause: Tooltip CSS used static bg/text classes without explicit dark-mode fallbacks.
insights.solution: Enhanced className to include dark-mode specific classes (dark:border-white/18 dark:bg-[#050504]) and swapped foreground/background classes appropriately.
insights.lesson_learned: When styling shared UI primitives, include explicit theme variants in the component to avoid per-callsite tweaks.

insights.title: Improve agent name check error reporting and classify failures
insights.problem: Network errors and backend-unreachable scenarios were not distinguished; UI displayed a generic check error.
insights.root_cause: checkAgentName() threw generic errors and lacked a typed error class to indicate backend unreachable vs request failed.
insights.solution: Added AgentNameCheckError class with reason field, detect fetch() failures and backend-unavailable HTTP statuses (502/503/504) and throw AgentNameCheckError with 'backend_unreachable' reason. Caller UI updated to inspect error and show network-specific message.
insights.lesson_learned: Distinguish network-level failures from application-level errors and surface meaningful messages to the UI; use typed error classes for flow control in UI.

frontend/src/core/api/api-client.ts.short_description: Client adapter for LangGraph API with stream helpers

insights.title: Keep separate LangGraph clients for mock and default modes
insights.problem: A single singleton LangGraphClient instance was reused for both mock and default modes. Creating a mock client after the default (or vice versa) could return the wrong client instance for the requested mode.
insights.root_cause: Global singleton variable _singleton didn't account for the isMock flag, so the first created client always persisted for subsequent calls regardless of isMock.
insights.solution: Replace single _singleton with a Map keyed by 'mock'/'default' and create/return a client per mode.
insights.lesson_learned: When caching factory outputs that depend on an input flag, cache by a derived key rather than a single global instance.

frontend/src/core/api/stream-mode.ts.short_description: Sanitizes LangGraph run stream options and warns on unsupported modes

frontend/src/core/artifacts/hooks.ts.short_description: Hook to load and provide artifact content for UI

insights.title: Expose url alongside content from useArtifactContent
insights.problem: Consumers needed the original URL for non-write artifact previews; hook only returned content.
insights.root_cause: Hook assumed content was always the primary preview input.
insights.solution: Return { content, url, isLoading, error } with url undefined for write files and content as text for write files.
insights.lesson_learned: Hook return shapes should reflect the different rendering paths consumers may need.

frontend/src/core/artifacts/loader.ts.short_description: Loader utilities to fetch artifact content and tool-call payloads

insights.title: Return both content and url from loadArtifactContent
insights.problem: Caller lost the original URL when loader only returned the text response; preview needed url.
insights.root_cause: loadArtifactContent returned only the text string.
insights.solution: Return object { content: text, url } so callers can decide to use src or srcDoc.
insights.lesson_learned: Preserve metadata (original url) when loading content to enable alternate rendering flows.

frontend/src/core/artifacts/utils.ts.short_description: Helpers to build artifact URLs and extract artifacts

insights.title: Provide SSR-safe fallback origin
insights.problem: getBaseOrigin returned undefined during SSR; some callers expected a string and could error.
insights.root_cause: No SSR fallback for origin.
insights.solution: Return 'http://localhost:2026' as SSR fallback origin.
insights.lesson_learned: When code runs both server- and client-side, provide safe SSR fallbacks for values that are only available in browser runtime.

insights.title: Avoid window access during SSR when constructing base URLs
insights.problem: Calls to window.location.origin were used unguarded to create URLs; that causes errors during server-side rendering or other non-browser contexts.
insights.root_cause: Direct use of window without checking environment leads to ReferenceError in SSR or tests.
insights.solution: Introduce getBaseOrigin() helper that returns window.location.origin only when window is defined (client-side), otherwise undefined; use that as the base when constructing new URL.
insights.lesson_learned: Guard access to browser globals in shared config functions; prefer helper that returns undefined on server so URL constructor uses only the provided absolute URLs.

insights.title: Return absolute URLs for configured base URLs
insights.problem: Config getters returned raw env values which might be relative paths; the frontend needs full absolute URLs.
insights.root_cause: NEXT_PUBLIC_* env values could be host-relative and the app needed full URLs for fetches depending on window.location.origin.
insights.solution: Wrap configured base URLs in new URL(..., window.location.origin).toString() so both relative and absolute env values produce valid absolute URLs.
insights.lesson_learned: Frontend configuration helpers should normalize base URLs to absolute forms to avoid subtle CORS/host resolution bugs.

insights.problem: Frontend needed export/import functions and better error detail formatting for different response shapes.
insights.root_cause: Memory import/export support required new client functions and the backend can return varied error shapes.
insights.solution: Added readMemoryResponse.formatErrorDetail to coerce varied 'detail' payloads to strings, and exportMemory/importMemory functions that call /api/memory/export and /api/memory/import endpoints.
insights.lesson_learned: Client-side error formatting should handle flexible backend error shapes (strings, arrays, objects) for clearer messages.

insights.root_cause: SDK message shape didn't include typed usage_metadata
insights.solution: Add getUsageMetadata, accumulateUsage, and formatTokenCount helpers to parse and present token counts

insights.title: Support hiding internal tool messages from UI
insights.problem: Hidden internal messages (e.g., background save requests) were shown in user-facing message groups.
insights.root_cause: Message grouping didn't consider an additional_kwargs.hide_from_ui flag.
insights.solution: Add isHiddenFromUI helper and skip hidden messages in groupMessages.
insights.lesson_learned: Client message rendering must respect metadata-based visibility flags so internal operations (hidden tool calls) don't confuse end users.

insights.title: Split settings hook and generalized state handler
insights.problem: Single useLocalSettings hook was not reusable for thread-scoped settings.
insights.root_cause: Settings logic duplicated between global and thread-scoped needs.
insights.solution: Introduced useSettingsState(getSettings, saveSettings) generic helper and reimplemented useLocalSettings and useThreadSettings using it. Ensures proper save callback and mounted guarding.
insights.lesson_learned: Factor hook logic that differs only by storage backend into a reusable function; prefer passing get/save callbacks instead of duplicating state hooks.

frontend/src/core/settings/index.ts.short_description: Exports for settings hooks and types

insights.title: Persist per-thread model selection in localStorage
insights.problem: Model selection was global in local settings and not persisted per thread; thread-level model overrides were not applied on fallback or persisted across reloads.
insights.root_cause: Local settings stored only a global settings blob under one key and did not record a per-thread model separately. No browser guards existed for server-side rendering.
insights.solution: Added thread-specific storage keys (deerflow.thread-model.{thread_id}), helper functions getThreadModelName/saveThreadModelName, applyThreadModelOverride, and isBrowser() guards. Extended LocalSettings.context to include model_name and reasoning_effort.
insights.lesson_learned: Thread-scoped user preferences should be stored separately from global settings and applied as overrides during settings load; always guard localStorage access behind an isBrowser() check for SSR safety.

insights.title: Add conversation export utilities (Markdown & JSON)
insights.problem: Users couldn't export conversation threads.
insights.root_cause: No frontend utilities existed to format threads for download.
insights.solution: Implement formatThreadAsMarkdown, formatThreadAsJSON, downloadAsFile, exportThreadAsMarkdown, exportThreadAsJSON with sanitization of filenames and inclusion of reasoning/tool-calls.
insights.lesson_learned: Implement client-side download helpers that produce clean filenames and respect content sanitization; separate formatting (pure functions) from download side-effects for testability.

frontend/src/core/threads/hooks.ts.short_description: React hook handling real-time thread streaming and UI state for agent threads.

insights.title: Normalize stored run id and robust reconnect-on-mount behavior
insights.problem: Stored run ids in sessionStorage could contain query parameters or extra suffixes leading to incorrect run id extraction on reconnect/resume.
insights.root_cause: Client-side run metadata normalization was brittle and didn't robustly extract run_id from URLs or query strings.
insights.solution: Add normalizeStoredRunId function to strip query/hash, extract run_id query parameter, parse /runs/ path segments, and normalize stored value. Use a runMetadataStorage wrapper to provide normalized get/set, and wire reconnectOnMount to use the storage accessor.
insights.lesson: Client-side run resume logic must canonicalize stored identifiers and tolerate various URL encodings so resume/reconnect features are reliable.

insights.title: Allow sendMessage to pass additionalKwargs (hide_from_ui/files) for optimistic UI and hidden tool calls
insights.problem: There was no way to mark an outgoing tool-invoking message as hidden from the UI (for internal save flows) or to attach additional kwargs consistently for optimistic messages.
insights.root_cause: useThreadStream's send API didn't accept options for additionalKwargs, and optimistic message construction always injected optimistic file blocks.
insights.solution: Add options parameter with additionalKwargs, allow hide_from_ui to suppress optimistic UI messages, and include files under additional kwargs consistently. Ensure hidden messages are not shown to UI.
insights.lesson_learned: APIs that create optimistic UI state need to accept visibility and metadata flags so internal operations can be de-emphasised in the UI.

insights.title: UI now triggers Gateway thread cleanup after LangGraph deletion
insights.problem: Frontend deleted LangGraph thread but did not remove DeerFlow-managed local thread data; local disk state became stale
insights.root_cause: Frontend lacked follow-up call to Gateway to remove on-disk thread assets
insights.solution: After LangGraph thread deletion, call DELETE /api/threads/{thread_id} and handle non-OK responses; adjust cache updates and invalidate queries explicitly
insights.lesson_learned: Client-side delete flows that operate across multiple backends must coordinate calls and update caches explicitly; handle and surface backend cleanup failures.

insights.title: Make getFileIcon accept className and add image support
insights.problem: getFileIcon previously used a hard-coded className which prevented callers from controlling icon size/style. Image file icons were missing.
insights.root_cause: Utility function had a fixed internal className string rather than accepting styling from callers.
insights.solution: Refactor getFileIcon to accept (filepath: string, className?: string) and remove internal hard-coded className; add cases for common image extensions returning ImageIcon.
insights.lesson_learned: Utility rendering helpers should accept presentation parameters rather than hard-coding styles; update all call sites when changing utility signatures.

insights.title: Add tests for context/configurable precedence and assistant_id mapping

insights.root_cause: Multiple changes to build_run_config and request shape required tests to prevent regressions.
insights.solution: Added tests verifying: context preferred over configurable, warning when both present, passthrough of other keys, fallback configurable behavior, assistant_id injection, and not overwriting explicit configurable keys.
insights.lesson_learned: When upstream API semantics change, add explicit tests covering both new and old paths and error/logging behavior.

insights.title: Add cooperative cancellation tests for subagent executor
insights.problem: Lack of tests for cooperative cancellation and race between timeout and cancellation.
insights.root_cause: No coverage for cancellation semantics and interactions with timeout handling.
insights.solution: Added TestCooperativeCancellation with tests covering pre-set cancel_event, mid-stream cancellation, request_cancel behavior, timeout guard preserving CANCELLED, and cleanup removal.
insights.lesson: When adding cooperative cancellation, add deterministic tests (use threading.Event) that avoid sleeping and exercise scheduler/timeouts deterministically.

insights.title: Add localized strings for network error / memory import/export
insights.problem: New UI flows needed i18n entries for network errors and memory import/export.
insights.root_cause: New features and improved error messaging required string additions.
insights.solution: Added or adjusted keys: nameStepNetworkError, import/export strings, etc.
insights.lesson_learned: Add i18n keys alongside UI changes to keep translations in sync.

insights.title: i18n updates for token usage and shortcuts strings
insights.problem: New UI features (token usage, command palette/shortcuts) lacked localization entries
insights.root_cause: Adding UI features without corresponding i18n updates
insights.solution: Add tokenUsage and shortcuts translation keys and update types
insights.lesson_learned: Always update i18n resources and translation types whenever adding UI text to avoid build errors and missing strings.

insights.title: Add English translations for new UI features
insights.problem: New UI strings (suggestions, pages titles, create-skill prompt, tooltips, create action) needed translations.
insights.root_cause: UI changes introduced new user-facing text.
insights.solution: Add keys and values for suggestions, suggestionsCreate, pages (appName, chats, newChat, untitled), inputBox.createSkillPrompt, createYourOwnSkill, and tooltips.
insights.lesson_learned: Always update all locale files and central types when adding UI strings to avoid missing translations and runtime errors.

insights.title: Expand translation Types to include new keys
insights.problem: TypeScript types for Translations lacked entries for new tokenUsage and shortcuts keys, causing type/build errors
insights.root_cause: i18n additions not mirrored in types file
insights.solution: Add tokenUsage and shortcuts types to translations interface
insights.lesson_learned: Keep translation keys and their TypeScript interface in sync to prevent build-time type errors.

insights.title: Extend Translations types for new UI strings
insights.problem: New UI features (suggestions, pages titles, skill texts) required new translation keys and type entries.
insights.root_cause: Previously the Translations interface did not include keys for suggestions, pages, createSkill texts, etc.
insights.solution: Add typed entries for pages, suggestions, suggestionsCreate, createSkill keys and skillInstallTooltip.
insights.lesson_learned: When adding new translation keys also update the central types to keep TypeScript coverage and avoid runtime missing-key errors.

insights.title: Update localized zh-CN strings for improved network messages and memory import/export
insights.problem: Chinese locale needed corresponding translations for new network and memory strings.
insights.root_cause: Locale files lagged behind UI improvements.
insights.solution: Added nameStepNetworkError, import/export strings and adjusted wording for clarity.
insights.lesson_learned: Keep locale files updated with UI changes to avoid inconsistent UX across locales.

insights.title: Add Chinese translations for token usage and shortcuts
insights.problem: New UI strings not localized for zh-CN
insights.root_cause: New UI features added after initial translations
insights.solution: Supply translations for tokenUsage and shortcuts
insights.lesson_learned: Localize new UI strings across supported locales to provide consistent user experience.

insights.title: Add Chinese translations for new UI features
insights.problem: New UI strings required Chinese translations.
insights.root_cause: UI changes introduced new user-facing text.
insights.solution: Add corresponding Chinese translations for suggestions, pages titles, create-skill prompt, createYourOwnSkill, and tooltips.
insights.lesson_learned: Keep all locale variants in sync when introducing UI text; maintain the types file in parallel.

insights.title: Unit tests cover OAuth billing header and metadata behavior
insights.problem: Provider-specific behaviors (billing header insertion and metadata.user_id) need validation to avoid regressions.
insights.root_cause: Complex normalization logic (list/string system, metadata type variants) can regress without tests.
insights.solution: Comprehensive tests exercise insertion, deduplication, ordering, and metadata handling for OAuth billing logic.
insights.lesson_learned: Add explicit unit tests for provider compatibility code that conditionally mutates request payloads.

insights.title: Add regression tests for Codex streaming merge behavior
insights.problem: Previously missing tests around Codex streaming merging meant regressions could slip by.
insights.root_cause: No tests were simulating SSE stream events with output_item.done and response.completed combinations.
insights.solution: Added fake httpx client/response helpers and tests that verify merge, ordering, and preference rules for streamed Codex output items.
insights.lesson: When implementing streaming merge logic, cover permutations (out-of-order items, placeholders, and empty final payloads) with unit tests; mocking SSE streams is effective.

insights.title: Tests updated to assert env is passed to spawn_agent_process and None when empty
insights.problem: spawn_agent_process signature changed to accept env kwarg; tests must capture and assert on env passed.
insights.root_cause: Behavioral change required test adaptations for capturing env argument.
insights.solution: Updated DummyProcessContext and monkeypatch for acp.spawn_agent_process to accept env param and added tests test_invoke_acp_agent_passes_env_to_spawn and test_invoke_acp_agent_passes_none_env_when_not_configured which assert captured env map or None.
insights.lesson_learned: When changing third-party integration call signatures (spawn_agent_process), update unit tests to validate both presence and absence of optional env argument.

insights.title: Cover expanded high/medium risk patterns and compound commands
insights.problem: New audit patterns and splitting required extensive test coverage to prevent regressions and bypasses.

insights.title: Expose DEER_FLOW_HOME and internal base URLs to dev containers
insights.problem: Dev containers needed environment variables to share .deer-flow and internal service base URLs between services.
insights.root_cause: docker-compose dev file lacked DEER_FLOW_HOME env var and internal base URL variables.
insights.solution: Added DEER_FLOW_HOME env and DEER_FLOW_INTERNAL_GATEWAY_BASE_URL / DEER_FLOW_INTERNAL_LANGGRAPH_BASE_URL where appropriate; also added uv sync before launching services in later commits.
insights.lesson_learned: Dev docker-compose must mirror runtime expectations and provide shared base directories and internal service URLs for intra-compose communication.

insights.title: Bind mounts for CLI auth directories (~/.codex, ~/.codex) added in dev compose
insights.problem: CodexChatModel and Claude Code CLI auth failed in dev mode due to missing host bind mounts, leading to 'credential not found' errors.
insights.root_cause: Dev compose omitted CLI auth directories that are present in production compose causing credential loaders to not find files in container.
insights.solution: Bind mount ${HOME}/.codex and ${HOME}/.codex into container with create_host_path=true, read_only to mimic production behavior and avoid file-bind failures.
insights.lesson_learned: Dev container compose should mirror production-critical bind mounts for credentials to avoid environment-specific failures; prefer directory binds over individual file binds.

insights.title: Escape shell variables and support allow-blocking flag for LangGraph container
insights.problem: Docker Compose command previously used unescaped shell variables causing Docker Compose to attempt substitution at parse time and warning 'allow_blocking_flag not set'.
insights.root_cause: Shell variables were not escaped in the compose command string.
insights.solution: Escape variables (use \${VAR}) and rename flag variable to allow_blocking for consistency, ensuring --allow-blocking passed correctly.
insights.lesson: Protect inline shell commands in docker-compose by escaping environment variable expansions to avoid compose-time substitution.

insights.title: Correct config.yaml mount path for langgraph service
insights.problem: docker-compose mounted config files to /app instead of /app/backend causing LangGraph to not find config in the container path.
insights.root_cause: Incorrect container-side mount target path used in compose file.
insights.solution: Changed mounts to /app/backend/config.yaml and /app/backend/extensions_config.json and updated respective env var defaults.
insights.lesson_learned: Ensure container-side mount paths match service expectations; small path mismatches break config discovery.

insights.title: Enable runtime DNS re-resolution for Docker upstreams
insights.problem: Nginx kept stale container IPs and proxied to dead addresses after container restarts, causing persistent 502s.
insights.root_cause: Upstream server blocks lacked `resolve` directive and zones, so nginx cached DNS results indefinitely.
insights.solution: Added `zone <name> 64k;` and `resolve` flags to upstreams (gateway, langgraph, frontend) so nginx re-resolves DNS at runtime.
insights.lesson_learned: In Docker Compose environments, configure nginx upstreams to re-resolve service DNS to handle container restarts; include zones for proper runtime resolution.

insights.title: Document security risks of improper deployment
insights.problem: Users could unknowingly deploy DeerFlow in publicly accessible environments and expose high-privilege capabilities (system commands, file ops) to unauthorized actors.
insights.root_cause: Project defaults and README previously lacked a prominent, cross-lingual security notice advising local-only deployment and hardening recommendations.
insights.solution: Add a prominent security notice (and translations) recommending local loopback deployments, IP allowlist, auth gateway, network isolation, and staying up-to-date.
insights.lesson_learned: For projects that enable system-level operations, explicit, localized deployment guidance is necessary to reduce risky default deployments.

insights.title: Add translations and docs updates
insights.problem: README lacked links to newly added translations and doc clarifications
insights.root_cause: Translations added separately without updating README links and docs had typos
insights.solution: Add French and Russian README links, fix documentation typos and add Docker permission troubleshooting hints in CONTRIBUTING
insights.lesson_learned: Keep README and CONTRIBUTING in sync with new localization and developer experience changes; small doc fixes reduce friction for contributors.

insights.title: Add build-arg support for UV image and apt mirror; pin Debian variant
insights.problem: Hardcoded uv image and lack of build-arg propagation made builds brittle for restricted networks and overrode Dockerfile ARG defaults when not set at compose time.
insights.root_cause: Compose passed empty UV_IMAGE which overwrote Dockerfile ARG default, and no APT_MIRROR handling existed.
insights.solution: Introduce ARG UV_IMAGE with a default and use a uv-source stage; add APT_MIRROR build arg and logic to replace apt sources when provided; dearmor NodeSource key to fix apt signature errors and copy uv from the uv-source stage.
insights.lesson_learned: Make Dockerfiles robust for restricted networks by exposing registry/mirror build args and avoid unintentionally overriding ARG defaults from compose; handle gpg key dearmor for apt trust.

backend/app/gateway/routers/artifacts.py.short_description: Artifact file retrieval and safe serving endpoints
backend/app/gateway/routers/artifacts.py.description: Provides an API endpoint to fetch artifact files (including extracting files from .skill zip archives) while detecting MIME types and choosing safe response modes (inline, text, or attachment). It includes protections against path traversal, returns proper content-disposition headers, and adds caching for archive extractions.

key_constructs.name: get_artifact
key_constructs.purpose: FastAPI handler to resolve virtual thread paths to disk, handle .skill archive extraction, and return appropriate File/PlainText/Response.

key_constructs.name: _extract_file_from_skill_archive
key_constructs.purpose: Extracts a specific internal file from a .skill zip archive, handling top-level prefix variations.

key_constructs.name: is_text_file_by_content
key_constructs.purpose: Heuristically detects text files by checking for null bytes in a content sample.

key_constructs.name: ACTIVE_CONTENT_MIME_TYPES
key_constructs.purpose: Set of MIME types considered active (HTML/XHTML/SVG) that must always be served as downloads to avoid execution in-app.

key_behaviors: serves artifact files for a thread with safe response modes
extracts and serves files from .skill zip archives
applies content-type and content-disposition rules to avoid XSS

insights.title: Prevent inline rendering of active web content to mitigate stored XSS
insights.problem: Artifacts containing active web content (HTML/XHTML/SVG) could be served inline from the Gateway, enabling stored XSS when users open artifacts served from the application origin.
insights.root_cause: The artifact endpoint permitted inline rendering for content types like text/html and image/svg+xml. Browsers would execute script content when opened inline, leading to potential XSS.
insights.solution: Force Content-Disposition attachment (download) for a small allowlist of active content MIME types and standardize Content-Disposition header construction. Added ACTIVE_CONTENT_MIME_TYPES, helper functions _build_content_disposition and _build_attachment_headers, and logic to always return attachments for these types (including files inside .skill archives).
insights.lesson_learned: When serving generated/static artifacts from the application origin, assume active web content is risky and force download attachments by default. Centralize Content-Disposition header construction to avoid encoding mistakes.

insights.title: Clarify download query param and unify behavior for archives and files
insights.problem: Download behavior was implemented differently across archive-extraction and direct file serving paths, and filename encoding for Content-Disposition was handled inline.
insights.root_cause: Multiple code paths duplicated header encoding and download logic, increasing risk of inconsistent behavior.
insights.solution: Introduce unified header helpers and a single `download` parameter on get_artifact; apply the same attachment logic for archive members and file system responses.
insights.lesson_learned: Unify cross-cutting response header logic to avoid subtle inconsistencies that can lead to security or UX bugs.

backend/packages/harness/deerflow/agents.short_description: Builds the lead agent's system prompt and manages a background cache of enabled skills for prompt injection
backend/packages/harness/deerflow/agents.description:
This module constructs the system prompt template used by the lead agent and injects runtime-aware guidance (skills, subagent rules, mounts, ACP, memory context). A central responsibility is to load the set of enabled skills and render a skills section for the prompt. Because loading skills can touch disk and be slow, the file provides a non-blocking, background refresh worker plus helpers to warm, invalidate, and synchronously read a cached list of enabled skills. The module also contains logic to render detailed subagent guidance (including a hard concurrency limit) that depends on runtime capability discovery (get_available_subagent_names()).

The design separates: (1) prompt generation (templates and section builders) and (2) cache & refresh state for enabled skills. The cache is guarded by threading primitives (Lock, Event) and is updated by a background worker thread. There are convenience wrappers for starting warm-up (prime/warm), async invalidation (refresh_skills_system_prompt_cache_async) and test-friendly synchronous reload helpers. The module is intended to be used by make_lead_agent and the agent creation flow so that the lead agent prompt is populated with currently enabled skills and environment-specific guidance without blocking request threads on disk I/O.

key_constructs.name: _load_enabled_skills_sync
key_constructs.purpose: Synchronous wrapper around load_skills(enabled_only=True) that returns a list of Skill objects
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by the background worker and synchronous helpers to perform the actual I/O-bound skill loading off the request path; callers must treat it as potentially slow and wrapped by thread-based concurrency primitives.

key_constructs.name: _start_enabled_skills_refresh_thread
key_constructs.purpose: Starts a daemon thread that runs _refresh_enabled_skills_cache_worker
key_constructs.reasoning: Encapsulates thread creation; callers should not start multiple overlapping workers and must use the module-level lock/event helpers to coordinate worker lifecycle.

key_constructs.name: _refresh_enabled_skills_cache_worker
key_constructs.purpose: Background worker that loads enabled skills and writes the module-level _enabled_skills_cache; handles concurrent invalidation while loading
key_constructs.reasoning: Contains the core logic to ensure the cache eventually converges to the most recent invalidation version. Important for avoiding races where multiple invalidations occur while a load is in progress.

key_constructs.name: _ensure_enabled_skills_cache
key_constructs.purpose: Ensure the background refresh worker is active or return an Event that signals when the cache is ready
key_constructs.reasoning: Clients call this to either get an immediate ready event when cache present or to start the refresh worker. It's central to warm/wait semantics and must not be bypassed.

key_constructs.name: _invalidate_enabled_skills_cache
key_constructs.purpose: Invalidate the current cache, bump the refresh version, and (if needed) start the background refresh worker
key_constructs.reasoning: Used when the system needs to force a reload of enabled skills (for example when skills are added/removed). It also clears the memoized prompt section helper cache. Editors must preserve the versioning and event semantics to avoid parallel worker explosions.

key_constructs.name: prime_enabled_skills_cache
key_constructs.purpose: Convenience function to trigger cache warm-up without waiting
key_constructs.reasoning: Intended to be used at process startup to start background warming; it should not block but should trigger the worker via _ensure_enabled_skills_cache.

key_constructs.name: warm_enabled_skills_cache
key_constructs.purpose: Optionally wait (with timeout) for the enabled-skills cache to be populated
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by code paths or tests that can tolerate waiting for the cache to be ready. It relies on the threading.Event returned by _ensure_enabled_skills_cache and logs a warning on timeout.

key_constructs.name: _get_enabled_skills
key_constructs.purpose: Return a copy of the cached enabled skills or trigger a background load and return an empty list if not ready
key_constructs.reasoning: Provides a resilient getter that avoids raising on I/O failures and avoids blocking the request path. Callers should handle empty lists as a transient state and can use warm_enabled_skills_cache if they require deterministic content.

key_constructs.name: _build_subagent_section
key_constructs.purpose: Return the subagent guidance section of the system prompt, including a hard limit on concurrent `task` calls and examples that vary by whether bash subagent is available
key_constructs.reasoning: This section encodes important runtime-enforced behavior (max concurrent tasks). It queries get_available_subagent_names() and formats the section accordingly, so it must reflect actual runtime subagent configuration.

key_constructs.name: _build_skill_evolution_section
key_constructs.purpose: Return the optional Skill Self-Evolution guidance block when skill evolution is enabled in config
key_constructs.reasoning: This text is included conditionally and must be included/excluded consistently with the get_skills_prompt_section cache signature to avoid stale wording in cached sections.

key_constructs.name: SYSTEM_PROMPT_TEMPLATE
key_constructs.purpose: A large formatted template string used to assemble the system prompt; placeholders are filled with soul, memory, skills, subagent, mounts, ACP and other sections
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the authoritative prompt structure. Any change to variables inserted here (names, placeholder tokens) will affect how agents interpret context and must preserve existing placeholders unless every caller and test is updated.

key_constructs.name: get_skills_prompt_section
key_constructs.purpose: Return the rendered skills section for the system prompt; supports filtering via available_skills and memoizes output with lru_cache
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the public helper used by apply_prompt_template to insert enabled skills into the prompt. It must respect the cache semantics and include relevant config toggles (skill evolution) in the memoization key so toggle changes take effect when expected.

key_constructs.name: apply_prompt_template
key_constructs.purpose: Assemble the full system prompt by filling SYSTEM_PROMPT_TEMPLATE placeholders with dynamic sections (skills, mounts, ACP, memory, subagent, etc.) and agent-specific values
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by make_lead_agent during agent creation. Tests assert that this function picks up runtime config (e.g., custom mounts) and the available_skills passed by the caller.

key_constructs.name: clear_skills_system_prompt_cache
key_constructs.purpose: Public invalidation helper that triggers cache invalidation and starts refresh if necessary
key_constructs.reasoning: Exposed so callers (or external events) can request an immediate cache refresh. Must preserve the event/version semantics to avoid spawning multiple workers.

key_constructs.name: refresh_skills_system_prompt_cache_async
key_constructs.purpose: Async wrapper that invalidates the cache using a thread and waits for completion in an async context
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by async code paths and tests (anyio.run) to synchronously wait for the background refresh without blocking the event loop.

key_constructs.name: _reset_skills_system_prompt_cache_state
key_constructs.purpose: Test helper to reset internal cache and synchronization state to a clean starting point
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by tests to ensure deterministic behavior across runs; editors must not remove or change its semantics as tests rely on it.

key_behaviors: renders system prompt with dynamic sections (skills, mounts, subagent rules)
maintains a background cache of enabled skills and supports warm/invalidate semantics
exposes async-friendly cache invalidation for coroutine code paths

pitfalls.mistake: Starting or invoking skill reloads directly on the main thread (i.e., performing synchronous disk I/O in request handlers)
pitfalls.consequence: Slow/blocking request handling and potential timeouts during agent creation or requests
pitfalls.prevention: Use the module's background refresh helpers (prime/warm/invalidate) and rely on _get_enabled_skills to return cached copy; avoid calling _load_enabled_skills_sync from request code.

pitfalls.mistake: Altering the versioning/event logic or removing Event/Lock coordination
pitfalls.consequence: Can spawn multiple concurrent background workers loading skills, causing unnecessary parallel I/O and race conditions; tests assert max_active_loads == 1
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the _enabled_skills_refresh_version, _enabled_skills_refresh_active flag, and use _start_enabled_skills_refresh_thread via the helper functions to coordinate starts/stops.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing the memoization key for get_skills_prompt_section without invalidating/clearing the lru_cache
pitfalls.consequence: Cached prompt sections may not reflect configuration toggles like skill_evolution or different available_skills filters
pitfalls.prevention: When modifying any inputs used in the cached computation, ensure _get_cached_skills_prompt_section.cache_clear() is invoked where appropriate or include the new toggle in the cache key.

pitfalls.mistake: Editing SYSTEM_PROMPT_TEMPLATE placeholders or semantics without updating callers/tests
pitfalls.consequence: make_lead_agent and tests that expect certain placeholder names or injected sections may break; prompt formatting and agent behavior can change unexpectedly
pitfalls.prevention: Treat the template as authoritative and update dependent code/tests when changing placeholder names or required inserted sections.

key_sections: _ensure_enabled_skills_cache: starts/returns Event for cache warm-up
_refresh_enabled_skills_cache_worker: core loading loop and versioned invalidation handling
get_skills_prompt_section: how skills are formatted and memoized into prompt
apply_prompt_template: assembly of SYSTEM_PROMPT_TEMPLATE with runtime sections
skip_unless_needed: _build_skill_evolution_section: static block text
SYSTEM_PROMPT_TEMPLATE: long template text (read when changing prompt wording)

relevant_snippets.description: Covers custom mounts rendering, apply_prompt_template inclusion, cache warm/invalidate behavior, concurrent refresh worker guarding, and timeout logging.
Covers filtering behavior of get_skills_prompt_section, skill evolution inclusion, cache sensitivity to the skill_evolution toggle, and make_lead_agent skill propagation.

related_files.relationship: Defines the Skill dataclass/type used by this module (Skill instances are loaded and cached)
related_files.reason_to_check: If Skill's structure changes (fields or names), the prompt formatting and memoization signature may need updates.

related_files.relationship: load_skills implementation is delegated here; prompt cache relies on its behavior
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to load_skills behavior (signature, exceptions) affect cache worker error handling and tests that monkeypatch load_skills.

related_files.relationship: Agent config (get_agent_soul etc.) and app config are used to build the prompt and conditionally include sections (ACP, mounts, skill_evolution)
related_files.reason_to_check: Config field changes (sandbox.mounts, skill_evolution toggle) alter prompt sections and tests that assert presence/absence of those sections.

related_files.relationship: Exposes get_available_subagent_names() which is used to render subagent guidance
related_files.reason_to_check: Subagent availability affects wording and examples in the subagent section.

insights.title: Make skills prompt cache refresh non-blocking (background worker + sync helpers)
insights.problem: Loading enabled skills at import time or blocking on disk I/O can slow startup and cause request path to do filesystem work synchronously.
insights.root_cause: Prompt module previously read skills synchronously during import or when cache invalidated, blocking request threads.
insights.solution: Introduce background refresh worker thread, threading.Lock and Event to manage an _enabled_skills_cache with async helper refresh_skills_system_prompt_cache_async that delegates to thread. Provide prime_enabled_skills_cache() at import to start warm-up without blocking, warm_enabled_skills_cache() with timeout to optionally wait, and invalidate logic.
insights.lesson: Cache IO-heavy resources using a background worker + synchronization primitives; provide async wrappers for request code to invalidate/refresh without blocking. Tests should include timing/timeouts for warm-up.

insights.title: Cache prompt section with LRU and signature to support memoization and skill-evolution section toggles
insights.problem: get_skills_prompt_section previously reloaded skills repeatedly; toggling skill_evolution required cache invalidation nuance.
insights.root_cause: Cache key was not sensitive to config toggles (skill_evolution) and repeated loads could be expensive.
insights.solution: Use lru_cache on a helper _get_cached_skills_prompt_section keyed by a skill_signature tuple, include skill_evolution_section in caching inputs, and expose clear/invalidate helpers.
insights.lesson: When caching generated prompt sections, use stable signatures and include config toggles in cache key; expose deterministic cache invalidation for mutations.

insights.title: Guard skill loading and support per-agent skill filtering
insights.problem: Loading skills at prompt build time could raise exceptions and break prompt construction or leak large I/O into the system prompt; also, there was no stable way to restrict skills per-agent.
insights.root_cause: Direct calls to load_skills(enabled_only=True) without defensive handling and no parameterization for available_skills in prompt generation.
insights.solution: Introduce _get_enabled_skills() with try/except and use it in get_skills_prompt_section; accept an available_skills set param to filter which skills are included in the system prompt (per-agent skill filtering).
insights.lesson_learned: Prompt-building code must be robust against file/IO failures and support explicit filtering hooks (e.g., per-agent available_skills) rather than assuming global state.

insights.title: Surface configured sandbox mounts to agents in prompt
insights.problem: Agents assumed files under /mnt/user-data and were unaware of custom mounted container paths configured via sandbox.mounts.
insights.root_cause: Prompt did not include configured container mount paths.
insights.solution: Added _build_custom_mounts_section to read get_app_config().sandbox.mounts and render a prompt section listing container_path and access (read-only/read-write); integrated into apply_prompt_template.
insights.lesson_learned: Make agents aware of environment-level mount points by including configuration-aware prompt sections so tools and agent reasoning can use the right absolute container paths.

insights.title: Replace print() with structured logging
insights.problem: print() used for error reporting in library code; unsuitable for services and inconsistent with logging policy.
insights.root_cause: Print statements produce unstructured output and are not controllable via logging configuration.
insights.solution: Introduce module logger = logging.getLogger(__name__) and use logger.error for error messages.
insights.lesson_learned: Use logging in library/service code for appropriate log levels and integration with observability systems.

insights.title: Dynamically include ACP agent guidance in system prompt
insights.problem: ACP guidance was being injected into prompts regardless of whether ACP agents were configured, polluting prompts for users who don't use ACP.
insights.root_cause: ACP instructions were static in the prompt; no runtime check existed to include them conditionally.
insights.solution: Add _build_acp_section() that checks deerflow.config.acp_config.get_acp_agents and returns empty string when none configured. Inject acp_section into apply_prompt_template formatting.
insights.lesson_learned: Conditionally include integration-specific guidance in system prompts only when those integrations are configured to avoid confusion and extra LLM tokens.

insights.title: Preserve Windows-style host paths and validate thread_id
insights.problem: Host path joining used Path which could normalize Windows paths incorrectly and thread_id validation occurred at multiple places.
insights.root_cause: Using pathlib.Path for host-side paths mixed separators; thread_id validation duplicated inconsistently.
insights.solution: Added PureWindowsPath-aware join_host_path helper and host_* methods that return raw strings for bind mounts; centralized thread_id validation via _validate_thread_id and ensured host_base_dir fallback environment handling.
insights.lesson_learned: When generating host-side bind mount strings, avoid coercing to Path/str in ways that change format; centralize validation for identifiers used in filesystem paths.

insights.title: Add delete_thread_dir to Paths for thread cleanup
insights.problem: Paths lacked a canonical method to remove persisted thread directories
insights.root_cause: No helper existed to perform idempotent recursive deletion of thread directories
insights.solution: Add delete_thread_dir(thread_id) that computes thread_dir and uses shutil.rmtree if exists
insights.lesson_learned: Centralize filesystem path operations in a Paths helper and provide idempotent deletion helpers for lifecycle operations; this simplifies API handlers and tests.

backend/packages/harness/deerflow/tools/.description: Determines which tools (builtins, MCP, ACP, subagent tools) should be exposed to agents given application configuration, model capabilities, and runtime flags. Handles conditional inclusion (vision tools, host bash disabling, tool search/MCP integration) and resolves tool class objects dynamically.

insights.title: Hide host-bash tools by default when LocalSandboxProvider is active
insights.problem: Host bash tool was exposed by default even when LocalSandboxProvider does not allow host bash execution.
insights.root_cause: get_available_tools resolved tools only by config entries and did not filter by sandbox security settings.
insights.solution: Added _is_host_bash_tool() detector and filtered tool_configs if is_host_bash_allowed(config) is False, so host-bash tool(s) are not loaded unless explicitly allowed or using an AIO sandbox.
insights.lesson_learned: Tool exposure must consider runtime sandbox configuration; prefer opt-in for dangerous tool surfaces.

insights.title: Stable IDs for resizable panels to preserve layout per-route
insights.problem: Resizable panels used non-stable/duplicate IDs which caused layout/restore issues when navigating between routes.
insights.root_cause: Panel group IDs were not based on a stable deterministic key; multiple instances could collide.
insights.solution: Use pathname-derived sanitized base to create stable ids for the resizable panel group and separator.
insights.lesson_learned: When creating DOM ids for stateful UI components, base them on a stable route-specific key (e.g., pathname) to avoid cross-instance collisions.

Implements searching/filtering, CRUD operations for facts, import/export flows, and UI dialogs for confirmation and editing using core memory hooks and APIs.

insights.title: UI support for memory export/import with validation and confirm flow
insights.problem: Users needed a way to export and import memory JSON backups from UI with validation.
insights.root_cause: Memory management UI had no import/export controls.
insights.solution: Added export button that downloads JSON file, import flow that validates JSON shape before showing confirm dialog, and calls importMemory mutation; added UI i18n strings and file input handling.
insights.lesson_learned: Validate imports client-side for schema shape before sending to backend; provide confirm dialog to prevent accidental overwrite.

insights.title: Add UI for manual create and edit memory facts
insights.problem: Memory settings lacked ability to add or edit facts manually in the frontend.
insights.root_cause: Frontend had only delete/clear and read-only summaries.
insights.solution: Added form dialog UI, create/update hooks, API functions, translations, validation and optimistic updates. Hooked createMemoryFact / updateMemoryFact to backend endpoints.
insights.lesson_learned: Frontend must mirror backend validation rules (non-empty content, confidence in [0,1]) and keep localized labels and placeholders; use shared types for inputs and patches.

backend/packages/harness/deerflow/agents.description: Holds multi-turn prompt templates used to instruct the memory manager to extract facts and update memory, plus helpers to format memory and conversations for injection into system prompts. Also contains token-counting utilities to fit facts within token budgets and confidence coercion utilities.

key_constructs.name: _count_tokens

insights.title: Inject longTermBackground into memory prompt
insights.problem: format_memory_for_injection omitted longTermBackground from history when building prompt injection, causing user's long-term background to be invisible to the agent.
insights.root_cause: Function only processed recentMonths and earlierContext and did not include longTermBackground even though it was persisted.
insights.solution: Read history_data['longTermBackground'] and append its summary as 'Background: ...' to history sections.
insights.lesson_learned: Memory insertion must consider all persisted history sections; missing a field can silently remove important user context.

insights.title: Structured reflection and correction guidance added to memory prompt
insights.problem: Memory extraction didn't explicitly instruct the LLM to surface corrections or include sourceError when appropriate.
insights.root_cause: Prompt lacked explicit instructions for recording errors/corrections in facts.
insights.solution: Added structured reflection steps for error/retry detection, user correction detection, use of 'correction' category, guidance for setting sourceError, and formatting changes in memory injection formatting to include sourceError as '(avoid: ...)' for correction facts.
insights.lesson_learned: Prompts should give explicit instructions for edge-case categories (like corrections) to ensure consistent structured output from the model.

backend/packages/harness/deerflow/agents.short_description: Middleware that converts tool exceptions into error ToolMessages and builds runtime middleware sets
backend/packages/harness/deerflow/agents.description: Defines a middleware that catches exceptions from tool calls and returns error ToolMessage objects to keep agent runs robust; also provides helper functions to assemble ordered runtime middleware lists used by lead agents and subagents. Integrates guardrails and other runtime middlewares and preserves LangGraph control-flow exceptions.

key_constructs.name: ToolErrorHandlingMiddleware
key_constructs.purpose: AgentMiddleware that wraps/safely handles tool calls, logging exceptions and returning standardized error ToolMessage objects.

key_constructs.name: _build_runtime_middlewares
key_constructs.purpose: Compose and return a shared list of runtime middlewares (thread data, sandbox, guardrails, audit, error handling) based on flags.

key_constructs.name: build_lead_runtime_middlewares
key_constructs.purpose: Convenience wrapper to build middleware list for the lead agent with uploads enabled.

key_constructs.name: build_subagent_runtime_middlewares
key_constructs.purpose: Convenience wrapper to build middleware list for subagents with appropriate flags.

key_behaviors: catch tool execution exceptions and convert them into error ToolMessages
assemble ordered middleware stacks for lead and subagent runtimes
preserve LangGraph control-flow signals when rethrowing

insights.title: Enable DanglingToolCallMiddleware patch for subagent runtimes
insights.problem: DanglingToolCallMiddleware (patch) was disabled for subagents which could allow stray tool call state to persist in subagent runs.
insights.root_cause: build_subagent_runtime_middlewares passed include_dangling_tool_call_patch=False for subagent runtime.
insights.solution: Enable include_dangling_tool_call_patch=True for subagent runtime middleware builders.
insights.lesson_learned: Middleware that guards tool-call lifecycle should be applied consistently for subagent runtimes to prevent dangling state; ensure runtime variants reuse same safety middleware.

insights.title: Inject GuardrailMiddleware into runtime middleware chain
insights.problem: Need to include guardrail middleware conditionally during middleware construction based on config.
insights.root_cause: Middleware chain is built at runtime and must accommodate optional features configured in config.yaml.
insights.solution: During _build_runtime_middlewares, load guardrails config, resolve provider class via resolve_variable, instantiate with optional 'framework' kwarg detection, and append GuardrailMiddleware(provider, fail_closed, passport) at correct chain position.
insights.lesson_learned: When instantiating third-party/provider classes dynamically, inspect constructor signature to decide which compatibility kwargs to inject (e.g., framework). Ensure middleware ordering is preserved.

backend/packages/harness/deerflow/commun.short_description: Manages lifecycle and pooling of AIO sandbox containers/backends
backend/packages/harness/deerflow/commun.description: Implements an AioSandboxProvider that composes a backend to provision sandboxes (local containers or remote provisioner), providing deterministic sandbox IDs per thread, warm-pool caching, cross-process file locking, idle timeout eviction, and mount computation for thread data and skills. Handles graceful shutdown, signal handling, and environment variable resolution for container configuration.

key_constructs.name: AioSandboxProvider
key_constructs.purpose: Primary provider class managing sandbox acquisition, warm pool, lifecycle, idle eviction, discovery and backend orchestration.

key_constructs.name: _deterministic_sandbox_id
key_constructs.purpose: Generate a deterministic short sandbox id from a thread id to allow cross-process discovery.

key_constructs.name: _get_thread_mounts
key_constructs.purpose: Return host->container mount tuples for a thread's workspace, uploads, outputs, and ACP workspace, creating dirs as needed.

key_constructs.name: _discover_or_create_with_lock
key_constructs.purpose: Cross-process file-lock protected logic to discover existing sandbox containers or create new ones safely.

key_constructs.name: _cleanup_idle_sandboxes
key_constructs.purpose: Background logic to detect and destroy idle active sandboxes and warm-pool containers.

key_behaviors: acquire and reuse thread-dedicated or anonymous sandboxes
reclaim warm-pool containers to avoid cold starts
evict and destroy idle sandboxes and warm pool entries
map thread directories into sandbox mounts and resolve host paths

insights.title: Expose host-side paths using Paths helpers for Windows-aware mount sources
insights.problem: Docker bind mounts on Windows required host-side paths in Windows-style; provider was converting Paths to POSIX strings incorrectly.
insights.root_cause: Previously used Paths(base_dir=...) and str(Path(...)) which could alter Windows drive/path format.
insights.solution: Return host paths using get_paths() helper methods that now expose host_* methods (host_sandbox_work_dir, etc.) which preserve Windows-style path joining.
insights.lesson_learned: When producing host bind mount sources, preserve native path style (Windows vs POSIX) to avoid Docker Desktop resolving failures.

insights.title: Platform-agnostic file locking and conditional unlock
insights.problem: Exclusive file locking used fcntl unconditionally which breaks on Windows, and unlock was always attempted even if lock acquisition failed.
insights.root_cause: Original code assumed POSIX fcntl availability and didn't guard unlocking when lock acquisition raised an exception.
insights.solution: Introduce platform fallback: import fcntl inside try/except and use msvcrt on Windows. Add _lock_file_exclusive and _unlock_file helpers and only call unlock if lock succeeded (track via local 'locked' flag).
insights.lesson_learned: Cross-platform locking must handle OS differences and ensure cleanup/unlock is only attempted when a lock was actually acquired to avoid masking lock failures.

backend/app/gateway/routers/suggestions..short_description: API endpoint to generate follow-up question suggestions from conversation
backend/app/gateway/routers/suggestions..description: FastAPI router exposing an endpoint that uses a chat model to generate exactly N concise follow-up questions given recent conversation messages. It formats messages, calls the chat model, extracts JSON arrays from potentially fenced responses, and returns cleaned suggestions.

key_constructs.name: _parse_json_string_list
key_constructs.purpose: Helper to parse a JSON array of strings from a possibly fenced or noisy model output.

key_behaviors: generate concise follow-up question suggestions from conversation
sanitize and parse model outputs into JSON arrays
support optional per-request model override

insights.title: Use structured messages and async invocation for follow-up suggestions
insights.problem: Some model backends require SystemMessage and HumanMessage objects (not a single combined string) and require async model invocation (ainvoke). Using a plain string + model.invoke caused models (e.g. MiniMax variants) to ignore system instructions and fail to generate correct follow-up questions.
insights.root_cause: Using a single combined string and synchronous invoke() instead of structured message objects and asynchronous API led to misinterpretation by some model adapters.
insights.solution: Construct SystemMessage and HumanMessage objects and call await model.ainvoke([...]).
insights.lesson_learned: When calling chat models, use the provider-appropriate message objects and prefer async APIs when available; tests must cover both content shaping and invocation type.

insights.title: Raise meaningful error when no retry attempts configured
insights.problem: In retry loops, code did raise last_exc but could pass None raising confusing type errors; callers expecting a raised exception needed meaningful error.
insights.root_cause: Code unconditionally raised last_exc which might be None if no attempts ran.
insights.solution: If last_exc is None, raise a RuntimeError('... without an exception') for clarity; otherwise re-raise last_exc.
insights.lesson_learned: Retry loops must handle the case where no attempt produced an exception and raise a deterministic error rather than None.

backend/app/channels/telegram.py.description: Provides a Telegram bot channel using python-telegram-bot long-polling in a dedicated thread, mapping incoming messages and commands into the internal MessageBus and sending messages/files back (with reply-threading). It enforces allowed users, keeps track of last bot messages for threading, and performs retry/backoff for sends.

key_constructs.name: TelegramChannel
key_constructs.purpose: Channel implementation that runs Telegram polling in a background thread and bridges messages/files to/from the MessageBus.

key_constructs.name: _run_polling
key_constructs.purpose: Starts and runs the Telegram event loop and polling lifecycle inside a dedicated thread.

key_constructs.name: _process_incoming_with_reply
key_constructs.purpose: Helper that acknowledges a message with a 'working' reply and publishes inbound messages to the main loop.

key_behaviors: receives Telegram messages and forwards them to the MessageBus
sends replies and files back to Telegram with threading behavior
responds to slash commands and enforces allowed-users policy

insights.title: Raise deterministic error when no retry attempts configured
insights.problem: Same as Slack: raising last_exc may be None and produce unclear error.
insights.root_cause: last_exc may be None when _max_retries==0.
insights.solution: Raise RuntimeError if last_exc is None, else raise last_exc.
insights.lesson_learned: See Slack note.

backend/packages/harness/deerflow/mcp/to.description: Discovers and loads LangChain tools from configured MCP servers using langchain-mcp-adapters, injects initial OAuth headers, and patches coroutine-only tools with synchronous wrappers so they can be invoked in sync contexts. It also uses a global thread pool to avoid nested event loop issues and gracefully shuts down that executor at process exit.

key_constructs.name: get_mcp_tools
key_constructs.purpose: Asynchronously loads and returns LangChain BaseTool instances from configured MCP servers, applying OAuth headers and interceptors.

key_constructs.name: _make_sync_tool_wrapper
key_constructs.purpose: Creates a synchronous wrapper around an async tool coroutine, using a thread pool when the current event loop is running.

key_constructs.name: _SYNC_TOOL_EXECUTOR
key_constructs.purpose: Global ThreadPoolExecutor used to run async tool coroutines in a separate thread to avoid nested event loop issues.

key_behaviors: loads tools from MCP servers and returns them to the runtime
wraps async-only tools for sync invocation
injects OAuth headers and interceptors for secure MCP communication

insights.title: Sync wrapper for async-only MCP tools
insights.problem: DeerFlowClient (synchronous streaming) attempted to call MCP tools that only expose an async coroutine — causing NotImplementedError or runtime loop errors when invoked synchronously
insights.root_cause: Some MCP adapters expose only an async coroutine; code expected a sync .func attribute. Also nested event loop situations (asyncio loop already running) caused RuntimeError if asyncio.run() was used directly.
insights.solution: Add _make_sync_tool_wrapper that wraps async tool coroutines into sync callables. It detects a running event loop; if present, it offloads asyncio.run(coro(...)) to a global ThreadPoolExecutor to avoid nested loop errors. Patch StructuredTool instances to add .func when missing.
insights.lesson_learned: When bridging sync and async layers, provide a robust wrapper that handles nested event loops (use a thread pool) and log errors. Also centralize the wrapper so tests can validate running-loop behavior and exception propagation.

backend/packages/harness/deerflow/models.short_description: Custom Claude chat model with OAuth support, prompt caching, and retries
backend/packages/harness/deerflow/models.description: Extends langchain's ChatAnthropic to support Claude OAuth Bearer auth, inject required billing headers, enable prompt caching and auto-thinking budget, and implement retry/backoff for rate limits and server errors. It auto-loads credentials from various sources and patches underlying Anthropic SDK clients to use Bearer tokens when needed.

key_constructs.name: ClaudeChatModel
key_constructs.purpose: Custom chat model subclass that adds OAuth bearer token handling, prompt caching, thinking budget allocation, and robust retry logic for Anthropic/Claude models.

key_constructs.name: OAUTH_BILLING_HEADER
key_constructs.purpose: Billing header string injected as the first system prompt block for OAuth requests; can be overridden by env var.

key_constructs.name: THINKING_BUDGET_RATIO
key_constructs.purpose: Fraction used to auto-allocate thinking budget (80% of max tokens) when thinking is enabled.

key_behaviors: detects and configures OAuth bearer token usage for Claude
injects billing metadata and cache control into model payloads
applies retry/backoff for race conditions and server errors

insights.title: Inject Anthropic OAuth billing header and metadata.user_id
insights.problem: Requests made with Claude Code OAuth tokens (sk-ant-oat*) to non-Haiku models failed with a generic 400 'Error' because the billing-identification block required by Anthropic was missing.
insights.root_cause: Anthropic's Messages API expects an 'x-anthropic-billing-header' text block as the first system prompt entry and a metadata.user_id when using OAuth tokens; the client did not inject this automatically.
insights.solution: Add _apply_oauth_billing to ClaudeChatModel that inserts a billing header block as the first system message (normalizing/removing duplicates) and ensures metadata.user_id is present (generating a stable device_id from hostname and a session_id). Call this when _is_oauth before prompt caching.
insights.lesson_learned: Third-party OAuth tokens can require provider-specific request metadata beyond standard headers—mirror CLI behavior when interoperating with provider services. Normalize injected blocks to avoid duplication and ensure metadata constraints are satisfied.

backend/packages/harness/deerflow/models.short_description: Factory to construct chat model instances from app config
backend/packages/harness/deerflow/models.description: Creates configured chat model instances by resolving model classes, merging configuration, and toggling thinking/reasoning options according to runtime flags and model capabilities. It also attaches tracing callbacks when enabled and handles provider-specific adjustments (e.g., Codex mapping).

key_constructs.name: create_chat_model
key_constructs.purpose: Builds and returns a chat model instance using app config, resolving class, merging settings, and enabling/disabling 'thinking' features.

callers.context: model = create_chat_model(name=model_name, thinking_enabled=False) if model_name else create_chat_mo
model = create_chat_model(name=model_name, thinking_enabled=False) if model_name else create_chat_mo

key_constructs.name: _deep_merge_dicts
key_constructs.purpose: Recursively merges two dictionaries to combine configuration without mutating inputs.

key_constructs.name: _vllm_disable_chat_template_kwargs
key_constructs.purpose: Helper to construct payload that disables thinking for vLLM/Qwen chat template kwargs.

key_behaviors: constructs model instances from configuration
merges and normalizes thinking-related settings
attaches tracing callbacks to model instances

insights.title: Normalize thinking toggle across provider types (vLLM/Hanlder) and merge extra_body safely
insights.problem: Different providers toggle 'thinking' in different places (extra_body.thinking, extra_body.chat_template_kwargs.enable_thinking, top-level thinking). Previous mapping missed vLLM style and could accidentally pass reasoning flags wrong.
insights.root_cause: Provider-specific thinking toggles were not normalized and merging of extra_body could overwrite existing config.
insights.solution: Add _deep_merge_dicts helper, implement _vllm_disable_chat_template_kwargs to map thinking->enable_thinking, apply deep merges into model_settings_from_config, and ensure reasoning_effort is removed when unsupported.
insights.lesson: When applying per-model 'when_thinking_enabled' overrides, merge settings rather than overwrite and normalize legacy field aliases for provider-specific behavior.

backend/packages/harness/deerflow/skills.short_description: Discovers and loads skills from public/custom directories
backend/packages/harness/deerflow/skills.description: Scans public and custom skills directories for SKILL.md files, parses them into Skill objects, and resolves their enabled state from extensions configuration. It returns a sorted list of skills and supports loading from config or a custom path.

key_constructs.name: get_skills_root_path
key_constructs.purpose: Computes the default skills directory root path relative to the repository layout.

key_constructs.name: load_skills
key_constructs.purpose: Walks skill directories, parses SKILL.md files into Skill objects, applies enabled-state from ExtensionsConfig, and returns skills sorted by name.

callers.context: skill = next((s for s in load_skills(enabled_only=False) if s.name == name), None)
updated = next((s for s in load_skills(enabled_only=False) if s.name == name), None)
return [skill for skill in load_skills(enabled_only=False) if skill.category == "custom"]

key_behaviors: discovers skills from public and custom directories
parses skill metadata and returns enabled/disabled state
supports loading skills from alternate paths or config

insights.title: Warn via logger when extensions config load fails
insights.problem: A print-based warning was used when skill extension config failed to load.
insights.root_cause: Inconsistent use of print() for recoverable errors.
insights.solution: Replace print with logger.warning and include exception detail.
insights.lesson_learned: Use warning-level logging for non-fatal configuration issues; this makes it easier to inspect misconfiguration in production logs.

insights.title: Follow symbolic links when scanning custom skills
insights.problem: Custom skills installed as symlinks under skills/custom/ were not being descended into by os.walk(), so SKILL.md files were never found and skills failed to load silently
insights.root_cause: os.walk() defaults to not following symlinks (followlinks=False)
insights.solution: Enable os.walk(..., followlinks=True) when scanning skill category directories
insights.lesson_learned: When scanning user-extensible directories that may contain symlinks (e.g., installed or linked plugins/skills), explicitly enable followlinks or otherwise canonicalize paths to avoid silently missing user-installed content.

backend/packages/harness/deerflow/utils/.short_description: Utilities for converting documents to Markdown and extracting outlines
backend/packages/harness/deerflow/utils/.description: Provides robust conversion utilities for PDFs and other office documents to Markdown, using pymupdf4llm when available or falling back to MarkItDown, with heuristics to detect image-based PDFs and async offloading for large files. It also extracts document outlines/headings from generated Markdown while normalizing bold-heading artifacts.

key_constructs.name: convert_file_to_markdown
key_constructs.purpose: Asynchronously converts supported document files to Markdown, offloading large files to a thread when needed and choosing an appropriate PDF converter.

key_constructs.name: extract_outline
key_constructs.purpose: Parses a Markdown file to extract heading outlines, recognizing standard headings and pymupdf4llm bold-heading patterns.

key_constructs.name: _pymupdf_output_too_sparse
key_constructs.purpose: Heuristic that checks pymupdf output length per page to detect image-based PDFs and trigger fallback conversion.

key_behaviors: converts PDFs and office documents to Markdown
detects image-based PDFs and falls back to OCR-friendly conversion
extracts document headings for outline generation

insights.title: Handle split-bold headings and bold-span artefacts produced by pymupdf4llm
insights.problem: Converted Markdown from pymupdf4llm sometimes emitted adjacent bold spans ("**A** **B**") or split-bold lines ("**1** **Introduction**") — extract_outline previously missed or polluted titles.
insights.root_cause: extract_outline assumed headings either as # or a single **...** block; it didn't merge adjacent bold spans nor recognise split-bold patterns; naive regex risked ReDoS on malformed input.
insights.solution: Add _clean_bold_title() to merge adjacent bold spans and strip wrappers; add _SPLIT_BOLD_HEADING_RE to recognise split-bold numeric+title lines while excluding pure-numeric table rows and cap repetition to avoid ReDoS; adjust extract_outline to use these helpers.
insights.lesson_learned: Post-conversion artefacts are common; cleaning/normalisation and safe regex patterns (bounded repetition, negative lookaheads) are necessary to avoid false positives and ReDoS. Tests must cover typical artifact cases.

frontend/src/components/workspace/artifa.short_description: React component listing thread artifacts with download/install actions

insights.title: Update callsites after getFileIcon signature change
insights.problem: getFileIcon signature changed to accept className; callers needed to pass className to preserve size.
insights.root_cause: Utility API change required coordinated updates in many UI files.
insights.solution: Pass 'size-6' className from artifact-file-list to getFileIcon(file, 'size-6').
insights.lesson_learned: When changing widely-used utilities, search-and-update callsites and run UI smoke tests; consider backward-compatible overloads to ease migration.

backend/packages/harness/deerflow/agents.short_description: Debounced memory update queue for batching conversation contexts
backend/packages/harness/deerflow/agents.description: Implements a thread-safe in-memory queue that batches conversation contexts and processes them after a configurable debounce interval, delegating actual persistence to MemoryUpdater. It supports replacing pending updates per-thread, flush/clear operations for testing, and a global singleton accessor.

key_constructs.name: ConversationContext
