key_constructs.name: MemoryUpdateQueue
key_constructs.purpose: Thread-safe queue with debounce timer that batches and processes memory update contexts using MemoryUpdater.

key_constructs.name: get_memory_queue
key_constructs.purpose: Returns the global singleton MemoryUpdateQueue instance, creating it if necessary.

key_behaviors: queues memory update requests per thread with debounce
batches and processes queued updates via MemoryUpdater
supports flush and reset for testing and shutdown

insights.title: Propagate reinforcement_detected through queue and preserve existing flags for a thread
insights.problem: Queue.add didn't track reinforcement flags; subsequent adds could overwrite previous reinforcement_detected True to False.
insights.root_cause: Queue merging logic only considered correction flags previously.
insights.solution: Add reinforcement_detected to ConversationContext and MemoryUpdateQueue.add merging logic (OR semantics) so once reinforcement detected it persists until processed. Forward reinforcement_detected to updater._process_queue.
insights.lesson: Queue contexts for deferred processing must merge signal flags conservatively (OR) to avoid losing positive signals from earlier messages.

insights.title: Preserve and merge correction_detected flag in memory queue
insights.problem: Correction detection signals could be lost when a queue entry was replaced by a later add() call for the same thread.
insights.root_cause: New queue.add replaced existing queued context without merging the correction_detected flag.
insights.solution: Modified add() to merge existing correction_detected with new flag so a prior correction signal is preserved, and exposed correction_detected on ConversationContext.
insights.lesson_learned: When deduplicating queued work for the same key, merge important flags instead of overwriting them.

insights.title: Use logging instead of print in memory update queue
insights.problem: print() used for queue lifecycle and error messages.
insights.root_cause: Ad-hoc prints were used for informational and error messages.
insights.solution: Add module logger and replace prints with appropriate logger levels (info/debug/warning/error).
insights.lesson_learned: Promote consistent logging usage (and appropriate log levels) across background processing modules so behavior can be tuned via logging config; this also helps tests avoid noisy output.

backend/packages/harness/deerflow/agents.short_description: Middleware that auto-generates thread titles after first exchange
backend/packages/harness/deerflow/agents.description: LangGraph/LangChain agent middleware that generates a concise thread title after the first user-assistant exchange using configured prompts or a local fallback. It normalizes message content, optionally calls an LLM asynchronously for titles, and respects config flags such as enabled/max lengths.

key_constructs.name: TitleMiddlewareState
key_constructs.purpose: AgentState-compatible schema extension that includes an optional title field.

key_constructs.name: TitleMiddleware
key_constructs.purpose: Agent middleware that decides when to generate a title, builds prompts, and produces synchronous or async title updates.

key_constructs.name: _build_title_prompt
key_constructs.purpose: Helper that constructs the model prompt from the first user and assistant messages and title template.

key_behaviors: automatically generates a thread title after the first exchange
falls back to a local title if model generation fails
respects title configuration such as max length and model selection

insights.title: Avoid synchronous blocking LLM calls during middleware hydration
insights.problem: Synchronous title generation path invoked model.invoke(), which can block event loop / thread startup and cause workspace hydration / concurrent thread stalls.
insights.root_cause: Middleware had a synchronous code path that called into blocking LLM invocation; that blocks threads/runtimes expecting non-blocking quick middleware updates.
insights.solution: Change the synchronous path to produce a local fallback title without calling the LLM; restore async title generation via agenerate path that calls model.ainvoke and falls back locally on errors. Improve error-handling and logging.
insights.lesson_learned: Middleware should avoid blocking external network/LLM calls synchronously; prefer async model calls and local fallbacks so the runtime can hydrate/work concurrently.

backend/packages/harness/deerflow/agents.short_description: Middleware that injects viewed image details into the conversation before LLM calls
backend/packages/harness/deerflow/agents.description: Inspects the last assistant message for view_image tool calls and, when all tool calls are completed, constructs and injects a human message containing image metadata and base64 data so the LLM can analyze images. It ensures the injection happens only once per view sequence and supports both sync and async middleware hooks.

key_constructs.name: ViewImageMiddlewareState
key_constructs.purpose: State schema aliasing ThreadState to maintain typed reducer-backed fields like viewed_images.

key_constructs.name: ViewImageMiddleware
key_constructs.purpose: Agent middleware that detects completed view_image tool calls and injects a composed HumanMessage with image details and base64 data.

key_constructs.name: _all_tools_completed
key_constructs.purpose: Checks whether all tool_call IDs from the assistant message have corresponding completed ToolMessage entries.

key_behaviors: detects when view_image tool outputs are ready
injects image content and base64 data into conversation before LLM call
prevents duplicate injections for the same view sequence

insights.title: Return properly formatted content block when no images have been viewed
insights.problem: When no images had been viewed, the middleware returned ['No images have been viewed.'] (an array of strings). OpenAI/other LLM APIs expect message content to be either a string or an array of typed content blocks (objects with 'type'). Returning plain string arrays caused BadRequestError.
insights.root_cause: A plain string array was returned instead of a content block object with 'type':'text'.
insights.solution: Return [{'type': 'text', 'text': 'No images have been viewed.'}] so it matches expected message content shape.
insights.lesson_learned: When building messages for LLM providers, ensure content shapes match provider schema (strings vs typed content blocks). Add tests or schema assertions when constructing provider payloads.

insights.title: Use logger.debug for image injection log
insights.problem: Used print() for injection notice.
insights.root_cause: Legacy prints.
insights.solution: Swap to logger.debug.
insights.lesson_learned: Combine API fixes with consistent logging changes to keep observability coherent.

backend/packages/harness/deerflow/commun.short_description: AIO Docker-backed sandbox client for remote file/exec ops
backend/packages/harness/deerflow/commun.description: Provides a Sandbox implementation that talks to the agent-infra AIO sandbox HTTP API to run shell commands and manipulate files inside a Docker-isolated sandbox. It serializes shell calls to avoid corrupting the container session, exposes file read/write/glob/grep/update operations, and handles retries and basic error normalization.

key_constructs.name: AioSandbox
key_constructs.purpose: Sandbox concrete implementing execute_command, read_file, list_dir, write_file, glob, grep and update_file via the AIO sandbox HTTP client.

callers.context: self._sandboxes: dict[str, AioSandbox] = {}  # sandbox_id -> AioSandbox instance

key_constructs.name: execute_command
key_constructs.purpose: Run a shell command in the remote sandbox with locking and retry on corrupted session.

key_constructs.name: grep
key_constructs.purpose: Search files in the sandbox using regex/literal modes and return truncated GrepMatch results.

key_constructs.name: _ERROR_OBSERVATION_SIGNATURE
key_constructs.purpose: Signature string used to detect corrupt ErrorObservation output and trigger a fresh-session retry.

key_behaviors: executes shell commands inside an AIO sandbox
reads and writes files inside the sandbox
searches and globs files with ignore and truncation logic

insights.title: Serialize concurrent exec_command and retry on ErrorObservation
insights.problem: Concurrent exec_command calls to the AIO sandbox corrupted a single persistent shell session, leading to 'ErrorObservation' outputs and cascading failures.
insights.root_cause: AioSandbox used a single underlying session and didn't serialize concurrent shell execs; outputs indicating corruption were not retried.
insights.solution: Added threading.Lock to serialize shell commands, used shlex.quote for path escaping in list_dir, detect a specific ErrorObservation signature and retry with fresh session id (uuid), and added tests.
insights.lesson_learned: When a remote service exposes a single mutable session, serialize access from within the process and implement narrowly-targeted retry logic for known corruption signatures. Ensure shell command inputs are safely quoted.

backend/packages/harness/deerflow/sandbo.short_description: Local sandbox provider that maps container paths to host paths
backend/packages/harness/deerflow/sandbo.description: Implements a SandboxProvider that returns a singleton LocalSandbox and constructs path mappings from app config (skills directory and configured mounts). It validates mount paths against reserved prefixes and logs warnings for invalid mounts, supporting reuse of a single local sandbox instance.

key_constructs.name: LocalSandboxProvider
key_constructs.purpose: Provider that sets up path mappings and manages a singleton LocalSandbox instance via acquire/get/release methods.

key_constructs.name: _setup_path_mappings
key_constructs.purpose: Builds container-to-local path mappings for skills and configured sandbox mounts with validation and read-only flags.

key_constructs.name: _singleton
key_constructs.purpose: Module-level singleton reference for the LocalSandbox instance to enable reuse across requests.

key_behaviors: provides a persistent local sandbox instance
maps configured host paths into container virtual paths
validates and warns about invalid mount configurations

insights.title: Allow custom mounts via sandbox config with read-only flags
insights.problem: Provider had no way to map additional configured host mounts into container paths and mark them read-only/writable safely.
insights.root_cause: Only skills mapping existed; custom mounts from config.yaml were ignored or not validated.
insights.solution: Return list[PathMapping] instead of dict, include skills mapping as read-only, iterate sandbox_config.mounts and validate host_path absolute, container_path absolute and non-conflicting against reserved prefixes, only add mounts whose host_path exists, respect mount.read_only flag. Log warnings for invalid mounts.
insights.lesson_learned: When exposing extra host directories to the sandbox, validate both host and container paths, filter conflicts with reserved prefixes, and preserve per-mount read-only semantics.

insights.title: LocalSandboxProvider supplies path mappings for LocalSandbox (skills mapping)
insights.problem: LocalSandbox needed to know where the host skills directory is to map /mnt/skills correctly.
insights.root_cause: Skills directory location is derived from config and was not passed into the LocalSandbox singleton.
insights.solution: LocalSandboxProvider._setup_path_mappings inspects get_app_config().skills and maps container skills path to the host skills directory; provider passes mappings into LocalSandbox on creation.
insights.lesson_learned: Providers that instantiate environment-specific helpers should prepare mappings/config and inject them to avoid global lookups and to keep LocalSandbox behavior deterministic.

backend/packages/harness/deerflow/tools/.short_description: Deferred tool discovery and runtime tool schema fetcher
backend/packages/harness/deerflow/tools/.description: Implements an in-request deferred tool registry and a LangChain tool ('tool_search') that performs regex/keyword/name-based searches to return full OpenAI-function-style tool schemas. It uses a ContextVar-scoped registry so each request has an isolated deferred tool list and promotes matched tools to active state once returned.

key_constructs.name: DeferredToolRegistry
key_constructs.purpose: Holds lightweight metadata for deferred tools and supports search and promotion of entries.

key_constructs.name: DeferredToolEntry
key_constructs.purpose: Dataclass storing name, description, and the full BaseTool object for a deferred tool.

key_constructs.name: tool_search
key_constructs.purpose: LangChain-annotated tool function that searches the per-request registry and returns matched tools serialized to OpenAI function JSON.

callers.context: from deerflow.tools.builtins.tool_search import tool_search as tool_search_tool

key_constructs.name: get_deferred_registry / set_deferred_registry / reset_deferred_registry
key_constructs.purpose: ContextVar helpers to manage a request-scoped DeferredToolRegistry so concurrent runs don't interfere.

key_behaviors: registers deferred tools for runtime discovery
searches deferred tools by pattern and returns full schemas
promotes matched tools so they become callable

insights.title: Promote deferred tools after tool_search returns schema
insights.problem: Deferred tools remained filtered out after tool_search returned a tool's schema; the LLM had schema but middleware kept stripping tool from bind_tools so tools could not be invoked.
insights.root_cause: Deferred registry entries were not removed (promoted) when search matched them; DeferredToolFilterMiddleware still filtered them.
insights.solution: Added DeferredToolRegistry.promote(names) to remove entries and invoked registry.promote() in tool_search for matched tools so subsequent calls see the tool active.
insights.lesson_learned: When using deferred-registration or lazy-loading, ensure search/exploration paths transition entries to active state to enable subsequent invocation. Test both registry and middleware integration.

backend/app/gateway/routers/__init__.py.short_description: Router package exports for gateway API modules
backend/app/gateway/routers/__init__.py.description: Collects and exposes the gateway router modules (artifacts, mcp, models, skills, suggestions, threads, thread_runs, uploads, assistants_compat) for the FastAPI application import. Simplifies importing all gateway sub-routers from a single package entry point.

key_constructs.name: __all__
key_constructs.purpose: Defines the public export list for the gateway routers package.

backend/app/gateway/routers/thread_runs..short_description: Run lifecycle endpoints: create, stream, wait, cancel, join
backend/app/gateway/routers/thread_runs..description: Implements LangGraph-compatible run endpoints on top of the RunManager and StreamBridge, allowing clients to create background runs, stream SSE events, wait for completion, cancel runs, and join existing run streams. It returns LangGraph-shaped metadata and uses sse_consumer to stream events in the expected format for the langgraph SDK.

key_constructs.name: RunCreateRequest
key_constructs.purpose: Pydantic model describing the payload to create or stream a run (assistant, input, config, metadata, streaming flags, etc.).

callers.context: async def stateless_stream(body: RunCreateRequest, request: Request) -> StreamingResponse:
async def stateless_wait(body: RunCreateRequest, request: Request) -> dict:

key_constructs.name: RunResponse
key_constructs.purpose: Pydantic model representing a run record returned to clients (run_id, thread_id, status, timestamps).

key_constructs.name: create_run
key_constructs.purpose: Endpoint handler to start a background run and return a RunResponse immediately.

key_constructs.name: stream_run
key_constructs.purpose: Endpoint handler that starts a run and returns an SSE StreamingResponse with Content-Location header for SDK compatibility.

key_constructs.name: cancel_run
key_constructs.purpose: Endpoint to cancel a run with configurable action (interrupt/rollback) and optional wait semantics.

key_behaviors: creates and starts background agent runs
streams run events via server-sent events
cancels or queries run status and joins existing streams

insights.title: LangGraph Platform-compatible runs endpoints (create, stream, wait, cancel, join)
insights.problem: Gateway lacked local Platform-compatible API surface for runs; previously developers used langgraph-cli dev server.
insights.root_cause: Missing implementation prevented Gateway from functioning as a dev replacement for langgraph-cli.
insights.solution: Implemented /api/threads/{thread_id}/runs endpoints including stream/wait/cancel/join with LangGraph-compatible SSE format, Content-Location header, and actions for interrupt/rollback. Uses RunManager and StreamBridge singletons via deps.
insights.lesson_learned: When emulating an upstream API for compatibility, match wire formats, headers, and parameter semantics exactly to support client SDKs (useStream etc.).

backend/app/gateway/routers/threads.py.short_description: Thread CRUD, state and history management endpoints
backend/app/gateway/routers/threads.py.description: Provides thread lifecycle endpoints (create, delete, get, patch, search) integrated with a Store and the LangGraph checkpointer to expose state, metadata, and checkpoint history. It lazily migrates threads discovered in the checkpointer into the Store, derives thread status from checkpoint contents, and cleans local thread filesystem data.

key_constructs.name: create_thread
key_constructs.purpose: Create a new thread record in the Store and write an initial empty checkpoint so state endpoints work immediately.

key_constructs.name: delete_thread_data
key_constructs.purpose: Delete local filesystem thread data, remove store record and checkpoints in a best-effort manner.

key_constructs.name: search_threads
key_constructs.purpose: Search threads via a two-phase approach (fast Store scan then checkpointer supplement) with lazy migration to the Store.

key_constructs.name: _derive_thread_status
key_constructs.purpose: Compute thread status (idle/interrupted/error) by inspecting checkpoint metadata like pending writes and tasks.

key_behaviors: creates and initializes threads with checkpoints
searches and lists threads combining store and checkpointer
deletes thread-local filesystem and checkpoint data

insights.title: Add Gateway endpoint to delete DeerFlow-managed local thread data
insights.problem: When LangGraph threads were deleted via its API, DeerFlow-managed local thread directories under .deer-flow/threads/{id} remained, causing stale on-disk state
insights.root_cause: No coordinated cleanup between LangGraph thread deletion and Gateway-managed filesystem state
insights.solution: Add DELETE /api/threads/{thread_id} route which validates thread_id and removes persisted thread directory via Paths.delete_thread_dir(), returning sanitized 500 on unexpected errors
insights.lesson_learned: When an external service manages primary lifecycle (LangGraph threads), provide a small, safe API to keep local persisted artifacts in-sync; return sanitized errors to avoid leaking internal paths.

frontend/src/app/workspace/layout.tsx.short_description: Workspace layout wrapper providing sidebar and global providers
frontend/src/app/workspace/layout.tsx.description: Next.js layout component that wraps workspace pages with QueryClientProvider, SidebarProvider (reads initial state from cookies), global UI components like the CommandPalette and Toaster, and renders the WorkspaceSidebar and page content inset. It reads a sidebar state cookie to respect previous user preference.

key_constructs.name: WorkspaceLayout
key_constructs.purpose: Async React Server Component that provides global providers and layout structure for workspace pages.

key_constructs.name: parseSidebarOpenCookie
key_constructs.purpose: Helper to parse the sidebar_state cookie to a boolean or undefined for default behavior.

key_behaviors: initializes workspace UI providers and layout
hydrates sidebar state from cookies

insights.title: Register CommandPalette in workspace layout
insights.problem: Command palette component needed to be globally available across workspace pages
insights.root_cause: Component wasn't mounted in layout
insights.solution: Include <CommandPalette /> in WorkspaceLayout
insights.lesson_learned: Place global UI shells (palettes, toasts) in layout to ensure availability across routes.

frontend/src/components/ui/word-rotate.t.description: Client-side React component that cycles through an array of words, animating transitions using motion/react and a custom AuroraText visual. It supports configurable duration and motion properties and cleans up its interval on unmount.

key_constructs.name: WordRotate
key_constructs.purpose: React component that rotates displayed words on an interval and animates transitions with AnimatePresence and motion.

key_behaviors: rotates and animates display of provided words at a set interval

frontend/src/components/workspace/thread.short_description: Document title updater and display for thread titles
frontend/src/components/workspace/thread.description: Updates the browser document.title based on thread state (title, loading, new thread) and renders a FlipDisplay with the thread title when present. It reads localized strings and thread loading state to provide user-friendly title text.

key_constructs.name: ThreadTitle
key_constructs.purpose: React component that updates document.title and conditionally renders the thread's title using FlipDisplay.

key_behaviors: sets browser tab title based on thread state
renders thread title in UI when available

frontend/src/components/workspace/worksp.short_description: Workspace sidebar header with brand and new-chat link
frontend/src/components/workspace/worksp.description: Client-side component rendering the workspace header area, showing brand text, a sidebar trigger, and a menu with a new chat action. It respects a static website mode and uses i18n and sidebar state hooks to adjust appearance and behavior.

key_constructs.name: WorkspaceHeader
key_constructs.purpose: React component showing the brand, sidebar trigger, and new-chat menu item, adapting to collapsed/expanded sidebar states.

key_behaviors: renders workspace header and new-chat menu item
toggles sidebar trigger and respects static website mode

backend/tests/test_suggestions_router.py.short_description: Unit tests for suggestions parsing and model integration
backend/tests/test_suggestions_router.py.description: Tests utility functions and the generate_suggestions workflow in the suggestions router: stripping markdown fences, parsing JSON string lists, formatting conversation, and handling model outputs and errors. Uses monkeypatch to mock model invocation and verifies parsing, limiting, and error-handling behavior.

key_constructs.name: test_strip_markdown_code_fence_removes_wrapping
key_constructs.purpose: Verifies fenced code blocks are unwrapped correctly.

key_constructs.name: test_parse_json_string_list_filters_invalid_items
key_constructs.purpose: Checks JSON list parsing removes invalid/non-string items.

key_constructs.name: test_generate_suggestions_parses_and_limits
key_constructs.purpose: Mocks a model response and ensures suggestions are parsed and capped to n results.

key_constructs.name: test_generate_suggestions_returns_empty_on_model_error
key_constructs.purpose: Confirms that model errors result in empty suggestion lists rather than exceptions.

frontend/eslint.config.js.short_description: Custom ESLint flat config for the frontend TypeScript codebase
frontend/eslint.config.js.description: Configures ESLint with FlatCompat for Next.js core rules and TypeScript-eslint recommendations, sets ignore globs, files patterns, parserOptions, and custom rule overrides tailored to the project's coding style. It integrates stylistic and ordering rules and enables reporting of unused disable directives.

frontend/next.config.js.short_description: Next.js configuration with internal service rewrites and i18n
frontend/next.config.js.description: Exports Next.js config enabling i18n, rewrites to internal LangGraph and Gateway service URLs (unless overridden by public env vars), and integrates Nextra for documentation pages. It also imports an env validation helper and trims trailing slashes from configured internal URLs.

insights.title: Add rewrites for local dev routing to gateway/langgraph
insights.problem: Frontend needed to route langgraph and gateway API calls to local dev services, and allow environment-based overrides.
insights.root_cause: Missing rewrites caused CORS or cross-origin fetch problems during dev.
insights.solution: Added getInternalServiceURL helper, rewrites for /api/langgraph and /api/agents to internal base URLs, and respected NEXT_PUBLIC_* overrides.
insights.lesson_learned: Provide dev-time proxy rewrites for internal services to simplify local development and avoid exposing internals directly to the browser.

frontend/prettier.config.js.description: Simple Prettier config that loads the prettier-plugin-tailwindcss to enforce Tailwind class ordering during formatting. Keeps formatting consistent across frontend code that uses Tailwind CSS.

scripts/check.sh.short_description: Check required developer dependencies (node, pnpm, uv, nginx)
scripts/check.sh.description: Simple CLI script that verifies the presence and minimum versions of developer tools (Node.js >=22, pnpm, uv, nginx). It prints instructions for installing missing tools and exits non‑zero when checks fail.
key_behaviors: verifies required dev tools are installed
reports missing tools and install hints

scripts/cleanup-containers.sh.short_description: Stop and cleanup sandbox containers for Docker and Apple Container runtimes
scripts/cleanup-containers.sh.description: Script to find and stop sandbox containers matching a prefix across Docker and Apple Container runtimes, printing found containers and status. It ensures cross-runtime cleanup so local sandbox containers do not linger after tests or dev runs.
key_behaviors: stops sandbox containers matching prefix
lists containers found and reports status

insights.title: Cross-runtime cleanup script for Docker and Apple Container
insights.problem: Makefile cleanup previously only targeted docker containers and missed Apple Container runtime.
insights.root_cause: New Apple Container support required runtime-aware cleanup.
insights.solution: Add cleanup-containers.sh which inspects docker and container runtimes, lists containers matching prefix and stops/removes them, and is invoked by Makefile targets.
insights.lesson_learned: Tooling should support all supported runtimes; provide a single script for consistent cleanup used by Makefile.

scripts/config-upgrade.sh.short_description: Upgrade and merge user config.yaml with the example template
scripts/config-upgrade.sh.description: Backs up and upgrades an existing config.yaml to match config.example.yaml by applying versioned text migrations and recursively merging missing fields. It uses an embedded Python routine (via uv) to handle YAML parsing, migrations, and to write the updated config while preserving a .bak backup.
key_behaviors: upgrades config.yaml to latest example schema
backs up original config and merges missing fields

scripts/deploy.sh.short_description: Build, start, or stop production Docker Compose services
scripts/deploy.sh.description: Production-oriented deploy helper that builds images, starts or brings down the docker-compose stack and auto-detects runtime modes (standard vs gateway) and sandbox modes. It manages env defaults (DEER_FLOW_HOME, secrets like BETTER_AUTH_SECRET), validates Docker socket for containerized sandbox modes, and prints URLs and usage hints.
key_behaviors: builds docker images
starts/stops production containers
auto-detects sandbox and runtime modes
initializes persistent secrets

scripts/docker.sh.short_description: Manage Docker development environment: init, start, logs, stop
scripts/docker.sh.description: Development helper to initialize (pull sandbox image), start, stop, restart and view logs for the Docker-based dev environment using a docker-compose-dev file. It auto-detects sandbox mode from config.yaml and provides user guidance when images cannot be pulled or Docker is unavailable.
key_behaviors: pre-pulls sandbox image
starts/stops dev compose services
shows service logs
detects sandbox mode

scripts/serve.sh.short_description: Unified local service launcher for dev/prod: LangGraph, Gateway, Frontend, Nginx
scripts/serve.sh.description: Orchestrates starting/stopping/restarting local services for development or production-like runs. It supports dev hot-reload, gateway mode, dependency sync, frontend env patching, waits for ports, runs services in foreground or daemonized background, and provides logs/cleanup utilities.
key_behaviors: starts LangGraph/Gateway/Frontend/Nginx locally
stops and restarts services
synchronizes dependencies and frontend env
supports daemonized runs

insights.title: Load .env file and pass log level to LangGraph server
insights.problem: Environment variables in .env were not being automatically loaded; LangGraph server log level was fixed and not synced with application config
insights.root_cause: serve.sh didn't source .env, and langgraph dev invocation lacked --server-log-level argument
insights.solution: If .env exists, export its variables (set -a / source); read log_level from config.yaml (fallback to env), and call langgraph with --server-log-level
insights.lesson_learned: Startup scripts should load .env for convenience and sync runtime config (log levels) into downstream services to keep logging behavior predictable across dev and prod.

scripts/start-daemon.sh.short_description: Backward-compatible wrapper to start DeerFlow in daemon mode
scripts/start-daemon.sh.description: Thin wrapper that invokes serve.sh with --dev and --daemon to run the DeerFlow stack in the background. It exists for backward compatibility with older tooling that expects a dedicated start-daemon entrypoint.
key_behaviors: starts DeerFlow in background (daemon) via serve.sh

scripts/tool-error-degradation-detection.short_description: Test that tool failures are downgraded and do not abort agent flows
scripts/tool-error-degradation-detection.description: Diagnostic/test script that loads the current middleware chains and simulates a failing tool call (SSL handshake) followed by a successful tool call to verify that a tool exception is downgraded and the conversation continues. It runs an embedded Python test under uv that composes middleware wrappers and asserts error+success outputs for both lead-agent and subagent middleware chains.

key_constructs.name: TOOL_CALLS
key_constructs.purpose: Defines simulated sequential tool calls for the test (one failing, one succeeding).

key_constructs.name: _make_ssl_error
key_constructs.purpose: Constructs a simulated SSLError handshake exception used to force a tool failure.

key_constructs.name: _sync_handler
key_constructs.purpose: Synchronous mock tool handler that raises the SSL error for a specific tool and returns ToolMessage for others.

key_constructs.name: _async_handler
key_constructs.purpose: Asynchronous mock tool handler counterpart to _sync_handler for astream tests.

key_constructs.name: _compose_sync
key_constructs.purpose: Composes sync middleware wrappers into a single callable to run the simulated tool sequence through middleware.

key_constructs.name: _validate_outputs
key_constructs.purpose: Validates that outputs contain an error ToolMessage followed by a success ToolMessage and checks message contents.

key_behaviors: detects whether tool exceptions are downgraded
validates that conversation flow continues after a tool error

scripts/wait-for-port.sh.short_description: Block until a TCP port becomes available (healthcheck)
scripts/wait-for-port.sh.description: Utility that polls multiple platform tools (lsof, ss, netstat, or a raw TCP connect) to determine when a local TCP port is listening, with configurable timeout and friendly messages. Used by other scripts to wait for services to become ready before continuing.
key_behaviors: waits for service port readiness
times out with an error message if port not open

backend/app/channels/base.py.short_description: Abstract base class for instant‑messaging channel implementations
backend/app/channels/base.py.description: Defines Channel, an abstract base for integrating external IM platforms: it wraps inbound events into InboundMessage objects and exposes hooks to send OutboundMessage replies and upload attachments. Concrete channel implementations must implement start/stop/send and may override file handling methods to materialize attachments into the sandbox.

key_constructs.purpose: Abstract base providing lifecycle, inbound factory, outbound handling, and optional file upload hooks for channel implementations.

sends outbound replies to external platforms
uploads attachments when available

backend/packages/harness/deerflow/agents.short_description: Public agent package exports and import-time initialization
backend/packages/harness/deerflow/agents.description: Package initializer that imports and re-exports key agent factories, state types, and checkpointer utilities, and primes the enabled-skills cache at import time to avoid synchronous filesystem hits during prompt imports. It centralizes commonly used agent entrypoints for LangGraph integration.

key_constructs.name: prime_enabled_skills_cache
key_constructs.purpose: Called at import time to warm the enabled-skills cache so request paths don't block on filesystem work.

key_constructs.name: make_lead_agent
key_constructs.purpose: Exported factory for constructing the primary lead agent (imported, not defined here).

key_constructs.name: SandboxState
key_constructs.purpose: Exported type representing sandbox-related thread state for agents.

key_constructs.name: ThreadState
key_constructs.purpose: Exported thread-level state schema for agent runs.

key_behaviors: primes skills cache on import
exposes agent factory and state types for consumers

insights.title: Prime enabled-skills cache at import time
insights.problem: LangGraph imports deerflow.agents on registration; reading skills synchronously could delay startup.
insights.root_cause: Prompt cache not primed, causing request-time blocking filesystem work.
insights.solution: Call prime_enabled_skills_cache() during package import to kick off background refresh without blocking.
insights.lesson: Lightweight non-blocking priming at import time helps common request paths avoid first-request I/O hiccups; ensure priming is safe and non-blocking.

backend/packages/harness/deerflow/config.short_description: Config accessors and exported config types
backend/packages/harness/deerflow/config.description: Re-exports configuration getters and types (app config, paths, skills, tracing and memory config) so callers can import them from a single module. It centralizes configuration API surface for the harness.

key_constructs.name: get_paths
key_constructs.purpose: Provides canonical filesystem paths used by the application.

key_constructs.name: get_memory_config
key_constructs.purpose: Returns memory subsystem configuration.

key_behaviors: provides centralized access to application configuration
exports config-related types and helpers

backend/packages/harness/deerflow/sandbo.short_description: Middleware to allocate and release sandboxes per agent/thread
backend/packages/harness/deerflow/sandbo.description: LangChain/Runtime middleware that acquires a sandbox from the global provider and assigns it to thread state, supporting lazy or eager initialization. It also releases sandboxes after agent runs and logs acquisition/release events, enabling safe sandbox lifecycle management for tools that need file execution or containerized operations.

key_constructs.name: SandboxMiddlewareState
key_constructs.purpose: State schema compatible with thread state that may include sandbox and thread_data entries.

key_constructs.name: SandboxMiddleware
key_constructs.purpose: AgentMiddleware managing sandbox acquisition (lazy or eager) and releasing sandboxes after runs.

key_constructs.name: _acquire_sandbox
key_constructs.purpose: Helper that requests a sandbox ID from the configured SandboxProvider for a thread.

key_constructs.name: before_agent
key_constructs.purpose: Eager-initialization hook to assign a sandbox into state when lazy_init is disabled.

key_constructs.name: after_agent
key_constructs.purpose: Releases sandbox back to provider when present in state or runtime context.

key_behaviors: acquires/reuses sandboxes per thread
releases sandboxes after agent runs
supports lazy vs eager acquisition

insights.title: Guard sandbox middleware against missing runtime.context
insights.problem: SandboxMiddleware attempted to access runtime.context['thread_id'] when runtime.context was None, causing exceptions.
insights.root_cause: LangGraph runtime may supply None as the context; code did not null-check before using it.
insights.solution: Use (runtime.context or {}).get('thread_id') and early-return to preserve original before_agent contract when thread_id missing.
insights.lesson_learned: Always null-check integration points where external runtimes may supply None; preserve middleware idempotency.

insights.title: Null-check runtime.context before releasing sandbox
insights.problem: Accessing runtime.context.get('sandbox_id') without checking runtime.context could raise AttributeError
insights.root_cause: Assumed runtime.context always exists
insights.solution: Check runtime.context truthiness before calling .get()
insights.lesson_learned: Always check runtime.context before accessing keys in middleware that may run in partial setups.

backend/packages/harness/deerflow/skills.short_description: Parse SKILL.md front matter into Skill objects
backend/packages/harness/deerflow/skills.description: Parses a SKILL.md file's YAML front matter (including basic multiline handling) and returns a Skill object with metadata (name, description, license, paths). It is used by the skills discovery/registry to index public/custom skills without requiring a full YAML parser for complex cases.

key_constructs.name: parse_skill_file
key_constructs.purpose: Reads SKILL.md, extracts YAML front matter, handles multiline blocks, validates required fields, and returns a Skill instance or None.

key_behaviors: parses SKILL.md files to extract metadata
returns Skill objects for skill discovery

insights.title: Support multiline YAML strings in SKILL.md frontmatter
insights.problem: Skill frontmatter YAML with folded (>) or literal (|) multiline strings in description or other fields were not parsed correctly by the simple line-split parser.
insights.root_cause: Parser treated frontmatter as simple top-level 'key: value' lines and lacked YAML block folding/literal handling.
insights.solution: Implemented minimal multiline block parsing: detect '>' or '|' markers, accumulate indented lines, support folded vs literal semantics, and finalize at block end. Added tests for folded and literal description cases.
insights.lesson_learned: Frontmatter commonly uses YAML multiline blocks; a minimal parser must handle folded and literal styles or use a YAML library for robustness.

insights.title: Use logger.error for SKILL parsing errors
insights.problem: Parsing errors printed to stdout.
insights.root_cause: Leftover prints.
insights.solution: Introduce module logger and use logger.error when parse fails.
insights.lesson_learned: Parser errors should use error-level logs to be visible in monitoring and CI output.

backend/packages/harness/deerflow/subage.short_description: Engine for running subagents synchronously or in background threads
backend/packages/harness/deerflow/subage.description: Provides SubagentExecutor and related types to run subagents with filtered tools, optional model inheritance, streaming execution, cooperative cancellation, timeouts and background scheduling. It collects AI messages during streaming, marshals final results, manages a background task registry, and enforces concurrency limits for subagent runs.

key_constructs.name: SubagentStatus
key_constructs.purpose: Enum describing lifecycle states of a subagent execution (PENDING, RUNNING, COMPLETED, FAILED, CANCELLED, TIMED_OUT).

callers.context: from deerflow.subagents.executor import SubagentStatus, cleanup_background_task, get_background_task

key_constructs.name: _filter_tools

key_constructs.name: SubagentExecutor
key_constructs.purpose: Core executor that creates an agent for a subagent, streams execution (astream) to capture AI messages, handles timeouts, supports execute/execute_async, and manages background task state updates.

callers.context: from deerflow.subagents import SubagentExecutor, get_available_subagent_names, get_subagent_config

key_constructs.name: execute_async
key_constructs.purpose: Schedules a subagent invocation on a scheduler pool, manages timeouts, updates the global background task registry, and supports cooperative cancellation.

key_behaviors: runs subagents synchronously or asynchronously
streams AI messages and accumulates partial outputs
supports cooperative cancellation and enforces timeouts
manages background task registry and concurrency

insights.title: Add cooperative cancellation for subagent threads
insights.problem: Subagent tasks run in threads (ThreadPoolExecutor) each using their own event loop; cancelling the parent asyncio.Task did not stop the running thread, allowing subagent threads to continue executing (e.g., LLM calls, file writes) after user requested cancellation.
insights.root_cause: Future.cancel() cannot forcefully terminate a running thread and asyncio.Event doesn't cross event-loop/thread boundaries. There was no cooperative cancellation mechanism between the parent and the thread's internal loop.
insights.solution: Introduce threading.Event cancel_event in SubagentResult, check it cooperatively within the _aexecute astream iteration loop (pre-check before starting stream and between chunks). Provide request_cancel_background_task() to set the cancel_event, set it on timeout, and add SubagentStatus.CANCELLED terminal state. Guard status updates with locks to avoid races between timeout handler and cooperative cancellation.
insights.lesson: When running async work inside threads (asyncio.run in thread), implement cooperative cancellation primitives (threading.Event) and check at iteration boundaries. Distinguish user CANCELLED vs TIMED_OUT vs FAILED.

insights.title: Add CANCELLED status and protect status updates with locks
insights.problem: Timeout handler could overwrite CANCELLED, and status updates in different threads could race.
insights.root_cause: Status transitions were not properly synchronized.
insights.solution: Add SubagentStatus.CANCELLED, use _background_tasks_lock around status check-and-set, preserve TIMED_OUT when set, and ensure cleanup recognizes CANCELLED as terminal.
insights.lesson: Explicit terminal states and locking around shared task state prevent races between scheduler/timeouts and cooperative cancellations.

backend/packages/harness/deerflow/subage.short_description: Registry and access helpers for built-in subagent configurations
backend/packages/harness/deerflow/subage.description: Provides functions to look up builtin subagent configurations, apply config.yaml overrides (timeouts, max turns) and expose names filtered by runtime constraints like host bash availability. It centralizes how subagent configs are surfaced to the runtime and logs when overrides are applied.

key_constructs.name: get_subagent_names

key_constructs.name: get_available_subagent_names
key_constructs.purpose: Return names visible to the runtime (filters out bash if host bash is disallowed).

callers.context: from deerflow.subagents import SubagentExecutor, get_available_subagent_names, get_subagent_config

key_behaviors: retrieve subagent configuration with applied overrides
list and filter available subagents based on runtime constraints

insights.title: Expose subagent names based on sandbox host bash allowance
insights.problem: The bash subagent was always listed as available even when host bash was disabled, exposing a capability not runnable in that environment.
insights.root_cause: get_subagent_names returned all built-ins without consulting sandbox policy.
insights.solution: Added get_available_subagent_names() that calls is_host_bash_allowed() and filters out 'bash' when host bash is not allowed; lead_agent prompt uses this to generate clarification/guidance.
insights.lesson_learned: APIs that list capabilities should reflect runtime security policies to avoid confusion and unsafe assumptions; dependent prompts should read capability list dynamically.

frontend/src/components/landing/sections.short_description: Landing page 'What's New' section with feature cards
frontend/src/components/landing/sections.description: React client component that renders a 'What's New in DeerFlow 2.0' section using a MagicBento card grid. It defines the feature card data and composes the Section wrapper to display the bento UI.

backend/packages/harness/deerflow/agents.short_description: Async factory providing a checkpointer context manager
backend/packages/harness/deerflow/agents.description: Provides an async context manager (make_checkpointer) that yields an async Checkpointer implementation based on configuration (memory, sqlite, postgres), handling resource setup and teardown appropriate for async servers. It abstracts backend differences and ensures proper initialization like sqlite parent directory creation.

key_constructs.name: _async_checkpointer

key_constructs.name: make_checkpointer

insights.title: Move ensure_sqlite_parent_dir off the event loop
insights.problem: ensure_sqlite_parent_dir performed filesystem mkdir synchronously on the event loop in async checkpointer setup, potentially blocking the loop.
insights.root_cause: Synchronous filesystem ops executed directly in async provider context.
insights.solution: Call asyncio.to_thread(ensure_sqlite_parent_dir, conn_str) so mkdir runs off the event loop.
insights.lesson: Perform blocking filesystem operations via to_thread when invoked from async code to avoid blocking the event loop; test by mocking asyncio.to_thread.

insights.title: Move SQLite helpers to runtime/store shared utilities
insights.problem: Multiple providers duplicated logic to create parent directories for SQLite connection strings.
insights.root_cause: SQLite path resolution and parent directory creation logic was repeated across checkpointer and store providers.
insights.solution: Refactored to use resolve_sqlite_conn_str and ensure_sqlite_parent_dir from deerflow.runtime.store._sqlite_utils and called ensure_sqlite_parent_dir(conn_str) before opening AsyncSqliteSaver.
insights.lesson_learned: Extract filesystem helpers used across providers to a single utility to avoid divergence and missing directory creation edge cases.

backend/packages/harness/deerflow/agents.short_description: Sync checkpointer singleton and context manager
backend/packages/harness/deerflow/agents.description: Provides synchronous factories for acquiring a Checkpointer: a process-global singleton (get_checkpointer) and a one-shot context manager (checkpointer_context). It supports memory/sqlite/postgres backends, logs selected backend, and exposes reset for tests or reconfiguration.

backend/packages/harness/deerflow/agents.description: Re-exports memory-related prompts, queue, storage and updater APIs so other modules can import memory functionality from a single place. This module centralizes the memory subsystem surface (fact extraction, memory queue, storage implementations and updater utilities).

insights.problem: Other modules need convenient imports for the new storage primitives.

insights.solution: Re-export FileMemoryStorage, MemoryStorage, get_memory_storage from agents.memory package.
insights.lesson_learned: When introducing new subsystems, update package exports so callers can import stable symbols; update tests to import from the intended public API.

backend/packages/harness/deerflow/agents.short_description: Middleware to audit and classify bash commands for security
backend/packages/harness/deerflow/agents.description: An AgentMiddleware that analyzes bash tool calls with regex and shlex parsing to classify commands as block/warn/pass, logs structured audit entries, blocks high-risk commands by returning error ToolMessage, and appends warnings for medium-risk commands. It supports both sync and async tool call hooks and includes robust splitting of compound commands with quote awareness.

key_constructs.name: SandboxAuditMiddleware
key_constructs.purpose: Middleware implementing bash command classification, audit logging, blocking and warning behavior.

key_constructs.name: _classify_command
key_constructs.purpose: Classify a compound command into 'block', 'warn', or 'pass'.

key_constructs.name: _split_compound_command
key_constructs.purpose: Quote-aware splitter to break compound shell commands into sub-commands.

key_constructs.name: _HIGH_RISK_PATTERNS
key_constructs.purpose: Regex patterns that indicate high-risk commands which should be blocked.

key_constructs.name: _MEDIUM_RISK_PATTERNS
key_constructs.purpose: Regex patterns that indicate medium-risk commands which should generate warnings.

key_behaviors: audit bash tool calls and log structured entries
block high-risk shell commands and warn on medium-risk ones

insights.title: Strengthen bash command auditing and add compound splitting
insights.problem: Dangerous commands could be hidden in compound commands (&&, ||, ;) or by using no-whitespace operators, base64 pipelines, command substitution, LD_PRELOAD, /proc/environ and other constructs; previous regex coverage missed many bypasses.
insights.root_cause: Original rule set was narrow (e.g., curl|sh), and the splitter used shlex which failed to separate operators without surrounding whitespace, allowing dangerous subcommands to be hidden.
insights.solution: Expanded high-risk and medium-risk regex patterns (13 high-risk, 6 medium-risk), added targeted command-substitution and base64 patterns, added fork-bomb detection, replaced shlex splitting with a custom quote/escape-aware scanner _split_compound_command, performed whole-command scan before splitting, and classified each sub-command with most severe verdict winning.
insights.lesson: Security detection must consider compound commands and no-whitespace operator forms; perform both whole-command structural checks and per-sub-command checks. Use conservative (fail-closed) behavior when in doubt.

insights.title: SandboxAuditMiddleware: classify and audit bash commands
insights.problem: Local bash tool calls can execute destructive host commands; there was no structured auditing or blocking of dangerous commands.
insights.root_cause: Bash tool exposed a broad shell surface without command classification and logging.
insights.solution: Implemented SandboxAuditMiddleware that classifies commands into high-risk (block), medium-risk (warn + append warning to result), and pass. It logs structured JSON audit entries and ensures high-risk commands return an error ToolMessage without executing the handler.
insights.lesson_learned: Use defense in depth: block known destructive patterns, warn for risky but useful operations, and always audit with structured logs. Ensure middleware applies to both sync and async tool call paths.

insights.title: Integrate SandboxAuditMiddleware in middleware chain
insights.problem: Security middleware needs correct ordering relative to Guardrail and ToolErrorHandling.
insights.root_cause: Middleware ordering determines whether the audit sees original ToolCallRequests and whether ToolErrorHandling would convert blocked results.
insights.solution: Registered SandboxAuditMiddleware after GuardrailMiddleware and before ToolErrorHandlingMiddleware in _build_runtime_middlewares.
insights.lesson_learned: Middleware ordering matters: place security checks before generic error handling so blocked decisions are clear and logged.

backend/packages/harness/deerflow/agents.short_description: Middleware that exposes per-thread filesystem paths for runs
backend/packages/harness/deerflow/agents.description: AgentMiddleware that computes (and optionally creates) per-thread directories for workspace, uploads, and outputs, injecting their paths into the agent state as thread_data. It supports lazy initialization to defer directory creation until needed and resolves thread_id from runtime context or config.

It centralizes run lifecycle operations and exposes errors for conflict/unsupported strategies.

callers.context: from .runs import ConflictError, DisconnectMode, RunManager, RunRecord, RunStatus, UnsupportedStrate

insights.title: Deterministic newest-first list_by_thread using insertion order
insights.problem: Sorting runs by timestamp could be unstable when timestamps tie.
insights.root_cause: Sorting by created_at isn't deterministic when timestamps are identical.
insights.solution: Return reversed insertion-order list (reversed(self._runs.values())) filtered by thread_id to preserve deterministic newest-first order even when timestamps tie; added test for tie-case.
insights.lesson_learned: When insertion order is available and meaningful, use it for deterministic ordering instead of relying solely on timestamps.

backend/packages/harness/deerflow/runtim.short_description: In-memory per-run event stream bridge for SSE/streaming
backend/packages/harness/deerflow/runtim.description: Implements a StreamBridge that holds a bounded in-memory event log per run, lets publishers append events and subscribers replay events from a Last-Event-ID, supports heartbeat entries and an end sentinel, and allows cleanup of per-run buffers. It's used to stream run events to clients (e.g., SSE) without external brokers.

key_constructs.name: MemoryStreamBridge
key_constructs.purpose: In-memory implementation of StreamBridge providing publish/subscribe/cleanup APIs.

callers.context: from .stream_bridge import END_SENTINEL, HEARTBEAT_SENTINEL, MemoryStreamBridge, StreamBridge, Strea

key_constructs.name: _RunStream
key_constructs.purpose: Dataclass storing events, condition variable, ended flag and start offset for a run.

key_constructs.name: _next_id
key_constructs.purpose: Generate monotonic event ids for entries in a run.

key_behaviors: publish events for runs and allow subscribers to replay from offsets
emit heartbeat and end sentinels and clean up buffers

insights.title: Replace per-run asyncio.Queue with in-memory event log supporting replay and bounded retention
insights.problem: MemoryStreamBridge previously used per-run asyncio.Queue which had no replay semantics and could drop END sentinel when queue full causing subscribers to hang indefinitely.
insights.root_cause: Queue-based design dropped events or END sentinel under backpressure; lacked replay and Last-Event-ID handling.
insights.solution: Switch to _RunStream event list + asyncio.Condition, retain bounded history with start_offset, support replay based on Last-Event-ID by resolving start offsets, notify subscribers on new events and ended flag; preserve END delivery and avoid dropping END sentinel. Remove dropped-count counters and queue eviction complexity.
insights.lesson: SSE-like in-memory bridges benefit from an append-only buffer with retention window and condition variable to support replay and guaranteed termination events; design must preserve END/termination semantics even under capacity pressure.

insights.title: Guarantee END sentinel delivery even when queue is full
insights.problem: If the MemoryStreamBridge queue filled up, publish_end() previously could drop the END sentinel (on timeout), causing subscribers to wait forever (hanging SSE connections) and leaking resources.
insights.root_cause: publish() used a timeout/drop strategy for all events; END sentinel used same path and could be dropped when queue full.
insights.solution: When the queue is full, publish_end() evicts oldest regular events (queue.get_nowait()) until there is space, logs eviction, and then enqueues END sentinel. Add _dropped_counts counters and observability helpers dropped_count/dropped_total; cleanup/close clear counters. Add tests for sentinel guarantee and drop counters.
insights.lesson_learned: Termination/END signals are critical — design queue behavior to guarantee delivery of termination sentinels even at capacity, and track dropped regular events separately for observability.

backend/packages/harness/deerflow/subage.short_description: Subagent configuration for bash command specialist
backend/packages/harness/deerflow/subage.description: Defines a SubagentConfig for the 'bash' subagent including system prompt, recommended behavior, allowed sandbox tools, and limits like max_turns. This config instructs how the bash-focused subagent should act and which tools it may use.

key_constructs.name: BASH_AGENT_CONFIG
key_constructs.purpose: SubagentConfig instance describing the bash subagent's prompt, tools, and constraints.

key_behaviors: define behavior, toolset and limits for bash-specialist subagent

backend/packages/harness/deerflow/tools/.short_description: Tool builder to invoke external ACP-compatible agents
backend/packages/harness/deerflow/tools/.description: Constructs a StructuredTool that spawns and interacts with external ACP agents, handling workspace isolation, MCP server wiring, permission auto-approval, subprocess spawning and streamed result collection. It provides error handling for missing binaries and builds a helpful description listing available external agents.

key_constructs.name: _InvokeACPAgentInput
key_constructs.purpose: Pydantic model describing the tool's input schema (agent name and prompt).

key_constructs.name: build_invoke_acp_agent_tool
key_constructs.purpose: Factory that returns a StructuredTool capable of invoking configured ACP agents.

key_constructs.name: _invoke
key_constructs.purpose: Async coroutine (closed over agents) that spawns the ACP agent, manages session, collects streamed text, and returns the final response.

insights.title: Pass per-agent env map to spawn_agent_process and resolve $VAR references
insights.problem: ACP agent invocation did not pass configured environment substitutions to spawn_agent_process; $VAR references in config were not resolved.
insights.root_cause: spawn_agent_process calls lacked env parameter and config had no mapping/resolution logic.
insights.solution: Resolve ACPAgentConfig.env values: if value starts with $, lookup os.environ[varname], otherwise use literal. Pass agent_env (or None if empty) as env keyword to spawn_agent_process.
insights.lesson_learned: When spawning subprocesses, allow explicit env control and keep convention consistent with other config fields (resolve $-prefixed values at invocation time). Pass None when no env provided so subprocess inherits parent environment.

insights.title: Replace os.system pip installs with subprocess.run
insights.problem: Using os.system to run pip install is fragile and can be insecure; exit codes were not being checked
insights.root_cause: os.system does not raise on failure and uses shell invocation
insights.solution: Use subprocess.run([...], check=True) to install packages, ensuring errors raise and shell is not used
constructs: top-level ImportError handlers invoking pip install
insights.lesson_learned: Avoid os.system for package installation or command execution; use subprocess with explicit argument lists and check=True to handle failures safely.

insights.title: Enterprise WeCom channel implementation (WebSocket-based) with streaming and media upload support
insights.problem: Need to integrate WeCom (enterprise WeChat) as an inbound/outbound channel supporting text, mixed, image and file messages, including decryption and streaming reply flows.
insights.root_cause: WeCom SDK differs from other channels and uses WebSocket long connections and AES-encrypted attachments; also requires chunked media upload over WS and streaming replies for partial model output.
insights.solution: Implement WeComChannel class: start/stop lifecycle (WS client), handlers for message.text/mixed/image/file, support AES decryption for files, generate req_id and use reply_stream for streaming replies; implement _upload_media_ws() that chunks files, uses aibot upload commands, and returns media_id. Ensure robust error handling if SDK missing, and align outbound flow to send attachments after text.
insights.lesson_learned: When adding a new IM channel, mirror patterns used by existing channels (start/stop lifecycle, inbound publish to bus, streaming reply patterns), ensure file ingestion & decryption are handled, and test both streaming and final message/file flows.

insights.title: Centralized accessors for app.state singletons (StreamBridge, RunManager, checkpointer, store)
insights.problem: Routers accessed app.state fields directly and repeatedly, scattering 503 checks and lifecycle code.
insights.root_cause: Ad-hoc access to app.state across modules led to duplicated code and fragile startup ordering.
insights.solution: Added langgraph_runtime asynccontextmanager to initialize singletons and get_* helpers (get_stream_bridge, get_run_manager, get_checkpointer, get_store) that raise 503 if missing (except get_store returns None).
insights.lesson_learned: Encapsulate shared runtime singletons and initialization in a small deps module used by FastAPI routers to simplify per-request dependency handling and consistent error codes.

insights.title: Portable home directory resolution and robust FD secret reading
insights.problem: Credential path resolution used Path.home() in ways that could be inconsistent in certain environments; reading secrets from /dev/fd/<n> is not portable.
insights.root_cause: Assumed POSIX /dev/fd path and HOME env behavior; reading via file path failed in some containers or when file-descriptor-backed secrets were provided.
insights.solution: Introduce _home_dir() which prefers $HOME env var then Path.home(). Replace reading /dev/fd/<fd> with os.read(fd, ...) to work across platforms. Use _home_dir() in composing default credential paths.
insights.lesson_learned: When handling credential-loading helpers, prefer robust cross-platform approaches and reading directly from file descriptors rather than path-based /dev/fd assumptions.

insights.title: Credential loading support for Claude Code OAuth and Codex CLI
insights.problem: No robust mechanism existed to auto-load CLI-backed credentials (Claude Code, Codex) causing friction for CLI-authenticated LLM providers.
insights.root_cause: Providers expect CLI-managed credential files or env handoff; previous code didn't implement unified loaders.
insights.solution: Implement credential loader utilities: load_claude_code_credential (env, fd, override path, ~/.codex/.credentials.json) and load_codex_cli_credential (~/.codex/auth.json). Provide structured dataclasses and expiry handling.
insights.lesson_learned: Credential loaders must be defensive (file vs directory, json parse errors), support multiple handoff mechanisms (env, fd, override path), and log sources. Tests should simulate env/FD and file shapes.

insights.title: Harden JSON file handling and directory-checks
insights.problem: Credential paths could be directories or invalid JSON causing exceptions.
insights.root_cause: Assumed file exists and is JSON content.
insights.solution: Add _load_json_file that checks existence, directory vs file, and catches JSON/OSError with warnings.
insights.lesson_learned: When reading user-supplied files, always guard for directory vs file and malformed content; log warnings and return None to allow graceful fallback.

backend/packages/harness/deerflow/models.description: Implements CodexChatModel, a LangChain BaseChatModel that talks to the ChatGPT Codex Responses endpoint using Codex CLI credentials. It handles conversion of LangChain messages/tools to the Responses API format, streams SSE responses, parses tool calls and reasoning, and supports binding tools and retries.

key_constructs.name: CodexChatModel
key_constructs.purpose: LangChain chat model implementation that communicates with the Codex Responses API including streaming and tool-call handling.

retry on transient HTTP errors

insights.title: Preserve streamed Codex output when completed.response.output is empty
insights.problem: When streaming Codex SSE events, the final response.completed event can have an empty response.output even though earlier stream events contained assistant output items. The previous implementation discarded streamed items if response.completed.output was empty, losing assistant content.
insights.root_cause: Only response.completed was used as the final response; streamed 'response.output_item.done' events were ignored and not merged into the completed_response when completed_response.output was empty.
insights.solution: Collect streamed output items keyed by output_index during SSE iteration, then merge them into completed_response.output when response.completed arrives. Prefer completed response output entries over streamed placeholders and filter out non-dict placeholders.
insights.lesson: Streaming protocols may emit final content in intermediate events rather than in the final 'completed' payload — code must merge streamed deltas with final metadata and prefer canonical completed fields when available.

insights.title: CodexChatModel: ChatGPT Codex Responses API support with CLI auth
insights.problem: Need to support Codex Responses endpoint (Codex CLI) including streaming, tool calling, parsing SSE frames, and CLI auth.
insights.root_cause: Codex Responses API differs from OpenAI Chat completions; requires special request/stream/parsing handling and CLI token management.
insights.solution: Implement CodexChatModel that converts LangChain messages to Instructions + input_items, converts tools, streams SSE, parses response.completed, normalizes content, handles function_call and tool_call outputs, validates tool-argument JSON, attaches invalid_tool_calls, and integrates retry/backoff and account headers.
insights.lesson_learned: Custom provider wrappers must carefully normalize LangChain content to the provider's expected shapes, robustly parse streaming SSE frames, and surface malformed tool-call arguments as structured invalid_tool_calls rather than raising. Tests should cover SSE parsing and invalid tool args.

insights.title: Graceful handling of malformed tool-call arguments
insights.problem: Codex API may send non-JSON or malformed 'arguments' fields which would crash parsing and break tool-calling flow.
insights.root_cause: Assumed 'arguments' is a JSON serializable string representing an object.
insights.solution: Add _parse_tool_call_arguments that returns (parsed_args, invalid_tool_call) — on JSON parsing error create structured invalid_tool_call reported to agent rather than exception.
insights.lesson_learned: When parsing remote tool-call payloads, treat parsing failures as structured invalid-tool events so the agent can decide how to proceed.

insights.title: Preserve Gemini 'thought_signature' across requests via OpenAI-compatible gateways
insights.problem: When using Gemini via an OpenAI-compatible gateway with thinking enabled, the gateway requires the 'thought_signature' on tool-call objects to be echoed back on subsequent requests. LangChain's ChatOpenAI serialization dropped those fields, causing 400 INVALID_ARGUMENT errors.
insights.root_cause: LangChain's serializer doesn't include gateway-specific extra fields stored in AIMessage.additional_kwargs (tool_calls), thus signatures were lost.
insights.solution: Add PatchedChatOpenAI that overrides _get_request_payload to re-inject thought_signature (and camelCase variant thoughtSignature) from original AIMessage.additional_kwargs['tool_calls'] into the outgoing payload tool_calls, matching by id or by positional fallback.
insights.lesson_learned: When proxying LLMs via OpenAI-compatible gateways, certain vendor-specific metadata must be preserved in the serialized payload; adapters may be required to maintain API contracts.

insights.title: Extract skill installer with robust zip safety checks and unified errors
insights.problem: Both Client and Gateway implemented zip extraction and validation in slightly different ways; this produced inconsistencies in traversal checks, symlink handling, and zip-bomb defenses.
insights.root_cause: Duplication across layers caused divergent behavior and made security fixes error-prone to apply in multiple places.
insights.solution: Extract installer into harness (install_skill_from_archive) that performs member-level path normalization, absolute path rejection, Windows absolute path detection, symlink skipping, streaming extraction with a real uncompressed size limit, single-resolve traversal checks and returns typed SkillAlreadyExistsError for duplicates. Gateway and Client now call this shared function and map exceptions.
insights.lesson_learned: Move security-sensitive filesystem logic into a single well-tested module to ensure consistent behavior and make audits simpler.

backend/packages/harness/deerflow/skills.description: Provides logic to validate the YAML frontmatter of a SKILL.md file including required fields, allowed properties, naming conventions and length limits. insights.title: Windows compatibility for make dev/start via explicit bash invocation
insights.problem: On Windows (MinGW/Git Bash) direct shell execution in Makefile failed with CreateProcess errors
insights.root_cause: Makefile invoked ./scripts/serve.sh directly; on Windows the SHELL/exec model can break
insights.solution: Detect Windows via $(OS)==Windows_NT and use explicit BASH invocation for dev/start targets (BASH ?= bash)
insights.lesson_learned: Makefiles that shell out to bash scripts should consider Windows environments (Git Bash) and provide a fallback to explicitly call bash to improve cross-platform DX.

It centralizes memory persistence for agent facts and summaries and falls back to a safe default on misconfiguration.

insights.title: Introduce configurable memory storage abstraction with file-backed default
insights.problem: Memory storage logic was embedded in updater module and directly used file operations, making it hard to extend and test alternative storage backends.
insights.root_cause: Tight coupling between memory updater and on-disk JSON file implementation.
insights.solution: Introduce MemoryStorage abstract base class and FileMemoryStorage implementation with methods load/reload/save. Provide get_memory_storage singleton factory that supports configurable storage_class via config (with thread-safe lazy init), caches by agent_name with file mtime checks, performs atomic writes via .tmp replace, validates agent_name against AGENT_NAME_PATTERN, and falls back to FileMemoryStorage on misconfiguration or errors.
insights.lesson_learned: Abstract storage early to permit alternate backends (databases, S3) and centralize caching, validation and error handling. Ensure singleton instantiation is thread-safe and provides clear fallbacks on misconfiguration.

insights.title: InfoQuest client: add image search support
insights.problem: Web image search wasn't previously provided by InfoQuest client.
insights.root_cause: Client only supported web_search and web_fetch; image-specific parameters and result shapes differ.
insights.solution: Add image_search_time_range and image_size config, implement image_search_raw_results and image_search methods, and update clean_results to extract 'original' field for images. Add logging and input validation for params.
insights.lesson_learned: When extending an HTTP client for a new endpoint, mirror existing patterns (headers, params, error handling) and sanitize/validate provider-specific fields. Tests must reflect new JSON shapes.

insights.title: Make JinaClient.crawl async and use httpx.AsyncClient
insights.problem: Synchronous requests blocked the async concurrency; web_fetch tool used blocking network calls.

insights.solution: Rewrote JinaClient.crawl as async using httpx.AsyncClient, added one-time missing-key warning (_api_key_warned), and replaced logger.error with logger.exception for stack traces.
insights.lesson_learned: When adding async flow, ensure I/O libraries used are async-safe; log warnings only once to reduce noise under concurrency.

insights.title: Make web_fetch_tool async and short-circuit error strings
insights.problem: web_fetch_tool blocked on synchronous Jina client and passed error strings into ReadabilityExtractor causing misleading results.
insights.root_cause: web_fetch_tool was sync and didn't treat crawl error strings specially.
insights.solution: Made web_fetch_tool async and await jina_client.crawl; short-circuit returns when crawl returns an 'Error:' string before extraction.
insights.lesson_learned: Tool wrappers should propagate errors cleanly and avoid sending error text into downstream extractors.

insights.title: Ensure thread_id present in config.context for LangGraph >= 0.6.0
insights.problem: When clients supply 'context' in the run config, thread_id might not be present inside context, causing middleware to miss thread identification.
insights.root_cause: start/run code created Runtime(context={'thread_id': thread_id}) but did not ensure thread_id existed in the request-supplied 'context' dict.
insights.solution: During run_agent setup, setdefault thread_id into config['context'] if present and dict-like.
insights.lesson_learned: When accepting caller-supplied structured fields, ensure required runtime keys are present in the runtime context so downstream middleware can rely on them.

insights.title: Guard agent_name retrieval from runtime.context

insights.title: Break circular import by moving sandbox.tools import into function
insights.problem: Top-level import of deerflow.sandbox.tools in view_image_tool caused circular import errors during test collection.
insights.root_cause: Module import order produced circular dependency chain: sandbox.tools -> agents.thread_state -> agents.factory -> tools.__init__ -> view_image_tool -> sandbox.tools.
insights.solution: Deferred import of get_thread_data and replace_virtual_path into the view_image_tool function body to break the circular chain.
insights.lesson_learned: If a top-level import causes circular dependencies, prefer lazy imports inside function scope; keep module-level imports minimal.

skills/public/github-deep-research/scrip.description: Provides a lightweight GitHub API client with convenience methods to fetch repo info, README, tree, files, languages, contributors, commits, issues, PRs, releases, tags and various statistics. It uses requests when available and falls back to a minimal urllib-based wrapper so the script can run with stdlib only; also includes a CLI main for ad-hoc use.

formats repository tree and summarizes repository info
provides CLI commands to inspect repo data

insights.title: Support optional GitHub PAT via GITHUB_TOKEN env var
insights.problem: The deep research skill could not leverage a GitHub personal access token (PAT) configured in .env for higher rate limits.
insights.root_cause: Script didn't read the environment variable; token parameter was unused.
insights.solution: Read token via os.getenv('GITHUB_TOKEN') in main and pass to GitHubAPI; update doc and configuration documentation to mention GITHUB_TOKEN.
insights.lesson_learned: Community skills should read and document optional env-based credentials for higher API rate limits; ensure default behavior works without token.

insights.title: Add GitHub API client for deep research
insights.problem: Skill needed programmatic access to GitHub repo metadata, tree, commits, issues, etc.
insights.root_cause: No helper script existed to summarize repository data for the skill.
insights.solution: Introduce github_api.py providing GitHubAPI class with get_repo_info, get_readme, get_tree, get_file_content, get_languages, get_contributors, get_recent_commits, get_issues, get_pull_requests, get_releases, get_tags, search_issues, get_commit_activity, get_code_frequency, summarize_repo, and a CLI wrapper.
insights.lesson_learned: When bundling scripts in public skills, provide a lightweight fallback for dependencies (here a requests fallback using urllib) and explicit error messages. However, be mindful of rate limits, authentication, and robust error handling in networked tools.

insights.title: GitHub token handling and unauthenticated requests risk
insights.problem: Script optionally accepts a token but does not instruct secure storage; unauthenticated requests are subject to strict rate limits.
insights.root_cause: Token passed via constructor only; no rate-limit/backoff, no secret management guidance.
insights.solution: Script supports an optional token header but should be used with care; consumers should set personal access tokens and consider retry/backoff and pagination handling for robust behavior.
insights.lesson_learned: Network tooling in skills must document token usage and rate limit handling; consider adding exponential backoff and clearer error propagation for failed API calls.

insights.title: Better TTS error handling and empty audio detection
insights.problem: When TTS generation failed, code produced 0-second audio files silently and downstream steps received empty bytes
insights.root_cause: No checks for empty or missing audio chunks, no validation of environment variables before spawning workers
insights.solution: Validate required env vars early, track failed TTS lines, log 1-based indices of failed lines, raise ValueError when all lines fail or when mixing produces empty bytes, and log success/failure ratio
insights.lesson_learned: Long-running media generation must validate configuration before heavy work and fail fast with descriptive errors; always validate outputs (empty bytes) before writingfinal artifacts.

insights.root_cause: AppConfig loads many optional configs; guardrails needed the same pattern.
insights.solution: Add GuardrailsConfig Pydantic model, get_guardrails_config(), load_guardrails_config_from_dict(), reset_guardrails_config() to support tests.
insights.lesson_learned: When adding global feature toggles, provide reset helpers for tests to avoid singleton leakage.

insights.title: AllowlistProvider: zero-dependency simple allow/deny provider
insights.problem: Need a simple out-of-the-box guardrail provider that doesn't require external packages or network.
insights.root_cause: Users might not want to install external OAP providers; a basic policy is often sufficient.
insights.solution: Provide AllowlistProvider with allowed_tools and denied_tools support and async delegator aevaluate.
insights.lesson_learned: Ship a lightweight builtin provider for quick setups; ensure async compatibility by delegating to sync implementation.

It translates tool-call requests into GuardrailRequest objects, handles provider errors (fail-closed or warn), and returns error ToolMessage responses for denied calls.
