key_constructs.purpose: Asynchronous interception path performing the same guardrail enforcement.

key_behaviors: intercepts tool calls and enforces guardrail allow/deny decisions
returns structured error messages to agents when calls are blocked
handles provider errors with configurable fail-closed behavior

insights.title: Pre-tool-call authorization with fail-open/fail-closed semantics and GraphBubbleUp passthrough
insights.problem: Tool calls needed to be authorized before execution; provider failures or control-flow exceptions (GraphBubbleUp) could be mishandled.
insights.root_cause: Middleware previously didn't exist; provider exceptions might be swallowed and GraphBubbleUp (LangGraph control signals) could be accidentally caught.
insights.solution: Implemented GuardrailMiddleware.wrap_tool_call and awrap_tool_call which call provider.evaluate/aevaluate, propagate GraphBubbleUp, log exceptions, and implement fail_closed vs fail_open behavior. Denials are converted to ToolMessage(status='error') so agents can adapt.
insights.lesson_learned: Middleware must explicitly preserve framework control exceptions and provide configurable safe defaults for provider failures (fail-closed by default). Convert denies into tool-level errors instead of raising to allow agent-level handling.

insights.title: Empty reasons and missing tool names handled
insights.problem: Providers may return deny decisions with empty reasons or requests might contain empty tool names.
insights.root_cause: Providers are external and can return minimal/empty data.
insights.solution: Middleware provides fallback reason text and handles empty tool name gracefully when building deny ToolMessage.
insights.lesson_learned: Sanitize provider responses and supply sensible defaults so downstream UI/messages are meaningful.

insights.title: Introduce GuardrailProvider protocol and dataclasses
insights.problem: No standard contract existed for pluggable pre-tool-call authorization providers.
insights.root_cause: Providers were previously ad-hoc; middleware needed a stable protocol and types to interoperate with multiple implementations (sync/async, OAP, custom).
insights.solution: Add GuardrailRequest/GuardrailDecision/GuardrailReason dataclasses and a runtime-checkable GuardrailProvider Protocol with evaluate/aevaluate methods.
insights.lesson_learned: Define small structural protocols (sync + async) when supporting pluggable providers — makes runtime isinstance checks and testing straightforward.

insights.title: Add vLLM provider preserving vLLM 'reasoning' field and support thinking toggles
insights.problem: LangChain's default OpenAI adapter dropped vLLM's non-standard reasoning field; reasoning flows (Qwen) require preserving reasoning across deltas and multi-turn tool-call flows.
insights.root_cause: LangChain/ChatOpenAI doesn't know about vLLM-specific 'reasoning' and streaming delta semantics.
insights.solution: Provide VllmChatModel subclass of ChatOpenAI that normalizes legacy thinking keys to vLLM chat_template_kwargs, restores 'reasoning' onto outgoing assistant messages, preserves 'reasoning' on non-streaming responses and streaming chunks, converts streaming deltas to LangChain chunk types while carrying reasoning and tool_call info.
insights.lesson: When supporting provider-specific non-standard fields in a compatible adapter, ensure request normalization (legacy flags) and preserve non-standard fields in both streaming deltas and full responses.

insights.title: Structured Grep/Glob search utilities with ignore lists and binary/size safeguards
insights.problem: A naive grep/glob implementation risks scanning binaries, huge files, and returning large result sets; also previous ignore rules were duplicated and inconsistent.
insights.root_cause: Search needs a central implementation to keep consistent ignore rules, handle binary detection, size limits, and prevent ReDoS via skipping super-long lines.
insights.solution: Add search.py with GrepMatch dataclass, should_ignore_name/path, path_matches, truncate_line, is_binary_file, find_glob_matches, find_grep_matches; enforce max file size, skip binary files, cap results and line lengths, and return truncated indicator.
insights.lesson_learned: Implement search primitives with conservative defaults: ignore common venv/build directories, detect binary files, cap file sizes and line lengths, and document semantics so tools and providers can reuse them.

insights.title: Skill manager utilities (history, atomic write, validation, safe paths)
insights.problem: Skill management needed structured utilities for safe writes, history, and path validation.
insights.root_cause: Ad-hoc file manipulation led to duplicated code and possible path traversal risks.
insights.solution: Add manager utilities: atomic_write, append_history, read_history, validate_skill_name, ensure_safe_support_path, get_custom_skill_file/dir/history file helpers, and read_custom_skill_content. These centralize validation and history writing.
insights.lesson: Centralize filesystem operations and validation for custom-managed content to prevent path traversal and ensure atomic updates and auditable history.

backend/packages/harness/deerflow/skills.short_description: AI-driven security scanner for skill content before disk writes
backend/packages/harness/deerflow/skills.description: Calls a configurable chat model to classify proposed skill content as allow/warn/block based on a security rubric, extracting JSON decisions from the model output and falling back to a conservative block if the model call fails. It is used to gate agent-managed skill writes and executable content for manual review when needed.

key_constructs.name: ScanResult

key_constructs.name: scan_skill_content
key_constructs.purpose: Invokes a chat model with a security rubric to classify skill content and returns a ScanResult, using a conservative fallback if the call fails.

key_behaviors: evaluates skill content for prompt injection and unsafe code
returns allow/warn/block decisions to gate skill writes
falls back to conservative blocking for executable content or failed scans

insights.title: Add security scanning for skill writes with model fallback
insights.problem: Custom skill writes could introduce prompt-injection or executable payloads; no model-based review existed.
insights.root_cause: No automated safety gate for agent-managed skill modifications.
insights.solution: Introduce scan_skill_content() that calls a configured moderation model to classify allow/warn/block and fallback to conservative 'block' when the model fails (block executable content always).
insights.lesson: For permissive mutation surfaces (agent-managed code/skills), run a model-based scan with conservative fallback and treat executable content more strictly.

insights.title: Support multiple tracing providers (LangSmith + Langfuse) and build callbacks
insights.problem: Tracing stack previously only supported LangSmith and lacked a factory to create the appropriate callbacks (and Langfuse support).
insights.root_cause: Model factory only attached LangSmith tracer conditionally and didn't support multiple provider callbacks.
insights.solution: Add tracing.factory that validates and builds callbacks for configured providers; create LangSmith tracer and Langfuse handler creators; update model factory to call build_tracing_callbacks() and attach returned callbacks to the model.
insights.lesson_learned: Support for multiple optional observability providers should be plumbed through a single factory, validate explicit enablement and fail fast for misconfiguration, and attach callbacks consistently.

insights.title: Create skill action navigates to new chat in skill mode and closes settings dialog
insights.problem: Creating a new skill from settings required manual navigation; dialog remained open.
insights.root_cause: Create button only logged to console; no navigation or dialog close behavior.
insights.solution: Use next/router push to navigate to /workspace/chats/new?mode=skill, call onClose callback to close settings dialog, and add SparklesIcon to button. SettingsDialog was extended to accept onOpenChange/onClose for this flow.
insights.lesson_learned: UI flows that cross components (settings dialog -> chat) need explicit callbacks and navigation; add a small onClose prop so settings list can close the dialog before routing.

insights.title: Button variant removal to allow icon
insights.problem: Button had variant='outline' which conflicted with icon styling and desired UI.
insights.root_cause: Button variant preset limited visual design for icon-included button.
insights.solution: Remove variant='outline' so the standard button supports an icon child and consistent spacing.
insights.lesson_learned: When adding icons to buttons, ensure button variants/styles are compatible or adjust the button component API.

insights.title: Add DuckDuckGo web search tool (no API key)
insights.problem: Provide a simple web search option that doesn't require API keys for users.
insights.root_cause: Existing web search tools required API keys (Tavily or InfoQuest).
insights.solution: Add ddg_search community tool using ddgs.DDGS to perform text search; normalize results and return JSON. Log error if ddgs not installed.
insights.lesson_learned: Community tools should gracefully degrade (log and return empty results) when optional dependencies are missing and normalize output shape for downstream consumers.

insights.title: Prevent horizontal overflow in ChainOfThoughtSearchResults
insights.problem: Search result bubbles could cause horizontal overflow.
insights.root_cause: Container used flex-wrap without overflow control.

frontend/src/app/workspace/chats/[thread.short_description: Client-side React page that mounts the generic chat interface for a thread
frontend/src/app/workspace/chats/[thread.description:
ChatPage is the client-only Next.js page that composes the main chat UI for a given thread id in the generic (non-agent-specific) workspace route. It wires thread-scoped settings, thread stream lifecycle hooks, header controls (token usage, export, artifact triggers), message list, todo list, and the input box. The page intentionally defers rendering of browser-dependent InputBox until client mount to avoid server/client markup/behavior differences and hydration mismatches. It also respects a static/demo environment via env.NEXT_PUBLIC_STATIC_WEBSITE_ONLY and disables interactive features accordingly.

This file exists to assemble the pieces of the per-thread chat experience using domain-level hooks from core (useThreadChat, useThreadSettings, useThreadStream) and UI primitives (MessageList, InputBox, ThreadTitle, TodoList, TokenUsageIndicator). It has explicit lifecycle handlers supplied to useThreadStream: onStart (creates/sets thread id and uses history.replaceState to change URL without remounting) and onFinish (raises a browser notification when the page is not visible). The mounted flag (useEffect setting setMounted(true)) is a deliberate guard: InputBox is gated behind mounted so server rendering does not produce browser-only markup — this is a known fix for Turbopack/Next hydration mismatches described in commit history.

Because the page is an integration point for many components, changes here must preserve the exact interaction points: using history.replaceState (not next router) on thread creation, retaining the mounted check for InputBox to avoid hydration issues, keeping the env-based guards for demo/static mode, and preserving padding calculation and layout classes that align MessageList and InputBox vertical spacing. This file is relatively short but critical: it orchestrates thread lifecycle and UI composition rather than implementing low-level behaviors itself.

key_constructs.name: ChatPage

key_constructs.reasoning: An agent or automation that edits this function must respect thread lifecycle hooks (useThreadStream), the mounted gating to avoid hydration mismatch, and the history.replaceState navigation approach used on thread creation—changing these can break state preservation and cause UI hydration issues or reloads.

key_constructs.reasoning: Modifying layout or padding assumptions in page.tsx should consider these constants because MessageList and InputBox vertical alignment depends on them.

key_constructs.reasoning: The page calls useThreadStream with callbacks (onStart/onFinish). Any edits that change the shape of the parameters or the expectations from onStart/onFinish must preserve how createdThreadId is handled and how state is updated after a run finishes.

key_constructs.name: useThreadSettings
key_constructs.purpose: Hook returning thread-specific settings and a setter used to supply InputBox context and persist thread-level preferences.
key_constructs.reasoning: ChatPage relies on per-thread settings so edits must continue supplying settings.context to InputBox and updating with setSettings('context', ...).

pitfalls.mistake: Using Next.js router navigation (router.push/replace) inside onStart to change thread URL
pitfalls.consequence: Triggers Next to remount the page and lose in-memory stream and component state; thread will lose in-progress state.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep using history.replaceState for thread navigation as currently implemented.

pitfalls.mistake: Removing the mounted gating (render InputBox unconditionally on server)
pitfalls.consequence: Hydration mismatch and visual overlay bugs in Turbopack/Next rendering; client may re-render or exhibit duplicate DOM.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the mounted useEffect + mounted state gating before rendering InputBox.

pitfalls.mistake: Not guarding interactive actions with env.NEXT_PUBLIC_STATIC_WEBSITE_ONLY
pitfalls.consequence: Buttons or triggers in static/demo builds may attempt server-backed operations that are unavailable, causing errors or confusing UX.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep existing env checks and disabled flags for demo/static mode.

key_sections: useThreadStream call: understand onStart and onFinish handlers and how sendMessage/thread are used
mounted gating: the useEffect setting mounted and the conditional rendering of InputBox
header area: ThreadTitle, TokenUsageIndicator, ExportTrigger, ArtifactTrigger

insights.title: Avoid hydration mismatch by deferring InputBox rendering until client mounted
insights.problem: Hydration mismatch overlays on chat page caused by Turbopack/Next rendering dynamic components (InputBox) that behaved differently server vs client.
insights.root_cause: Components referencing browser-only primitives rendered on server or differed before client hydration.
insights.solution: Add useEffect mounted flag and render placeholder skeleton until mounted to avoid mismatch.
insights.lesson_learned: For components that depend on browser-only APIs or produce different server/client markup, gate rendering behind a mounted check to avoid hydration mismatches.

insights.title: Use thread-scoped settings in chat page
insights.problem: Chat page used global settings and ignored thread overrides.
insights.root_cause: Global settings hook used instead of thread-level hook.
insights.solution: Switched to useThreadSettings(threadId).
insights.lesson_learned: Apply thread-level preferences consistently across chat UI to respect user choices.

insights.title: Integrate TokenUsageIndicator into chat header
insights.problem: Token usage indicator not present in main chat header
insights.root_cause: Feature addition required header placement
insights.solution: Add TokenUsageIndicator and adjust header spacing
insights.lesson_learned: Small UI features should be placed near context where users expect quick telemetry (e.g., chat header).

insights.title: Integrate PromptInputProvider and use prompt controller to set input
insights.problem: Initial input for skill-creation flow was previously passed via props or directly managed in DOM. Provider chain and input state were not centralized leading to duplicated approaches.
insights.root_cause: Input state was managed by both prop-based initialValue and direct DOM manipulation. The chat layout did not wrap children with PromptInputProvider, so components relying on usePromptInputController would fail.
insights.solution: Wrap Chat layout children with PromptInputProvider (layout.tsx) and use usePromptInputController() in ChatPage to set textInput via promptInputController.textInput.setInput(). Also adjusted useEffect dependency to include promptInputController.textInput so controller updates propagate.
insights.lesson_learned: Prefer a single source of truth (provider + controller hooks) for complex input state. When converting to a provider pattern, update all callsites and avoid mixing DOM-only approaches with controller-driven state.

insights.title: Set default collapsed state for Todo list
insights.problem: The todo list panel defaulted to expanded which caused a noisy UI for many users.
insights.root_cause: useState was initialized to false; UX expectation was collapsed by default.
insights.solution: Change default to useState(true) so to-do list is collapsed initially.
insights.lesson_learned: Default UI state should reflect common usage patterns; when toggling default booleans, search for other places depending on that state.

insights.title: Adjust layout width and demo-label position
insights.problem: Chat main area could overflow and demo mode label was mispositioned.
insights.root_cause: CSS utility classes caused unintended overflow/translate behavior.
insights.solution: Add max-w-full to main container to avoid horizontal overflow and change translate-y offset of demo-mode text from -translate-y-2 to translate-y-12 to correctly position it.
insights.lesson_learned: Small Tailwind/utility tweaks can have large layout effects; prefer intent-driven classes (max-w-full) and test different screen sizes after CSS changes.

insights.title: Dynamic document title
insights.problem: Document title was static and didn't reflect thread state (loading, new, untitled).
insights.root_cause: No useEffect updating document.title based on thread metadata.
insights.solution: Add useEffect to update document.title using localization keys and thread title/Loading state.
insights.lesson_learned: Update page-level metadata (document title) to reflect app state; tie to i18n strings for localization.

insights.title: Guard actions for static demo environment
insights.problem: Certain interactive behaviors (closing sidebar when selecting artifact, installing skills) must be disabled in static demo mode.
insights.root_cause: The app supports a static demo build (NEXT_PUBLIC_STATIC_WEBSITE_ONLY) where some server-backed actions are unavailable; earlier UI code didn't consistently guard on this flag.
insights.solution: Add env.NEXT_PUBLIC_STATIC_WEBSITE_ONLY checks in artifacts/context select behavior and in install skill UI to avoid triggering unavailable actions and to disable buttons. Also add tooltips explaining disabled behavior.
insights.lesson_learned: When the app has a static/demo mode, centralize guards around env flags and ensure interactive elements are disabled and annotated with tooltips to avoid user confusion.

frontend/src/components/workspace/messag.short_description: Renders a threaded, grouped view of messages and special assistant groups (files, subtasks, clarifications)
frontend/src/components/workspace/messag.description:
MessageList is responsible for rendering the conversation content area: it consumes the thread stream object (BaseStream<AgentThreadState>) and renders a grouped sequence of message UI blocks. The component uses groupMessages (core utility) to transform a flat message array into logical groups (human, assistant, assistant:clarification, assistant:present-files, assistant:subagent, etc.), and then maps each group to specific UI components (MessageListItem, MarkdownContent, ArtifactFileList, SubtaskCard, MessageGroup). It also provides a loading skeleton when the thread is still loading and no messages exist, attaches rehype plugins for word-splitting animations during streaming, and consistently reserves a bottom padding area to keep the input box visually separated.

This file exists as the central rendering switchboard for message types. Design decisions reflect the need to treat certain assistant groups differently: clarifications render as a single MarkdownContent; present-files groups aggregate artifact filenames across messages and attach an ArtifactFileList; subagent groups extract and track subtask tool calls and tool results and interact with task state (useUpdateSubtask). The subagent logic both updates task state via updateSubtask and renders SubtaskCard(s) keyed by tool call id. The file encodes UX-level behavior (showing subtasks count text via i18n, showing streaming indicator when the thread is streaming) and cares about key stability (keys use group.id and message ids to minimize reordering issues).

Because MessageList touches task updating and file artifact rendering, it must coordinate with other components (MessageListItem, MessageGroup, SubtaskCard, ArtifactFileList) that expect certain message shapes (tool_calls for 'task' tool calls, message.tool_call_id in tool responses). Past commits fixed edge cases here: specifically filtering tool_calls for name === 'task' before extracting ids to avoid rendering SubtaskCard for non-task tool calls that would cause runtime errors. Any edits must preserve these filters and the mapping between tool call ids and Subtask state updates.

pitfalls.mistake: Mapping all message.tool_calls IDs to SubtaskCard without filtering by tool call name
pitfalls.consequence: Can render SubtaskCard for non-task tools leading to runtime errors (TypeError when subtask is expected but not present).
pitfalls.prevention: Retain explicit filtering of tool_calls where toolCall.name === 'task' before extracting ids and rendering SubtaskCard (this was fixed in commit history).

pitfalls.mistake: Changing group keying strategy (group.id, message.id) or rendering order
pitfalls.consequence: May cause React to reuse DOM nodes incorrectly leading to visual glitches or lost state in components like SubtaskCard or MessageGroup.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep stable keys (current strings built from group.id and msg.id) for mapped components; ensure message.group ids remain stable.

pitfalls.mistake: Altering paddingBottom handling without coordinating InputBox translation classes
pitfalls.consequence: Messages can be occluded by the input area or create unexpected empty space at the bottom of the conversation.
pitfalls.prevention: If adjusting padding constants or how paddingBottom is passed, check InputBox layout classes and ChatPage to maintain visual alignment.
