insights.title: Only render SubtaskCard for task tool calls
insights.problem: Previously code mapped all tool_calls IDs and attempted to render SubtaskCard even for non-task tool calls, which led to TypeError when task.status was accessed for unsupported IDs
insights.root_cause: No filtering on tool call name before mapping to subtask IDs
insights.solution: Filter message.tool_calls to only include those where toolCall.name === 'task' before extracting ids
insights.lesson_learned: When rendering components tied to a specific tool type, explicitly filter by tool name to avoid rendering mismatches and runtime errors.

frontend/src/components/workspace/artifa.short_description: React UI to view, preview, and act on a single artifact/file in the workspace.
frontend/src/components/workspace/artifa.description:
This file implements the visual and interaction logic for an artifact file detail pane in the workspace UI. It exists to let users inspect an artifact's contents (code, markdown, HTML, or a binary/resource URL), copy/download it, open it in a new tab, and (for .skill files) trigger a skill-install action. The component centralizes artifact-specific decisions: whether a file is treated as a code file, whether to show a preview vs code editor, how to handle 'write-file:' virtual artifacts created by tool calls, and how to obtain content vs URL for rendering.

>Main design decisions: (1) The code distinguishes remote/URL-backed artifacts vs turn-local "write-file:" artifacts. "write-file:" artifacts are encoded as a URL and should be rendered inline using srcDoc, while non-write artifacts may be served by the backend and expose a URL used as an iframe src. (2) Code vs preview rendering is decided by a language classification (checkCodeFile, special-casing .skill files as markdown). (3) The component leverages the useArtifactContent hook which returns both content and url; the hook is enabled only for code files that are not 'write-file:' artifacts (to avoid unnecessary fetches). (4) The UI uses a unified artifact selection control (Select) driven by useArtifacts context and supports multiple actions via ArtifactAction components.

The file also contains ArtifactFilePreview, a small specialized renderer used when previewing markdown (Streamdown) or HTML (iframe with appropriate sandbox attributes / src or srcDoc depending on whether the artifact is a write-file). This separation exists because different artifact backends (inline content vs remote URL) need different iframe usage: srcDoc for inline content, src for remote resources. The component also integrates UX/feature flags: skill installation is disabled when env.NEXT_PUBLIC_STATIC_WEBSITE_ONLY === "true" and shows a tooltip; copy/download actions use displayContent for clipboard, and window.open calls set opener to null for security.

key_constructs.name: ArtifactFileDetail
key_constructs.purpose: Main React component to display and operate on a single artifact/file within the workspace UI.
key_constructs.reasoning: An agent editing this component must respect its responsibilities: selecting artifacts (via useArtifacts.select), rendering either code or preview based on language detection and isWriteFile, delegating to useArtifactContent for content+url, and exposing actions (install skill, open in new window, copy, download, close). Changing any of these behaviors may affect how artifacts are fetched or how write-file virtual artifacts are displayed. Note the enablement flag passed to useArtifactContent (isCodeFile && !isWriteFile) — this avoids fetching content for write-file virtual artifacts which are expected to be served as inline content via message metadata.

key_constructs.name: ArtifactFilePreview
key_constructs.purpose: Small renderer used by ArtifactFileDetail to preview markdown via Streamdown or HTML via an iframe (srcDoc for write-file inline content or src for URL-backed artifacts).
key_constructs.reasoning: This function encodes the distinction between inline vs remote artifact rendering: markdown uses Streamdown with streamdownPlugins and a custom link component; html uses sandboxed iframe and chooses srcDoc for write-file inline content and src for remote URL-enabled content. Editing this function must preserve sandbox attributes and conditional prop selection for src/srcDoc to avoid security regressions and broken preview behavior.

key_constructs.name: isWriteFile
key_constructs.purpose: Flag (derived from filepath) indicating the artifact is a 'write-file:' virtual artifact encoded as a URL.
key_constructs.reasoning: This constant is used across rendering decisions (decode pathname for display name, choose srcDoc vs src, skip useArtifactContent fetch). Keep URL parsing logic intact (new URL(filepathFromProps) and decodeURIComponent on pathname) — changing could break selection and linking of write-file artifacts that include query params (message_id, tool_call_id).

key_constructs.name: useArtifactContent
key_constructs.purpose: Hook (imported) that provides { content, url } for an artifact given threadId and filepath.
key_constructs.reasoning: This module relies on the hook's returned shape: content (string | undefined) and url (string | undefined). The component conditionally enables the hook only for code files and non-write files. Any change to what the hook returns (e.g., renaming url property) or its enablement semantics will require updating this component.

key_constructs.name: handleInstallSkill
key_constructs.purpose: Async handler to call installSkill API for .skill files, with isInstalling state for spinner/disable logic.
key_constructs.reasoning: This handler uses installSkill API and displays toast feedback. It must respect the ENV flag disabling installs on static deployments and keep error-handling semantics (uses result.message ?? fallback). Also note UI disables the action via disabled prop when isInstalling or env indicates static demo; tooltip wraps the control to explain why it's disabled.

key_behaviors: renders artifact code via CodeEditor when file is a code file
renders markdown via Streamdown and html via iframe preview
opens artifact in a new tab using backend-provided artifact URL
copies artifact content to clipboard and shows toast feedback
initiates skill installation for .skill files (unless disabled by env)

pitfalls.mistake: Remove or alter the isWriteFile URL parsing that uses new URL(filepathFromProps) and decodeURIComponent(url.pathname)
pitfalls.consequence: Write-file virtual artifacts (created by tool calls) may display incorrect filenames or fail to open with correct message_id/tool_call_id query params, breaking auto-open and linking behavior.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the URL parsing logic and ensure write-file artifacts remain recognized by the leading 'write-file:' prefix.

pitfalls.mistake: Change useArtifactContent enablement to always true or remove the isCodeFile && !isWriteFile guard
pitfalls.consequence: It could trigger unnecessary network fetches for write-file artifacts or for non-code artifacts where content is not expected, or lose the intended fallback to using url for remote resources.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep the current enablement semantics or update the hook and component together to maintain equivalent behavior.

pitfalls.mistake: Switch iframe rendering to always use srcDoc or always use src
pitfalls.consequence: Remote resources may not load (if srcDoc used incorrectly) or inline write-file content may leak (if src used without a blob or URL), causing preview failures or security issues.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect the distinction: use srcDoc for inline write-file content and src for URL-backed artifacts.

pitfalls.mistake: Remove sandbox attributes from preview iframe or loosen them
pitfalls.consequence: Introduces security risk by allowing untrusted artifact HTML to execute scripts/actions in the app context.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep sandbox="allow-scripts allow-forms" as currently used for HTML previews (and review any addition of other sandbox flags carefully).

pitfalls.mistake: Alter skill install enabling without respecting NEXT_PUBLIC_STATIC_WEBSITE_ONLY env flag
pitfalls.consequence: Users on static demo builds may see enabled install button and encounter confusing failures; tests or demos may break.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the existing env gating and tooltip affordance when editing install behavior.

key_sections: isWriteFile: identifies virtual write-file artifacts and decodes their pathname
isCodeFile and language detection: determines code vs preview and language mapping (special-case .skill files)
useArtifactContent usage: note enabled flag and how content and url are consumed
UI action handlers: handleInstallSkill, copy, download, open in new window, close
skip_unless_needed: styling/className wrappers and small UI helper elements

related_files.relationship: These components are coupled: message-group may emit 'write_file' tool calls that produce 'write-file:' artifacts shown by ArtifactFileDetail.
related_files.reason_to_check: If message-group changes how it formats write_file URLs (query params, path encoding) or auto-select logic, ArtifactFileDetail must be updated to parse/display accordingly.

related_files.relationship: useArtifactContent hook is consumed here to fetch artifact content and url.
related_files.reason_to_check: If the hook's return shape changes (e.g., url renamed), this file must be updated.

related_files.relationship: handleInstallSkill uses installSkill API from this module.
related_files.reason_to_check: If the installSkill API signature or response shape changes, update error handling and toast messaging here.

insights.title: Support rendering artifact content via URL for non-write files
insights.problem: Artifacts loaded via loader returned raw text only; non-write files such as remote-served resources needed URLs to be previewed (iframe src).
insights.root_cause: useArtifactContent previously returned content only; loader returned just text. The preview component always used srcDoc, which is incorrect for URL-backed content.
insights.solution: Updated loader to return { content, url }, hooks to surface url, and ArtifactFilePreview to use src for URL-backed (non-write) files and srcDoc for write files. ArtifactFileDetail passes isWriteFile and url down to preview.
insights.lesson_learned: Different artifact storage backends require distinct rendering strategies (inline srcDoc vs remote src); hooks should expose both content and source URL.

insights.title: Copy/download uses content without citations and unified CitationLink for external links
insights.problem: Copying or downloading artifacts could include citation blocks or inconsistent external link badges.
insights.root_cause: Clipboard and download actions used raw content; external link rendering duplicated logic.
insights.solution: Compute contentWithoutCitations via removeAllCitations for copy/download operations; use CitationLink for external badges; ensure clipboard copies sanitized content.
insights.lesson_learned: When offering copy/download, sanitize streaming-only artifacts (citations) to produce user-facing clean outputs. Reuse shared components to avoid inconsistent behaviors.

insights.title: Disable skill install in static demo and add tooltip
insights.problem: Users in static/demo build could attempt to install skills which are server-backed and would fail silently or show confusing errors.
insights.root_cause: Install control was always enabled at UI level; error messages used || which could hide falsy but meaningful messages.
insights.solution: Wrap the install ArtifactAction in a Tooltip with i18n content, disable the action when env.NEXT_PUBLIC_STATIC_WEBSITE_ONLY === 'true', and use nullish coalescing (result.message ?? 'Failed to install skill') to avoid swallowing empty-string messages.
insights.lesson_learned: Guard actions that require server capabilities behind environment flags; annotate disabled actions with explanatory tooltips. Use ?? for fallback strings to preserve intentionally empty messages.

frontend/src/components/workspace/messag.short_description: Converts a sequence of messages into a chain-of-thought UI with tool-call steps and previews.
frontend/src/components/workspace/messag.description:
This file implements grouping and rendering of AI chain-of-thought content (reasoning steps and tool calls) inside a conversation UI. It consumes Message arrays and converts them into a sequence of CoT steps (reasoning or toolCall) that are presented using ChainOfThought primitives. The component exists to surface the assistant's internal reasoning, tool calls (search, web_fetch, ls, read_file, write_file, bash, image_search, etc.), and small interactive affordances like opening files referenced by write_file tool calls.

Notable design choices: (1) Messages are converted to a simplified CoT step list by convertToSteps(). That function extracts reasoning content via extractReasoningContentFromMessage and iterates over tool_calls on AI messages, skipping 'task' tool calls which are subagent-related. (2) ToolCall maps known tool names to UI representations, sometimes interpreting args/result into linkable or clickable items (e.g., web_search results, image thumbnails, read_file or ls paths). (3) There is an auto-open behavior: for write_file/str_replace tool calls, if conditions (isLoading, isLast, autoOpen, autoSelect, path) are met, the code programmatically selects the artifact after a 100ms setTimeout to allow the artifact to be created and then opens it. (4) The component leverages i18n t keys, rehype plugins for split-word spans (for streamdown/markdown rendering), and uses useArtifacts context to coordinate artifact selection/opening.

The file is the primary place that maps tool_call names and args to visual representations — changes here directly affect how tool calls are presented and how users are guided to artifacts. It also includes parsing of tool call results: if the tool call result is found it attempts JSON.parse, otherwise treats it as string. The convertToSteps contract and the ToolCall mapping are central to interoperability with backend message/tool schemas.

key_constructs.name: MessageGroup
key_constructs.purpose: Root React component that renders an array of Message objects as a ChainOfThought UI, including show/hide controls for earlier reasoning/tool steps and last thinking steps.
key_constructs.reasoning: Edits here must preserve the component's responsibilities: compute steps via convertToSteps, find the last tool call and last reasoning steps, manage local UI toggles (showAbove, showLastThinking), and pass correct rehype plugins to MarkdownContent. MessageGroup orchestrates rendering of the step list and uses FlipDisplay for animated transitions; altering step boundaries or indexing logic could change which step is considered 'last' and therefore which gets auto-open semantics in ToolCall.

key_constructs.name: ToolCall
key_constructs.purpose: Function that maps a single toolCall step (name, args, result) into a ChainOfThoughtStep with appropriate children and interactions.
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the file's core mapping of tool names → UI. It contains specializations for web_search, image_search, web_fetch, ls, read_file, write_file/str_replace (with auto-open/select), bash, ask_clarification, write_todos, and a default fallback. Modifying this function requires careful attention to each tool's argument shapes and result handling. For write_file, the onClick/select behavior builds a 'write-file:' URL including message_id and tool_call_id; altering this URL formation will require corresponding updates in ArtifactFileDetail and any code that expects these query params.

key_constructs.name: convertToSteps
key_constructs.purpose: Transforms raw Message[] into a flattened array of CoTStep (reasoning or toolCall) while extracting tool call results (JSON-parsed if possible) and skipping 'task' tool calls.
key_constructs.reasoning: This function establishes the canonical steps consumed by MessageGroup. It relies on extractReasoningContentFromMessage and findToolCallResult helpers (imported). Its behavior — e.g., JSON.parse attempt for tool call result — influences whether a tool's result is presented as an object (rendered as list/search results) or as plain text. Keep the skip for tool_call.name === 'task' if subagent tasks are separately handled elsewhere.

key_constructs.name: convertToSteps:CoTToolCallStep
key_constructs.purpose: The CoTToolCallStep structure created by convertToSteps to represent tool calls in the ChainOfThought.
key_constructs.reasoning: The UI expects step objects with { id, messageId, type:'toolCall', name, args, result }. If this shape changes, the ToolCall renderer and any consumers must be updated. Note that result may be string or object.

key_constructs.name: useRehypeSplitWordsIntoSpans
key_constructs.purpose: Provides rehype plugins used when rendering markdown content in reasoning blocks to support split-word animations/behaviors.
key_constructs.reasoning: MessageGroup passes isLoading to this hook to toggle streaming animations. If this hook's API changes, callers must be updated to maintain consistent streaming/animation rendering.

key_behaviors: converts raw messages into reasoned steps and tool call steps for display
renders specialized UI for known tool calls (search, fetch, ls, read_file, write_file, bash, image results)
auto-selects and opens artifacts produced by write_file/str_replace when configured
skips 'task' tool calls (handled by subtask rendering) during step conversion

pitfalls.mistake: Removing or renaming the check that skips tool_call.name === 'task' in convertToSteps
pitfalls.consequence: Subagent 'task' tool calls would be rendered as regular tool calls in the chain-of-thought UI rather than being handled as subtasks; subtask lifecycle updates elsewhere may become inconsistent.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep the 'task' exclusion or update the subtask flow in tandem across message-list / subtask components.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing the auto-open flow (setTimeout 100ms) for write_file artifacts without coordinating with artifact creation timing
pitfalls.consequence: Auto-select may happen too early (artifact not yet created) or too late, resulting in no-op selection or race conditions where the artifact list is not populated and selection fails silently.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect the existing timeout and autoOpen/autoSelect flags; if changing timing, test end-to-end with actual write_file tool calls to ensure selection works reliably.

pitfalls.mistake: Altering the structure of the URL created for write-file artifacts (e.g., omitting message_id or tool_call_id query params)
pitfalls.consequence: Other components (ArtifactFileDetail) rely on these query params for context (e.g., copy/download/search); mismatched URL formats will break linking/navigation.
pitfalls.prevention: Coordinate any URL format changes across MessageGroup, ArtifactFileDetail, and useArtifactContent hook/providers.

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming findToolCallResult always returns JSON when messages may contain plain text
pitfalls.consequence: Uncaught JSON.parse errors are caught here, but shifting to always treat result as JSON could cause runtime exceptions or misrendered results.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep the try/catch JSON.parse pattern or ensure the backend guarantees JSON-typed results for specific tools.

pitfalls.mistake: Removing use of env.NEXT_PUBLIC_STATIC_WEBSITE_ONLY for initial show flags
pitfalls.consequence: Demo/static builds could change the default visibility of collapsed sections, affecting UX and expectations for static deployments.
pitfalls.prevention: If changing defaults, confirm desired behavior for static builds and adjust both MessageGroup and any demo documentation.

key_sections: convertToSteps: how messages become CoT steps and the shape of resulting step objects
ToolCall: mapping of individual tool names to UI (this is the main switch statement that must be preserved)
Auto-open logic in write_file/str_replace branch: how URLs are constructed and selection is triggered
skip_unless_needed: styling and icon imports at the top
less-used branches like ask_clarification/write_todos if not modifying behavior

related_files.relationship: MessageGroup emits write_file/str_replace tool calls that produce 'write-file:' artifact URLs which ArtifactFileDetail must parse and render.
related_files.reason_to_check: If the write-file URL format or selection semantics change, both files must be updated to remain compatible.

related_files.relationship: Subagent 'task' tool calls are intentionally skipped in convertToSteps and handled by subtask-card and message-list groupings.
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to task tool call handling or naming will require coordinated updates in convertToSteps, message-list grouping, and subtask-card logic.

related_files.relationship: Utility functions (extractReasoningContentFromMessage, findToolCallResult) used to extract reasoning and tool results are imported from here.
related_files.reason_to_check: If these utilities change their output shape, MessageGroup and convertToSteps must be updated accordingly.

data_constants_to_check: autoOpen/autoSelect flags provided by useArtifacts context

insights.title: Tool call tooltip content wrapping and clickable path tooltip
insights.problem: Large tool result content could overflow the tooltip and not wrap properly; paths shown as clickable items lacked tooltip explaining action.
insights.root_cause: Tooltip used <pre> without wrap constraints; clickable path lacked tooltip content.
insights.solution: Wrap <pre> with className max-w-[95vw] whitespace-pre-wrap and add a tooltip with i18n key clickToViewContent around path items.
insights.lesson_learned: Tooltips may contain large/preformatted text—add max-width and wrap rules. When making inline elements clickable, add affordance tooltips to indicate action.

backend/packages/harness/deerflow/client.short_description: An embedded Python client that constructs and invokes an in-process DeerFlow agent (LangGraph/LangChain) and exposes convenience APIs (chat/stream/config queries/uploads/memory).
backend/packages/harness/deerflow/client.description:
This module provides DeerFlowClient: a programmatic, in-process client that builds a LangChain/LangGraph agent (the "lead agent" configuration used by the service) and exposes high-level convenience methods to drive it. It exists to let integrations/tests run the same agent logic without starting the LangGraph server or gateway HTTP endpoints. The client lazily constructs the agent the first time it is used and caches it until configuration-affecting parameters change (model name, thinking flag, plan mode, subagent enable, agent_name, available_skills). This caching is keyed in _ensure_agent and ensures prompt/tool set and middleware do not get rebuilt every call in long-lived processes.

Key responsibilities: build and configure the chat model (via create_chat_model), collect available tools, build middleware chain (_build_middlewares), assemble the system prompt (apply_prompt_template), and create the agent (create_agent). It exposes conversational APIs: stream() (generator yielding StreamEvent objects for incremental updates) and chat() (convenience that returns the final AI text). It also exposes configuration and management helpers (list_models, list_skills, memory import/export, upload helpers) to mirror Gateway endpoints for embedded use. The module contains careful handling for streaming semantics (accepts both plain value chunks and (mode, chunk) tuples), deduplication of messages by id, aggregation of usage metadata, and specialized extraction of textual content from possibly block-structured message content.

Design decisions visible in the code: (1) lazy agent creation with a cache key tuple to avoid recreation unless a relevant configuration changes; (2) explicit checkpointer handling — accepts an external checkpointer or will try to get a default from agents.checkpointer; this is critical to multi-turn state preservation; (3) stream API mirrors LangGraph SSE semantics (values/custom/messages-tuple/end) so callers can reuse event handling between embedded and HTTP streaming modes; (4) middleware injection: accepts custom middlewares on client construction and passes them into _build_middlewares so consumer code can extend runtime behavior; (5) available_skills filter is accepted and included in cache key to allow per-client skill gating. Several non-trivial utilities are implemented: atomic JSON write helper, message serialization, and _extract_text that contains heuristics to join lists of strings or mixed block/dict structures while attempting to preserve chunked JSON and token deltas.

key_constructs.name: StreamEvent
key_constructs.purpose: Typed container for events emitted by DeerFlowClient.stream (type + data).
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to normalize streaming output to a small set of event types ('values', 'messages-tuple', 'custom', 'end') so embedded consumers can process the same event types as the HTTP SSE protocol. When modifying streaming code, ensure StreamEvent shapes remain stable.

key_constructs.name: DeerFlowClient
key_constructs.purpose: Main embedded client encapsulating agent construction and high-level APIs (stream, chat, config queries, uploads, memory).
key_constructs.reasoning: All consumer interaction with the in-process agent is through this class. It manages lazy agent creation, caching, config reload behavior, and delegates to many backend subsystems (tools, middlewares, prompt templates, checkpointer). Changes to its constructor parameters or _ensure_agent cache key affect agent recreation semantics; be careful to keep cache key consistent with all parameters that influence agent tooling/middleware/prompt.

key_constructs.name: _atomic_write_json
key_constructs.purpose: Write JSON to disk atomically using a temporary file and replace.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to persist small JSON artifacts safely (avoids partial writes). When editing, maintain correct temp file creation/cleanup and preserve replace semantics for cross-filesystem safety. The function must close file descriptors and unlink temp files on errors to avoid leaked temp files.

key_constructs.name: _get_runnable_config
key_constructs.purpose: Build a RunnableConfig holding configurable runtime flags for the agent call (thread_id, model_name, thinking_enabled, plan_mode, subagent_enabled) and recursion_limit.
key_constructs.reasoning: This encapsulates the dynamic configuration passed into the agent's Runnable invocation; tests and _ensure_agent rely on keys inside this config. Keep keys and names stable; altering names requires updating _ensure_agent and tests.

key_constructs.name: _ensure_agent
key_constructs.purpose: Create (or recreate) the underlying agent when config-dependent parameters differ from a cached key.
key_constructs.reasoning: Crucial to avoid unnecessary rebuilds; builds model, tools, middleware, system_prompt, and sets checkpointer. The cache key contains model_name, thinking_enabled, is_plan_mode, subagent_enabled, agent_name, and available_skills (frozenset or None). Altering what is included in the key or the equality semantics will change when agents are recreated — tests assert this behavior.

key_constructs.name: _get_tools
key_constructs.purpose: Lazy import wrapper that returns available tools for the agent given the model_name and whether subagents are enabled.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to avoid module-level circular imports; any change to tool discovery semantics must respect the lazy import pattern to prevent import cycles with the larger DeerFlow tool registry.

key_constructs.name: _serialize_message
key_constructs.purpose: Serialize LangChain message objects (AIMessage, ToolMessage, HumanMessage, SystemMessage) to plain dicts for 'values' events.
key_constructs.reasoning: Normalization logic here determines how messages appear in 'values' payloads. It extracts tool_calls, usage_metadata, and tool-specific fields; tests assert shapes. Keep property names and presence stable to remain Gateway-compatible.

key_constructs.name: _extract_text
key_constructs.purpose: Extract plain text from message content that may be a string, list of strings, or list of dict-blocks.
key_constructs.reasoning: Contains heuristics to handle chunked string lists (concatenated) vs. human-readable block lists (joined with newlines). This function is exercised by tests around list-of-blocks, short string chunks containing punctuation (to avoid corrupting JSON deltas), and empty lists. Changing its heuristics may alter stream events and break tests relying on specific output.

key_constructs.name: stream
key_constructs.purpose: Send a single user message to the agent and yield StreamEvent objects until the turn finishes (supports custom events and values snapshots).
key_constructs.reasoning: Mirrors the LangGraph SSE event model; handles agent.stream yielding either plain chunk objects or (mode, chunk) tuples. Also deduplicates messages by id, aggregates token usage, and forwards custom events. Tests depend on normalization of custom modes to strings and agent.stream called with stream_mode ['values','custom'].

key_constructs.name: chat
key_constructs.purpose: Convenience wrapper that runs stream() and returns the last AI text emitted (or empty string).
key_constructs.reasoning: Simple aggregator over stream(). Tests check behavior when multiple partial AI messages are emitted and when no AI messages are produced.

key_behaviors: lazily creates an in-process agent on first use
streams agent events as StreamEvent objects
serializes LangChain messages to Gateway-compatible dicts
caches the agent and recreates when config key changes

pitfalls.mistake: Mutating the agent cache key contents (e.g., adding/removing elements) without updating all places that call _ensure_agent or tests.
pitfalls.consequence: Agents may not be recreated when required (stale prompt/tools) or will be recreated too often, causing performance and state consistency issues.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep the cache key tuple in _ensure_agent in sync with any constructor params that affect prompt/tools/middlewares and update tests accordingly.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing the stream() assumption that agent.stream yields either a plain object or a (mode, chunk) tuple and/or changing the expected stream_mode list.
pitfalls.consequence: Custom stream events may be dropped or mis-classified; tests that expect 'custom' events and stream_mode propagation will fail.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the logic that normalizes non-string modes to str() and forward ('custom', chunk) as StreamEvent(type='custom'). Ensure agent.stream is called with stream_mode=['values','custom'].

pitfalls.mistake: Altering the heuristics in _extract_text without considering chunked-string concatenation vs. block-structured text.
pitfalls.consequence: Token-delta/JSON-chunked streams and readability may be broken; downstream UI/tests relying on exact extracted text may fail.
pitfalls.prevention: When changing _extract_text, run the stream-related tests that assert block joining and chunk concatenation behaviors.

pitfalls.mistake: Neglecting to maintain atomic write semantics in _atomic_write_json (e.g., switching to non-atomic write).
pitfalls.consequence: Partial/corrupted JSON files can appear if process crashes during write; tests or other components expecting well-formed JSON may break.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep the temporary-file + replace pattern and proper cleanup on exceptions.

key_sections: _ensure_agent: agent creation and cache key — core to lifecycle
stream: streaming contract, custom event forwarding, and message deduplication
_extract_text: complex text extraction heuristics affecting output
skip_unless_needed: detailed upload helper methods (file system/upload plumbing) if you only change conversation behavior
_atomic_write_json: small utility unless editing file persistence logic

relevant_snippets.description: Tests that custom stream events are forwarded and that stream_mode includes 'custom' and normalization of non-string modes.
Basic stream tests: messages, deduplication, context propagation, and tool-call/result behavior.

related_files.relationship: unit tests that validate streaming, agent creation caching, middleware injection, and message serialization
related_files.reason_to_check: Any change to stream(), _ensure_agent, _extract_text, or cache-key semantics must be validated by test_client.py and might require test updates.

related_files.relationship: module package for the harness; changes to public API surfaces may require exports here
related_files.reason_to_check: If new helper functions are added or constructor signature changes, the package exports may need updating for compatibility.

related_files.relationship: server-side lead agent factory and middleware composition that DeerFlowClient tries to mirror; prompt template and middleware ordering should remain consistent
related_files.reason_to_check: Client uses _build_middlewares and apply_prompt_template to construct similar runtime behavior; changes server-side need coordination with client to ensure parity.

insights.title: Expose custom stream events from DeerFlowClient.stream()
insights.problem: DeerFlowClient.stream previously assumed agent.stream yielded values-only and did not forward custom stream-mode events (custom modes) to the caller.
insights.root_cause: Client only supported 'values' streaming mode and treated agent.stream items as value chunks, ignoring tuple (mode, chunk) items.
insights.solution: Accept stream_mode list including 'custom', unpack (mode, chunk) tuple items, normalize mode to string, yield StreamEvent(type='custom', data=chunk) when mode == 'custom'.
insights.lesson: Streaming client APIs should accept multiplexed stream modes and forward custom event types; normalize non-string mode values to strings for robustness.

insights.title: Allow DeerFlowClient to pass available_skills for runtime skill filtering
insights.problem: Clients needed a way to limit which skills are available to an in-process agent (dynamic runtime filtering) but DeerFlowClient had no API to pass available_skills.
insights.root_cause: DeerFlowClient constructed agents using global skill discovery only; no parameter allowed per-client skill restrictions.
insights.solution: Add available_skills parameter to DeerFlowClient constructor, store in instance, include in agent config cache key, and pass through to make_lead_agent so prompt injection and tool availability honor the requested skill filter.
insights.lesson_learned: Expose per-client runtime flags for feature gating (skills) and include them in cache keys so agent recreation happens when the effective available-skill set changes.

insights.title: Add client methods export_memory and import_memory
insights.problem: Programmatic clients lacked import/export helpers.
insights.root_cause: Client previously only exposed read-only memory retrieval.
insights.solution: Added DeerFlowClient.export_memory and import_memory that delegate to agents.memory.updater functions.
insights.lesson_learned: Client convenience methods help integration tests and programmatic automation; keep them thin wrappers over backend functionality.

insights.title: Support custom middleware injection in DeerFlowClient
insights.problem: Users of embedded DeerFlowClient couldn't inject custom middlewares into the created agent graph.
insights.root_cause: Client previously built middleware internally with no API to add custom middleware list.
insights.solution: Added `middlewares` parameter to DeerFlowClient constructor and store as list; pass into _build_middlewares as custom_middlewares to be injected before ClarificationMiddleware.
insights.lesson_learned: Expose middleware injection at client/SDK level but ensure injection point preserves invariants (ClarificationMiddleware must remain last). Avoid mutating shared lists with extend; copy inputs.

insights.title: DeerFlowClient supports agent_name injection
insights.problem: Client lacked the ability to set agent_name to isolate memory and customize system prompts.
insights.root_cause: Agent name affects middleware behavior and system prompt templates and must propagate through the client-created agent factory.
insights.solution: Add agent_name parameter to DeerFlowClient constructor, validate against AGENT_NAME_PATTERN, store as self._agent_name, and propagate into _build_middlewares and apply_prompt_template contexts. stream() now includes agent_name in context.
insights.lesson_learned: Parameter that affects middleware/prompt must be validated early and passed into all places that construct agents or prompts. Add unit tests to verify propagation and validation.

insights.title: Agent name validation to prevent invalid names
insights.problem: Previously, arbitrary agent names could be passed leading to potential filesystem or config issues.
insights.root_cause: Lack of validation for agent_name parameter.
insights.solution: Validate agent_name against AGENT_NAME_PATTERN and raise ValueError when invalid.
insights.lesson_learned: Validate externally-provided identifying strings early to avoid downstream injection or path traversal issues.

backend/tests/test_client.py.short_description: Unit tests verifying DeerFlowClient behavior: construction, configuration queries, streaming/chat semantics, and message serialization.
backend/tests/test_client.py.description:
This test module exercises the DeerFlowClient public behavior comprehensively. It creates a mocked AppConfig (via get_app_config patching) and uses many focused unit tests to validate constructor parameter handling, agent creation/caching, streaming event shapes and ordering, message deduplication, context propagation, tool call/result handling, text extraction heuristics, and convenience query methods (list_models, list_skills, memory export/import). The tests use MagicMock to stub the underlying agent.stream and create_agent functions so they isolate client logic without requiring the full runtime.

The tests are organized into logical sections: initialization tests (TestClientInit), configuration query tests (TestConfigQueries), streaming/chat tests (TestStream, TestChat), unit tests for internal helpers (TestExtractText), and tests for _ensure_agent behavior (TestEnsureAgent). Many tests assert precise shapes of events (StreamEvent types, 'messages-tuple' content), that agent.stream is called with specific kwargs (stream_mode and context), and that custom event modes and Enum-like mode objects are normalized correctly. The file is the authoritative suite for the client and will fail if any public streaming/serialization contract changes.

key_constructs.name: mock_app_config
key_constructs.purpose: pytest fixture providing a minimal AppConfig mock used to initialize DeerFlowClient in tests.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to avoid loading real configuration; tests patch get_app_config to return this mock so client instance creation is deterministic. Keep fixture shape (models list) compatible with list_models expectations.

key_constructs.name: client
key_constructs.purpose: pytest fixture that constructs a DeerFlowClient with get_app_config patched to mock_app_config.
key_constructs.reasoning: Central fixture used by many tests to ensure client is constructed with a mocked config. If DeerFlowClient constructor signature changes, update this fixture accordingly.

key_constructs.name: TestClientInit
key_constructs.purpose: TestCase grouping tests for DeerFlowClient.__init__ parameter handling and validation.
key_constructs.reasoning: Covers agent_name validation, config_path reload, middlewares and available_skills propagation to the client instance.

key_constructs.name: TestConfigQueries
key_constructs.purpose: TestCase for list_models, list_skills, get_memory/export_memory wrappers on DeerFlowClient.
key_constructs.reasoning: Confirms the client exposes Gateway-like config query functions and forwards to underlying loader/updater functions.

key_constructs.name: TestStream
key_constructs.purpose: TestCase covering stream() behavior: events emission, custom-mode forwarding, context propagation, deduplication, and tool call/result handling.
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the most important test group for streaming semantics; many detailed assertions depend on exact StreamEvent shapes and ordering.

key_constructs.name: TestExtractText
key_constructs.purpose: Unit tests for DeerFlowClient._extract_text text-extraction heuristics.
key_constructs.reasoning: Ensures the function handles strings, block lists, chunk lists, empty lists, and other types as intended. Any changes to _extract_text must preserve these behaviors or update tests.

key_constructs.name: TestEnsureAgent
key_constructs.purpose: Tests for _ensure_agent: verifies agent creation, checkpointer injection, and custom middleware propagation.
key_constructs.reasoning: Validates that the agent is created with the expected arguments and that client caching/side-effects behave correctly.

key_behaviors: validate DeerFlowClient constructor behavior and parameter validation
assert stream() yields custom events and values snapshots in order
verify agent context propagation and stream_mode usage
test _extract_text heuristics with multiple block shapes

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming agent.stream yields only plain dicts rather than (mode, chunk) tuples.
pitfalls.consequence: Tests that check forwarding of custom events will fail; client behavior may diverge from server SSE contracts.
pitfalls.prevention: Ensure client.stream supports tuple yields and normalizes non-string modes to str(); when changing stream semantics, update tests accordingly.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing DeerFlowClient.__init__ signature (new required params) without updating the client fixture.
pitfalls.consequence: Many tests will fail at client construction time.
pitfalls.prevention: Update the 'client' fixture to reflect constructor changes and adjust get_app_config patching.

pitfalls.mistake: Altering message id deduplication logic or _serialize_message output shape.
pitfalls.consequence: Assertions that count message events or check exact content/fields will break.
pitfalls.prevention: Run the streaming-related tests after changes to ensure event shapes remain compatible.

key_sections: TestStream class: covers most streaming behavior and is the primary consumer contract
TestEnsureAgent: ensures agent creation args and middleware injection
skip_unless_needed: smaller tests for list_models/list_skills if working only on streaming logic

relevant_snippets.description: test_custom_events_are_forwarded: asserts that custom events are forwarded and agent.stream called with stream_mode including 'custom'.
tool call/result and basic streaming tests (deduplication and values events).

related_files.relationship: tests the client implementation; directly coupled.
related_files.reason_to_check: Any edits to DeerFlowClient's streaming or agent creation code must be validated here.

related_files.relationship: client uses get_checkpointer when none provided; tests patch this to inject a default checkpointer.
related_files.reason_to_check: If checkpointer API changes, tests that rely on get_checkpointer must be updated.

tests_to_run: pytest -q to validate no regressions across other tests

insights.title: Tests for custom stream events and mode normalization
insights.problem: Ensure DeerFlowClient forwards custom events and handles non-string enum-like modes.
insights.root_cause: Client previously dropped custom events or broke on Enum-like mode types.
insights.solution: Added tests to verify agent.stream called with stream_mode including 'custom', that custom events are forwarded, and that Enum-like modes are normalized to strings.
insights.lesson: Cover non-string and tuple stream yields in client tests to prevent runtime mismatches between agent and client.

backend/packages/harness/deerflow/sandbo.short_description: Sandbox-related file-path, command validation and built-in sandbox tools (bash, glob, grep, read/write).
backend/packages/harness/deerflow/sandbo.description:
This module implements the sandbox-aware filesystem and command helpers used by DeerFlow agent tools. It exists to: (1) map virtual container paths (e.g., /mnt/user-data, /mnt/skills, /mnt/acp-workspace) to host filesystem locations, (2) validate paths and shell commands for security and sandbox boundaries, (3) mask host filesystem paths in outputs so user-visible output does not leak host layout, and (4) expose a set of built-in tools (bash_tool, glob_tool, grep_tool, read_file/write_file/str_replace and others) that operate safely across both LocalSandbox and remote/AioSandbox providers.

Design decisions visible in the file: path handling preserves host-style separators and trailing separators when mapping virtual → host (helpers _path_separator_for_style and _join_path_preserving_style), validation explicitly distinguishes read-only vs write operations and treats skills and ACP workspace as read-only, and several behaviors are configurable via get_app_config tool-specific model_extra entries. The module centralizes truncation for potentially huge outputs (_truncate_bash_output, _truncate_read_file_output, _truncate_ls_output) to protect LLM context windows. It also caches certain config-dependent lookups (skills container/host path, custom mounts) but intentionally retries on failure for some lookups to allow config to become available later.

This file is heavily coupled with sandbox providers, thread state and app config: it uses ThreadDataState to map per-thread workspace/uploads/outputs, calls ensure_sandbox_initialized / sandbox_from_runtime to get a Sandbox instance, consults the config for custom mounts and tool-specific max_results / output-size limits, and uses search utilities (sandbox.search) for glob/grep features. Many unit tests exercise the validation, mapping and search behaviors; changes here must preserve security guarantees (path traversal detection, write restrictions for skills/ACP/custom mounts, host bash gating) and masking behavior for local sandboxes so host paths are never leaked to users.

key_constructs.name: _get_skills_container_path
key_constructs.purpose: Return the configured container-side path prefix for skills (cached fallback to default).
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to detect and map skills virtual paths; implementations that change detection or caching must keep cached semantics and fallback behavior intact.

key_constructs.name: _get_skills_host_path
key_constructs.purpose: Return the host filesystem path for skills, or None if unavailable (cached on success).
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to resolve skills virtual paths to host paths; callers rely on None to indicate unconfigured skills host and to raise FileNotFoundError accordingly.

key_constructs.name: _is_skills_path
key_constructs.purpose: Check whether a virtual path lies under the configured skills container prefix.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used in validation/resolution to allow read-only access to skills paths and to block writes; must be consistent with _get_skills_container_path.

key_constructs.name: _resolve_skills_path
key_constructs.purpose: Resolve a skills virtual path to the host filesystem path or raise FileNotFoundError.
key_constructs.reasoning: Performs container->host mapping using _get_skills_container_path/_get_skills_host_path; must preserve separator style and raise when host path unavailable.

key_constructs.name: _is_acp_workspace_path
key_constructs.purpose: Detect if a virtual path is in the ACP workspace virtual prefix (/mnt/acp-workspace).
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to treat ACP workspace as a read-only shared virtual mount; detection must match exact prefix rules used elsewhere.

key_constructs.name: _resolve_acp_workspace_path
key_constructs.purpose: Resolve ACP workspace virtual path to the host path for global or per-thread ACP workspace; enforces path-traversal prevention.
key_constructs.reasoning: Must enforce same traversal checks as user-data and preserve per-thread behavior; callers expect PermissionError for traversal and FileNotFoundError when not present.

key_constructs.name: _get_custom_mounts
key_constructs.purpose: Return configured custom mount objects (cached on success), filtering out non-existing host paths.
key_constructs.reasoning: Custom mounts are used in validation and path resolution; caching and filtering behavior affects what is considered allowed—edits must preserve the check that only existing host paths are returned.

key_constructs.name: _get_custom_mount_for_path
key_constructs.purpose: Return the configured mount whose container_path most specifically matches the supplied path (longest prefix).
key_constructs.reasoning: Callers use this to determine read_only semantics per-mount; ensuring longest-prefix ties are handled is critical to avoid accidental privilege escalation.

key_constructs.name: replace_virtual_path
key_constructs.purpose: Translate /mnt/user-data virtual paths to per-thread actual host paths, preserving separator style and trailing slashes.
key_constructs.reasoning: Central mapping routine used by command replacement and tools; must preserve style/trailing slash to avoid mixed separators or broken concatenations (Windows edge-cases tested).

key_constructs.name: replace_virtual_paths_in_command
key_constructs.purpose: Replace virtual paths appearing inside a shell command string with host paths (and apply workspace CWD prefixing for local sandbox when appropriate).
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by bash_tool to make commands runnable on the host/local sandbox; it must maintain security checks (no traversal), handle skills & ACP mapping, and ensure relative paths are anchored to the thread workspace when using a local, host-backed sandbox.

key_constructs.name: mask_local_paths_in_output
key_constructs.purpose: Mask host absolute paths in tool output back to virtual equivalents (user-data, skills, ACP workspace).
key_constructs.reasoning: Prevents leaking host directory layout in outputs; must consider both raw and resolved host path variants and preserve path separator style in replacements.

key_constructs.name: validate_local_tool_path
key_constructs.purpose: Validate that a requested read/write path is allowed (under virtual user-data, skills read-only, ACP read-only, or configured mounts), and enforce traversal protection.
key_constructs.reasoning: Critical security gate used by read_file/ls/write_file/str_replace etc. to prevent reads/writes outside allowed areas and to enforce skills/ACP read-only semantics.

key_constructs.name: validate_local_bash_command_paths
key_constructs.purpose: Validate that a bash command string does not contain unsafe absolute host paths, file:// URLs, or traversal sequences; allows system paths (/bin/) and configured MCP filesystem server paths.
key_constructs.reasoning: Prevents agents from issuing host-absolute operations when running in LocalSandbox; changes must keep URL allowances and file:// blocking semantics, and respect is_host_bash_allowed gating.

key_constructs.name: _apply_cwd_prefix
key_constructs.purpose: Return a command prefixed with `cd <workspace> &&` when thread workspace exists, quoting workspace appropriately.
key_constructs.reasoning: Anchors relative paths in local bash execution to the per-thread workspace; important security mitigation to stop relative-path escapes to backend CWD.

key_constructs.name: bash_tool
key_constructs.purpose: Tool wrapper exposing shell execution to agents; performs replacement, validation, sandbox initialization, and truncation before returning output or an error string.
key_constructs.reasoning: This high-level tool interfaces with sandbox providers. It must respect is_host_bash_allowed for local provider, call ensure_sandbox_initialized, apply CWD anchoring, and present sanitized output (masked paths) to the agent.

key_constructs.name: glob_tool
key_constructs.purpose: Tool wrapper exposing file-globbing; uses sandbox.glob/find to enumerate files, masks host paths back to virtual paths and formats results, enforcing max_results.
key_constructs.reasoning: Allows agents to search repo contents without invoking bash. Must respect ignore patterns (node_modules etc.), custom mounts, skills and ACP mappings, and clamp result counts to configured maxima.

key_constructs.name: grep_tool
key_constructs.purpose: Tool wrapper exposing grep-style search across files using sandbox.grep/find_grep_matches helpers; supports literal/case-sensitive flags and result truncation.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by agents to find code references. Must use sandbox-provided grep or local file traversal logic, skip binaries/symlinks outside root, and mask results to virtual paths.

key_constructs.name: _truncate_bash_output
key_constructs.purpose: Truncate long bash outputs with a middle-truncation strategy, preserving head and tail and adding a skipped-count marker.
key_constructs.reasoning: Protects LLM context windows from huge outputs. Tests rely on exact marker presence and max-length guarantees.

key_constructs.name: _truncate_read_file_output
key_constructs.purpose: Truncate long read_file outputs by keeping the head only and inserting a truncation marker with start/end line hints.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by read_file_tool and tested for marker content and length invariants.

key_constructs.name: _truncate_ls_output
key_constructs.purpose: Head-truncate directory listings returned by ls_tool with a marker, keeping directory listing head.
key_constructs.reasoning: Directory listing can be very long; tests enforce that marker exists and output length is bounded.

key_constructs.name: _resolve_and_validate_user_data_path
key_constructs.purpose: Resolve a /mnt/user-data path to a per-thread host path and reject traversal that would escape the per-thread workspace/uploads/outputs roots.
key_constructs.reasoning: Core path resolution for per-thread user-data. Must enforce that resolved result is inside the allowed roots; used by many tools and validators.

key_behaviors: maps virtual container paths to host filesystem paths
validates and gates reads/writes to user-data/skills/ACP/custom mounts
masks host paths in outputs to avoid information leakage
provides search (glob/grep) and shell execution tools with truncation

pitfalls.mistake: Assume runtime.context is always a dict and write into it without guard
pitfalls.consequence: TypeError in lazy-init scenarios where runtime.context is None (LangGraph runtime), causing sandbox initialization failures
pitfalls.prevention: Check runtime.context is not None before writing and fallback to runtime.config.configurable for thread_id where required

pitfalls.mistake: Normalize separators using pathlib.Path when joining container→host paths
pitfalls.consequence: Loses Windows-style backslashes or trailing separators, leading to mixed-separator outputs or broken concatenations in downstream code (regressions covered by tests)
pitfalls.prevention: Use _path_separator_for_style/_join_path_preserving_style to preserve original host path separator style and trailing slash semantics

pitfalls.mistake: Remove or weaken path traversal checks in resolution/validation helpers
pitfalls.consequence: Agents could read or write files outside allowed virtual roots (security breach)
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve explicit traversal checks in _reject_path_traversal and subsequent commonpath/relative_to checks used by resolution functions

pitfalls.mistake: Modify truncation strategies or marker strings without updating tests
pitfalls.consequence: Unit tests will fail because they assert presence of markers and length invariants
pitfalls.prevention: If altering truncation behavior, update tests in test_tool_output_truncation.py accordingly

key_sections: replace_virtual_path: core mapping from virtual -> actual per-thread paths
validate_local_tool_path: security gate used by read/write tools
validate_local_bash_command_paths and _apply_cwd_prefix: host-bash validation and CWD anchoring
bash_tool: high-level flow integrating replacement, validation, truncation and sandbox execution
glob_tool/grep_tool: search tools building on sandbox.glob/grep and formatting/masking results
_truncate_* functions: truncation strategies and invariants used by tools
skip_unless_needed: deep caching helpers (_get_custom_mounts/_get_skills_host_path) unless debugging config lookups
path variant utilities if only working at tool-level behavior

relevant_snippets.description: Unit tests verifying replace_virtual_path behavior and trailing-slash/separator preservation.
Tests for glob/grep tools; sets up runtime fixture and uses LocalSandbox to exercise search APIs.

related_files.relationship: Defines abstract Sandbox API (execute_command/read_file/write_file/list_dir) that tools call into
related_files.reason_to_check: If changing tool->sandbox invocation semantics or expected return formats, update both sides.

related_files.relationship: LocalSandboxProvider implements local filesystem behavior and is used by unit tests via LocalSandbox instances
related_files.reason_to_check: Local-specific behavior (path resolution, allowed system paths) must be consistent with validator assumptions in tools.py.

related_files.relationship: ThreadDataState definitions and thread-data keys used by replace_virtual_path and mapping helpers
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to thread_data keys or layout will break path mappings and tests.

related_files.relationship: App config accessors (get_app_config) and tool-specific configs affect max_results and output-size limits
related_files.reason_to_check: Adjusting defaults for sizes, custom mounts or skills container path requires coordinated changes.

insights.title: Preserve virtual path separator style and trailing slash
insights.problem: replace_virtual_path converted virtual paths to actual paths but could drop trailing separators or change separator style causing incorrect concatenations and mixed separators on Windows-style actual paths.
insights.root_cause: Path joining used Path(base) which normalised separators and lost trailing slash style; code didn't preserve original virtual trailing slash vs required actual separator.
insights.solution: Add _path_separator_for_style to detect separator style, implement _join_path_preserving_style to join paths preserving separator, and ensure trailing slash/backslash preserved when virtual path ended with a slash.
insights.lesson: When mapping virtual -> physical paths, preserve original separator style and trailing slash semantics to prevent subtle path-concatenation bugs and mixed-separator outputs on Windows-like paths.

insights.title: Add ls tool output truncation to prevent context window overflow
insights.problem: ls_tool could return multi-MB listings with no truncation, exceeding LLM context windows.
insights.root_cause: ls_tool lacked an output-size limit while other tools (bash/read_file) had truncation.
insights.solution: Introduce sandbox config ls_output_max_chars and _truncate_ls_output head-truncation preserving listing head and marker; ls_tool uses config with safe default 20000 chars.
insights.lesson: All tools that can produce large textual outputs must enforce truncation limits; choose truncation strategy appropriate to the content (head for directory listings).

insights.title: Guard against None runtime.context in sandbox helper writers
insights.problem: sandbox_from_runtime() and ensure_sandbox_initialized() wrote into runtime.context without checking if runtime.context is None (LangGraph default), causing TypeError in lazy_init scenarios.
insights.root_cause: Assumed runtime.context always present; LangGraph may set runtime.context=None.
insights.solution: Add `if runtime.context is not None` guards before assigning runtime.context['sandbox_id'].
insights.lesson: When writing into an optional runtime.context object, guard writes; reads had earlier guards but writes must too.

insights.title: Add grep and glob built-in tools
insights.problem: Agents relied on bash/find/grep which is fragile across sandboxes and inefficient for structured agent workflows; no structured file search tools existed.
insights.root_cause: Only shell-based exploration and read_file/ls existed, causing high token usage and inconsistent behaviour when host bash disabled.
insights.solution: Implement glob_tool and grep_tool that use sandbox.glob/grep (local provider fallback) and enforce max_results and safe defaults; mask host paths back to virtual paths for local sandboxes; expose configuration-driven limits and clamp values according to tool config.
insights.lesson_learned: Provide structured read-only search tools to improve agent repo exploration: enforce limits, ignore patterns, and map host paths to virtual paths to avoid leaking host details.

insights.title: Truncate oversized bash and read_file outputs at tool layer
insights.problem: Large tool outputs (multi-MB) can overflow the model context and cause high token usage; previously no deterministic truncation was enforced.
insights.root_cause: Truncation was not centralized; caller code could return huge outputs, leading to context window issues.
insights.solution: Add _truncate_bash_output (middle-truncate preserving head and tail) and _truncate_read_file_output (head-truncate) and apply these truncations at bash_tool and read_file_tool using sandbox config limits (bash_output_max_chars, read_file_output_max_chars). Default limits and strict guarantee that result length <= max_chars applied. Add tests.
insights.lesson_learned: Enforce output truncation at tool boundary (not middleware) so all codepaths benefit; choose truncation strategies appropriate for content (middle for bash, head for files).

insights.title: Respect configured custom mounts in path validation
insights.problem: New custom mounts needed to be considered by validation and path resolution logic to avoid accidental denial or exposure of host files.
insights.root_cause: validate_local_tool_path and bash path replacement didn't consider custom mounts.
insights.solution: Add _get_custom_mounts caching helper, _is_custom_mount_path/_get_custom_mount_for_path helpers, and update validate_local_tool_path and bash command path replacement logic to accept custom mount container paths and respect per-mount read_only flags.
insights.lesson_learned: Security checks must be extended when introducing new mount semantics; validate and respect read_only flags and protect path traversal for custom mounts the same way as virtual user-data paths.

insights.title: Anchor relative paths to thread workspace in local sandbox
insights.problem: Relative paths in local sandbox bash commands were resolved against the process CWD (backend/), allowing writes to escape the per-thread workspace and break isolation.
insights.root_cause: validate_local_bash_command_paths and replace_virtual_paths_in_command only handled absolute paths; relative paths passed through unchanged and were executed in the process working directory.
insights.solution: After virtual path translation the command is prefixed with `cd <workspace> &&` (using shlex.quote) via an extracted helper _apply_cwd_prefix so relative paths are anchored to the thread workspace in local mode.
insights.lesson_learned: Always normalize and anchor execution CWD for local subprocesses; checks that look only at absolute paths can miss relative-path escape vectors.

insights.title: Fallback to config.configurable for thread_id during lazy sandbox init
insights.problem: Lazy sandbox acquisition failed when runtime.context lacked thread_id (LangGraph Server passes thread_id in config.configurable).
insights.root_cause: ensure_sandbox_initialized only read runtime.context for thread_id.
insights.solution: Added fallback to runtime.config.get('configurable', {}).get('thread_id') before raising error.
insights.lesson_learned: LangGraph runtime may carry important metadata in configurable; middleware and tools should natively accept both context and configurable fallbacks.

insights.title: Gate host bash execution via configuration and explicit checks
insights.problem: Host bash execution by default in LocalSandboxProvider is risky and can lead to host-shell escape.
insights.root_cause: Local sandbox exposed a bash execution surface that could be used without isolation.
insights.solution: Added is_host_bash_allowed checks and security messages (coordinated with sandbox/security.py); in bash_tool the local-sandbox path validation is only applied when local sandbox and host bash allowed, otherwise returns an error string. Also ensured thread directories are created only when needed.
insights.lesson_learned: Expose dangerous capabilities only behind an explicit opt-in; add clear user-visible messages and programmatic checks.

insights.title: Extracted CWD prefixing helper and tests
insights.problem: Previously logic to anchor CWD was embedded in bash_tool and not unit-tested.
insights.root_cause: Lack of small isolated helper made testing and reasoning harder.
insights.solution: Extracted _apply_cwd_prefix helper and added focused unit tests covering quoting, missing workspace, and None thread_data.
insights.lesson_learned: Small helpers with focused unit tests make security-sensitive behavior clearer and easier to verify.

insights.title: Allow MCP filesystem server paths in local bash validation
insights.problem: Local bash path validation blocked legitimate MCP filesystem server paths used by the MCP filesystem server integration.
insights.root_cause: Allowed-path configuration used by the MCP filesystem extension wasn't consulted by the bash command path validator.
insights.solution: Added _get_mcp_allowed_paths which reads extensions_config and allows absolute paths exported by MCP filesystem server args; tests added to ensure allowed paths are permitted while traversal remains blocked.
insights.lesson_learned: Cross-extension allowed-path semantics must be shared; validators should consult known extension configs to avoid false positives.

insights.title: Preserve host path style and centralize virtual path handling
insights.problem: Path translation code assumed a single path style (POSIX), leading to incorrect joins and potential traversal checks on Windows hosts.
insights.root_cause: Using pathlib.Path mixing with string operations led to inconsistent path styles when translating virtual container paths to host paths (e.g., Windows backslashes).
insights.solution: Add _join_path_preserving_style to join without changing path separators, replace direct Path joins where virtual container paths are mapped to host paths. Use posixpath for pure-posix checks when host path uses '/' style.
insights.lesson_learned: When mapping between container-virtual and host filesystem paths, preserve original path style to avoid path traversal false negatives/positives and unexpected normalization.

insights.title: Introduce ACP workspace virtual path with per-thread isolation and read-only enforcement
insights.problem: ACP agent integration required sharing results via filesystem but previously used thread-local user-data workspace causing coupling and potential write access from agent runs.
insights.root_cause: ACP agents write outputs in separate workspaces; earlier code lacked a dedicated virtual path and safeguards (per-thread isolation and read-only semantics) for ACP outputs.
insights.solution: Add _ACP_WORKSPACE_VIRTUAL_PATH '/mnt/acp-workspace', per-thread host path resolution via _get_acp_workspace_host_path and Paths.acp_workspace_dir(), implement _resolve_acp_workspace_path with traversal checks, and treat ACP paths as read-only in validate_local_tool_path and replace_virtual_paths_in_command. Mask ACP host paths in outputs and support mapping in local sandbox tools.
insights.lesson_learned: External agent integrations that produce files should expose a dedicated, minimally-permissioned virtual path and enforce read-only behavior for consumers to avoid privilege escalations.

insights.title: Ensure sandbox initialization checks for runtime.context
insights.problem: ensure_sandbox_initialized attempted to retrieve thread_id via runtime.context.get(...) and crashed when runtime.context was None
insights.root_cause: Unprotected context access when tool invoked in atypical runtime
insights.solution: Use runtime.context.get('thread_id') if runtime.context else None and raise a descriptive SandboxRuntimeError if missing
insights.lesson_learned: Sandbox tools must validate runtime state early and raise clear errors when required context is missing to aid debugging.

backend/tests/test_sandbox_tools_securit.short_description: Unit tests for sandbox path validation, command validation, mapping and masking security behaviors.
backend/tests/test_sandbox_tools_securit.description:
This test module verifies security-critical behaviors of sandbox tooling: virtual→host path mapping, trailing-slash and separator preservation, path traversal rejection, read-only semantics for skills and ACP workspace, custom-mount handling, file:// URL blocking, and gating of host bash execution for local sandboxes. The tests construct synthetic ThreadDataState values, patch configuration lookups (skills container/host paths, custom mounts), and monkeypatch sandbox initialization to exercise validation and mapping functions in isolation. Many tests assert that host paths are masked in tool output and that invalid operations raise PermissionError or SandboxRuntimeError.

The file is extensive and covers many edge cases (Windows-style paths, trailing slash preservation, URL allowances in bash commands, MCP filesystem allowed paths) reflecting production hardening. Edits to tools.py that change behavior or marker text (error messages) will likely require updates to these tests.

key_constructs.name: test_replace_virtual_path_maps_virtual_root_and_subpaths
key_constructs.purpose: Assert virtual user-data root and subpath mapping to per-thread host paths.

key_constructs.name: test_replace_virtual_path_preserves_trailing_slash
key_constructs.purpose: Regression test ensuring trailing slash is preserved after mapping.

key_constructs.name: test_replace_virtual_path_preserves_trailing_slash_windows_style
key_constructs.purpose: Windows-style path separator preservation case.

key_constructs.name: test_mask_local_paths_in_output_hides_host_paths
key_constructs.purpose: Validates host path masking for user-data paths in tool output.

key_constructs.name: test_validate_local_tool_path_rejects_non_virtual_path
key_constructs.purpose: Checks that arbitrary absolute host paths are rejected for local tool operations.

key_constructs.name: test_validate_local_bash_command_paths_blocks_host_paths
key_constructs.purpose: Ensures bash command validation flags /etc/passwd style absolute paths as unsafe.

key_constructs.name: test_bash_tool_rejects_host_bash_when_local_sandbox_default
key_constructs.purpose: Ensures host bash execution is disabled by default in local sandbox and returns user-visible message rather than executing.

key_behaviors: asserts path mapping preserves trailing separators and separator style
asserts traversal and absolute-host-path blocking
asserts masking of host paths in outputs
asserts custom mounts and skills/ACP read-only semantics

pitfalls.mistake: Change validator error messages or exception types
pitfalls.consequence: Tests matching on exact messages or exception classes will fail
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve message contents referenced in tests or update tests accordingly

pitfalls.mistake: Remove or change mocking/patch points used in tests (e.g., _get_skills_host_path)
pitfalls.consequence: Tests relying on patched config lookups will not exercise intended branches and may fail
pitfalls.prevention: Keep the same importable symbol names and patch targets

key_sections: replace_virtual_path tests: verify mapping semantics and trailing-slash preservation
mask_local_paths_in_output tests: verify masking for user-data and skills paths
validate_local_tool_path and validate_local_bash_command_paths tests: coverage of read-only and traversal blocks
bash_tool host-bash gating tests
skip_unless_needed: long lists of similar traversal tests once core validation behavior is understood

relevant_snippets.description: Initial tests around replace_virtual_path and trailing slash behavior.
Tests exercising validate_local_bash_command_paths including URL and file:// cases.

related_files.relationship: The tests target nearly all public and many internal functions of this module
related_files.reason_to_check: Any change to tools.py must be verified against these tests; especially path mapping, validation and truncation logic.

insights.title: Tests for path separator preservation and command URL allowances
insights.problem: Ensure replace_virtual_path preserves trailing separators and Windows-style separators, and that URL paths are allowed in bash validation.
insights.root_cause: Regression from earlier path mapping changes and potential false-positive blocking of URLs.
insights.solution: Added tests that assert trailing slash preserved for POSIX and Windows-style actual bases and allow http/https URLs.
insights.lesson: Edge-case path and command validation behaviors (Windows separators, trailing slash, URL allowances) need explicit tests to avoid regressions.

backend/tests/test_sandbox_search_tools..short_description: Unit tests for sandbox search utilities and glob/grep tool wrappers.
backend/tests/test_sandbox_search_tools..description:
This module tests the glob and grep built-in tools and underlying sandbox search helpers. It sets up temporary workspaces and injects LocalSandbox or AioSandbox client behaviors via monkeypatch/patch to exercise both local filesystem and AioSandbox JSON payload parsing. Tests verify that glob_tool returns virtual paths (not host paths), ignores common ignored directories (node_modules), supports skills virtual paths, enforces include_dirs and max_results behavior, that grep_tool skips binary files and honors literal/case-sensitive flags, and that the AioSandbox JSON-parsing logic handles find_files/search_in_file responses and mismatched payload sizes robustly.

The tests also exercise find_glob_matches/find_grep_matches helpers (NotADirectoryError behavior, symlink skipping) and rely on ensure_sandbox_initialized being patched to return LocalSandbox instances for local tests. They check that results are masked to virtual paths and that result truncation honors configured and requested maxima.

key_constructs.name: test_glob_tool_returns_virtual_paths_and_ignores_common_dirs
key_constructs.purpose: Verifies that glob returns virtual container paths and excludes node_modules.

key_constructs.name: test_grep_tool_filters_by_glob_and_skips_binary_files
key_constructs.purpose: Ensures grep tool respects glob filters and skips binary files and non-matching file extensions.

key_constructs.name: test_grep_tool_truncates_results
key_constructs.purpose: Asserts grep respects max_results and truncation behavior and that config overrides can be mocked.

key_constructs.name: test_aio_sandbox_grep_parses_json
key_constructs.purpose: Validates AioSandbox client search parsing logic in grep path

key_constructs.name: test_find_glob_matches_raises_not_a_directory
key_constructs.purpose: Asserts filesystem helper raises NotADirectoryError for file inputs.

key_behaviors: ensures glob and grep tools return virtual (container) paths
ensures ignored directories are omitted
ensures AioSandbox JSON payload parsing yields GrepMatch instances
verifies truncation and max_results enforcement

pitfalls.mistake: Assume LocalSandbox and AioSandbox clients return identical payload shapes
pitfalls.consequence: Parsers in tools.py may mis-handle AioSandbox JSON and tests will surface mismatches
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the JSON handling logic and test adapters when modifying sandbox client interfaces

pitfalls.mistake: Change the virtual-path masking behavior (replace_virtual_path) without updating search tests
pitfalls.consequence: Tests asserting virtual container paths will fail if host paths leak into output
pitfalls.prevention: Keep masking consistent or update tests accordingly

key_sections: tests that patch ensure_sandbox_initialized to LocalSandbox: useful to see LocalSandbox expectations
AioSandbox parsing tests: demonstrate expected JSON shapes from AioSandboxClient
skip_unless_needed: low-level AioSandbox client patch stubs unless modifying AioSandbox parsing
