frontend/src/components/workspace/messag.short_description: React component that renders a collapsible card view for a subtask with status, prompt, and result.
frontend/src/components/workspace/messag.description:
This file exports a single React functional component SubtaskCard which displays the UI for an individual subtask inside a workspace message thread. It exists to present a compact, interactive summary (collapsed) and a detailed view (expanded) of a subtask's prompt, live status, tool call explanation, result (rendered as Markdown), and errors. The component is intentionally composed from smaller presentational pieces (ChainOfThought, ChainOfThoughtStep, ChainOfThoughtContent, MarkdownContent, FlipDisplay, CitationLink) so it can fit into the larger 'chain of thought' message UI used across the application.

SubtaskCard reads the subtask data via useSubtask(taskId) from the tasks context and localizes labels via useI18n(). It interprets task.status (expected values: "completed", "failed", "in_progress") to decide what icon, border/shimmer, and content to show. When a subtask is in progress and the latest message includes tool calls (detected by hasToolCalls), it shows an explanation generated by explainLastToolCall. Rehype plugins and Streamdown are used to animate words and render HTML/Markdown safely with interactive citation link components. Visual affordances (ShineBorder, Shimmer, FlipDisplay) are used to animate and highlight in-progress or changing content.

Because the component composes many domain-specific hooks and UI primitives, it must respect expectations those dependencies impose: the task object returned by useSubtask is assumed non-null (the code uses a non-null assertion), task.latestMessage is optional, and the i18n translation object t must expose t.subtasks keys. The component is UI-only (no network calls) and intended to reflect state from context and derived information from message contents (tool calls, latestMessage id).

key_constructs.name: SubtaskCard
key_constructs.purpose: Renders a collapsible card for a single subtask showing status, prompt, results, and errors.
key_constructs.reasoning: Editing or extending the subtask display logic must start here. This function orchestrates composition of smaller UI primitives and hooks (useSubtask, useI18n, useRehypeSplitWordsIntoSpans) and encodes conditional rendering based on task.status and task.latestMessage. Any change to layout, animation, or which fields are shown needs to preserve the conditional branches and expected properties accessed on the task object.

key_behaviors: displays subtask description and status icon
toggles expanded/collapsed view on click
shows animated shimmer and border when in progress
renders prompt and markdown result with animations
explains last tool call when present in latest message

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming useSubtask(taskId) can return undefined and removing the non-null assertion
pitfalls.consequence: If the context hook returns undefined at runtime and the component does not guard for it, accesses like task.status will throw causing rendering errors.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep the expectation that a valid task exists when this component is mounted, or add explicit guards and fallback UI if changing lifecycle assumptions.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing expected task.status string values or removing branches for 'in_progress'/'completed'/'failed'
pitfalls.consequence: Icons, animations, and text keys (t.subtasks[...]) may no longer match and the UI may show empty/incorrect states or omit important visual cues.
pitfalls.prevention: When modifying status handling make sure to update all dependent UI branches, icons, translation keys, and any code that derives behavior from these statuses (e.g., explaining tool calls).

pitfalls.mistake: Altering rehype plugin usage shape or Streamdown plugin config without confirming types
pitfalls.consequence: Word animation or markdown rendering may break, causing runtime errors or lost interactivity in the rendered content.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect the hook signature useRehypeSplitWordsIntoSpans(isLoading) and streamdownPluginsWithWordAnimation contract; ensure passed objects match downstream component prop expectations.

pitfalls.mistake: Removing or changing FlipDisplay uniqueKey behavior
pitfalls.consequence: FlipDisplay relies on a stable key (task.latestMessage?.id) to animate transitions; changing this could break animation synchronization and visual transitions.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the uniqueKey usage tied to latestMessage id or update FlipDisplay and its callers together if animation semantics must change.

key_sections: task acquisition: const task = useSubtask(taskId)! — confirms this component expects a valid subtask from context
status/icon logic: useMemo block that maps task.status to an icon and class names
collapsed/expanded rendering: Button and ChevronUp logic controlling ChainOfThought open prop
conditional content blocks under ChainOfThoughtContent: prompt rendering, in-progress tool call explanation, completed result rendering, and failed error rendering
skip_unless_needed: import list at top (boilerplate imports)
styling/className merging utilities unless changing visual classes

related_files.reason_to_check: If MarkdownContent changes how it accepts rehypePlugins, or the shape of content prop, SubtaskCard must adapt.

related_files.reason_to_check: If the task object shape (field names like status, description, latestMessage, prompt, result, error) changes, SubtaskCard will break.

related_files.relationship: Defines explainLastToolCall and hasToolCalls which SubtaskCard uses to detect and explain tool calls.
related_files.reason_to_check: If tool-call detection or explanation output changes format, the displayed text/markup here needs to remain compatible.

Build the frontend to catch compilation/typing issues: cd frontend && pnpm build

frontend/src/components/workspace/messag.short_description: React component that renders a single chat message with rich handling for files, images, reasoning and upload-state.
frontend/src/components/workspace/messag.description:
This file exports the UI for a single message in the conversation view. It exists to centralize rendering rules for different message types (human vs assistant vs tool/tooling states), provide integrations with artifact URLs, display uploaded files, and surface special internal states such as file uploads and chain-of-thought reasoning. The component is consumed by higher-level lists (MessageList -> MessageListItem) and grouped displays (MessageGroup) to build the chat UI.

Design decisions visible in the file: separation between the outer message wrapper (MessageListItem) and the actual content renderer (MessageContent_/MessageContent memo). The code explicitly handles multiple scenarios: human messages (with optional uploaded file previews), assistant messages that contain only reasoning (chain-of-thought), streaming/loading states, and temporary mock AI messages used during uploads (additional_kwargs.element === "task"). Artifact paths (virtual /mnt/*) are resolved to external URLs via resolveArtifactURL and images are wrapped with anchors to open full-sized files. Backwards-compatibility for older message formats is recognized through logic that parses a special <uploaded_files> tag when additional_kwargs.files is absent.

This component is tightly coupled to message extraction utilities (extractContentFromMessage, extractReasoningContentFromMessage, parseUploadedFiles, stripUploadedFilesTag), the artifact URL resolver (resolveArtifactURL), the Markdown renderer (MarkdownContent), and a family of UI primitives (AIElementMessage, MessageToolbar, Task, Reasoning etc.). It is meant to be purely presentational but needs to respect the shapes of Message and FileInMessage provided by the core message utilities and LangGraph SDK. The commit history indicates clipboard/copy behavior was adjusted elsewhere (CopyButton uses sanitized clipboard text), so this component must not duplicate or undo that sanitization when composing the clipboard content.

key_constructs.name: MessageListItem
key_constructs.purpose: Top-level exported React component that wraps a single message with toolbar and copy behavior.
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the entry point other components use to render messages. Agents editing must preserve the mapping of message.type to from prop, the inclusion of CopyButton content, and the toolbar's hover behaviour/positioning.

key_constructs.name: MessageImage
key_constructs.purpose: Custom image component that resolves artifact virtual paths and wraps images in links.
key_constructs.reasoning: Converts /mnt/ style artifact paths to public URLs via resolveArtifactURL and returns an anchor-wrapped img. Any changes to URL resolution or className formatting affect how artifacts are displayed and opened.

key_constructs.name: MessageContent_
key_constructs.purpose: Internal renderer for message content, handling human vs AI, reasoning-only, upload-state, and file lists.
key_constructs.reasoning: Contains the bulk of conditional rendering logic for messages; respects isLoading flag and uses many utility functions. Changes here can alter when reasoning-only messages are shown or how uploaded files are parsed and displayed.

key_constructs.name: getFileExt
key_constructs.purpose: Returns lowercased file extension or empty string.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by file-type labeling and image detection; must remain consistent with FILE_TYPE_MAP and IMAGE_EXTENSIONS lookups.

key_constructs.name: FILE_TYPE_MAP
key_constructs.purpose: Mapping of extension -> human-readable file type label.
key_constructs.reasoning: Authoritative mapping used for labels shown in RichFileCard badges; editing it will change UI labels across files rendered here.

key_constructs.name: IMAGE_EXTENSIONS
key_constructs.purpose: Array of extensions treated as images for preview rendering.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by isImageFile to decide if a file should render as an image preview; must align with backend artifact mime/type assumptions where possible.

key_constructs.name: getFileTypeLabel
key_constructs.purpose: Derives label to show on file badges using FILE_TYPE_MAP or fallback.
key_constructs.reasoning: Single place for human-readable file type in the file card UI; changes affect displayed badges for many artifacts.

key_constructs.name: isImageFile
key_constructs.purpose: Boolean helper checking if a filename is an image by extension.
key_constructs.reasoning: Determines image preview path; wrong classification breaks previews or shows images as generic file cards.

key_constructs.name: formatBytes
key_constructs.purpose: Formats numeric byte size into KB/MB human string, or — for zero.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by RichFileCard to display file size text; UI-consumed string format should be kept stable for i18n alignment if present elsewhere.

key_constructs.name: RichFilesList
key_constructs.purpose: Renders a row of file cards from files array.
key_constructs.reasoning: Simple mapping to RichFileCard; must maintain key generation scheme to avoid React list key collisions when files can repeat names.

key_constructs.name: RichFileCard
key_constructs.purpose: Renders an individual file card, supporting uploading state, image preview, or generic file card.
key_constructs.reasoning: Contains branching UI for uploading vs uploaded states and image vs non-image; changes here alter UX for uploads, thumbnails and size/label display.

key_constructs.name: MessageContent
key_constructs.purpose: Memoized export of MessageContent_ to avoid unnecessary re-renders.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used for render performance — replacing or removing memoization will affect re-renders and could impact large conversation lists.

key_behaviors: renders human and assistant messages with correct styling
renders uploaded files with previews and badges
resolves virtual artifact paths (/mnt/*) to public URLs
shows reasoning-only (chain-of-thought) blocks and streaming state
exposes copy-to-clipboard content via CopyButton

pitfalls.mistake: Modify how clipboard content is derived or remove use of extractContentFromMessage/extractReasoningContentFromMessage before passing to CopyButton.
pitfalls.consequence: Clipboard content may include internal instrumentation tokens, citation blocks or other artifacts; regressions observed historically (commit linking copy sanitization).
pitfalls.prevention: Respect the existing extraction utilities and CopyButton sanitization; do not replace with raw message fields.

pitfalls.mistake: Treat additional_kwargs.files as always present and ignore legacy <uploaded_files> tag parsing.
pitfalls.consequence: Older messages using the <uploaded_files> inline tag would stop showing file previews, causing loss of backward compatibility.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the current conditional that parses uploaded files from rawContent when additional_kwargs.files is missing or empty.

pitfalls.mistake: Change resolveArtifactURL usage or the condition that checks src.startsWith('/mnt/').
pitfalls.consequence: Artifact images and downloadable files could link to incorrect URLs, breaking previews and download/open-in-new-window behavior.
pitfalls.prevention: Continue routing /mnt/ artifact paths through resolveArtifactURL(threadId) and keep threadId dependency intact.

pitfalls.mistake: Remove memoization around MessageContent_ (MessageContent = memo(...)) or change hook dependency arrays incorrectly.
pitfalls.consequence: Performance regressions in long conversations (excessive re-renders) and possible stale closures leading to wrong thread_id or component props used during render.
pitfalls.prevention: Retain memoization; when editing hooks or dependencies, ensure they remain correct (e.g., thread_id in the components memo).

pitfalls.mistake: Assume message.additional_kwargs.element === 'task' is only used for tasks and change the branch to render differently.
pitfalls.consequence: Uploading state UI (mock AI message while files upload) may be removed or misrepresented, causing confusing UX during uploads.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep the special-case branch for 'task' element to render the upload mock with loader and contentToDisplay.

key_sections: MessageContent_: core conditional rendering logic for human vs assistant, reasoning-only, uploading-state and files list
MessageImage: artifact URL resolution and anchor-wrapped img previews
RichFileCard: UI branches for uploading, image-preview and generic file card (badge + size)
skip_unless_needed: FILE_TYPE_MAP: mapping table for file labels
getFileExt/formatBytes: small helpers

related_files.reason_to_check: If you change message rendering rules or props of MessageListItem (e.g., memory of isLoading), update how MessageList passes props and groups messages.

related_files.relationship: Renders grouped/chain-of-thought UI that often uses the same MarkdownContent and reasoning extraction utilities.
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to reasoning content extraction or MarkdownContent props may require coordinating rendering logic with MessageGroup.

related_files.relationship: Artifact detail and file content viewers operate on artifact URLs produced by resolveArtifactURL used in this file.
related_files.reason_to_check: If artifact path resolution or public URL conventions change, both files must be updated to keep preview and open-in-new-window behavior consistent.

data_constants_to_check: ensure artifact URL resolver (resolveArtifactURL) expectations about /mnt/ prefixes

insights.title: Copy button strips citations when copying message content
insights.problem: Copying message content included citation blocks/links leading to messy clipboard text.
insights.root_cause: CopyButton previously used extractContentFromMessage directly without removing citations.
insights.solution: Wrap clipboardData with removeAllCitations(...) so copied text excludes citation artifacts.
insights.lesson_learned: Clipboard UX must return clean user content; remove internal instrumentation tokens/blocks before copying.

backend/packages/harness/deerflow/agents.short_description: LLM-driven memory updater and helpers for CRUD and persistence of 'facts' and summaries
backend/packages/harness/deerflow/agents.description:
This module implements the memory update pipeline that (a) provides CRUD helpers for manual memory facts, (b) invokes an LLM to compute structured memory updates from a recent conversation, and (c) applies and persists those updates via a storage provider. It intentionally delegates all I/O to a storage abstraction (get_memory_storage) so the core logic focuses on validation, merging/deduplication, and LLM response parsing. The module also contains utilities to normalize LLM response content shapes and to strip ephemeral upload-event mentions from stored summaries/facts to avoid persisting session-scoped upload metadata.

Design decisions visible in the file: (1) persistence is delegated to get_memory_storage().load/save/reload instead of performing file I/O inline which simplifies testing and pluggable storage backends; (2) fact deduplication uses a Unicode-aware casefold-based key function (_fact_content_key) to avoid storing the same fact with only case differences; (3) numeric inputs (fact confidence) are strictly validated with math.isfinite and bounds checking to avoid NaN/Inf leaking into JSON; (4) the MemoryUpdater class encapsulates model selection/invocation and applies structured updates that the LLM is expected to return as JSON. The module contains small backward-compatible wrappers (_create_empty_memory, _save_memory_to_file) used by higher layers or tests.

Interactions: Tests and middleware call into the convenience function update_memory_from_conversation or instantiate MemoryUpdater directly. The module calls format_conversation_for_update and MEMORY_UPDATE_PROMPT from the memory.prompt module, and uses get_memory_config to respect thresholds and limits (max_facts, fact_confidence_threshold). Tests patch get_memory_config and get_memory_storage to assert correct behavior without touching disk.

key_constructs.name: _create_empty_memory
key_constructs.purpose: Backward-compatible wrapper around the storage-layer empty-memory factory
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by callers/tests expecting a local helper; delegates to create_empty_memory() to centralize shape/version of empty memory.

key_constructs.name: _save_memory_to_file
key_constructs.purpose: Backward-compatible wrapper that calls the configured storage provider save()
key_constructs.reasoning: Tests and legacy code expect a simple function to persist memory; ensure calls follow storage provider semantics (return True/False).

key_constructs.name: get_memory_data
key_constructs.purpose: Loads memory data via the storage provider for a given agent_name
key_constructs.reasoning: Single place to fetch normalized memory objects used by update and CRUD helpers; callers mock this function in tests.

key_constructs.name: reload_memory_data
key_constructs.purpose: Reloads memory via storage provider (semantically different from load in some storage implementations)
key_constructs.reasoning: Exposes reload semantics to consumers who need to refresh any caches in storage.

key_constructs.name: import_memory_data
key_constructs.purpose: Persist an externally provided full memory payload via storage and return the normalized load
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by the API to import memory blobs; raises OSError if storage.save returns False.

key_constructs.name: clear_memory_data
key_constructs.purpose: Create an empty memory structure and persist it
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by API endpoints to reset persistent memory; calls storage.save and raises OSError on failure.

key_constructs.name: _validate_confidence
key_constructs.purpose: Ensure confidence is finite and within [0, 1]
key_constructs.reasoning: Prevents NaN/Infinity or out-of-range floats from being persisted; tests depend on raising ValueError('confidence') for invalid inputs.

key_constructs.name: create_memory_fact
key_constructs.purpose: Add a manual fact to memory (validates content, category and confidence) and persist
key_constructs.reasoning: Provides manual CRUD operation used by the gateway API; enforces trimming/validation, assigns id, createdAt, and source='manual'.

key_constructs.name: delete_memory_fact
key_constructs.purpose: Delete a fact by id from persisted memory and persist the result
key_constructs.reasoning: Removes only the matching id and raises KeyError for unknown ids; storage.save failure produces OSError.

key_constructs.name: update_memory_fact
key_constructs.purpose: Update fields of an existing fact and persist the result
key_constructs.reasoning: Supports partial updates (omitted fields are preserved), validates non-empty content and confidence values, raises KeyError when fact not found.

key_constructs.name: _extract_text
key_constructs.purpose: Normalize LLM response content shapes (str, list of blocks, other) into plain text
key_constructs.reasoning: LLM model outputs can be structured (e.g., list of strings/blocks). This function concatenates string chunks without separators, joins dict text blocks with newlines, and avoids producing Python reprs for lists/dicts — crucial before JSON.loads on model output.

key_constructs.name: _UPLOAD_SENTENCE_RE
key_constructs.purpose: Regex to match sentences that describe file-upload events
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by stripping logic to remove ephemeral upload-event sentences from summaries and facts before saving to long-term memory.

key_constructs.name: _strip_upload_mentions_from_memory
key_constructs.purpose: Remove upload-event sentences from memory summaries and drop facts that describe uploads
key_constructs.reasoning: Prevents session-scoped upload events (paths, uploaded_files blocks) from being stored in long-term memory; called before saving LLM-driven updates.

key_constructs.name: _fact_content_key
key_constructs.purpose: Return a canonical casefolded content string for deduplication or None for non-strings/empty
key_constructs.reasoning: Case-insensitive (Unicode-aware casefold) deduplication key to avoid near-duplicate facts differing only by case or whitespace.

key_constructs.name: MemoryUpdater
key_constructs.purpose: Encapsulates LLM selection/invocation and applies structured LLM updates to memory
key_constructs.reasoning: Holds model name override, constructs prompt using MEMORY_UPDATE_PROMPT and format_conversation_for_update, parses JSON output from the model, and delegates actual memory mutation to _apply_updates.

key_constructs.name: _apply_updates
key_constructs.purpose: Merge structured updates (user/history sections, remove/add facts) into current memory respecting config thresholds
key_constructs.reasoning: Core merging logic (thresholding by confidence, preventing duplicates using _fact_content_key, preserving source/sourceError when provided and non-empty, trimming to max_facts by confidence) used both from MemoryUpdater and test harnesses.

key_constructs.name: update_memory_from_conversation
key_constructs.purpose: Convenience wrapper that creates a MemoryUpdater and calls update_memory
key_constructs.reasoning: Simplifies call sites that don't need to manage MemoryUpdater instances; tests exercise both wrapper and class directly.

key_behaviors: parses LLM JSON responses for structured memory updates
deduplicates new facts against existing facts using casefold keys
validates and persists manual fact create/update/delete operations
removes session-scoped upload mentions before saving memory

pitfalls.mistake: Assume model.invoke returns a plain string
pitfalls.consequence: JSON parsing will fail if the model returns structured content (list/dicts) or wraps JSON in markdown; code attempts to normalize but fragile changes to response format can break parsing.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect _extract_text behavior and the markdown-block stripping logic; ensure tests covering JSONDecodeError-handling are run after edits.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing deduplication to use lower() or simple normalization instead of casefold()
pitfalls.consequence: Unicode case variants may be stored as duplicates (regression reintroducing duplicates across locales).
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve use of casefold() in _fact_content_key when modifying dedup rules.

pitfalls.mistake: Bypassing get_memory_storage() and directly accessing files
pitfalls.consequence: Breaks storage abstraction and test mocks; unit tests patch get_memory_storage to supply MagicMock storage objects.
pitfalls.prevention: Always call get_memory_storage().load/save/reload for persistence; update tests to patch public interface if behavior changes.

pitfalls.mistake: Allowing NaN/Inf into confidence fields
pitfalls.consequence: JSON or downstream ranking/formatting may behave incorrectly; tests expect ValueError('confidence') for such inputs.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep _validate_confidence with math.isfinite + range checks for all code paths that accept confidence floats.

pitfalls.mistake: Dropping sourceError field unconditionally
pitfalls.consequence: Loses provenance information for correction facts; tests verify non-empty sourceError is preserved and empty strings are ignored.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve non-empty trimmed sourceError strings when present; skip adding if string is only whitespace.

key_sections: _extract_text: how model outputs are normalized before JSON parsing
MemoryUpdater.update_memory: prompt building, model invocation, JSON parsing and save flow
_apply_updates: merging rules, deduplication, thresholding, trimming, and sourceError preservation
_strip_upload_mentions_from_memory: upload-event filtering logic before persist
skip_unless_needed: _create_empty_memory and _save_memory_to_file wrappers (backwards compatibility helpers)

tests.example_command: cd backend && uv run pytest backend/tests/test_memory_updater.py -q

relevant_snippets.description: Unit tests for _apply_updates and CRUD helpers, plus _extract_text tests validating normalization behavior.

related_files.relationship: Provides MEMORY_UPDATE_PROMPT and format_conversation_for_update used to build LLM prompts
related_files.reason_to_check: Any changes to prompt format or expected structured JSON shape must be coordinated between prompt and updater parsing logic.

related_files.relationship: Defines storage provider used via get_memory_storage(); persistence behavior and save()/load() semantics live here
related_files.reason_to_check: Storage API contract (return values, exceptions) is relied on by updater; test mocks patch get_memory_storage.

related_files.relationship: Queue consumer constructs ConversationContext instances and calls MemoryUpdater.update_memory; flags (correction/reinforcement) are propagated
related_files.reason_to_check: Queue tests and logic depend on updater signature and flag handling.

tests_to_run: cd backend && uv run pytest backend/tests/test_memory_updater.py -q
data_constants_to_check: backend/packages/harness/deerflow/agents/memory/prompt.py: expected JSON schema produced by model

insights.title: Case-insensitive fact deduplication and reinforcement hint support
insights.problem: Memory stored duplicate facts differing only by case; reinforcement signals (user positive confirmation) were not surfaced to the memory updater so it could treat confirmations differently.
insights.root_cause: _fact_content_key used simple strip/lower behavior was inconsistent for Unicode and reinforcement detection was missing.
insights.solution: Use casefold() for Unicode-aware case-insensitive equality in _fact_content_key. Add reinforcement_detected parameter to update_memory and inject reinforcement_hint into the memory update prompt when detected (combined with correction_hint when both present).
insights.lesson: Fact deduplication must be Unicode-aware (casefold); positive reinforcement signals should be treated separately from corrections and forwarded so memory stores higher confidence suggestions.

insights.title: Memory updater supports correction hints and sourceError preservation
insights.problem: Memory updater didn't surface correction hints into prompts and dropped sourceError from incoming facts.
insights.root_cause: update_memory and _apply_updates lacked handling for correction_detected and sourceError fields.
insights.solution: Added correction_detected parameter through update_memory and update_memory_from_conversation; inject correction_hint into prompt when detected; preserve non-empty sourceError strings on saved facts and ignore empty sourceError.
insights.lesson_learned: Preserve provenance fields from structured model output and surface behavioral hints into prompts when signals (like corrections) exist; validate incoming strings before persisting.

insights.title: Add manual create and update operations for memory facts
insights.problem: Memory UI needed the ability to add and edit saved facts manually; existing updater lacked explicit create/update operations.
insights.root_cause: Updater previously supported reload/delete and automatic extraction, but manual CRUD for facts was missing.
insights.solution: Added create_memory_fact and update_memory_fact helpers with validation (non-empty content, confidence finite in [0,1]) and persisted via existing storage wrapper. Also added _validate_confidence and restored compatibility helpers for create_empty_memory and _save_memory_to_file.
insights.lesson_learned: Updater helpers should provide stable validation messages (ValueError('confidence') etc.) that routers can map to HTTP 400 responses. Keep storage-layer wrappers for backward compatibility.

insights.title: Use math.isfinite to validate confidence values
insights.problem: Floating special values like NaN and Infinity could be passed as confidence and stored, producing invalid JSON semantics for confidence.
insights.root_cause: Validation previously only checked numeric bounds without ruling out NaN/Inf.
insights.solution: Added _validate_confidence using math.isfinite and explicit range checks; raises ValueError('confidence') for invalid values.
insights.lesson_learned: Validate numeric inputs against non-finite values when persisting to JSON or external stores.

insights.title: Delegate memory file load/save to storage provider
insights.problem: Updater module duplicated file IO and caching logic making extension/hardening harder.
insights.root_cause: Updater contained both LLM-based update logic and raw file handling.
insights.solution: Refactor to call get_memory_storage().load/reload/save for memory operations; remove duplicated file path and caching helpers.
insights.lesson_learned: Keep I/O and persistence responsibilities in storage layer; core logic should call a stable interface for load/save to allow test doubles and alternative backends.

backend/tests/test_memory_updater.py.short_description: Unit tests for memory updater CRUD, merging, deduplication and LLM response normalization
backend/tests/test_memory_updater.py.description:
This test module validates the memory updater behavior: manual fact create/update/delete operations, applying structured updates produced by an LLM, confidence validation, duplicate suppression, source/sourceError preservation, upload mention stripping, and normalization of model response content shapes. Tests heavily mock get_memory_config and the storage layer (via get_memory_data or get_memory_storage) so the tests exercise merging logic and validation without performing actual I/O. The file also includes a TestExtractText class that ensures _extract_text correctly handles strings, lists of text blocks, mixed lists of string chunks and blocks, empty content, and non-string types before downstream JSON parsing.

The tests reflect historical fixes (commit insights): confidence validation with math.isfinite, case-insensitive Unicode-aware deduplication, preservation/ignoring behavior for sourceError, and the prompt injection for correction/reinforcement hints. Many tests assert specific exceptions (ValueError('content'), ValueError('confidence'), KeyError(fact_id)) which act as a small API contract relied on by the gateway router code to map to HTTP status codes.

key_constructs.name: test_apply_updates_skips_existing_duplicate_and_preserves_removals
key_constructs.purpose: Asserts that applying updates removes explicit factsToRemove and does not add duplicates that match existing facts (case-insensitive logic implied)

backend/tests/test_memory_queue.py.short_description: Unit tests for memory update queue behavior and flag propagation to MemoryUpdater
backend/tests/test_memory_queue.py.description: This test module validates the MemoryUpdateQueue behavior: merging updates for the same thread while preserving correction_detected and reinforcement_detected flags, and ensuring queued items are forwarded to MemoryUpdater.update_memory with the correct flags and agent_name. It patches get_memory_config to enable the queue and patches internal timer reset methods and the MemoryUpdater constructor to isolate behavior from timing and external LLM invocation. The tests ensure the queue deduplicates by thread_id and that once a flag has been set for a thread, subsequent adds that unset the flag do not clear it (preserve 'true' semantics).

key_constructs.name: test_queue_add_preserves_existing_correction_flag_for_same_thread
key_constructs.purpose: Asserts that adding messages for the same thread preserves an earlier correction_detected=True even if later add sets it False
key_constructs.reasoning: Protects signaling semantics: once a correction is detected in a thread, it should not be silently overwritten to False by subsequent turns.

key_constructs.name: test_process_queue_forwards_correction_flag_to_updater
key_constructs.purpose: Ensures the queue calls MemoryUpdater.update_memory with correction_detected set as recorded
key_constructs.reasoning: Validates correct propagation of flags to the updater where they change prompt hints/behavior.

key_constructs.name: test_queue_add_preserves_existing_reinforcement_flag_for_same_thread
key_constructs.purpose: Asserts reinforcement flag preservation across queue.add calls for the same thread
key_constructs.reasoning: Mirrors correction-flag semantics for positive reinforcement signals; queue must not overwrite True with False.

key_constructs.name: test_process_queue_forwards_reinforcement_flag_to_updater
key_constructs.purpose: Ensures the queue calls MemoryUpdater.update_memory with reinforcement_detected set as recorded
key_constructs.reasoning: Confirms the flag is forwarded; MemoryUpdater will inject reinforcement_hint into prompts when present.

pitfalls.mistake: Assume queue.add always overwrites existing flags
pitfalls.consequence: Tests will fail and real runs could lose correction/reinforcement signals; prompt hints may not be set.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect existing flag preserving logic in queue when updating an item for the same thread.

pitfalls.mistake: Replace MemoryUpdater signature (add/remove parameters) without updating queue._process_queue call
pitfalls.consequence: Queue tests will fail and runtime will raise TypeError; flags may not be forwarded.
pitfalls.prevention: Coordinate signature changes with queue._process_queue and update tests accordingly.

frontend/src/components/workspace/welcom.short_description: React client component that renders the workspace welcome/greeting UI with mode-specific variants and simple animation.
frontend/src/components/workspace/welcom.description:
This file exports a small React client component Welcome that renders the welcome header and description shown in the workspace area of the frontend. It exists to centralize the onboarding/greeting text and visual treatment (emoji, aurora-styled text, color variants, and a wave animation) for several usage modes (normal, ultra, and a special URL-driven "skill" mode). The component is intentionally a client component ("use client") because it uses client-only hooks from next/navigation and React hooks to manage memoized values and side effects.

Design decisions visible in the code: i18n is used via a custom useI18n hook to pull localized strings (t.welcome.*). The component accepts an optional prop mode ("ultra" | "pro" | "thinking" | "flash") but also reads the URL search parameter mode via useSearchParams() to decide whether to show the "skill"-creation heading/description (searchParams.get("mode") === "skill"). Visual differences: an ultra mode uses a three-color aurora gradient (hardcoded hex array), while other modes fall back to a CSS variable for text color. The implementation detects newline characters in localized description strings and conditionally wraps them in a <pre> to preserve line breaks.

There is a module-level waved boolean used to show a single-time waving animation for the emoji; the value is flipped on mount via useEffect so the animation runs only once across component mounts (module lifecycle). The component composes with a small local utility (cn) for className concatenation and an AuroraText component to render the stylized greeting text. Because this file wires UI, i18n keys, CSS classes, and search param logic together, any changes must respect the i18n contract and Next.js client-only constraints.

key_constructs.name: waved
key_constructs.purpose: Module-level boolean flag that tracks whether the emoji wave animation has already run.
key_constructs.reasoning: This variable lives at module scope so the wave animation is shown only once per application/module lifecycle. An agent editing the file must respect that changes to this variable change the animation replay semantics across component instances and navigation.

key_constructs.name: Welcome
key_constructs.purpose: Default exported React component that renders the welcome heading and description with mode-dependent visuals and i18n text.
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the main UI entrypoint in the file. It composes i18n text, URL search params, an optional prop mode, and visual helper components (AuroraText) to show different greeting states. When editing, agents should preserve the use of useSearchParams (client-only) and the newline-to-<pre> behavior for localized descriptions.

key_behaviors: renders greeting heading text from i18n
renders alternate heading/description when URL mode=skill
shows an aurora-styled greeting with a color variant for ultra mode
plays a one-time wave animation using a module-scoped flag
preserves multi-line localized descriptions by rendering <pre> when string contains a newline

pitfalls.mistake: Removing the "use client" directive or converting the component to a server component.
pitfalls.consequence: useSearchParams and React hooks will fail at runtime (Next.js will throw or behavior will break), since those hooks require a client component.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep the "use client" directive and avoid introducing server-only APIs into this file.

pitfalls.mistake: Relying only on the mode prop and removing the searchParams.get('mode') check (or vice versa) without synchronizing them.
pitfalls.consequence: The component may show inconsistent headings/description depending on navigation method (query param vs prop), causing unexpected UI when URL indicates skill-creation but prop does not (or the opposite).
pitfalls.prevention: If altering mode handling, ensure both prop and URL-driven mode semantics are considered and documented; preserve the existing behavior unless deliberately changing the UX contract.

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming waved is per-component instance rather than module-level.
pitfalls.consequence: Changing waved to local state or removing its module-level persistence will change when (and how often) the wave animation runs — possibly re-triggering the animation on every mount or never playing.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect that waved is module-level and intentionally persistent for the life of the module; if changing it, be explicit about new animation semantics and test across navigations and HMR.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing newline detection logic (t.welcome.*.includes('\n')) to a different check without handling Windows CRLFs.
pitfalls.consequence: Strings with '\r\n' may not be detected as multi-line and will be rendered in a single paragraph losing intended formatting.
pitfalls.prevention: If modifying multi-line detection, consider trimming or normalizing CRLF sequences or using a more robust newline test.

key_sections: mode prop & useMemo hooks: isUltra and colors — important for visual variants
useEffect that sets waved — determines one-time animation behavior
render branch that checks searchParams.get('mode') === 'skill' — special-case heading/description
description rendering that conditionally uses <pre> when the i18n string contains a newline

insights.problem: Welcome text did not change when creating a skill (mode=skill).
insights.root_cause: Component only had a single greeting/description path.
insights.solution: Detect searchParams.get('mode') === 'skill' and render alternate heading and description (supporting multi-line descriptions via <pre>). Use new i18n keys createYourOwnSkill and createYourOwnSkillDescription.
insights.lesson_learned: Use URL mode flags to adapt onboarding content; include safe rendering for multi-line localized text.

backend/app/channels/manager.py.short_description: Bridges inbound IM messages to the DeerFlow lead agent (LangGraph) and handles file ingestion, streaming, and artifact delivery.
backend/app/channels/manager.py.description:
This module implements the ChannelManager and many helper routines that translate inbound instant-message events into LangGraph runs, handle streaming updates, ingest inbound attachments into the controlled uploads area, and publish outbound responses back to the IM channel via the MessageBus. It exists to centralize channel-specific concerns: per-channel file readers (e.g., WeCom AES decryption), safe filename/ingestion logic, security checks on artifact virtual paths, formatting of uploaded-file metadata blocks for the agent context, and friendly handling of concurrency conflicts when multiple inbound messages target the same agent thread. The module is designed to be the single place that enforces safety (no arbitrary filesystem exfiltration), normalizes legacy channel configuration (assistant_id → agent_name injection), and reconciles heterogeneous LangGraph run payload shapes (stream vs final results) into the textual and attachment responses that IM channels expect.

Design decisions visible in the file: a registry of inbound file readers (INBOUND_FILE_READERS) provides pluggable per-channel download/decryption hooks; a virtual-path prefix for outputs (_OUTPUTS_VIRTUAL_PREFIX) and strict resolve/relative_to checks enforce that only files produced in the sandbox outputs dir are delivered; streaming payload parsing/accumulation functions (_accumulate_stream_text, _merge_stream_text, _extract_text_content, _extract_stream_message_id) attempt robust, best-effort extraction of user-facing AI text from a variety of streaming event shapes; and a specific helper _is_thread_busy_error maps LangGraph conflict-style errors into a user-friendly THREAD_BUSY_MESSAGE. The ChannelManager class (core entrypoint) wires these helpers together with MessageBus and ChannelStore, reads channel commands, and orchestrates runs.wait and streaming behavior (see tests for expected behaviors). The module relies on several external components (MessageBus, ChannelStore, deerflow.uploads.manager functions, and deerflow.config.paths) that must be kept consistent with any edits here.

key_constructs.name: DEFAULT_LANGGRAPH_URL
key_constructs.purpose: Default URL used to contact the LangGraph server if none provided.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used as fallback for manager instantiation; tests or runtime may rely on this default.

key_constructs.purpose: Default reader that downloads a file from a URL using httpx.

key_constructs.purpose: Reader for WeCom attachments that optionally decrypts data using an AES key.
key_constructs.reasoning: Conditionally imports decrypt_file from aibot.crypto_utils; absence or failure to import is handled gracefully (logs and returns None).

key_constructs.name: InvalidChannelSessionConfigError
key_constructs.purpose: Raised for invalid channel session configuration (e.g., bad assistant_id).
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by normalization/validation logic to fail fast with a clear error type recognized by ChannelManager.

key_constructs.name: _is_thread_busy_error
key_constructs.purpose: Heuristic to detect that a LangGraph run conflict indicates the thread is busy.
key_constructs.reasoning: Abstracts detection of ConflictError or message text heuristics so ChannelManager can return a friendly user message.

key_constructs.name: _normalize_custom_agent_name
key_constructs.purpose: Normalize and validate legacy assistant_id into a sanitized agent name.
key_constructs.reasoning: Enforces allowed characters and canonical form for mapping legacy configs to agent_name in run_context.

key_constructs.name: _extract_response_text
key_constructs.purpose: Extract the last meaningful AI text response from a runs.wait result payload.
key_constructs.reasoning: Walks the messages in reverse to avoid returning text from prior human turns and handles tool interrupts and compound content formats.

key_constructs.name: _accumulate_stream_text
key_constructs.purpose: Accumulate/merge streamed message chunks into a latest displayable text per message_id.
key_constructs.reasoning: Supports various incoming streaming event shapes (string payloads, mapping payloads, tool messages) and manages per-message buffers.

key_constructs.name: _extract_artifacts
key_constructs.purpose: Collect newly-created artifact virtual paths from messages after the last human message.
key_constructs.reasoning: Ensures only artifacts produced by the current agent response are returned (not the full historical artifacts state).

key_constructs.name: _resolve_attachments
key_constructs.purpose: Resolve virtual artifact paths to actual filesystem paths and produce ResolvedAttachment metadata.
key_constructs.reasoning: Performs security checks (prefix whitelist, resolve().relative_to(outputs_dir), file existence) and guesses MIME types; important for preventing exfiltration.

key_constructs.name: _prepare_artifact_delivery
key_constructs.purpose: Combine response text and artifacts into a final textual fallback plus a list of attachments ready for outbound delivery.
key_constructs.reasoning: Ensures unresolved artifacts are included as text fallbacks and that resolved attachments are always listed in the text.

key_constructs.name: _ingest_inbound_files
key_constructs.purpose: Download/decrypt inbound files and write them into the thread uploads dir with claimed unique filenames.
key_constructs.reasoning: Uses uploads.manager.claim_unique_filename and ensure_uploads_dir, iterates attachments, uses the channel-specific reader, handles missing filename/type, and returns created file metadata for inclusion in the agent context.

key_constructs.name: _format_uploaded_files_block
key_constructs.purpose: Render a machine- and human-readable <uploaded_files> block describing newly ingested files to be prepended to the agent prompt.
key_constructs.reasoning: Gives the agent clear context about uploaded files and which tools to use (read_file, view_image).

key_constructs.name: ChannelManager
key_constructs.purpose: Central dispatcher that consumes messages from MessageBus and bridges them to LangGraph runs, streaming, and outbound publishing.
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the main entry point for channel-to-agent flow. It coordinates ingestion, run parameter preparation (including assistant_id→agent_name normalization), calls to runs.wait/stream, formatting of replies, handling concurrency conflicts, and attachment delivery.

callers.context: from app.channels.manager import DEFAULT_GATEWAY_URL, DEFAULT_LANGGRAPH_URL, ChannelManager

key_behaviors: ingests inbound attachments into a per-thread uploads directory
converts LangGraph streaming events into displayable AI text snapshots
detects thread-busy conflicts and returns a user-facing busy message

pitfalls.mistake: Assume arbitrary virtual paths returned by agents are safe to resolve and attach
pitfalls.consequence: Could allow exfiltration of uploads/workspace files outside the intended outputs dir
pitfalls.prevention: Respect the _OUTPUTS_VIRTUAL_PREFIX check and the resolve().relative_to(outputs_dir) verification; do not remove these checks without a full security review.

pitfalls.mistake: Remove or change the per-channel reader registry semantics without updating ingestion logic
pitfalls.consequence: Channel-specific decryption (e.g., WeCom AES) may stop working and inbound files could be missing or corrupted
pitfalls.prevention: Keep INBOUND_FILE_READERS and register_inbound_file_reader semantics intact; ensure _ingest_inbound_files uses the registry.

pitfalls.mistake: Change streaming merge heuristics or ID extraction without validating against tests and actual LangGraph payload shapes
pitfalls.consequence: Streaming snapshots may appear truncated, duplicated, or out-of-order to the user
pitfalls.prevention: Run streaming-related tests and exercise channels that support streaming (feishu) after edits.

pitfalls.mistake: Assume external optional dependencies are always present (e.g., aibot.crypto_utils.decrypt_file)
pitfalls.consequence: Imports failing at runtime could crash ingestion; tests expect graceful handling
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve try/except import patterns and the logger.exception usage; keep behavior of returning None when decrypt module unavailable.

pitfalls.mistake: Change timeouts or httpx usage without running integration tests
pitfalls.consequence: Inbound file downloads could hang or fail under different network conditions or test mocks
pitfalls.prevention: Respect the current httpx.Timeout(20.0) and test expectations; update tests if timeout semantics change.

key_sections: _ingest_inbound_files: downloads and persists inbound files (critical for attachments)
_accumulate_stream_text & helpers: streaming extraction and merge behavior that affect UX
_resolve_attachments & _prepare_artifact_delivery: security checks and final attachment assembly

insights.title: Ingest inbound files (HTTP + WeCom AES) and present uploaded files block to agent
insights.problem: Inbound IM channels send file attachments (often via temporary URLs or encrypted blobs). The system lacked a unified ingestion pipeline to claim a safe filename, persist file bytes into uploads dir, and expose a structured uploaded_files block in the agent context.
insights.root_cause: Channels previously forwarded only metadata; no pluggable inbound file readers nor a standard ingestion process existed for different channel formats (wecom AES payloads require decryption).
insights.solution: Add INBOUND_FILE_READERS registry and register wecom reader; implement _ingest_inbound_files() to download, decrypt (via aibot.decrypt_file for WeCom), claim_unique_filename and write bytes to uploads dir, and return created file metadata. Add _format_uploaded_files_block() to render a machine- and human-readable <uploaded_files> block. Integrate ingestion in ChannelManager so uploaded files are available to the agent.
insights.lesson_learned: Treat inbound attachments as first-class inputs: persist them to controlled uploads directory, normalise filenames, support per-channel readers (for encryption schemes), and expose the files in agent context with clear tool usage guidance.

insights.title: Reject concurrent same-thread runs and report friendly message
insights.problem: Concurrent run attempts targeting the same thread could cause confusing errors and lead to lost user feedback.
insights.root_cause: Manager forwarded LangGraph run stream/wait calls without converting multitask conflicts into user-friendly replies.
insights.solution: Detect ConflictError or messages indicating 'already running a task' with _is_thread_busy_error (widened to BaseException); log as warning and return THREAD_BUSY_MESSAGE to the channel user when appropriate. Also set default multitask_strategy='reject' for channel-invoked runs.
insights.lesson_learned: Translate backend concurrency errors into user-facing messages; guard against heterogeneous exceptions by matching on known error types and message heuristics.

insights.title: Support custom assistant_id routed through lead_agent via agent_name
insights.problem: Channel configs using custom assistant IDs (legacy formats) were not routed correctly to lead_agent with agent_name injection.
insights.root_cause: Channels assumed assistant_id directly mapped to an agent graph id.
insights.solution: Added normalization _normalize_custom_agent_name and logic to inject agent_name into run_context while switching assistant_id to lead_agent. Validation raises InvalidChannelSession Config errors when names invalid.
insights.lesson_learned: Maintain backward compatibility by mapping legacy config forms into current runtime patterns but validate and fail early with clear messages for invalid formats.

backend/packages/harness/deerflow/config.short_description: Application configuration loader, cache, and runtime override utilities for DeerFlow
backend/packages/harness/deerflow/config.description:
This module centralizes how the DeerFlow application loads, validates, and caches its runtime configuration (config.yaml). It provides a Pydantic AppConfig model that represents the authoritative in-memory config object and a set of helper functions to resolve the config file path deterministically, load/validate YAML, resolve environment-variable placeholders, apply per-subsystem loader functions, and maintain a reloadable cached singleton. The module also introduces runtime-scoped overrides using ContextVar so a single process can temporarily swap the active AppConfig for the current logical execution context (helpful for concurrent runs/tests).

Design reasons: (1) deterministic config discovery — _default_config_candidates() computes backend and repo-root candidate locations relative to this module rather than relying on the current working directory; (2) progressive loading — from_file delegates loading and calls various sub-configuration loader functions (title, summarization, memory, subagents, tool_search, guardrails, checkpointer, stream_bridge, and loads ACP & extensions) to ensure subsystem registries are refreshed; and (3) caching with mtime detection — get_app_config keeps a cached AppConfig but will reload automatically if the resolved config file changes on disk. The module purposely separates setting a custom in-memory config (set_app_config) from the auto-reload path (controlled by _app_config_is_custom), and exposes push/pop context API for temporary overrides using ContextVar.

How it fits: This file is the single Python-side entry point for other modules to obtain configuration state. Modules calling get_app_config() rely on its caching and reload semantics. Several imported loader functions (e.g., load_memory_config_from_dict, load_acp_config_from_dict, ExtensionsConfig.from_file) indicate this module coordinates global side-effects (registering or refreshing subsystem configs) as part of loading the YAML file. Because it calls load_dotenv() at import time, .env variables are loaded early and influence resolve_env_variables behavior.

key_constructs.name: AppConfig
key_constructs.purpose: Pydantic model representing the overall application configuration (models, tools, sandbox, memory, subagents, etc.)
key_constructs.reasoning: Agents or runtime code will instantiate or validate against this class; editing it affects the shape of the app-wide config and how downstream code expects fields to be present and typed.

key_constructs.name: _default_config_candidates
key_constructs.purpose: Return deterministic candidate Paths for config.yaml (backend dir and repo root) computed relative to this module file.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by resolve_config_path to avoid relying on cwd; relies on module layout (parents[4]) and therefore any change to package layout must respect or update this calculation.

key_constructs.name: resolve_config_path
key_constructs.purpose: Resolve which config file to use using explicit arg, DEER_FLOW_CONFIG_PATH env var, or deterministic defaults.
key_constructs.reasoning: This function defines the precedence for configuration: explicit path passed to API, environment override, then default candidate search. Callers expect deterministic behavior; raising FileNotFoundError for missing specified paths is part of its contract.

key_constructs.name: from_file
key_constructs.purpose: Load YAML config, run subsystem loaders, resolve env variable placeholders, and return an AppConfig instance.
key_constructs.reasoning: Performs multiple side effects: calls _check_config_version, resolve_env_variables, multiple load_*_from_dict functions, always refreshes ACP via load_acp_config_from_dict, and unconditionally loads ExtensionsConfig.from_file into the config dict. Any edits must preserve this sequence because those loaders likely maintain global registries elsewhere.

key_constructs.name: _check_config_version
key_constructs.purpose: Warn if user's config.yaml has a lower config_version than a nearby config.example.yaml (searches up to 5 parent directories).
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to alert users on outdated config schema; it tolerates missing or malformed example file and never raises — only logs a warning if version increases; preserve search depth and non-fatal behavior.

key_constructs.name: resolve_env_variables
key_constructs.purpose: Recursively replace strings starting with '$' with environment variable values, raising ValueError when a referenced env variable is missing.
key_constructs.reasoning: This is an early gate: missing env vars in config will raise and stop startup. Many upstream subsystems expect secrets/API keys to be resolved here so preserving strictness is important.

key_constructs.name: _get_config_mtime
key_constructs.purpose: Return file modification time for a given Path, or None when stat fails.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to determine whether config file changed on disk and requires reload; it intentionally suppresses OSErrors and returns None on failures.

key_constructs.name: _load_and_cache_app_config
key_constructs.purpose: Internal helper to load AppConfig.from_file and update module-level cache and metadata (_app_config, _app_config_path, _app_config_mtime, _app_config_is_custom).
key_constructs.reasoning: Centralized cache update logic; other APIs call this to refresh cached instance. It sets _app_config_is_custom to False because a load from file should clear custom flag.

key_constructs.name: get_app_config
key_constructs.purpose: Public accessor that returns an AppConfig instance, honoring runtime ContextVar override, custom injected config, and automatic reload-on-mtime-change behavior.
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the main entry point used by the rest of the process; callers expect caching semantics and auto-reload on disk changes. It consults _current_app_config context override first, then _app_config with special handling for _app_config_is_custom to prevent reload of custom configs.

key_constructs.name: set_app_config
key_constructs.purpose: Inject a custom AppConfig instance and mark it as custom (prevents auto-reload behavior in get_app_config).
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by tests or runtime code to supply an in-memory config; when set, get_app_config will return it without reloading from disk.

key_constructs.name: push_current_app_config
key_constructs.purpose: Push a context-local (ContextVar) AppConfig override for the current execution context; stores previous value on a ContextVar stack.
key_constructs.reasoning: Allows temporary override for a unit of work or request without affecting global cached config; the stack handling must stay consistent to avoid leaking overrides between contexts.

key_constructs.name: pop_current_app_config
key_constructs.purpose: Pop the latest runtime-scoped AppConfig override, restoring the previous context value from the stack.
key_constructs.reasoning: Must correctly handle empty stack (sets current override to None) and restore previous item when present. Call sites expect that pairing push/pop restore prior state.

key_constructs.name: peek_current_app_config
key_constructs.purpose: Return the active context-local AppConfig override if present, otherwise None.
key_constructs.reasoning: Diagnostic utility that callers can use to check whether a runtime override is active without modifying state.

key_behaviors: resolve config file path with precedence
load and validate YAML into AppConfig
resolve environment-variable placeholders recursively
refresh global subsystem configs via loader functions
cache AppConfig with automatic reload on file mtime change
support context-local (ContextVar) runtime overrides

pitfalls.mistake: Changing the relative path calculation in _default_config_candidates or altering Path(__file__).resolve().parents[4] without verifying package layout.
pitfalls.consequence: Deterministic config discovery may break; resolve_config_path may fail to find the intended defaults or pick the wrong file in different deployments.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect that _default_config_candidates computes backend/repo-root locations relative to module file and update callers that rely on that determinism.

pitfalls.mistake: Removing or reordering calls to subsystem loader functions in from_file (load_title_config_from_dict, load_memory_config_from_dict, load_acp_config_from_dict, ExtensionsConfig.from_file, etc.).
pitfalls.consequence: Subsystem registries and side-effects will not be refreshed, causing stale or inconsistent runtime behavior across reloads.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep the sequence of loader calls intact; assume they perform important global state refreshes rather than returning values used only locally.

pitfalls.mistake: Altering resolve_env_variables to silently ignore missing environment variables or to change the '$' syntax handling.
pitfalls.consequence: Secrets and keys referenced in config.yaml could end up unresolved or set to None/empty, causing runtime credential errors or silent misconfiguration.
pitfalls.prevention: Maintain strict behavior (raise ValueError when an env var referenced with $ is unset) unless callers are updated to handle relaxed semantics.

pitfalls.mistake: Directly modifying the module-level cache variables (_app_config, _app_config_path, _app_config_mtime, _app_config_is_custom) from external code without using provided APIs.
pitfalls.consequence: Cache invariants break; get_app_config reload behavior and custom-config semantics become inconsistent and hard-to-debug.
pitfalls.prevention: Use set_app_config, reset_app_config, reload_app_config, push_current_app_config, and pop_current_app_config to manipulate configuration state.

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming get_app_config always returns the same object across threads/processes without using context overrides.
pitfalls.consequence: Concurrent flows requiring different configs may mistakenly share state; tests relying on thread-local overrides may fail.
pitfalls.prevention: Use push_current_app_config/pop_current_app_config for per-request overrides, and set_app_config only for process-global injection (not for temporary scopes).

key_sections: AppConfig: model schema and fields — central reference for what shape the configuration has
from_file: YAML loading pipeline and subsystem loader calls — shows side-effects and sequence
_default_config_candidates + resolve_config_path: precedence and deterministic discovery behavior
ContextVar APIs (peek_current_app_config, push_current_app_config, pop_current_app_config): how to do runtime-scoped overrides
skip_unless_needed: _check_config_version: only relevant when diagnosing config version warnings
_get_config_mtime: small utility used by reload logic

related_files.relationship: Defines MemoryConfig and loader used by AppConfig.from_file; co-changes when config fields for memory are added or renamed
related_files.reason_to_check: Updates to memory config keys or types require updating both the Pydantic model and calls to load_memory_config_from_dict

related_files.relationship: ExtensionsConfig.from_file is invoked and its model_dump is injected into the AppConfig data before validation
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to extensions loading or format affect how AppConfig composes the final config dict

related_files.relationship: load_acp_config_from_dict is always invoked to refresh ACP agent config entries; AppConfig.from_file delegates to it
related_files.reason_to_check: ACP config schema changes or loader side-effects should be coordinated with AppConfig.from_file

related_files.relationship: Optional checkpointer section is loaded using load_checkpointer_config_from_dict if present in YAML
related_files.reason_to_check: Changing checkpointer config semantics affects how from_file deals with that section

data_constants_to_check: config_version field in config.example.yaml and config.yaml

insights.title: Deterministic config discovery and runtime-scoped AppConfig overrides
insights.problem: Config and path resolution relied on the current working directory (cwd) and global variables, which caused nondeterministic behaviour across different invocation contexts (e.g., services started from different cwd or concurrent threads) and made it hard to temporarily override config during a single logical run.
insights.root_cause: Code used Path(os.getcwd()) and parent directory fallbacks for discovering config.yaml; there was no runtime-scoped override mechanism to isolate temporary configuration for concurrent runs.
insights.solution: Introduce _default_config_candidates() that uses module location to discover backend/repo defaults; add ContextVar-based runtime override APIs (peek_current_app_config, push_current_app_config, pop_current_app_config) and make get_app_config consult the runtime override first.
insights.lesson_learned: Avoid relying on cwd for locating repository configuration in code that may be executed from different working directories; use deterministic paths relative to module file location and provide ContextVar-based runtime overrides for concurrency/isolation.

backend/tests/test_lead_agent_prompt.py.short_description: Unit tests validating prompt generation and background skills cache behavior (mounts, warming, invalidation, concurrency)
backend/tests/test_lead_agent_prompt.py.description:
This test module exercises prompt generation (custom mounts inclusion and formatting) and the enabled-skills cache lifecycle in the lead_agent prompt module. It covers behavior for: building custom mounts sections, apply_prompt_template inclusion of mounts, asynchronous invalidation and refresh of the skills cache, ensuring that clearing the cache does not spawn parallel refresh workers, and that warm_enabled_skills_cache logs a warning on timeout. The tests use monkeypatch to replace get_app_config and the load_skills call and employ threading.Event coordination to simulate concurrent loads and timeouts.

Tests use anyio.run to call the async refresh helper in an async context and explicitly call the module's test helper _reset_skills_system_prompt_cache_state to ensure deterministic starting state. Tests assert both functional output (presence of strings in the assembled prompt) and concurrency invariants (max_active_loads == 1).

key_constructs.name: test_build_custom_mounts_section_returns_empty_when_no_mounts
key_constructs.purpose: Verify _build_custom_mounts_section returns empty string when sandbox.mounts is empty
key_constructs.reasoning: Ensures prompt does not show irrelevant mount guidance when none configured; relies on monkeypatching get_app_config.

key_constructs.name: test_build_custom_mounts_section_lists_configured_mounts
key_constructs.purpose: Verify mounted directories are listed with read-only/read-write annotations
key_constructs.reasoning: Validates formatting that agents rely on to find files in container paths.

key_constructs.name: test_apply_prompt_template_includes_custom_mounts
key_constructs.purpose: Ensure apply_prompt_template integrates the custom mounts section into the final prompt
key_constructs.reasoning: Asserts that make_lead_agent will pass environment-specific mount info into the system prompt.

key_constructs.name: test_refresh_skills_system_prompt_cache_async_reloads_immediately
key_constructs.purpose: Verify async invalidation and refresh updates cached skills quickly (using anyio.run to drive the async helper)
key_constructs.reasoning: Confirms that refresh_skills_system_prompt_cache_async waits for the background reload to complete and that _get_enabled_skills returns the new list after the call.

key_constructs.name: test_clear_cache_does_not_spawn_parallel_refresh_workers
key_constructs.purpose: Check that multiple invalidations do not create overlapping background loaders
key_constructs.reasoning: Simulates a slow load (blocking first call until release) and ensures the maximum concurrent active loads observed is 1.

key_constructs.name: test_warm_enabled_skills_cache_logs_on_timeout
key_constructs.purpose: Assert warm_enabled_skills_cache logs a warning when the cache Event is not set within timeout
key_constructs.reasoning: Validates timeout/diagnostic behavior of warm_enabled_skills_cache.

key_behaviors: monkeypatches get_app_config and load_skills to simulate runtime environment
validates cache warm/invalidate and concurrency properties of background loader
checks prompt text contains mount and formatting information

pitfalls.mistake: Relying on the module-level cache state being clean across tests
pitfalls.consequence: Flaky tests due to background thread or event state persisting across tests
pitfalls.prevention: Tests call _reset_skills_system_prompt_cache_state in setup/teardown or ensure they coordinate with Events to reset state deterministically.

pitfalls.mistake: Using very short timeouts without ensuring the worker actually started
pitfalls.consequence: Intermittent test failures on slow CI machines
pitfalls.prevention: Tests use reasonable wait timeouts (e.g., 5s) and coordinate with Events; altering those timeouts impacts reliability.

key_sections: tests that exercise cache concurrency (clear_cache_does_not_spawn_parallel_refresh_workers)
prompt formatting checks (build_custom_mounts_section* and apply_prompt_template)

related_files.reason_to_check: If prompt module changes event/version semantics or function names, these tests must be updated.

backend/tests/test_lead_agent_skills.py.short_description: Unit tests for skill section rendering and skill selection propagation into make_lead_agent
backend/tests/test_lead_agent_skills.py.description: This test module validates get_skills_prompt_section behavior (filtering by available_skills, returning empty when none match or when available_skills set is empty, returning all when available_skills is None), inclusion of the optional skill-evolution guidance when enabled in config, cache sensitivity to toggling the skill_evolution config, and that make_lead_agent passes the correct representation of an agent's configured skills to prompt generation. It uses a helper _make_skill to fabricate Skill instances and monkeypatches to replace internal dependencies. The make_lead_agent-related test also exercises how None and empty lists are distinguished and forwarded as None vs empty set to apply_prompt_template.

key_constructs.name: _make_skill
key_constructs.purpose: Test-local helper to create Skill instances for assertions
key_constructs.reasoning: Used repeatedly to synthesize Skill objects with predictable properties without touching disk.

key_constructs.reasoning: Confirms per-agent skill filtering works and that non-matching skills are omitted.

frontend/src/app/workspace/agents/[agent.short_description: Client React page that renders an agent-scoped chat UI for a specific thread
frontend/src/app/workspace/agents/[agent.description:
This is a Next.js client component that implements the agent-specific chat page. It exists so users can interact with a named agent in a conversational thread: send messages, view streaming responses, monitor token usage, access artifacts/exports, and manage per-thread settings. It wires together several higher-level UI primitives (ChatBox, MessageList, InputBox, ThreadTitle, TodoList, TokenUsageIndicator, AgentWelcome) and thread runtime hooks (useThreadChat, useThreadSettings, useThreadStream) to provide a cohesive chat experience scoped to a specific agent_name route parameter.

Design choices reflected in the file: (1) useThreadSettings(threadId) is used to support thread-scoped model/context overrides (commit history shows this was migrated from a global settings hook); (2) useThreadStream is configured with an onStart callback that uses the native history.replaceState to update the URL with the created thread id — a deliberate choice to avoid router-driven remounts that would lose component state; (3) the thread stream onFinish handler surfaces a browser notification when the page is not focused; (4) agent_name is explicitly injected into the stream context and also passed to sendMessage so the back-end run knows which agent is being addressed. The page also respects NEXT_PUBLIC_STATIC_WEBSITE_ONLY env flag to disable message submission in demo/static mode. Overall it composes existing primitives and hooks rather than implementing low-level chat logic itself.

key_constructs.name: AgentChatPage
key_constructs.purpose: Default exported React component that renders the full agent-specific chat page and orchestrates thread lifecycle.
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the main entry point for the route and composes various UI and runtime hooks; editing work should focus here when adjusting behavior of the agent-scoped chat page (URL handling, settings injection, rendering layout).

key_constructs.name: handleSubmit
key_constructs.purpose: Callback invoked by InputBox to send a message via useThreadStream's sendMessage, passing threadId and agent_name.
key_constructs.reasoning: Message send uses this wrapper to ensure agent_name is forwarded. Any change to message dispatching, payload shape, or agent routing must respect this callsite.

key_constructs.name: handleStop
key_constructs.purpose: Callback to stop the current streaming thread by calling thread.stop().
key_constructs.reasoning: Stop logic relies on the thread object exposed by useThreadStream; ensure this continues to reference the same runtime thread instance to avoid race conditions.

key_behaviors: initializes or resumes a Thread when chat starts
sends messages to the Thread stream with agent context
stops the Thread streaming execution
updates browser history on thread creation without router remount
shows notifications when conversation finishes while page is not focused
displays per-thread settings and token usage

pitfalls.mistake: Using Next.js router navigation to change the URL when a thread is created (e.g., router.push in onStart).
pitfalls.consequence: Causes the chat page to re-mount and lose in-memory state for the running thread (interferes with stream lifecycle).
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the existing history.replaceState usage in onStart; do not replace it with next/navigation router for the created-thread URL update.

pitfalls.mistake: Removing the agent_name injection from the useThreadStream context or from sendMessage options.
pitfalls.consequence: Backend agent dispatch may not receive the agent identifier and could select the wrong agent or fall back to generic behavior.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep { ...settings.context, agent_name } and passing agent_name in sendMessage parameters.

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming thread or thread.messages is always defined synchronously.
pitfalls.consequence: Accessing undefined fields (e.g., thread.messages) can cause runtime exceptions if hooks return uninitialized values.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect that thread may be a runtime object that populates fields asynchronously; avoid assumptions about immediate shape when refactoring render or hooks.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing the demo-mode gating (NEXT_PUBLIC_STATIC_WEBSITE_ONLY) without updating related UI paths or tests.
pitfalls.consequence: Demo mode could enable UI elements that rely on backend APIs not present in static deployments, causing runtime errors.
pitfalls.prevention: When changing this flag's usage, verify all controlled components (InputBox disabled prop and demo message text) are consistent and test static build behavior.

key_sections: useThreadStream invocation and its callbacks (onStart/onFinish): critical for thread lifecycle and url handling
InputBox props and how settings.context and isNewThread flow into onSubmit and extraHeader
Header section where TokenUsageIndicator and Agent badge are rendered (integration points)
skip_unless_needed: styling/className strings for layout positioning
icon/button markup that only affects presentation

related_files.reason_to_check: If changing thread lifecycle, URL handling, or settings hooks, ensure parity and consistent behavior across generic and agent-specific chat pages.

related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to how threadId or isNewThread are persisted across navigation would require updating these hooks/providers.

related_files.relationship: Defines useThreadStream (the streaming message hook) invoked here.
related_files.reason_to_check: Any change to useThreadStream's API (parameters, return values, onStart/onFinish semantics) will require updates in this page.

related_files.reason_to_check: Editing settings shape or storage keys affects context passed to agent runs.

insights.title: Use thread-scoped settings in agent chat page
insights.problem: Agent chat pages used the global local settings hook which prevented per-thread model overrides.
insights.root_cause: Pages were wired to useLocalSettings instead of a thread-aware hook.
insights.solution: Switched to useThreadSettings(threadId) to load per-thread settings.
insights.lesson_learned: UI pages must use thread-scoped settings hooks when behavior needs to vary by thread.
