insights.root_cause: Only core chat pages were updated initially
insights.solution: Add <TokenUsageIndicator messages={thread.messages} /> to agent chat header
insights.lesson_learned: Keep cross-route UI features consistent across equivalent pages (agent-specific vs generic chats).

backend/tests/test_channels.py.short_description: Unit tests for the channels subsystem: MessageBus, ChannelStore, Channel base, response extraction, and ChannelManager.
backend/tests/test_channels.py.description:
This file contains comprehensive unit tests for the IM channel subsystem of the application. It validates low-level primitives (MessageBus FIFO semantics, outbound subscription behavior and error isolation), persistent channel-thread mapping (ChannelStore), and the Channel base class wiring that routes outbound messages to concrete channel implementations. A large section of tests focuses on response text extraction logic (_extract_response_text) used by the ChannelManager to derive user-visible text from LangGraph run results and tool messages. The latter part of the file exercises ChannelManager behavior in an integration-like fashion by mocking the langgraph client and channel service: it tests thread creation, inbound handling of file attachments, and streaming behaviors with mocked async iterators/stream parts.

These tests are designed to be run in the backend test suite and to exercise the contracts that production code must honor: ordering and resilience of the MessageBus, durable JSON-backed ChannelStore semantics (including behavior when the file is corrupt), and the protocol by which ChannelManager interacts with external services (langgraph client and channel service). The tests use AsyncMock and MagicMock heavily to isolate network and external dependencies and rely on ephemeral filesystem state using pytest tmp_path / tempfile for persistence tests. The tests also include helper utilities (_run, _wait_for) to run async coroutines synchronously and to poll for conditions in async flows.

For anyone editing related source code, this test file should be used as an executable specification: it codifies expected behavior such as FIFO semantics, what default fields models must set, how ChannelStore timestamps (created_at/updated_at) should behave on updates, how corrupt JSON must be handled gracefully, and edge cases in extracting response text (e.g., prioritizing tool messages like ask_clarification when AI content is empty, not leaking previous-turn AI text). The ChannelManager tests show expected interactions with the langgraph client API: threads.create, runs.wait, and the structure of the 'input' messages passed to runs.wait. Mocking patterns in these tests (monkeypatching get_channel_service, swapping manager._client) are important to preserve when modifying ChannelManager or service discovery behaviors.

key_constructs.name: _run
key_constructs.purpose: Runs an async coroutine synchronously in a new event loop for use in sync test methods.
key_constructs.reasoning: Tests invoke async test code from standard (non-async) pytest tests. Altering this or replacing usage with pytest-asyncio fixtures will change execution semantics; tests rely on a fresh event loop per call.

key_constructs.name: _wait_for
key_constructs.purpose: Asynchronously polls a condition until true or times out.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to wait for asynchronous side effects (e.g., outbound messages captured by subscription callbacks). Tests depend on the default timeout/interval behavior; changing these values will affect test timing sensitivity.

key_constructs.name: TestMessageBus
key_constructs.purpose: Test suite validating MessageBus behavior: inbound queue, outbound callbacks, unsubscribe, error isolation, and default message field values.
key_constructs.reasoning: This class documents required MessageBus guarantees (FIFO inbound, non-crashing outbound notification pipeline, default/missing fields). Edits to MessageBus must preserve these behaviors.

key_constructs.name: TestChannelStore
key_constructs.purpose: Test suite for persistent channel-to-thread mapping (ChannelStore): set/get/remove/list/persistence/created_at preservation and corrupt file handling.
key_constructs.reasoning: These tests define the API and persistence semantics for ChannelStore. The created_at/updated_at behavior and corrupt-file fallback are explicit expectations.

key_constructs.name: DummyChannel
key_constructs.purpose: Concrete test Channel subclass used to exercise Channel base class wiring (subscribe/unsubscribe routing and send).
key_constructs.reasoning: Provides a minimal channel implementation for routing tests; tests rely on its subscribe behavior and that Channel base class invokes channel.send for matching channel_name only.

key_constructs.name: TestExtractResponseText
key_constructs.purpose: Test suite for the _extract_response_text helper (in channels.manager) that produces user-visible text from complex run/message structures.
key_constructs.reasoning: Enumerates many edge cases (lists of content blocks, last-AI-message selection, skipping empty AI content, tool-call messages such as ask_clarification, not leaking previous-turn text). Maintainers must preserve these extraction rules when changing manager logic.

key_constructs.name: _make_mock_langgraph_client
key_constructs.purpose: Creates a MagicMock-based fake async langgraph client providing threads.create, threads.get and runs.wait AsyncMocks.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by ChannelManager tests to validate interactions without a real LangGraph server. Tests assume certain structure in the client responses (e.g., runs.wait returns a messages dict/list); changes to ChannelManager's client usage may require adapting this mock.

key_constructs.name: _make_stream_part
key_constructs.purpose: Creates a simple namespace with 'event' and 'data' attributes to simulate stream parts during streaming tests.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to build items yielded by mocked async iterators that imitate SSE/streamed run outputs. The shape 'event'/'data' is expected by ChannelManager streaming parsing logic.

key_constructs.name: _make_async_iterator
key_constructs.purpose: Wraps an iterable into an async generator yielding given items; used to simulate async streams in tests.
key_constructs.reasoning: Test code uses asynchronous streaming semantics; this helper produces an async iterator that ChannelManager will consume.

key_constructs.name: TestChannelManager
key_constructs.purpose: Contains tests that exercise high-level ChannelManager behavior, including handling inbound messages with files, thread creation, streaming run handling, and integration points with channel service and langgraph client.
key_constructs.reasoning: These tests function like integration tests with mocked external systems. They assert on calls to mock_client and mock_channel, verifying that ChannelManager creates threads, passes expected input to runs.wait, and delegates file handling to channel.receive_file.

key_behaviors: verifies inbound MessageBus FIFO ordering
ensures outbound subscribers receive messages and are resilient to subscriber errors
tests ChannelStore create/get/remove/list/persistence and created_at/updated_at semantics
verifies Channel base routes outbound messages to matching channel_name only
ensures _extract_response_text handles AI/tool message edge cases
validates ChannelManager interactions with a langgraph client and channel service via mocks

pitfalls.mistake: Modify MessageBus semantics (e.g., change queue ordering or callback delivery to be non-deterministic) without updating tests.
pitfalls.consequence: MessageBus tests will fail asserting FIFO order and callback behavior; higher-level components may rely on deterministic delivery ordering.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect FIFO semantics and non-crashing callback delivery when changing MessageBus implementation; ensure tests reflect intended semantics.

pitfalls.mistake: Change ChannelStore timestamp handling (created_at/updated_at) or persistence format.
pitfalls.consequence: Tests verifying created_at preservation and persistence across instances will fail; existing persisted files may become incompatible.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep JSON schema stable or provide backward compatibility when changing persisted fields.

pitfalls.mistake: Alter _extract_response_text semantics (e.g., returning previous AI messages when current turn has tool calls).
pitfalls.consequence: Multiple unit tests in TestExtractResponseText will fail and user-facing behavior may regress (showing stale responses).
pitfalls.prevention: Maintain the selection and precedence rules exercised by the tests: last AI message with non-empty content, prefer tool message content for clarification tools when AI is empty, and never leak previous-turn AI content into the current-turn response.

pitfalls.mistake: Modify ChannelManager to call different methods on the langgraph client (or change payload structure) without updating mocks.
pitfalls.consequence: ChannelManager tests that assert on mock_client.threads.create or mock_client.runs.wait input will break.
pitfalls.prevention: When changing client interactions, update _make_mock_langgraph_client and related assertions in tests.

pitfalls.mistake: Replace synchronous test helpers (_run/_wait_for) with event-loop fixtures without adjusting test code that expects a freshly-created loop per call.
pitfalls.consequence: Test timing or resource cleanup issues may appear (e.g., pending tasks on loop close), causing flakiness.
pitfalls.prevention: If migrating to pytest-asyncio, ensure tests are rewritten consistently to use the same async fixtures and remove manual loop management.

key_sections: TestMessageBus: validates low-level message bus contract (FIFO, outbound subscribe/unsubscribe, error isolation)
TestChannelStore: persistence and timestamp behavior for channel-thread mapping
DummyChannel + TestChannelBase: how Channel base routes outbound messages to a Channel subclass
TestExtractResponseText: many edge cases for converting run/messages to user text — read these tests to understand expected precedence rules
TestChannelManager: integration-style tests using _make_mock_langgraph_client and monkeypatching get_channel_service
skip_unless_needed: the multiple repetitive small tests for default fields (they document defaults but are short)
helpers used only by specialized streaming tests if you are not working on streaming

tests.example_command: cd backend && uv run pytest backend/tests/test_channels.py -q

relevant_snippets.description: Async helpers and TestMessageBus tests validating queueing and outbound subscription behavior.
ChannelStore tests: set/get/remove/list persistence and corrupt file handling.
DummyChannel and Channel base routing tests, and _extract_response_text test cases for message extraction rules.
ChannelManager helper mocks (_make_mock_langgraph_client, _make_stream_part, _make_async_iterator) and initial ChannelManager tests involving mocked langgraph client and channel service.

related_files.relationship: Contains the MessageBus, InboundMessage, OutboundMessage and related types that these tests exercise directly.
related_files.reason_to_check: Any change to message types, queue semantics, or subscription API should be validated against the tests here.

related_files.relationship: Implements ChannelStore persistence and timestamp semantics validated by TestChannelStore tests.
related_files.reason_to_check: Modification to storage format, timestamp fields, or error handling for corrupt files will affect tests in this file.

related_files.relationship: Defines Channel base class behavior being tested (subscribe/unsubscribe, send contract).
related_files.reason_to_check: Channel base changes could alter routing, start/stop lifecycle, or expected callbacks used by DummyChannel tests.

related_files.relationship: Houses _extract_response_text and ChannelManager; many tests in this file verify its logic and interaction with langgraph client.
related_files.reason_to_check: Any logic changes here (message extraction, streaming parsing, client API usage) must be reflected in the tests and mocks used in this test file.

related_files.relationship: Provides get_channel_service used by ChannelManager tests via monkeypatch to return mocked channel implementations.
related_files.reason_to_check: If service discovery changes, these tests that monkeypatch get_channel_service will need updating.

tests_to_run: cd backend && uv run pytest backend/tests/test_channels.py -q
cd backend && make test (runs full backend test suite)

frontend/src/components/workspace/messag.short_description: Client-side React component that renders Markdown content with custom link/citation handling.
frontend/src/components/workspace/messag.description:
This file defines a client-only React component (marked by "use client") that renders markdown content using a shared MessageResponse component and a controlled set of remark/rehype plugins. Its main purpose is to centralize how agent/chat messages containing markdown are displayed in the workspace UI and to provide special handling for anchor elements: converting citation:... anchors into internal CitationLink components and ensuring external links open in a new tab with appropriate rel attributes. The component also merges any components passed via props so callers can override or extend rendering behavior.

The design keeps the file small and focused: it exposes a typed props shape (MarkdownContentProps) so callers provide content, plugin arrays, and optional overrides for components and className. The rendering logic uses useMemo to memoize the components mapping (notably the <a> renderer) to avoid rebuilding functions on every render unless componentsFromProps changes. The file relies on several external pieces: MessageResponse (the wrapper that actually converts markdown -> HTML with remark/rehype and React components), streamdownPlugins for default remarkPlugins, CitationLink for citation anchors, and cn utility to merge class names consistently.

Non-obvious but important behaviors: when the anchor's children is exactly a string matching /^citation:(.+)$/, it returns a CitationLink and strips the citation: prefix from display text. For normal anchors, it inspects the href and treats http/https links as external, defaulting target="_blank" and rel="noopener noreferrer" unless overridden. If content is falsy (empty string, null, undefined) the component returns null. The component intentionally limits its logic to link handling and delegates markdown parsing and rendering to MessageResponse and plugin arrays provided.

key_constructs.name: isExternalUrl
key_constructs.purpose: Detects whether an href string references an external http(s) URL.
key_constructs.reasoning: This small helper centralizes the external-link detection logic used to decide default target/rel behavior for anchor elements. An editor must preserve its regex semantics (it accepts only http/https schemes) because changing it alters when links open in new tabs and whether rel attributes are applied.

key_constructs.name: MarkdownContentProps
key_constructs.purpose: TypeScript type describing the props accepted by the MarkdownContent component.
key_constructs.reasoning: This exported type is part of the component's public typing surface. Edits that change fields, optionality, or types will affect callers and TypeScript checks across the UI; maintainers must update call sites accordingly.

key_constructs.name: MarkdownContent
key_constructs.purpose: React component that renders markdown via MessageResponse and customizes anchor rendering (citation links, external links).
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the primary export used by workspace message UI. It composes plugin arrays, a custom components map (with specialized <a> rendering), and returns a MessageResponse wrapper. Changes here impact message rendering, link behavior, and how caller-provided components are merged — so edits must respect useMemo dependency and 'use client' context.

key_behaviors: renders markdown content using MessageResponse
converts anchors with text 'citation:...' into CitationLink
treats http(s) hrefs as external and sets default target/rel
returns null when content is falsy

pitfalls.mistake: Removing the "use client" directive or converting this to a server component.
pitfalls.consequence: Hooks like useMemo and DOM-related link behaviors may break; Next.js may attempt SSR and produce hydration mismatches or errors.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep the "use client" directive at the top when editing; ensure any newly added APIs are compatible with client-side rendering.

pitfalls.mistake: Altering the isExternalUrl regex to accept other schemes or to match relative URLs.
pitfalls.consequence: Default target/rel behavior may change; links that previously opened in the same tab may start opening in new tabs (or vice versa), causing a UX or security regression.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the intent: only http(s) should be considered external unless other code paths explicitly require different handling.

pitfalls.mistake: Removing or changing useMemo dependency array (currently [componentsFromProps]) or inlining the components mapping unconditionally.
pitfalls.consequence: Could cause re-creation of functions on every render, breaking referential equality assumptions downstream and potentially causing unnecessary re-renders in MessageResponse/markdown renderers.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep the useMemo usage and ensure componentsFromProps is included in dependencies if memoization is retained.

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming props.children for anchor will always be a string and modifying logic to treat non-string nodes the same way.
pitfalls.consequence: If props.children is a React element or array, string-based citation detection would fail or throw; changing it indiscriminately could introduce runtime errors.
pitfalls.prevention: Maintain the current guard (typeof props.children === 'string') when performing the citation: match; if broader handling is added, account for nodes that are not plain strings.

key_sections: MarkdownContent: main component, composes plugins and components and returns MessageResponse
isExternalUrl: small helper used inside the <a> renderer to decide target/rel
MarkdownContentProps: the typed props shape to check caller expectations
skip_unless_needed: the detailed composition of className via cn (utility), small import boilerplate

related_files.relationship: MessageResponse is the wrapper that does markdown parsing/rendering; this file delegates primary markdown rendering and plugin application to it.
related_files.reason_to_check: If MessageResponse API (props or expected children shape) or plugin contract changes, update MarkdownContent to pass correct props.

related_files.relationship: Provides streamdownPlugins used as default remarkPlugins in this component.
related_files.reason_to_check: If default remarkPlugins change shape/name, the default behavior here could need updates; callers relying on implicit defaults may be affected.

related_files.relationship: CitationLink is used to render anchors whose children start with 'citation:'.
related_files.reason_to_check: If CitationLink's props or display semantics change, MarkdownContent's conversion and displayed text expectations must be revisited.

related_files.relationship: Provides cn utility used to compose className for anchor elements.
related_files.reason_to_check: If cn behavior changes (e.g., API or class merging rules), anchor styling here may be affected.

scripts/check.py.short_description: Cross-platform CLI that verifies presence and versions of local dev dependencies.
scripts/check.py.description:
This script is a small command-line dependency checker intended for local developer environments. It verifies that key developer tools required by the project are installed and meet minimal version requirements (Node.js >= 22, pnpm, uv, nginx). It prints human-facing guidance and returns a shell exit code (0 on success, 1 on failure) so it can be used in local workflows or Makefile targets (e.g., make check). The script contains platform-aware behavior (Windows pnpm.exe handling, preferring corepack if available, and UTF-8 reconfiguration on Windows consoles) and defensive subprocess handling so failure to probe a tool doesn't crash the script.

Why it exists: this is a convenience / gate script used by developers to ensure their machine or CI environment has the required toolchain before attempting builds, running the dev stack, or running tests. It centralizes the list of developer prerequisites and the messaging describing how to install missing components. It is invoked directly (python3 scripts/check.py) and indirectly via Makefile targets such as make check.

How it fits into the system: the script is part of developer tooling (not the application runtime). Other project artifacts (Makefile, CI configs, README) reference the same tools; this script codifies the checks in an executable form. It must remain robust across OSes (Linux, macOS, Windows/WSL) and must not introduce security risks when probing external commands.

Design decisions and behaviors to respect: subprocess.run is used with list args and shell=False to avoid shell injection and to make behavior predictable (commit history shows this change for security). The script intentionally falls back to stderr when stdout is empty (some commands emit useful info to stderr, e.g., nginx -v). It also handles the presence of corepack (common on modern Node installs) and probing for pnpm via corepack if available. The configure_stdio() function attempts to reconfigure stdout/stderr encoding to UTF-8 so Unicode markers display correctly on Windows consoles, but it is written defensively for streams that do not support reconfigure().

key_constructs.name: configure_stdio
key_constructs.purpose: Set stdout/stderr encoding to UTF-8 where supported so Unicode output renders on Windows.
key_constructs.reasoning: When editing/maintaining the script, preserve the defensive approach: call reconfigure only if available and catch OSError/ValueError. Removing or altering this can cause Unicode render or crash issues on some consoles.

key_constructs.name: run_command
key_constructs.purpose: Execute an external command and return trimmed stdout or stderr; return None on any execution error.
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the central helper for probing installed tools. It uses subprocess.run(..., capture_output=True, text=True, check=True, shell=False) and returns None on OSError or CalledProcessError. Any edits must respect the security posture (list args and shell=False) and the fallback to stderr for commands that write to stderr.

key_constructs.name: find_pnpm_command
key_constructs.purpose: Return a pnpm-compatible command list present on the system (['pnpm'], ['pnpm.cmd'], or ['corepack','pnpm']).
key_constructs.reasoning: This abstracts platform differences and corepack usage. It uses shutil.which on the first element of each candidate and appends the corepack candidate only when corepack is present. Editing must preserve the ordering and the choice logic so pnpm detection remains accurate on Windows and on systems using corepack.

key_constructs.name: parse_node_major
key_constructs.purpose: Parse Node.js version text (e.g., 'v22.4.0' or '22.4.0') and return the major version as int, or None if unparsable.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to validate the Node.js major version meets the minimum (22). It strips a leading 'v' and returns None for non-numeric majors. Maintain its simple parsing semantics so the version check remains predictable.

key_constructs.name: main
key_constructs.purpose: Entry point: run dependency checks (Node, pnpm, uv, nginx), print messages and return an exit code.
key_constructs.reasoning: All command-line behavior is concentrated here. Changes should preserve the order and messaging expectations because downstream automation (Makefile, docs, CI hints) may rely on output text and non-zero exit codes for failure.

key_behaviors: detects if Node.js is installed and enforces major version >= 22
detects pnpm and supports corepack-backed pnpm
verifies uv presence and reports version
checks for nginx and reports presence/version or install hints
prints human-readable remediation instructions and exits with code

pitfalls.mistake: Changing run_command to use shell=True or string commands.
pitfalls.consequence: Introduces shell injection risks and platform-dependent behavior; breaks security assumption enforced in commit history.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep subprocess.run call with list args and shell=False, and preserve error handling that returns None on CalledProcessError/OSError.

pitfalls.mistake: Removing fallback to stderr when stdout is empty.
pitfalls.consequence: Some commands (e.g., nginx -v) emit version information to stderr; losing this fallback makes the script incorrectly report unknown versions.
pitfalls.prevention: Maintain the logic result.stdout.strip() or result.stderr.strip() when returning output.

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming stream.reconfigure always exists and calling it without try/except.
pitfalls.consequence: May raise AttributeError or OSError on some Python environments/streams and break the check before performing checks.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the current defensive checks: hasattr(stream, 'reconfigure') and catching OSError/ValueError.

pitfalls.mistake: Altering find_pnpm_command ordering or removing corepack detection.
pitfalls.consequence: Causes incorrect pnpm detection on machines that use corepack or Windows (pnpm.cmd), yielding false negatives.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep the candidate order and shutil.which first-element check; only append corepack candidate when corepack is available.

key_sections: run_command: central subprocess probe helper and fallback semantics
find_pnpm_command: platform/corepack-aware pnpm detection
parse_node_major: version parsing behavior for Node >= 22 checks
skip_unless_needed: shebang and module docstring (boilerplate)
print message text blocks if you're changing only detection logic

related_files.relationship: Make targets (make check) invoke this script or present similar dependency checks; the Makefile and this script should present consistent messaging.
related_files.reason_to_check: If you change exit codes, messages, or required tools, update the Makefile targets and documentation that reference them.

related_files.relationship: Documentation that lists required developer tools and setup instructions; must stay consistent with checks and suggested install commands.
related_files.reason_to_check: If the script's required versions or installation hints change, update README instructions accordingly.

tests_to_run: Run the script locally: python3 scripts/check.py
Run the Makefile target that uses it: make check

insights.title: Use subprocess.run with shell=False in run_command
insights.problem: Using subprocess.run without explicit shell control or using os.system could allow shell injection or silent failures
insights.root_cause: run_command previously constructed commands without explicit shell=False behavior in some flows
insights.solution: Call subprocess.run(..., shell=False) and check for CalledProcessError; return None on failure
insights.lesson_learned: Prefer subprocess.run with list args and shell=False for security and predictable behavior when executing external commands.

backend/app/channels/feishu.py.short_description: WebSocket-based Feishu/Lark IM channel implementation that handles inbound/outbound messages, attachments, and running-status cards.
backend/app/channels/feishu.py.description:
This module implements a Feishu (Lark) instant-messaging channel that connects to Feishu's WebSocket API via the lark-oapi SDK. It exists to integrate DeerFlow with Feishu without requiring a public IP (using long-lived WebSocket connections). The class manages lifecycle (start/stop), translates inbound Feishu events into the project's InboundMessage model, publishes and receives outbound messages through the MessageBus, handles file upload/download and mapping them into the sandbox virtual paths, and maintains 'running' interactive cards and emoji reactions to reflect work-in-progress and completion states.

The design addresses several operational constraints: lark-oapi's module-level event loop capture (worked around by spinning up a dedicated thread + new loop and patching the SDK's module-level reference), optional presence of the lark-oapi dependency (gracefully logs and disables the channel), and sandbox synchronization for downloaded attachments (writes to thread-local uploads directory and calls sandbox_provider.update_file for non-local sandboxes). It also includes retry logic for send operations, size checks for attachments, filename sanitization when persisting resources, and careful background-task tracking to surface errors from fire-and-forget tasks. The module integrates with other parts of the system via MessageBus (subscribe_outbound), path management (get_paths), and sandbox provider (get_sandbox_provider).

key_constructs.name: _is_feishu_command
key_constructs.purpose: Determine whether a text starts with a known slash-command recognized by channel command registry.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to quickly detect commands that should be routed to the channel's command handling logic; respects the shared KNOWN_CHANNEL_COMMANDS registry imported from app.channels.commands.

key_constructs.name: FeishuChannel
key_constructs.purpose: Primary channel class implementing the Channel interface for Feishu/Lark via lark-oapi WebSocket client.
key_constructs.reasoning: Encapsulates lifecycle, event handling, message send/receive, file upload/download, background task tracking, and integration with the MessageBus and sandbox; the editing agent must preserve constructor fields and lifecycle behavior when modifying the class.

key_constructs.name: FeishuChannel.start
key_constructs.purpose: Initialize the channel: import lark-oapi, configure API client, set up event loop, subscribe to outbound messages, and spawn the dedicated thread to run the SDK WebSocket client.
key_constructs.reasoning: Start is critical: it handles the optional dependency import, constructs SDK objects lazily, and ensures the right event loop semantics by creating a separate thread for the SDK; any changes touching start must preserve these initialization responsibilities and error handling/logging.

key_constructs.name: FeishuChannel._run_ws
key_constructs.purpose: Thread target that creates a fresh asyncio loop, patches the lark_oapi.ws.client module-level loop, constructs the SDK ws.Client and starts it.
key_constructs.reasoning: Workaround for SDK's loop capture when uvloop is used in the main thread; editing this could break WebSocket startup semantics and lead to RuntimeError from conflicting running loops.

key_constructs.name: FeishuChannel.stop
key_constructs.purpose: Tear down the channel: stop running flag, unsubscribe outbound callbacks, cancel background tasks, wait for the SDK thread to join, and clear running-card state.
key_constructs.reasoning: Ensures clean shutdown and cancels background operations to avoid dangling tasks or resource leaks.

key_constructs.name: FeishuChannel.send
key_constructs.purpose: Send an OutboundMessage to Feishu with retry logic and exponential-backoff delays; delegates to internal send helpers.
key_constructs.reasoning: Includes attempt loop and deterministic error behavior (see commit insight about raising RuntimeError when last_exc is None). Changing retry semantics requires updating expectations in tests and consistent behavior across other channel implementations.

key_constructs.name: FeishuChannel.send_file
key_constructs.purpose: Upload an attachment (image or generic file) to Feishu and send it as a message (reply or new message).
key_constructs.reasoning: Performs size checks and maps message types (image/file) to appropriate upload endpoints; relies on _upload_image/_upload_file helpers and the SDK client; must maintain file-size caps and thread-safety when invoked concurrently.

key_constructs.name: FeishuChannel.receive_file
key_constructs.purpose: Download files referenced in inbound messages into the thread uploads directory and return modified InboundMessage with virtual paths.
key_constructs.reasoning: Coordinates on-disk persistence via get_paths.ensure_thread_dirs and synchronizes content into non-local sandboxes through sandbox_provider.acquire/get/update_file; editing must respect virtual path format (VIRTUAL_PATH_PREFIX) and sandbox synchronization.

key_constructs.name: FeishuChannel._receive_single_file
key_constructs.purpose: Fetch a single file resource from Feishu via message_resource.get, persist to thread uploads, sanitize filename, and sync into non-local sandbox.
key_constructs.reasoning: Contains multiple failure points with detailed logging and fallbacks returning human-readable failure strings; it uses a thread lock to avoid filename conflicts and must maintain sanitation rules to avoid path traversal.

key_constructs.name: FeishuChannel._upload_image
key_constructs.purpose: Upload binary image content to Feishu and return image_key.
key_constructs.reasoning: Wraps synchronous SDK call with asyncio.to_thread and validates SDK response.success(); maintain error semantics for callers.

key_constructs.name: FeishuChannel._upload_file
key_constructs.purpose: Upload generic files to Feishu with a file_type based on extension and return file_key.
key_constructs.reasoning: Maps common extensions to Feishu's expected file_type values and uses to_thread for blocking IO; size limits are enforced in send_file before this is called.

key_constructs.name: FeishuChannel._build_card_content
key_constructs.purpose: Construct JSON payload for Feishu interactive card containing markdown content.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by reply/create/patch card methods to ensure consistent card structure and rendering of markdown content within Feishu cards.

key_constructs.name: FeishuChannel._add_reaction
key_constructs.purpose: Add an emoji reaction to a Feishu message via the SDK.
key_constructs.reasoning: Wraps SDK call in to_thread and logs exceptions; used to reflect states (OK/DONE) on source messages.

key_constructs.name: FeishuChannel._reply_card
key_constructs.purpose: Reply to an existing message in-thread with an interactive card and return the created card message ID.
key_constructs.reasoning: Creates running cards and other threaded replies; callers rely on returned message_id to perform subsequent updates or to cache running-card IDs.

key_behaviors: start a dedicated WS client thread when enabled
translate Feishu inbound events into InboundMessage objects
publish outbound replies into Feishu threads or chats
download and persist attachments into thread uploads and sandbox
upload attachments to Feishu and send as messages
create/update interactive running cards and add emoji reactions
track and surface errors from background tasks

pitfalls.mistake: Assume the lark-oapi SDK will work in the main process event loop or that importing it is always safe.
pitfalls.consequence: If not using the dedicated thread and patched loop, Client.start() may call loop.run_until_complete on an already-running uvloop and raise RuntimeError, preventing the WS client from starting.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the _run_ws thread creation and module-level loop patching behavior; do not instantiate the SDK Client in the main thread's running loop.

pitfalls.mistake: Write downloaded files without sanitizing names or without acquiring thread_lock.
pitfalls.consequence: Risk of filename conflicts, path traversal, or I/O race conditions leading to corrupted uploads directory or security issues.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep filename sanitization logic and the _thread_lock around writes; when modifying, ensure replacement of path chars remains intact and the lock is used for disk writes.

pitfalls.mistake: Assume sandbox_provider.get will always return a sandbox or that sandbox is local.
pitfalls.consequence: If sandbox lookup fails or sandbox id is unexpected, syncing into a non-local sandbox may be skipped silently and the virtual path will not be available inside the sandbox runtime.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect the acquire/get/update_file pattern used: sandbox_provider.acquire(thread_id) and only call update_file for non-local sandbox ids if get() returns a sandbox object.

pitfalls.mistake: Remove or alter retry/last_exc handling in send() without preserving deterministic error raising.
pitfalls.consequence: Tests and callers may observe inconsistent exceptions (e.g., last_exc None causing unexpected behavior); previous commit addressed last_exc None case to raise RuntimeError.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep the final block that raises a clear error when last_exc is None and re-raise last_exc otherwise.

pitfalls.mistake: Cancel background tasks but forget to clear tracking structures.
pitfalls.consequence: Memory leaks or attempts to reference canceled tasks; inconsistent running-card state across start/stop cycles.
pitfalls.prevention: Maintain the pattern of canceling, clearing self._background_tasks and self._running_card_tasks, and joining the thread on stop().

key_sections: FeishuChannel.start: initialization, lark-oapi import, API client construction and spawning the WS thread
FeishuChannel._run_ws: reason for dedicated thread and event loop patching
send/send_file: outbound send flow including retries and size checks
receive_file/_receive_single_file: inbound file handling, filename sanitation, disk write and sandbox sync
skip_unless_needed: small helper methods that wrap SDK calls (_upload_image/_upload_file/_build_card_content/_add_reaction)
background task bookkeeping methods (_track_background_task/_finalize_background_task) unless debugging concurrency

relevant_snippets.description: Module contains MessageBus and ChannelStore tests; channel-level integration tests are in the same file (file truncated in provided snippet).

related_files.relationship: FeishuChannel uses MessageBus to subscribe to outbound messages and to publish inbound events; message models (InboundMessage/OutboundMessage/ResolvedAttachment) come from here.
related_files.reason_to_check: Modifications to message shapes or publish/subscribe semantics must be reflected here.

related_files.relationship: FeishuChannel subclasses Channel and must conform to its interface (start/stop/send/etc.).
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to Channel base class lifecycle or abstract methods affect FeishuChannel implementations.

related_files.relationship: get_paths() and VIRTUAL_PATH_PREFIX are used to write files and construct virtual sandbox paths for attachments.
related_files.reason_to_check: If virtual path schema or directory layout changes, the mapping of downloaded resources to sandboxes must be adjusted accordingly.

related_files.relationship: get_sandbox_provider() is used to acquire sandbox ids and update files within non-local sandboxes.
related_files.reason_to_check: Changing sandbox acquisition/update API or semantics will require updates to how resources are synchronized from Feishu downloads.

data_constants_to_check: deerflow.config.paths: VIRTUAL_PATH_PREFIX and thread uploads directory conventions
any global retry/timeout constants used by other channel implementations

insights.title: Raise deterministic error when no retry attempts configured
insights.problem: Same retry pattern fixed across channels: ensure a sensible error when no attempts produce exceptions.
insights.root_cause: last_exc might be None.
insights.solution: Raise RuntimeError when last_exc is None; otherwise re-raise last_exc.
insights.lesson_learned: Consistent error semantics across channel implementations reduces unexpected None raises.

frontend/src/app/workspace/agents/new/pa.short_description: UI page to create a new agent: name validation, bootstrap chat, and explicit save flow
frontend/src/app/workspace/agents/new/pa.description:
This React (Next.js) client component implements the new-agent creation flow in the workspace UI. It provides a two-step experience: first collect and validate a unique agent name, then open a short bootstrap chat that seeds the agent and offers an explicit Save action. The component coordinates remote API calls (checkAgentName, createAgent, getAgent) with optimistic UI state and a message-based bootstrap via the thread streaming abstraction (useThreadStream). It also implements read-after-write retry logic when the backend may be eventually consistent.

The file exists to centralize the UX and control flow for creating agents in the browser: validating names locally and remotely, creating the agent record through the API, sending an initial 'bootstrap' chat message that triggers server-side subagent/tool execution, and an explicit Save flow that sends a hidden 'setup_agent' command. The design separates the name step and in-chat step, uses local Storage to show a one-time save hint, and relies on the thread streaming system (messages + tools) to persist and detect server-side completion via onToolEnd and onFinish callbacks. Error handling distinguishes network failures from other errors to present localized messages, and getAgentWithRetry is implemented to tolerate eventual consistency when reading the newly created agent back from the server.

This file integrates with a number of cross-cutting pieces: core agent APIs (checkAgentName, createAgent, getAgent), the thread/message subsystem (useThreadStream, ThreadContext, MessageList), artifact provider, and localization (useI18n). It must respect the message-streaming semantics (thread.isLoading, tool names such as 'setup_agent', and hide_from_ui flag when issuing hidden tool commands), as well as UI constraints like IME composition (isIMEComposing) and retry/backoff timing for fetching the created agent. Commit history shows deliberate additions for explicit save and read-after-write retry behavior.

key_constructs.purpose: Retry delays (ms) used by getAgentWithRetry to poll for created agent availability
key_constructs.reasoning: Defines read-after-write backoff strategy; editing affects how tolerant the UI is to eventual consistency

key_constructs.name: wait
key_constructs.purpose: Small helper that returns a promise that resolves after the given ms
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by getAgentWithRetry for backoff; typical to keep simple for readability

key_constructs.name: getAgentWithRetry
key_constructs.purpose: Tries to fetch the agent by name with a sequence of delays to tolerate eventual consistency
key_constructs.reasoning: Critical for post-save logic (onToolEnd) to find the agent record after a write; the retry timings and failure behavior must be preserved

key_constructs.name: getCreateAgentErrorMessage

key_constructs.name: handleSaveAgent
key_constructs.purpose: Invoked by the explicit Save action: sends a hidden 'setup_agent' tool message, manages setupAgentStatus and shows toast feedback
key_constructs.reasoning: Sends the special hide_from_ui payload and toggles setupAgentStatus; onToolEnd callback reacts to this tool name to complete save flow

key_behaviors: validates agent name locally and remotely
creates an Agent via createAgent API
bootstraps a Thread with a localized initial message after creation
sends a hidden 'setup_agent' command to persist/save agent configuration
retries reading agent record after write to tolerate eventual consistency

pitfalls.mistake: Removing or altering the isIMEComposing check in handleNameKeyDown
pitfalls.consequence: Users using IME may accidentally submit incomplete names while composing characters
pitfalls.prevention: Keep the IME composition check when handling Enter in the name input

pitfalls.mistake: Changing AGENT_READ_RETRY_DELAYS_MS or getAgentWithRetry behavior without considering eventual consistency
pitfalls.consequence: UI may declare the agent creation as pending or fail to detect created agents in environments with eventual write propagation delays
pitfalls.prevention: Respect existing retry/backoff timing or adjust in coordinated fashion with backend write semantics

pitfalls.mistake: Altering the sendMessage invocation for Save (particularly removal of additionalKwargs.hide_from_ui)
pitfalls.consequence: Internal tool-run messages will appear in the UI stream and may reveal internal artifacts or break optimistic grouping
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve hide_from_ui when sending the hidden setup command; ensure onToolEnd logic still relies on tool name 'setup_agent'

pitfalls.mistake: Changing the onToolEnd tool name check (name !== 'setup_agent') or the setupAgentStatus lifecycle
pitfalls.consequence: The component may fail to transition to completed state and to fetch the created agent, leaving the UI in an inconsistent state
pitfalls.prevention: Keep the tool name and related status transitions stable; coordinate any renames with server/tooling that emits the tool name

pitfalls.mistake: Altering SAVE_HINT_STORAGE_KEY or the logic that sets/shows the hint without considering localStorage persistence
pitfalls.consequence: The save hint may be shown repeatedly or never shown depending on key changes, resulting in confusing UX
pitfalls.prevention: If changing the key/persistence behavior, consider migration or deliberate reset behavior

tests_to_run: run integration/manual flow in dev to exercise create->bootstrap->save behavior

insights.title: Add explicit Save action and hidden-save flow for agent creation
insights.problem: Creating an agent relied on implicit save flows and caused UI prompts to surface internal save artifacts; also, read-after-write ordering needed retries for agent retrieval.
insights.root_cause: Agent creation relied on tool-run side effects visible in UI; no explicit save action and no retry for eventual consistency on reading agent after creation.
insights.solution: Add explicit Save menu action that sends a hidden 'setup_agent' command (hide_from_ui), add optimistic UI logic to hide internal messages from message grouping, implement getAgentWithRetry to retry reading the agent after write, and show a persistent save hint once per session.
insights.lesson_learned: When tool calls persist external artifacts (agents), expose explicit user-initiated save actions and hide internal tool-run messages from optimistic UI; implement read-after-write retries to tolerate eventual consistency.

insights.title: Use AgentNameCheckError to show network-specific message
insights.problem: UI displayed misleading messages for CORS/network errors when checking agent name.
insights.root_cause: Page catch block didn't differentiate error types coming from checkAgentName.
insights.solution: Catch AgentNameCheckError and TypeError 'Failed to fetch', set localized network message; otherwise show generic check error.
insights.lesson_learned: UI catch logic should inspect error types and reasons to show more actionable messages.

backend/packages/harness/deerflow/agents.short_description: Middleware that injects uploaded-file metadata, outlines and previews into the last human message.
backend/packages/harness/deerflow/agents.description:
This module provides an AgentMiddleware implementation that surfaces uploaded-file information to the agent by prepending a formatted <uploaded_files> block to the last HumanMessage. It exists to make uploaded artifacts (files) discoverable and actionable for the agent: it reports new files in the current message, scans a thread-local uploads directory for historical files, attaches a document outline when a converted Markdown (.md) sibling exists, and otherwise provides short preview lines and guidance (grep/glob/read_file) so agents use file tools rather than impulsively falling back to web search.

Design choices: uploads are presented as a human-readable block instead of structured objects inside the message body so the model receives clear textual guidance alongside the user's content. The middleware preserves the original additional_kwargs of the HumanMessage (including the original files metadata) to allow the frontend to consume structured metadata from the streaming messages. It is defensive about runtime.context possibly being None and falls back to LangGraph configuration for thread_id when available, matching similar middleware patterns (see commit history implications). Outline extraction is delegated to deerflow.utils.file_conversion.extract_outline, and the physical uploads directory resolution is provided by Paths/get_paths.

How it fits: This middleware runs before the agent executes (before_agent) and mutates the messages list in state (replacing the last human message with an updated HumanMessage containing the <uploaded_files> block). It returns an updated state mapping containing updated messages and a placed uploaded_files key listing newly uploaded files (used by other parts of the runtime if needed). Tests in the repository exercise parsing of incoming files metadata, existence checks, outline & preview behavior, formatting, and preservation of message fields.

key_constructs.name: _OUTLINE_PREVIEW_LINES
key_constructs.purpose: Number of non-empty lines to extract as preview when an .md has no headings
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by the fallback preview path; editors must preserve this constant's semantics when changing preview length because tests and UX expect a small anchor snippet for outline-less documents.

key_constructs.name: _extract_outline_for_file
key_constructs.purpose: Given a file Path, attempt to read a sibling .md, extract headings (outline) or return a fallback preview of first non-empty lines
key_constructs.reasoning: This encapsulates the logic of deciding whether an uploaded file has a structured outline or requires a preview. It calls extract_outline(md_path) and returns (outline, preview). When editing callers or the function, preserve the return shape (tuple[list[dict], list[str]]) and the preference for outline over preview.

key_constructs.name: UploadsMiddlewareState
key_constructs.purpose: AgentState schema used to store uploaded_files in the middleware state
key_constructs.reasoning: Defines the typed state schema expected by the middleware. Edits that change state keys used by other middleware or tests must keep the uploaded_files key name and semantics intact or update dependent code/tests accordingly.

key_constructs.name: UploadsMiddleware
key_constructs.purpose: Agent middleware that reads frontend file metadata, validates existence against thread uploads dir, enriches entries with outlines/previews, and prepends a formatted <uploaded_files> block to the last human message
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the main integration point. Its responsibilities include: resolving the uploads directory based on runtime.context/thread_id (with a fallback to LangGraph get_config when context is absent), parsing and validating additional_kwargs.files via _files_from_kwargs, scanning historical files in the uploads directory (excluding newly uploaded ones), attaching outline/preview to file dicts, creating the formatted message with _create_files_message, and replacing the last HumanMessage while preserving id and additional_kwargs. Any changes must respect message preservation behaviors, runtime.thread_id resolution patterns, return values (state update map), and the format expected by tests/consumers.

key_constructs.name: _files_from_kwargs
key_constructs.purpose: Parse message.additional_kwargs['files'] into normalized file dicts, perform validation and optional existence check against uploads_dir, and construct virtual /mnt/user-data/uploads/ paths
key_constructs.reasoning: This function enforces filename hygiene (only basename allowed), coerces size to int with default 0, and conditionally checks disk existence when uploads_dir is supplied. Consumers expect the returned dicts to contain filename, size, path, and extension keys; tests assert behavior when uploads_dir is None vs present.

key_constructs.name: _create_files_message
key_constructs.purpose: Render the formatted textual <uploaded_files> block combining new and historical file entries with guidance and formatted size/outline/preview details
key_constructs.reasoning: The exact phrasing and tags in this rendered block are verified by tests (presence of markers, wording like 'uploaded in this message', grep/read_file guidance, size formatting). Preserve structure and phrasing if tests or downstream models depend on them.

key_constructs.name: _format_file_entry
key_constructs.purpose: Render the lines describing a single file entry (size formatting, path, outline or preview and search hints)
key_constructs.reasoning: Helper used by _create_files_message; it implements the branching that displays an outline (with truncation hint) or preview + grep hint. It must keep the same keys and expected text fragments because unit tests and the model prompt expect them verbatim.

key_constructs.name: before_agent
key_constructs.purpose: Middleware hook that performs the end-to-end flow: discover new/historical files, enrich them, create the files message and prepend it to the last human message, returning updated state
key_constructs.reasoning: Entry point used by LangGraph runtime. It returns None when nothing to inject and a dict with messages and uploaded_files when injection occurs. It also carefully preserves last message id and additional_kwargs; tests assert these behaviors and other edge-cases (thread_id==None behavior, historical scanning). Any edits should preserve the return contract and the defensive runtime.context handling.

key_behaviors: parses frontend-supplied uploaded-files metadata
validates files against a thread uploads directory when available
attaches Markdown outline or preview lines to file metadata
prepends a formatted <uploaded_files> block to the last HumanMessage while preserving additional_kwargs and message id

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming runtime.context is always a dict and indexing it directly
pitfalls.consequence: AttributeError or crash in environments where runtime.context is None (tests and commit history show this occurred)
pitfalls.prevention: Use safe access patterns (runtime.context or {}).get('thread_id') as currently implemented

pitfalls.mistake: Changing the textual content/formatting of the <uploaded_files> block without updating tests or downstream consumers
pitfalls.consequence: Unit tests that assert presence of phrases (e.g., 'uploaded in this message', 'Document outline', 'grep', 'read_file') will fail and model guidance may break
pitfalls.prevention: Keep wording and tag markers stable or update tests/consumers together

pitfalls.mistake: Removing preservation of additional_kwargs or message id when creating the updated HumanMessage
pitfalls.consequence: Frontend/consumer code that expects to read structured files metadata from message.additional_kwargs or rely on original message.id will break (tests assert preserved fields)
pitfalls.prevention: Maintain additional_kwargs and id on the new HumanMessage

pitfalls.mistake: Altering the shape of file dicts returned by _files_from_kwargs (missing keys, different key names)
pitfalls.consequence: Other code/tests that depend on filename, size, path, extension, outline, outline_preview will fail
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve keys and their semantics; if changing, update dependent code/tests

key_sections: UploadsMiddleware.before_agent: main entry point; handles runtime thread resolution, new/historical file collection and message replacement
_files_from_kwargs: validation/existence checks and virtual path normalization (important for tests)
_extract_outline_for_file: how .md siblings are used to build outlines or fall back to previews
_create_files_message/_format_file_entry: exact rendered text and guidance displayed to model
skip_unless_needed: logging configuration and trivial helper code

related_files.reason_to_check: If extract_outline return shape or MAX_OUTLINE_ENTRIES changes, UploadsMiddleware logic and tests must be updated

related_files.relationship: Provides Paths.sandbox_uploads_dir used to resolve thread upload directories
related_files.reason_to_check: Changing directory layout or sandbox path semantics affects existence checks and virtual paths produced by _files_from_kwargs

related_files.relationship: get_config() fallback path used to resolve a thread_id when runtime.context lacks one
related_files.reason_to_check: If get_config API or configurable.thread_id location changes, before_agent's fallback must be updated

insights.title: Provide document outline and grep/glob guidance for uploaded files
insights.problem: Agents often fell back to web search or inefficient browsing when dealing with uploaded documents because the agent context lacked structured guidance and a document outline.
insights.root_cause: Uploaded-document pipeline produced .md conversions, but the agent context didn't include an outline or actionable search guidance.
insights.solution: Inject document outline into <uploaded_files> context with line numbers; when no outline, provide the first N lines as preview and a clear grep/glob/read_file workflow guidance. Also update formatting helpers to render outlines and previews.
insights.lesson_learned: Providing structured navigation hints (outline + search tools) dramatically improves agent workflows for large uploaded artifacts; middleware should deliver guidance (not just metadata).

insights.title: Resolve uploads_dir when runtime.context lacks thread_id
insights.problem: runtime.context sometimes did not carry thread_id depending on invocation path which caused uploads directory resolution to be skipped and outline/historical-files not injected.
insights.root_cause: Middleware relied solely on runtime.context['thread_id'] and did not fallback to configurable.thread_id from LangGraph configuration.
insights.solution: If runtime.context lacks thread_id, attempt get_config().configurable.thread_id with a RuntimeError guard for non-runnable contexts.
insights.lesson_learned: Middleware that reads runtime.context must be resilient to different LangGraph invocation paths and apply the same fallback logic as ThreadDataMiddleware.

insights.title: Null-check runtime.context to avoid NPE when context is None
insights.problem: Assumptions that runtime.context is present caused attribute errors when LangGraph runtime passed None (context may be None).
insights.root_cause: Some LangGraph runtimes set runtime.context to None; code accessed runtime.context.get(...) without guarding.
insights.solution: Use (runtime.context or {}).get('thread_id') pattern to safely extract thread_id.
insights.lesson_learned: Middleware must defensively handle absent runtime context when integrating with external runtimes; prefer safe access patterns.

insights.title: Avoid AttributeError when runtime.context is None in UploadsMiddleware
insights.problem: UploadsMiddleware read runtime.context.get('thread_id') unguarded and crashed when context was None
insights.root_cause: Unconditional context access
insights.solution: Use runtime.context.get('thread_id') if runtime.context else None and handle uploads_dir accordingly
insights.lesson_learned: Middleware should be resilient to incomplete runtime objects, and compute dependent paths only when context values are present.

backend/tests/test_uploads_middleware_co.short_description: Unit tests validating parsing, formatting and injection logic of UploadsMiddleware.
backend/tests/test_uploads_middleware_co.description:
This test module provides comprehensive core-behaviour tests for UploadsMiddleware. It targets three main areas: _files_from_kwargs parsing/validation (including existence checks and path normalization), _create_files_message formatting (presence of tags, size formatting, outline/preview blocks and guidance text), and before_agent end-to-end behavior (message replacement for both string and list content, historical file scanning, outline extraction, fallback preview and message field preservation).

Tests use pytest with tmp_path fixtures to create temporary sandbox upload directories and files, and MagicMock to stub Runtime objects. The tests exercise both positive and negative edge-cases (missing thread_id, missing files on disk, mixed existing/non-existing lists, outline truncation logic using MAX_OUTLINE_ENTRIES from the file conversion utility). They assert both structured outputs (the uploaded_files state returned) and textual artifacts inside the generated <uploaded_files> message (phrases such as 'Document outline', 'read_file', 'grep', and the presence of filenames and paths).

key_constructs.purpose: Create a HumanMessage with optional additional_kwargs.files and other extra kwargs
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to simulate incoming user messages with varying content shapes (string or list blocks) and attached files metadata

key_constructs.reasoning: Exercises the fine-grained validation conditions the middleware must enforce

key_constructs.reasoning: Ensures the presentation layer used by the model is stable and follows expectations

key_constructs.name: TestBeforeAgent
key_constructs.purpose: End-to-end tests for before_agent covering injection logic, preservation of additional_kwargs and message id, outline handling and previews
key_constructs.reasoning: These tests validate the overall middleware behavior and integration with Paths/outline extraction

backend/packages/harness/deerflow/tools/.short_description: Async LangChain tool that delegates work to background subagents and streams lifecycle events.
backend/packages/harness/deerflow/tools/.description: This module implements the 'task' tool used by the lead agent to delegate complex work to specialized subagents that run in their own isolated context. It exists to: (1) validate requested subagent types (including rejecting host-bash when not permitted), (2) create a SubagentExecutor with the parent run's runtime metadata and available tools, (3) start background execution (non-blocking) and then poll the background task result, and (4) emit structured stream events about task lifecycle (task_started, task_running, task_completed/failed/timed_out/cancelled). The tool is async (async def task_tool) so it can integrate safely into an asyncio-based runtime and avoid blocking the event loop while polling.

key_constructs.name: task_tool
key_constructs.purpose: Main async tool entrypoint invoked by LangChain; starts a subagent via SubagentExecutor and polls for results while emitting stream events.
key_constructs.reasoning: Any edits must preserve the async polling loop, the event emission protocol (task_started/task_running/task_completed/task_failed/task_timed_out/task_cancelled), and the cancellation semantics: cooperative cancellation is signaled to the running subagent via request_cancel_background_task and deferred cleanup is scheduled using asyncio.create_task.

callers.context: from deerflow.tools.builtins import ask_clarification_tool, present_file_tool, task_tool, view_image

key_constructs.name: cleanup_when_done
key_constructs.purpose: Inner async coroutine scheduled on CancelledError to poll the background task result until it reaches a terminal state, then call cleanup_background_task.
key_constructs.reasoning: This deferred cleanup prevents resource leaks when the polling coroutine is cancelled; changing its behavior, timeouts or removal will alter cancellation semantics and can cause orphaned background tasks.

key_constructs.name: log_cleanup_failure
key_constructs.purpose: Inner callback attached to the scheduled deferred cleanup task to log exceptions from cleanup_when_done.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to capture and log exceptions from the deferred cleanup; editing how exceptions are surfaced or swallowed affects observability of cleanup failures.

key_behaviors: validates and rejects disallowed subagent types (e.g., bash) based on sandbox policy
constructs SubagentExecutor with overrides (e.g., injected skills section, max_turns)
starts background execution asynchronously with execute_async and uses tool_call_id as task_id
polls background task results every 5s (asyncio.sleep) and emits task lifecycle events via get_stream_writer
on asyncio.CancelledError requests cooperative cancellation and schedules deferred cleanup
avoids cleanup on polling-safety timeout (when task still RUNNING) to prevent racing with background executor

pitfalls.mistake: Removing or changing the call to request_cancel_background_task in the CancelledError handler
pitfalls.consequence: Background thread running the subagent will continue executing despite parent cancellation, leading to orphaned work and resource leaks.
pitfalls.prevention: Retain request_cancel_background_task(task_id) in the CancelledError path and maintain deferred cleanup polling behavior.

pitfalls.mistake: Replacing asyncio.sleep with a blocking sleep or synchronous loop
pitfalls.consequence: Blocks the asyncio event loop, causing worker starvation or deadlocks in the runtime.
pitfalls.prevention: Always use asyncio.sleep (or an awaitable stub in tests) for polling.

pitfalls.mistake: Calling cleanup_background_task on polling safety timeout when the result.status indicates RUNNING
pitfalls.consequence: May race with the still-running background executor and prematurely remove task state; tests expect no cleanup in this case.
pitfalls.prevention: Only call cleanup_background_task for terminal statuses; preserve current behavior where polling safety timeout returns without cleanup.

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming runtime.context is always present and contains thread_id
pitfalls.consequence: AttributeError or missing thread_id; behavior differs between LangGraph and other callers.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve fallback logic that checks runtime.context is not None and falls back to runtime.config.get('configurable', {}).get('thread_id').

pitfalls.mistake: Allowing subagent tools to be available inside subagents (nesting)
pitfalls.consequence: Recursive subagent spawning and potential runaway nesting or unexpected behaviors.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep get_available_tools(model_name=..., subagent_enabled=False) usage that explicitly disables subagent tools for the subagent.

key_sections: subagent_type validation and is_host_bash_allowed() check: ensures forbidden bash usage is rejected
overrides building (skills_section and max_turns): shows how configuration is merged into SubagentConfig
executor creation (SubagentExecutor instantiation): key input mapping from runtime into executor
polling loop: message forwarding, status checks and event emission logic
asyncio.CancelledError handler: request_cancel_background_task and deferred cleanup scheduling
skip_unless_needed: module imports and top-level logger setup

relevant_snippets.description: test_task_tool_emits_running_and_completed_events: sets up DummyExecutor, simulates RUNNING then COMPLETED results, asserts events sequence and executor kwargs (skills injected, max_turns override, thread_id/model passed).

related_files.relationship: Implements the SubagentExecutor runtime that task_tool constructs and starts; changes to execution lifecycle or terminal statuses require checking task_tool's polling logic.
related_files.reason_to_check: If SubagentStatus values, execute_async semantics, or background task storage change, the polling loop and terminal checks in task_tool must be updated accordingly.

related_files.relationship: task_tool lazily imports get_available_tools from deerflow.tools; tests patch deerflow.tools.get_available_tools. Ensure public API surface and import names remain stable.
related_files.reason_to_check: Renaming or relocating get_available_tools will break the lazy import and tests that patch it.

related_files.relationship: Provides is_host_bash_allowed and LOCAL_BASH_SUBAGENT_DISABLED_MESSAGE used to validate 'bash' subagent usage.
related_files.reason_to_check: If the host bash allowlist or error messaging changes, task_tool's user-facing error strings and tests must remain consistent.

data_constants_to_check: get_stream_writer contract (callable that accepts dict events)
request_cancel_background_task / cleanup_background_task semantically implement cooperative cancellation and cleanup

insights.title: Request cooperative cancellation when task polling is cancelled
insights.problem: When task_tool polling coroutine was cancelled (asyncio.CancelledError), the background thread running the subagent would continue processing because the thread could not be force-cancelled.
insights.root_cause: CancelledError handling didn't signal the subagent thread to stop cooperatively.
insights.solution: Call request_cancel_background_task(task_id) on CancelledError path; handle SubagentStatus.CANCELLED on polling results and emit task_cancelled events. Ensure cleanup recognizes CANCELLED as terminal.
insights.lesson: Higher-level cancellation (task-level) must propagate cooperative cancellation to background threads; ensure tool plumbing reacts to CANCELLED status in polling loops.

insights.title: Fallback to config.configurable for thread_id in task_tool
insights.problem: task_tool failed when runtime.context lacked thread_id because LangGraph Server sometimes provides thread_id in config.configurable.
insights.root_cause: task_tool only read runtime.context for thread_id.
insights.solution: Added fallback to runtime.config.get('configurable', {}).get('thread_id') consistent with ThreadDataMiddleware behavior.
insights.lesson_learned: Tools invoked from different runtimes (LangGraph vs Gateway) may pass thread metadata in different places; implement consistent fallbacks.

insights.title: Reject bash subagent and provide clear message when host bash is disabled
insights.problem: Subagent creation could expose a bash subagent in local mode even when host bash is unsafe.
insights.root_cause: task_tool accepted subagent_type 'bash' without consulting host bash allow-list.
insights.solution: Check is_host_bash_allowed() and return a clear error message (LOCAL_BASH_SUBAGENT_DISABLED_MESSAGE) when bash subagent is requested but not permitted.
insights.lesson_learned: Subagent exposure should respect sandbox security configuration; tools should return clear user-facing guidance when a capability is disabled.

insights.title: Make task tool polling non-blocking and handle cancellation cleanup
insights.problem: task tool used synchronous blocking time.sleep in a coroutine-like tool, blocking the event loop and risking worker starvation. Cancellation left background tasks uncleaned.
insights.root_cause: Original implementation was synchronous (tool.func) using time.sleep; it didn't use asyncio and had no robust cancellation cleanup path.
insights.solution: Convert the tool to async (async def task_tool), replace time.sleep with asyncio.sleep, and implement CancelledError handler that schedules deferred cleanup coroutine via asyncio.create_task which polls background task result until completion or bounded timeout and calls cleanup_background_task. Also ensure logging of cleanup failures.
insights.lesson_learned: Tools that poll background state in an async runtime must be async and use asyncio primitives; implement bounded deferred cleanup on cancellation to avoid resource leaks and race conditions.

insights.title: Null-safe thread_id retrieval in task tool
insights.problem: task_tool accessed runtime.context.get('thread_id') unguarded leading to AttributeError when context was None
insights.root_cause: Unconditional context access in tool logic
insights.solution: Use runtime.context.get('thread_id') if runtime.context else None and handle missing thread_id appropriately
insights.lesson_learned: All built-in tools must assume runtime.context may be None and fail fast with clear messages for easier debugging.

backend/tests/test_task_tool_core_logic..short_description: Unit tests covering core orchestration, polling and cancellation behavior of the task tool.
backend/tests/test_task_tool_core_logic..description: This test module verifies core behaviors of the 'task' tool: subagent type validation, security checks for bash subagents, event emission during task lifecycle (running/completed/failed/timed_out/cancelled), polling-safety timeout behavior, cleanup invocation for terminal statuses, and cooperative cancellation handling. Tests are written to exercise the module-level symbols as referenced inside task_tool by importing the module and monkeypatching its attributes. Many tests stub SubagentExecutor, SubagentStatus and background task accessors (get_background_task_result, execute_async), and replace asyncio.sleep with deterministic stubs so the async polling loop runs deterministically during unit tests.

key_constructs.name: FakeSubagentStatus
key_constructs.purpose: Enum used to mimic production SubagentStatus values in tests so branch comparisons behave identically.
key_constructs.reasoning: Tests rely on matching enum values with task_tool's status checks; renaming or changing the enum values will break many tests.

key_constructs.name: _make_runtime
key_constructs.purpose: Creates a minimal ToolRuntime-like SimpleNamespace used to supply runtime.state, context and config to task_tool in tests.
key_constructs.reasoning: Many tests depend on fields returned by this helper (sandbox/thread_data/thread_id/model trace_id), so changes to runtime shape in task_tool must be mirrored here.

key_constructs.name: _run_task_tool
key_constructs.purpose: Helper that detects whether task_tool is a LangChain tool wrapper (async coroutine) and executes it correctly (via asyncio.run or direct call).
key_constructs.reasoning: The test suite supports both the old sync wrapper and the new async coroutine style; modifying how task_tool is exported (e.g., renaming attributes) will require test updates.

key_constructs.name: test_task_tool_emits_running_and_completed_events
key_constructs.purpose: Test that task_tool forwards AI messages as task_running events and emits task_completed with result; also validates passed executor kwargs (skills injection, max_turns override, thread_id/model).
key_constructs.reasoning: This is a core integration-like unit test that verifies event ordering and that runtime metadata is forwarded into SubagentExecutor.

key_behaviors: patches task_tool module-level dependencies (get_subagent_config, get_background_task_result, SubagentExecutor, get_stream_writer, asyncio.sleep) to drive deterministic flows
verifies event sequences emitted by the stream writer stub (task_started, task_running, task_completed/failed/timed_out/cancelled)
asserts that cleanup_background_task is invoked for terminal statuses but not for polling safety timeouts
asserts request_cancel_background_task is called on cancellation and that deferred cleanup scheduling occurs

pitfalls.mistake: Renaming module-level symbols in task_tool (e.g., get_background_task_result, cleanup_background_task) or changing how task_tool is exported
pitfalls.consequence: Monkeypatching in tests will fail because tests import the module and patch names exactly as referenced.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the exact module-level symbol names or update the tests to patch the new names.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing the export shape of task_tool (e.g., no longer having .coroutine for async wrapper scenarios)
pitfalls.consequence: _run_task_tool helper may not detect and run the tool correctly, causing test failures.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep LangChain tool wrapper interface intact or update _run_task_tool accordingly.

pitfalls.mistake: Altering timing or message-index semantics (e.g., 1-based message_index) without updating tests
pitfalls.consequence: Assertions around event contents and ordering will fail.
pitfalls.prevention: When editing event payloads, update tests to match the new payload structure.

reading_guide.start_here: _make_runtime and _make_subagent_config helpers to understand expected runtime shape
key_sections: _run_task_tool: shows how the tool is invoked (async vs sync wrapper)
test_task_tool_emits_running_and_completed_events: primary behavior test validating event emission and executor args
tests around cleanup and cancellation: show expected semantics for cleanup_background_task and request_cancel_background_task
skip_unless_needed: repeated fixture-like helper functions (ai message builders) once you understand test patterns

related_files.relationship: The module under test; tests import it via importlib and monkeypatch its module-level symbols.
related_files.reason_to_check: Any changes to task_tool's public module-level symbol names, event payload shapes, or cancellation behavior will require test updates.

data_constants_to_check: Event payload fields expected by tests: 'type', 'task_id', 'message', 'message_index', 'total_messages', 'result', 'error'

insights.title: Add tests for task cancellation propagation
insights.problem: Need to assert that CancelledError path triggers request_cancel_background_task and appropriate task_polling behavior.
insights.root_cause: Earlier tests didn't verify CancelledError->request_cancel behavior or CANCELLED polling behavior.
insights.solution: Added tests verifying request_cancel invoked on CancelledError and polling returns 'Task cancelled by user.' with corresponding events and cleanup.
insights.lesson: Tools that spawn background work must have tests validating both cancellation propagation and user-visible messages.

insights.title: Update task tool tests to run async tool and assert cleanup scheduling
insights.problem: task_tool was converted to async; tests needed to be adapted from calling synchronous function to running the coroutine and patching asyncio.sleep.
insights.root_cause: Change to async API and new cancellation semantics required test refactor.
insights.solution: Introduce helper _run_task_tool using asyncio.run, replace time.sleep patches with asyncio.sleep stubs, add tests verifying deferred cleanup scheduled on CancelledError and that deferred cleanup gives up after bounded polls.
insights.lesson_learned: When converting synchronous code to async, update tests to run the coroutine (asyncio.run) and assert async scheduling and cancellation behavior. Use deterministic sleep stubs to avoid flakiness.

backend/packages/harness/deerflow/sandbo.short_description: Local sandbox implementation that maps container-style paths to host paths and enforces read-only mounts.
backend/packages/harness/deerflow/sandbo.description:
This module implements a local sandbox that mimics container mount behavior by mapping virtual/container paths to real host filesystem paths. It exists so the codebase can run "sandboxed" file operations and shell commands against the host filesystem while presenting a container-like view to callers (so tooling and skills behave similarly in both local and containerized sandboxes). The file centralizes path translation in both directions, enforces read-only mounts, and sanitizes error messages to show container-style paths to callers rather than leaking host paths.

Key design decisions: Path mappings are represented by a frozen dataclass PathMapping(container_path, local_path, read_only) so mappings are explicit and typed. Resolution algorithms (_resolve_path and _reverse_resolve_path) sort mappings by prefix length to prefer the most specific mapping (longest prefix) — an important behavior for nested mounts.
