The module also implements translation of paths inside shell commands before executing them on the host (_resolve_paths_in_command) and masks local host paths in command output back to container paths (_reverse_resolve_paths_in_output). Reads and writes are performed against the resolved host paths, but OSError raised to callers is re-thrown with the original container path to avoid exposing internal host layout.

How it fits into the larger system: LocalSandbox is one of the sandbox providers used by SandboxMiddleware (and LocalSandboxProvider) in the agents/middleware stack. For local development (host-backed sandboxing) it enables running shell commands, file IO, globbing and grep operations on the developer's filesystem while keeping the agent-facing paths consistent with container-mounted behavior. Tests in backend/tests/test_local_sandbox_provider_mounts.py exercise the mapping, read-only enforcement, and translation behavior. The module also contains OS-aware shell detection logic to support Windows and Unix shells.

Non-obvious behaviors to respect: mapping specificity (longest-prefix first) influences resolution and read-only checks; path normalization uses Path.resolve() which can change symlinks and absolute form; regex-based reverse resolving must be kept in sync with how local paths are normalized; write/update operations check read-only via the resolved host path and raise errno.EROFS when blocked.

key_constructs.name: PathMapping
key_constructs.purpose: Dataclass representing a single mapping from container_path to local_path with a read_only flag.
key_constructs.reasoning: Treats mappings as first-class objects so callers (and tests) can express mount behavior and read-only semantics; important to preserve exact fields and default read_only=False when editing.

key_constructs.name: LocalSandbox
key_constructs.purpose: Main sandbox class that implements file IO, command execution, glob/grep, and path translation between container and host.
key_constructs.reasoning: All public sandbox behaviors (execute_command, read_file, write_file, update_file, list_dir, glob, grep) are implemented on this class; changes here affect how the agent interacts with the host filesystem.

key_constructs.name: _resolve_path
key_constructs.purpose: Translate a container-style path into the underlying host path using path_mappings, preferring the most specific mapping.
key_constructs.reasoning: Correct mapping order (sorted by len(container_path) desc) is critical for nested mounts. Maintains the container-to-host translation semantics expected by tests and other modules.

key_constructs.name: _reverse_resolve_path
key_constructs.purpose: Translate an absolute host path back into its container-style path using path_mappings, preferring the most specific mapping.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to mask host paths in outputs and in API responses; relies on normalization via Path.resolve(), so changes must preserve how host paths are matched and replaced.

key_constructs.name: _is_read_only_path
key_constructs.purpose: Determine whether a resolved host path falls under a read-only mapping, picking the most specific mapping when multiple match.
key_constructs.reasoning: Enforced by write_file and update_file via raising OSError(errno.EROFS). Any edit must keep the specificity selection logic intact to support nested writable mounts under read-only parents.

key_constructs.name: _resolve_paths_in_command
key_constructs.purpose: Replace container paths inside a shell command string with the corresponding host paths before execution.
key_constructs.reasoning: Uses regex built from container paths (with lookahead to avoid partial matches). This function directly affects what path strings are passed to subprocess; incorrect regex changes can break command execution or cause wrong path substitutions.

key_constructs.name: _reverse_resolve_paths_in_output
key_constructs.purpose: Mask host paths found in arbitrary command output back to container-style paths for safety and UX.
key_constructs.reasoning: Uses regexes built from resolved local paths and handles both forward and backslash separators; changes can cause leaking of host paths or false replacements.

key_constructs.name: execute_command
key_constructs.purpose: Execute a shell command on the host after resolving container paths; capture stdout/stderr, append exit code if non-zero, and mask host paths in the result.
key_constructs.reasoning: Contains OS-aware logic for calling subprocess.run (different handling for Windows vs Unix). It is the only place that runs external commands and therefore must preserve timeout, capture_output and post-processing behavior.

key_constructs.name: write_file
key_constructs.purpose: Write text content to a file resolved from container path, creating directories as needed and blocking writes to read-only mounts.
key_constructs.reasoning: Raises OSError(errno.EROFS) when the resolved path is read-only and re-raises OS errors using the original container path in the exception to hide host paths.

key_constructs.name: update_file
key_constructs.purpose: Write binary content to a file resolved from container path, similar to write_file but for bytes and with read-only enforcement.
key_constructs.reasoning: Also enforces read-only semantics and re-raises errors referencing the original path, so tests around update_file expect errno.EROFS on blocked writes.

key_constructs.name: list_dir
key_constructs.purpose: List directory tree entries for a resolved host path and mask host paths in the returned entries.
key_constructs.reasoning: Delegates to an internal list_dir helper and then reverse-resolves each returned entry; output formatting must remain stable for tests that assert on returned path strings.

key_constructs.name: glob
key_constructs.purpose: Perform glob matching on the resolved host path and return container-masked results with truncation flag.
key_constructs.reasoning: Relies on find_glob_matches helper; returns masked matches and a boolean truncated marker used by callers.

key_constructs.name: grep
key_constructs.purpose: Search files for a pattern under a resolved host path and return container-masked GrepMatch objects.
key_constructs.reasoning: Relies on find_grep_matches helper and must wrap results into GrepMatch instances with path values transformed via _reverse_resolve_path.

key_constructs.name: _get_shell
key_constructs.purpose: Detect an appropriate shell executable on the running platform, with fallbacks for Windows and Unix.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by execute_command to determine how to invoke subprocess. Edits must preserve the search order and error messages because tests patch this to force a particular shell path.

key_behaviors: translates container paths to host paths before filesystem/command operations
masks host paths back to container paths in outputs
enforces read-only mounts and raises errno.EROFS on blocked writes
executes shell commands with OS-aware invocation

pitfalls.mistake: Altering mapping selection order or removing length-based sorting
pitfalls.consequence: Nested mounts or more specific mount prefixes will be matched incorrectly, breaking tests and allowing wrong read/write behavior
pitfalls.prevention: Keep sorting by mapping prefix length (most specific first) in both resolve and reverse-resolve flows

pitfalls.mistake: Changing Path.resolve() usage or normalization semantics
pitfalls.consequence: Path matching (including reverse resolution and regex replacement) may fail, causing false negatives/positives and leaking host paths
pitfalls.prevention: Respect existing Path.resolve() normalization and be aware of symlink effects when editing matching logic

pitfalls.mistake: Modifying regex patterns used for path substitution without keeping separator/backslash handling
pitfalls.consequence: Reverse resolution may fail on Windows-style backslashes or may produce partial/overreaching replacements
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve escaping and backslash handling; keep longest-first ordering for mappings before building regexes

pitfalls.mistake: Bypassing _is_read_only_path checks in write/update operations
pitfalls.consequence: Host files under read-only mounts could be modified, violating intended safety guarantees and causing test failures
pitfalls.prevention: Always invoke _is_read_only_path on the resolved host path prior to write/update and raise OSError(errno.EROFS) when blocked

pitfalls.mistake: Changing execute_command subprocess invocation (args, shell flags, timeout) casually
pitfalls.consequence: Command execution semantics may differ across platforms (Windows vs Unix) and tests that monkeypatch _get_shell or subprocess.run will fail
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve OS-specific branches and default timeout/capture behavior or adjust tests accordingly

key_sections: _resolve_path: container→host translation and why length-sorting matters
_reverse_resolve_path: host→container translation and normalization
_is_read_only_path: how read-only mapping is selected
execute_command: how shell detection, command substitution and output masking are performed
write_file/update_file: how writes are blocked and OSError is re-raised with virtual path
skip_unless_needed: helper static methods for shell name detection if not changing shell behavior

relevant_snippets.description: Tests resolving container paths to host paths and longest-prefix matching for nested mounts.
Tests read-only enforcement and that write_file raises errno.EROFS for read-only mounts and succeeds for writable mounts.
Tests execute_command path replacement by monkeypatching subprocess.run and _get_shell to capture resolved command.

related_files.relationship: LocalSandboxProvider constructs the PathMapping list and instantiates LocalSandbox; provider setup logic determines which mappings are available to LocalSandbox.
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to mapping fields, normalization or reserved container path handling in the provider must be aligned with LocalSandbox mapping expectations.

related_files.relationship: Unit tests that validate LocalSandbox behavior (mapping, read-only enforcement, command masking).
related_files.reason_to_check: When modifying LocalSandbox behavior, run these tests to validate semantics remain intact.

insights.title: Support read-only host path mappings and robust path resolution
insights.problem: Local sandbox previously supported basic mapping of a single container skills path to host path but lacked support for multiple custom mounts and read-only flags. There was also potential path-matching edge cases and no enforcement of read-only mounts.
insights.root_cause: Path mappings were a simple dict and path-resolution logic did not consider mapping specificity, nested mounts, or read-only semantics.
insights.solution: Introduce PathMapping dataclass (container_path, local_path, read_only). Adjust _resolve_path/_reverse_resolve_path to iterate sorted mappings (most specific first) and to properly normalise separators. Add _is_read_only_path to prefer most specific mapping and enforce EROFS on write operations (write_file/update_file raise OSError errno EROFS). Improve regex for reverse-resolve to support backslashes and avoid partial prefix false matches.
insights.lesson_learned: When mapping host paths into container virtual paths, represent mappings as explicit structured objects, match the most specific mapping first, and enforce read-only policy at filesystem operation boundaries to avoid accidental host writes.

insights.title: Local sandbox path-mapping and reverse-resolution for user-friendly I/O
insights.problem: Local sandbox previously executed host commands with container-style paths and returned host paths in outputs, leaking host filesystem layout and making interactive commands confusing.
insights.root_cause: No centralized path mapping existed between container virtual paths and host paths for local sandbox; command execution and outputs used host paths directly.
insights.solution: Introduce LocalSandbox.path_mappings, implement _resolve_path and _reverse_resolve_path to translate between container and host paths. Add _resolve_paths_in_command to translate container paths before executing host shell, and _reverse_resolve_paths_in_output to mask host paths back to container virtual paths in outputs. Update file ops to resolve paths and re-raise OSError with original virtual path for clearer UX.
insights.lesson_learned: When implementing a 'local' sandbox that mimics container mounts, centralize path translation both inbound (commands) and outbound (outputs) to avoid leaking host paths and to produce predictable behavior matching containerized deployments.

backend/tests/test_local_sandbox_provide.short_description: Unit tests for LocalSandbox path mappings, read-only semantics, command substitution, and LocalSandboxProvider mapping setup.
backend/tests/test_local_sandbox_provide.description:
This test module verifies the LocalSandbox mapping and enforcement semantics. It covers: the PathMapping dataclass semantics and defaults; container→host and host→container path resolution including nested and longest-prefix matching; read-only enforcement (write_file/update_file raising EROFS on blocked writes); correct behavior for nested writable mounts under a readonly parent; command path substitution (ensuring container paths are replaced with host paths before subprocess.run); and reverse-masking of host paths in outputs including handling backslashes (Windows-style). The tests also exercise LocalSandboxProvider's mapping setup by patching get_app_config to simulate application configuration and ensure provider mapping logic normalizes and filters configured mounts.

Why it exists: These tests capture the expected sandbox behavior that guarantees safety and UX parity between local and container sandboxes. They guard against regressions that could leak host paths, allow unintended writes, or break path translation logic that agents and skills rely on.

How tests are organized: Tests are grouped into classes by concern (PathMapping, path resolution, read-only paths, multiple mounts, provider mounts). Several tests use pytest tmp_path fixtures to create temporary directories and monkeypatch/patch to simulate subprocess behavior and app configuration.

key_constructs.name: TestPathMapping
key_constructs.purpose: Validate PathMapping dataclass fields and default values.

key_constructs.name: TestLocalSandboxPathResolution
key_constructs.purpose: Verify _resolve_path and _reverse_resolve_path behaviors, including nested and longest-prefix matching.

key_constructs.name: TestReadOnlyPath
key_constructs.purpose: Test read-only detection logic and that write/update operations raise errno.EROFS as expected.

key_constructs.name: TestMultipleMounts
key_constructs.purpose: Tests interactions across multiple mounts: writable vs read-only mounts, nested writable under read-only, command substitution and reverse-masking of backslash-separated paths.

key_constructs.name: TestLocalSandboxProviderMounts
key_constructs.purpose: Test LocalSandboxProvider setup of path mappings by patching deerflow.config.get_app_config to supply custom sandbox config.

key_behaviors: asserts PathMapping defaults and values
asserts container→host and host→container translation correctness
ensures read-only mounts block writes via EROFS
ensures nested mounts and mapping priority behave as expected
verifies LocalSandboxProvider ignores invalid mount configurations

pitfalls.mistake: Running tests on a system with different shell availability without mocking _get_shell
pitfalls.consequence: execute_command tests may execute different shells or fail; captured command expectations may not match
pitfalls.prevention: The tests monkeypatch LocalSandbox._get_shell or subprocess.run; keep that behavior when editing tests or the production function

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming absolute path forms across OSes without normalization
pitfalls.consequence: reverse-resolve tests that rely on Path.resolve() may behave differently on Windows vs Unix
pitfalls.prevention: Use tmp_path fixtures which produce platform-appropriate absolute paths; be cautious if altering Path.resolve() usage

pitfalls.mistake: Modifying LocalSandboxProvider behavior without updating these tests
pitfalls.consequence: Provider mount normalization and filtering tests will fail; mapping lists may differ
pitfalls.prevention: When changing provider mapping rules, update test expectations in TestLocalSandboxProviderMounts

key_sections: TestLocalSandboxPathResolution: core path resolution expectations
TestReadOnlyPath: read-only blocking semantics for write/update operations
TestMultipleMounts.test_execute_command_path_replacement: how command substitution is validated (monkeypatch usage)
TestLocalSandboxProviderMounts: how provider reads app config and filters mounts
skip_unless_needed: TestPathMapping: small dataclass sanity checks

relevant_snippets.description: Imports and setup used across tests (pytest, tmp_path, monkeypatch, patch).
Path resolution tests (exact, nested, no-mapping, longest-prefix).
Read-only enforcement and write/update tests, asserting errno.EROFS.
execute_command path replacement test that monkeypatches subprocess.run and _get_shell to capture resolved command.

related_files.relationship: Direct unit-under-test; these tests validate that module's path translation and enforcement logic.
related_files.reason_to_check: Any change in LocalSandbox API/semantics must be validated by updating these tests or ensuring behavior is preserved.

related_files.relationship: Provider setup logic is tested here by patching config; changes to provider mapping normalization will affect these tests.
related_files.reason_to_check: Provider mapping normalization and filter rules are asserted in TestLocalSandboxProviderMounts.

frontend/src/components/workspace/comman.short_description: Client-side React component that provides a global command palette and keyboard-shortcuts overlay.
frontend/src/components/workspace/comman.description:
CommandPalette is a client-only React component that implements a global command palette UI (searchable command dialog) and a keyboard-shortcuts help dialog. It centralizes keyboard-driven UX for workspace actions: create a new chat (route navigation), open settings (show SettingsDialog), and show the shortcuts overlay. The component registers platform-aware keyboard shortcuts (meta vs ctrl and shift labelling) via a custom useGlobalShortcuts hook and uses i18n for all user-facing strings. It composes existing UI primitives (CommandDialog/CommandList/CommandInput/CommandItem and Dialog/DialogContent) and the SettingsDialog component.

This file exists to provide a consistent, discoverable keyboard-driven surface across the web app. It is implemented as a client component ("use client"), so it can access browser globals, Next.js client navigation (useRouter), and local state from useState/useEffect. The component focuses on stable references (useCallback, useMemo) for handlers and the shortcuts list to avoid unnecessary re-registering of global key handlers. It also computes platform-specific labels (metaKey and shiftKey) and renders a dedicated dialog describing the available shortcuts.

Key integration points: it uses the app's i18n hook (useI18n) for translations; it uses Next.js' useRouter to navigate to the new chat route; it depends on a project-level useGlobalShortcuts hook to register/unregister keyboard shortcuts; it renders SettingsDialog and various UI primitives from the project's shared UI components. Because it's client-side, it must be careful with browser globals and SSR mismatches.

Design decisions evident in the code: stable callbacks and memoized shortcuts array to keep global-shortcut registration predictable; separate boolean states for the command dialog, settings dialog, and shortcuts dialog; immediate closure of the command dialog when other overlays are opened (setOpen(false) before opening settings/shortcuts), keeping UX deterministic when chaining actions.

key_constructs.name: CommandPalette
key_constructs.purpose: Main React component that renders the command palette and shortcuts help dialog.
key_constructs.reasoning: Primary entry point for the file; contains state, handlers, and composed UI primitives. Agents modifying behavior should start here to understand component lifecycle and dialog state flow.

key_constructs.name: handleNewChat
key_constructs.purpose: Callback that navigates the app to /workspace/chats/new and closes the command dialog.
key_constructs.reasoning: Performs client navigation (useRouter.push). Changes here affect routing behavior and must preserve closing the command dialog to avoid stale overlays.

key_constructs.name: handleOpenSettings
key_constructs.purpose: Callback that closes the command dialog and opens the SettingsDialog.
key_constructs.reasoning: Coordinates two dialogs; modifications must preserve the close-then-open sequence to avoid overlapping modals.

key_constructs.name: handleShowShortcuts
key_constructs.purpose: Callback that closes the command dialog and opens the keyboard shortcuts Dialog.
key_constructs.reasoning: Same coordination as handleOpenSettings; important for predictable dialog state transitions.

key_constructs.name: shortcuts
key_constructs.purpose: Memoized array of global shortcut definitions passed to useGlobalShortcuts.
key_constructs.reasoning: Holds key, modifier flags and action callbacks. The shape and identity stability matter for the custom global-shortcuts hook registration/unregistration.

key_constructs.name: metaKey
key_constructs.purpose: Computed string label for the platform's modifier key (⌘ or Ctrl+).
key_constructs.reasoning: Used for UI labels and must reflect the same platform detection logic used for shortcut handling to avoid confusing users.

key_behaviors: registers global keyboard shortcuts
opens/closes command palette
navigates to new chat route
opens settings dialog
displays keyboard shortcuts overlay

pitfalls.mistake: Access navigator.userAgent or other browser globals during render/SSR.
pitfalls.consequence: Server-side rendering can cause undefined globals or markup mismatch between server and client, leading to hydration warnings or runtime errors.
pitfalls.prevention: Only access navigator inside effects or guarded mounted checks; ensure platform detection is computed on the client only.

pitfalls.mistake: Modify the shortcuts array identity on every render.
pitfalls.consequence: The useGlobalShortcuts hook may re-register handlers unnecessarily or fail to clean up previous registrations, causing duplicated handlers or memory leaks.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep the shortcuts array memoized (as implemented) and preserve stable handler references (useCallback) when changing behavior.

pitfalls.mistake: Open a modal without closing the command dialog first.
pitfalls.consequence: Overlapping dialogs or unexpected focus capture can confuse keyboard interactions and UX flow.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the established pattern of calling setOpen(false) before opening the other dialog states (settingsOpen, shortcutsOpen).

pitfalls.mistake: Change metaKey/shiftKey labels without aligning actual shortcut handling.
pitfalls.consequence: UI labels will not match real shortcut behavior on different platforms, confusing users.
pitfalls.prevention: When altering platform detection or labels, ensure the underlying key handling logic in useGlobalShortcuts is consistent with label changes.

key_sections: shortcuts: memoized array that defines the global shortcut bindings and their actions
handleNewChat / handleOpenSettings / handleShowShortcuts: dialog and navigation coordination points
useEffect (isMac): platform detection for label rendering and its relationship to SSR
skip_unless_needed: Icon imports (lucide-react) and small presentational JSX inside CommandItem elements

related_files.relationship: The hook used to register/unregister global keyboard shortcuts; changes to shortcuts shape or registration expectations here require coordinated changes in that hook.
related_files.reason_to_check: If you modify the structure of the shortcuts array, key mapping behavior, or lifecycle expectations (register/unregister), inspect and update this hook.

related_files.relationship: UI primitives (CommandDialog, CommandInput, CommandList, CommandItem) used to render the actual command palette UI.
related_files.reason_to_check: If you change how commands are rendered or the props they expect, update usages here to match.

related_files.relationship: SettingsDialog component that CommandPalette opens; state flow expects setSettingsOpen(true) to display it.
related_files.reason_to_check: If SettingsDialog props or open/onOpenChange API changes, update the call site in this file.

insights.title: Avoid using navigator during SSR by gating on mounted
insights.problem: CommandPalette used navigator.userAgent during render which is undefined during SSR and caused mismatches.
insights.root_cause: Client-only global navigator used without mounted guard.
insights.solution: Set mounted flag via useEffect and compute isMac only when mounted; return null until mounted.
insights.lesson_learned: Guard browser globals in components that render on both server and client; prefer mounted guards and avoid SSR-time usage of navigator.

insights.title: Add global command palette and keyboard shortcuts
insights.problem: No global command palette or consistent keyboard shortcuts were available
insights.root_cause: Feature not previously implemented
insights.solution: Add CommandPalette component with commands for new chat, settings, and shortcuts overlay and wire it into layout
insights.lesson_learned: Global keyboard UX helpers should account for input focus (suppress shortcuts in inputs except cmd+k) and provide platform-appropriate labels.

frontend/src/components/workspace/settin.short_description: Client React component that renders the app's About text using the Streamdown markdown renderer.
frontend/src/components/workspace/settin.description:
This is a tiny client-side React component that exists solely to render the project's About content in the workspace settings area using a streaming Markdown renderer. It imports a Streamdown component (a markdown-to-React streaming renderer) and an aboutMarkdown value (the markdown source text) from a sibling module. The component is marked with "use client" which forces Next.js/React to treat it as a client component; this is important because Streamdown likely uses browser-only APIs (DOM streaming/hydration) or expects to run in a browser environment.

The file's purpose is to separate content (aboutMarkdown) from presentation (Streamdown) and to provide a simple named export AboutSettingsPage which can be used by the settings route/page in the frontend. Because it's a minimal wrapper, its primary responsibilities are: import the correct renderer and content, ensure client-side execution, and return the renderer with the markdown passed as children. This pattern prevents duplication of content and centralizes the About text in about-content while keeping rendering decisions in this component.

In the larger system, this component fits into the frontend workspace/settings UI. It will typically be imported into a settings page or a settings route and rendered as part of the settings UI. Because it is a client component, it will not be server-side rendered; any changes to how it is authored (for example removing "use client") or changes to the Streamdown API will affect runtime behavior and hydration in the Next.js app.

key_constructs.name: AboutSettingsPage
key_constructs.purpose: React functional component that renders the project's About markdown using Streamdown.
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the main exported component in the file; edits to layout/props/hook usage will directly change how the About content displays. Any consumer imports this named export.

key_constructs.name: Streamdown
key_constructs.purpose: Imported React component that streams/renders markdown passed as children.
key_constructs.reasoning: Although imported, Streamdown is the rendering engine used here. Changes to how it is invoked (props/children) or upgrades to the dependency may require adapting this usage. It likely depends on being executed in a browser environment, which is why "use client" is present.

key_constructs.name: aboutMarkdown
key_constructs.purpose: Imported markdown string or node containing the About text to render.
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the authoritative source for the About content used by this component; edits to the wording or structure of the About page usually come from this value. File-level changes that move or rename it must be reflected in this import.

key_behaviors: renders about markdown content using a streaming renderer
executes as a client-side React component (use client)
delegates markdown source to a separate content module

pitfalls.mistake: Remove or omit the "use client" directive
pitfalls.consequence: Streamdown may rely on browser APIs, causing build-time or runtime errors and hydration mismatches in Next.js if treated as a server component.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep the "use client" directive when the component remains dependent on client-only rendering; validate with a local dev server build.

pitfalls.mistake: Change the children usage to a prop or alter the invocation without checking Streamdown's API
pitfalls.consequence: Streamdown may expect markdown as children; changing how the markdown is passed can break rendering or streaming behavior.
pitfalls.prevention: Verify Streamdown's API before modifying the invocation; run the frontend dev server and try the settings page after edits.

pitfalls.mistake: Rename or relocate aboutMarkdown without updating the import path
pitfalls.consequence: Build or runtime import errors will occur and the About page will fail to render.
pitfalls.prevention: When moving/renaming about-content, update imports and run a local build (pnpm build) or dev server to catch missing module errors.

key_sections: Streamdown: imported renderer component used to stream/render markdown
aboutMarkdown: markdown source imported from about-content
skip_unless_needed: module boundary boilerplate (there is none here beyond imports)

tests.example_command: cd frontend && pnpm dev (no unit tests available for this small component)

related_files.relationship: Provides the aboutMarkdown content this component renders; content and formatting changes here affect what is displayed.
related_files.reason_to_check: If you modify the markdown structure or move/rename the module, update the import or adjust rendering expectations.

related_files.relationship: Contains Streamdown dependency/version used by this component
related_files.reason_to_check: Upgrading or changing the Streamdown package may change its API or runtime requirements; verify compatibility.

related_files.relationship: This component will be imported and used inside the settings UI route/page.
related_files.reason_to_check: When altering the export name or props of AboutSettingsPage, update its consumers in the settings route.

tests_to_run: cd frontend && pnpm dev (manual UI verification)
cd frontend && pnpm build (catch build-time errors)

backend/app/gateway/routers/uploads.py.short_description: FastAPI router that handles thread-scoped file uploads, listings, and deletions with sandbox syncing.
backend/app/gateway/routers/uploads.py.description:
This module implements the HTTP endpoints for uploading files to a thread-specific uploads directory, listing uploaded files, and deleting uploaded files. It exists to centralize Gateway-level behavior for file artifacts that are later mounted into sandboxes used by agents. The router delegates validation, normalization, listing and deletion logic to the shared harness helpers in deerflow.uploads.manager and uses the sandbox provider abstraction to optionally mirror files into containerized sandboxes.

Design decisions reflected here: (1) reuse of manager helpers (normalize_filename, ensure_uploads_dir, get_uploads_dir, list_files_in_dir, delete_file_safe, upload_virtual_path, upload_artifact_url, enrich_file_listing) to ensure consistent path handling and security checks across code paths; (2) decision to relax host filesystem permissions for non-local sandboxes (AIO) so sandbox-side users with different UID/GID can modify uploads; (3) conservative handling of symlinks (skip chmod for symlinks). The module integrates with the sandbox provider (get_sandbox_provider) to acquire a sandbox id and update files inside the sandbox when the provider is non-local. File conversion to markdown (for convertible extensions) is invoked and those artifacts are also mirrored and included in the response.

key_constructs.name: UploadResponse
key_constructs.purpose: Pydantic model for successful upload responses, containing success flag, list of uploaded file metadata, and a message.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used as the FastAPI response_model to provide a stable response shape for clients and tests.

key_constructs.name: _make_file_sandbox_writable
key_constructs.purpose: Set write bits on a host-side file so that a non-local sandbox runtime can write to the mounted file; skips symlinks.
key_constructs.reasoning: Central to permission-handling for AIO sandboxes: callers must respect its symlink check and OS-specific follow_symlinks handling to avoid chmoding symlinks or causing platform errors.

key_constructs.name: upload_files
key_constructs.purpose: POST handler that accepts multiple UploadFile items and writes them to the thread uploads dir, optionally mirrors into a non-local sandbox, and converts certain files to markdown.
key_constructs.reasoning: Primary entrypoint; it must call normalization and dir-creation helpers (and map their exceptions to HTTP errors), write bytes atomically, call permission relaxation only for non-local sandboxes, and handle conversion results that may create companion files.

key_constructs.name: list_uploaded_files
key_constructs.purpose: GET handler that lists files in a thread's uploads dir and augments entries with sandbox-relative paths and artifact URLs.
key_constructs.reasoning: Uses manager-provided list_files_in_dir and enrich_file_listing to keep listing semantics centralized; also needs to compute sandbox-relative paths for Gateway consumers.

key_constructs.name: delete_uploaded_file
key_constructs.purpose: DELETE handler that removes an upload (and associated markdown companion) using manager.delete_file_safe and maps exceptions to HTTP responses.
key_constructs.reasoning: Relies on delete_file_safe to safely validate paths and remove convertible companions; must translate FileNotFoundError / PathTraversalError / other exceptions into appropriate HTTP status codes.

key_behaviors: accepts and stores thread-scoped files on host storage
normalizes and rejects unsafe filenames
mirrors files into non-local sandboxes and relaxes permissions
converts convertible files to markdown and returns companion metadata
lists and deletes uploaded artifacts (including generated companions)

pitfalls.mistake: Calling _make_file_sandbox_writable unconditionally for local sandboxes
pitfalls.consequence: Alters permissions unnecessarily for local mode where sandbox user shares host permissions; tests expect no chmod call for local provider.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect sandbox_id == 'local' check before invoking permission relaxation (as current code does).

pitfalls.mistake: Using os.chmod with follow_symlinks when os.chmod does not support follow_symlinks on a platform
pitfalls.consequence: TypeError or unexpected behavior on platforms lacking supports_follow_symlinks support.
pitfalls.prevention: Use os.supports_follow_symlinks to decide kwargs (the module already constructs chmod_kwargs accordingly).

pitfalls.mistake: Modifying symlinks instead of their targets
pitfalls.consequence: Potential security issues or broken links; tests expect lstat detection and skipping.
pitfalls.prevention: Use os.lstat and skip chmod when S_ISLNK is true (current implementation does this).

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming convert_file_to_markdown always returns a Path
pitfalls.consequence: If conversion returns None, code must not attempt to read/write the md path; current code checks if md_path before using it.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the existing guard 'if md_path:' before accessing md_path.

key_sections: upload_files: main HTTP POST path, handles normalization, writing, sandbox mirroring, conversion, and response shaping
_make_file_sandbox_writable: permission handling and symlink safety
delete_uploaded_file: deletion flow and exception-to-HTTP mappings
skip_unless_needed: import block and logger setup

relevant_snippets.description: Covers upload_files behavior: local vs non-local sandbox syncing, permission adjustments, markdown conversion mirroring, and deletion of companion files.
Validates normalization, path traversal validation, list/delete helpers which upload router delegates to.

related_files.relationship: authoritative helpers for normalization, path traversal, listing, deletion and virtual path/URL construction used by this router
related_files.reason_to_check: Any change in manager function signatures, exception types, or behavior requires corresponding changes in how this router calls them and maps exceptions to HTTP errors.

related_files.relationship: provides get_sandbox_provider and sandbox object used to acquire sandbox id and update files
related_files.reason_to_check: If sandbox provider acquire/get semantics or sentinel 'local' id change, router logic that branches on sandbox_id must be updated.

related_files.relationship: contains CONVERTIBLE_EXTENSIONS and convert_file_to_markdown used to detect and produce markdown companions
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to conversion API or extension lists affect what files get converted and how the router mirrors companion files.

insights.title: Make uploaded files world-writable for non-local (AIO) sandboxes
insights.problem: When uploads are written by the Gateway on the host and then mounted into an isolated AIO sandbox, permission mismatches could prevent the sandbox runtime (different UID/GID) from modifying the mounted files.
insights.root_cause: Files written by the gateway retained host ownership and restrictive modes that could block writes by sandbox-side users.
insights.solution: Add _make_file_sandbox_writable(file_path) which lstat() checks for symlinks and then chmods to add write bits for user/group/other (respecting follow_symlinks support). Call this for non-local sandboxes after host-side write and converted markdown writes.
insights.lesson_learned: When mounting host files into containerized sandboxes that may run as different users, explicitly relax permissions on host-side files to allow sandbox modifications; avoid changing symlinks and handle OS nuances.

insights.title: Delegate upload logic to shared harness manager to reduce duplication and harden validation
insights.problem: Multiple implementations of uploads logic (Gateway and Client) duplicated filename normalization, traversal checks, URL construction, and conversion handling — leading to inconsistency and potential security gaps.
insights.root_cause: Business logic scattered across layers produced subtle differences (e.g., Path.name checks, mkdir side effects, URL encoding).
insights.solution: Delegate to deerflow.uploads.manager functions (normalize_filename, ensure_uploads_dir, get_uploads_dir, list_files_in_dir, delete_file_safe, upload_virtual_path, upload_artifact_url, enrich_file_listing). Map shared exceptions to appropriate HTTP errors. Use percent-encoded artifact_url helper and ensure read/write path separation.
insights.lesson_learned: Centralize file/URL/path handling into harness-level helpers to ensure consistent security checks and reduce TOCTOU and duplication across HTTP and in-process clients.

backend/tests/test_uploads_manager.py.short_description: Unit tests for shared upload manager helpers: normalization, path traversal, listing, and safe deletion.
backend/tests/test_uploads_manager.py.description:
This test module exercises core utility functions in the upload manager that enforce filename normalization, prevent path traversal, list files in a directory, and safely delete files (including convertible companions). It exists to ensure the central manager functions provide the security and semantics that the Gateway router and in-process clients rely on. The tests focus on boundary and security cases (empty names, '.' and '..', path traversal via symlink escape), deterministic ordering of listings, and deletion behavior including raising appropriate exceptions for missing files or traversal attempts.

Tests are written with pytest and use tmp_path for isolated filesystem state. Symlink creation is guarded to skip tests on Windows where symlink creation requires elevated privileges (the test detects winerror 1314 and skips accordingly). This cross-platform consideration is explicit in the tests and important when running on CI or developer machines without appropriate privileges.

key_constructs.name: TestNormalizeFilename
key_constructs.purpose: Group of tests validating normalize_filename behavior for safe/unsafe inputs and path component stripping.
key_constructs.reasoning: Shows the expectations for what normalize_filename accepts, strips, and rejects — important for any code that accepts user-supplied filenames.

key_constructs.name: TestDeduplicateFilename
key_constructs.purpose: Tests claim_unique_filename to ensure unique filename generation and mutation of 'seen' set.
key_constructs.reasoning: Ensures deduplication function produces expected suffix numbering and mutates its seen set in-place.

key_constructs.name: TestValidatePathTraversal
key_constructs.purpose: Tests validate_path_traversal to ensure files remain inside a base directory and symlink escapes are detected.
key_constructs.reasoning: Emphasizes path validation semantics; symlink escape test includes platform-aware skip logic.

key_constructs.name: TestListFilesInDir
key_constructs.purpose: Validates listing behavior: empty directory, non-existent dir, sorting, metadata keys, and ignoring subdirectories.
key_constructs.reasoning: Establishes the structure and ordering the router relies on when presenting file lists to clients.

key_constructs.name: TestDeleteFileSafe
key_constructs.purpose: Validates safe deletion semantics: successful deletion, missing file raises FileNotFoundError, traversal raises PathTraversalError.
key_constructs.reasoning: Delete behavior is critical (companion deletion on file removal) and tests prove manager.delete_file_safe enforces traversal checks and exception semantics.

key_behaviors: asserts that normalization strips path components and rejects unsafe names
ensures deduplication yields unique names and mutates seen set
validates path traversal and symlink escape detection
verifies sorted file listing and metadata fields
ensures deletion raises correct exceptions and removes companion markdown

pitfalls.mistake: Running symlink tests on Windows without elevated privileges
pitfalls.consequence: Test raises OSError instead of skipping and may fail CI or local runs
pitfalls.prevention: Tests explicitly catch OSError and skip when winerror==1314; avoid removing that guard.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing exceptions thrown by manager helpers
pitfalls.consequence: Tests (and calling routers) expect PathTraversalError and FileNotFoundError; altering these will break consumers and tests.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve exception types or update tests and callers in tandem.

reading_guide.start_here: TestNormalizeFilename or TestValidatePathTraversal depending on area of interest
key_sections: TestNormalizeFilename: baseline normalization contracts
TestValidatePathTraversal.test_symlink_escape: platform-aware symlink handling and expected traversal exception
TestDeleteFileSafe: ensures deletion semantics, including companion removals
skip_unless_needed: simple list sorting checks once basic semantics are understood

relevant_snippets.description: Entire file is relevant: validates manager functions used by router and other parts.

related_files.relationship: source under test: contains normalize_filename, validate_path_traversal, list_files_in_dir, delete_file_safe, claim_unique_filename
related_files.reason_to_check: Any change to manager logic or exception types requires updating these tests.

insights.title: Windows-aware symlink creation tests and validation of traversal
insights.problem: Symlink creation can fail on Windows unless developer mode or elevated privileges are present, causing tests to error rather than skip.
insights.root_cause: Tests attempted os.symlink without handling Windows privileges.
insights.solution: Wrap symlink creation in try/except and skip test when PermissionError indicates symlink creation is not permitted (winerror 1314).
insights.lesson_learned: Cross-platform tests touching filesystem symlinks must detect platform limitations and skip gracefully when the environment cannot create symlinks.

backend/tests/test_uploads_router.py.short_description: Unit tests for the Gateway uploads router covering local vs non-local sandbox syncing, permission changes, conversion, and deletion.
backend/tests/test_uploads_router.py.description:
This test module exercises the FastAPI router functions implemented in backend/app/gateway/routers/uploads.py. Tests simulate UploadFile objects and patch out filesystem helpers, sandbox provider, and conversion functions to validate router control flow: writing to the thread uploads dir, skipping sandbox syncing for 'local' provider, syncing files and markdown companions into non-local sandboxes, and invoking permission relaxation only for non-local sandboxes. It also contains direct tests for the internal _make_file_sandbox_writable behavior (chmod semantics and symlink skipping). The tests heavily use unittest.mock to isolate the router from the real sandbox provider and conversion logic, asserting calls and file contents on tmp_path.

These tests are important as they confirm the contract between the Gateway and sandbox layers (when to mirror files, when to change permission bits) and that dangerous filenames like '.' and '..' are rejected or normalized appropriately.

key_constructs.name: test_upload_files_writes_thread_storage_and_skips_local_sandbox_sync
key_constructs.purpose: Asserts that for local sandbox provider, files are written to thread storage and sandbox.update_file is not called.
key_constructs.reasoning: Verifies the branching on sandbox_id == 'local' which affects whether permissions are adjusted and files mirrored.

key_constructs.name: test_upload_files_syncs_non_local_sandbox_and_marks_markdown_file
key_constructs.purpose: Asserts that non-local sandboxes receive file bytes and markdown companion metadata is included in response.
key_constructs.reasoning: Checks conversion integration and that converted md files are mirrored into sandbox.

key_constructs.name: test_upload_files_makes_non_local_files_sandbox_writable
key_constructs.purpose: Ensures that _make_file_sandbox_writable is called for both original and converted files when sandbox is non-local.
key_constructs.reasoning: Tests permission change side-effects are invoked for non-local providers.

key_constructs.name: test_upload_files_does_not_adjust_permissions_for_local_sandbox
key_constructs.purpose: Ensures _make_file_sandbox_writable is not invoked when sandbox provider returns 'local'.
key_constructs.reasoning: Prevents unnecessary chmod operations for local mode and matches expectations in the router implementation.

key_constructs.name: test_make_file_sandbox_writable_adds_write_bits_for_regular_files
key_constructs.purpose: Integration-style test that checks chmod result on a real file in tmp_path.
key_constructs.reasoning: Validates that the implementation actually sets IW bits when invoked.

key_constructs.name: test_make_file_sandbox_writable_skips_symlinks
key_constructs.purpose: Mocks os.lstat to simulate a symlink and asserts chmod is not called.
key_constructs.reasoning: Confirms symlink detection and skipping behavior.

key_constructs.name: test_upload_files_rejects_dotdot_and_dot_filenames
key_constructs.purpose: Ensures unsafe filenames '.' and '..' are rejected and path traversal prefixes are stripped to basename.
key_constructs.reasoning: Verifies normalize_filename behavior is respected and only safe normalized files are persisted.

key_constructs.name: test_delete_uploaded_file_removes_generated_markdown_companion
key_constructs.purpose: Verifies delete_uploaded_file removes both original and generated markdown companion and returns expected JSON.
key_constructs.reasoning: Confirms delete_file_safe behavior is invoked and companion removal semantics hold.

handles_entities: Host filesystem files and markdown companions
key_behaviors: asserts branch behavior for local vs non-local sandbox syncing
validates md conversion mirror and response metadata
checks permission relaxation invocation and symlink safety
verifies rejection and normalization of unsafe filenames
checks deletion removes companion markdown

pitfalls.mistake: Removing or changing the string sentinel 'local' used by provider.acquire in tests/production
pitfalls.consequence: Tests that patch acquire to return 'local' will no longer exercise correct branch and behavior expectations will break.
pitfalls.prevention: If sandbox provider's sentinel changes, update all router logic and tests to use the new sentinel consistently.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing UploadFile constructor signature or behavior in FastAPI without updating tests
pitfalls.consequence: Test UploadFile usage (passing filename and BytesIO) may fail; tests rely on creating UploadFile directly.
pitfalls.prevention: When altering UploadFile usage patterns, update tests accordingly.

pitfalls.mistake: Altering convert_file_to_markdown to return types other than Path or None
pitfalls.consequence: Tests and router code assume truthy Path or falsy None; changing that contract can break mirroring and assertions.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve conversion return contract or update tests and router handling in tandem.

key_sections: tests that patch get_uploads_dir/ensure_uploads_dir/get_sandbox_provider: understand how router is isolated from external dependencies
tests that mock convert_file_to_markdown: conversion path and companion file assertions
permission tests: real chmod test and symlink mock test
skip_unless_needed: boilerplate imports and repeated tmp_path setup

related_files.relationship: system under test: router endpoints that read/write files and interact with sandbox provider
related_files.reason_to_check: If router behavior or function names change, this test module must be updated accordingly.

related_files.relationship: convert_file_to_markdown used in tests (often patched) and CONVERTIBLE_EXTENSIONS constant
related_files.reason_to_check: Tests patch convert_file_to_markdown; changes to conversion API or extension list affect test expectations.

insights.title: Upload router tests assert permission changes for non-local sandboxes
insights.problem: Need to ensure permission relaxation is applied only for non-local sandboxes and that symlinks are not chmod'd.
insights.root_cause: New _make_file_sandbox_writable behavior must be validated to avoid regressions.
insights.solution: Add tests to assert _make_file_sandbox_writable called for non-local provider and not called for local; unit test chmod semantics by creating files with read-only mode and verifying write bits added; test symlink skipping by mocking lstat to simulate symlink.
insights.lesson_learned: Filesystem permission changes require explicit tests for symlink safety and differentiation between local and non-local sandbox modes.

backend/app/gateway/services.py.short_description: Run lifecycle and SSE formatting service layer for Gateway endpoints
backend/app/gateway/services.py.description:
Centralized service layer that encapsulates the Gateway's run lifecycle logic and Server-Sent Events (SSE) framing. This module exists to keep router handlers thin and consistent: it formats SSE frames, normalizes incoming run input/config parameters to LangChain/LangGraph shapes, constructs the RunnableConfig used by the agent factory, creates RunRecords via the RunManager, launches agent run tasks, and exposes an SSE consumer generator that streams events from a StreamBridge to HTTP clients while enforcing on_disconnect semantics.

Design decisions visible in the file: (1) SSE frame formatting is centralized in format_sse to guarantee a consistent wire format (matching LangGraph Platform and langgraph-sdk). (2) build_run_config reconciles older 'configurable' and newer 'context' request fields (LangGraph >= 0.6.0), injects thread-level fields and optionally maps assistant_id to configurable.agent_name with normalization and validation. (3) run creation is delegated to RunManager.create_or_reject and run_agent to avoid duplicating lifecycle and concurrency logic in routers; start_run is responsible for post-create bookkeeping (upserting thread record into the Store and scheduling title-sync after run completion).

This module interacts with many runtime constructs: it fetches singletons from FastAPI Request state using dependency helpers (get_stream_bridge, get_run_manager, get_checkpointer, get_store), calls the lead agent factory via resolve_agent_factory (returns make_lead_agent), and uses run_agent and StreamBridge to coordinate background runs and streaming. It intentionally defers some imports inside helper functions to avoid circular imports with router modules that define Store operations and request models.

key_constructs.name: format_sse
key_constructs.purpose: Format a single SSE frame (event/data/(optional)id) as a string matching LangGraph wire format.
key_constructs.reasoning: Other components (routers, tests) rely on the exact field ordering and JSON-encoding behavior here; callers expect 'event: ...', 'data: ...', optional 'id: ...', and two trailing newlines.

key_constructs.name: normalize_stream_modes
key_constructs.purpose: Normalize the incoming stream_mode request parameter to a list of stream mode strings.
key_constructs.reasoning: Entrypoints and run invocation expect a list of modes; this helper provides defaults and accepts str/list/None to match client behavior (useStream).

key_constructs.name: normalize_input
key_constructs.purpose: Convert LangGraph Platform input format (dict with messages) into LangChain-friendly state dict, converting message dicts to HumanMessage where appropriate.
key_constructs.reasoning: Runs created via the Gateway may supply messages in a platform format; the agent factory/run machinery expects LangChain message objects in graph_input['messages'] so this conversion ensures compatibility.

key_constructs.name: _DEFAULT_ASSISTANT_ID
key_constructs.purpose: Default assistant id string used to decide when to inject configurable.agent_name.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by build_run_config to determine when the assistant_id should be forwarded as agent_name into configurable (only when non-default).

key_constructs.name: resolve_agent_factory
key_constructs.purpose: Return the agent factory callable for a given assistant_id (currently always make_lead_agent).
key_constructs.reasoning: Abstracts how assistant_id maps to the factory so changes in routing logic later can be localized here; tests assert it always returns make_lead_agent.

key_constructs.name: build_run_config
key_constructs.purpose: Assemble the RunnableConfig dict used by run_agent from thread id, request config, metadata, and optional assistant_id.
key_constructs.reasoning: This function centralizes the normalization and merging of request-provided 'configurable' vs 'context', injects thread_id, validates/injects assistant_id as configurable.agent_name when appropriate, and copies other request_config keys.

key_constructs.name: _upsert_thread_in_store
key_constructs.purpose: Create or refresh a thread record in the Gateway's Store so stateless runs appear in /threads/search.
key_constructs.reasoning: Stateless endpoints like /runs/stream may never call POST /threads; this helper ensures threads started from start_run are visible in search results. It defers import to avoid circular deps.

key_constructs.name: _sync_thread_title_after_run
key_constructs.purpose: After a run completes, read the final checkpoint and persist TitleMiddleware-generated title into the Store record.
key_constructs.reasoning: TitleMiddleware writes the title into the agent's checkpoint; the gateway Store is separate and must be updated after the run finishes to keep thread metadata in sync. Runs as a background task and swallows errors to avoid impacting the main run path.

key_constructs.name: start_run
key_constructs.purpose: Create a RunRecord via the RunManager and launch the background run_agent task; upsert thread store and schedule title sync as needed.
key_constructs.reasoning: Router-level endpoints call this to centralize the create-or-reject flow, conflict/unsupported strategy handling, stream mode normalization, config assembly, and task creation. It wires together RunManager, StreamBridge, Checkpointer, and Store.

key_constructs.name: sse_consumer
key_constructs.purpose: Async generator that subscribes to a StreamBridge and yields SSE frames for HTTP streaming; enforces on_disconnect semantics.
key_constructs.reasoning: Provides the generator used by router responses to stream SSEs. It handles HEARTBEAT_SENTINEL, END_SENTINEL, JSON framing via format_sse, Last-Event-ID replay, and finalization behavior (cancel vs continue on client disconnect).

key_behaviors: formats SSE frames for streaming clients
normalizes inbound run input and stream modes
builds runnable config including configurable/context merging and assistant_id mapping
creates RunRecord and launches background run tasks
streams events from StreamBridge to HTTP clients with disconnect semantics

pitfalls.mistake: Unconditionally inserting 'configurable' when the caller already supplied 'context'.
pitfalls.consequence: LangGraph >= 0.6.0 rejects run configs containing both 'context' and 'configurable', causing 400 errors; tests and commit history show this bug was fixed.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect presence of top-level 'context' in request_config and avoid adding a separate configurable field in that case; log a warning if both are present as the code already does.

pitfalls.mistake: Altering the SSE field order or JSON encoding behavior in format_sse.
pitfalls.consequence: Client-side decoders (useStream React hook and langgraph-sdk) expect the exact ordering and JSON payload; mismatch can break clients and tests.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep 'event:' then 'data:' then optional 'id:' then two trailing newlines; use json.dumps with ensure_ascii=False and default=str to match current encoding.

pitfalls.mistake: Injecting assistant_id into configurable without normalization/validation.
pitfalls.consequence: Could allow invalid agent names (path traversal or invalid chars) and lead to agent factory lookups failing or reading unexpected files; commit history added normalization and validation.
pitfalls.prevention: Use the existing normalization (strip, lower, replace '_' with '-') and regex validation; do not overwrite an explicit configurable['agent_name'] sent by the caller.

pitfalls.mistake: Cancelling vs continuing background tasks incorrectly on client disconnect.
pitfalls.consequence: Could either leave long-running tasks cancelled unexpectedly or keep running tasks blocked by bridge backpressure on missing consumers.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect record.on_disconnect semantics: if DisconnectMode.cancel then cancel via run_mgr.cancel; if continue, let the task run and simply stop streaming (code already handles this in the finally block).

key_sections: format_sse: authoritative SSE wire formatter used across the gateway
build_run_config: how request fields map into the runnable config (context vs configurable and assistant_id support)
start_run: orchestration of RunManager.create_or_reject, run_agent invocation, and post-create bookkeeping (store upsert, title sync)
sse_consumer: streaming loop, heartbeat/end sentinel handling, and disconnect finalization
skip_unless_needed: _upsert_thread_in_store
_sync_thread_title_after_run

relevant_snippets.description: Verifies JSON 'data:' encoding, presence of 'event:' header and optional 'id:' for different events.
Multiple unit tests validate format_sse, normalize helpers, build_run_config behavior including assistant_id injection and context handling.

related_files.relationship: Direct unit tests that assert SSE framing behavior from format_sse.
related_files.reason_to_check: If format_sse changes, update these tests and any clients that parse SSE frames.

related_files.relationship: Unit tests covering normalize helpers and build_run_config (context/configurable merging and assistant_id mapping).
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to build_run_config or normalize_input should be validated against these tests.

related_files.relationship: Defines END_SENTINEL, HEARTBEAT_SENTINEL, run_agent, RunManager, RunRecord, DisconnectMode, etc., which services.py uses heavily.
related_files.reason_to_check: Runtime-level contract changes (sentinel semantics, run_agent API) will require updates here.

insights.title: Avoid 400 when client sends 'context' together with 'configurable'
insights.problem: LangGraph >= 0.6.0 rejects run configs containing both 'context' and 'configurable'. The gateway would generate 'configurable' even when callers provided 'context', causing requests to be rejected (400).
insights.root_cause: build_run_config unconditionally created a 'configurable' dict and merged caller-provided configurable into it, without considering the presence of a top-level 'context' key.
insights.solution: Updated build_run_config to prefer caller-supplied 'context' when present (and log a warning if both were supplied), move non-conflicting request_config keys through, and ensure runtime/thread_id injection is compatible. Also added assistant_id handling to inject configurable.agent_name when appropriate.
insights.lesson_learned: When integrating with evolving upstream API contracts, prefer honoring caller-supplied modern fields (context) and avoid silently dropping or duplicating fields; emit warnings when both deprecated and new fields are present.

insights.title: Forward assistant_id as configurable.agent_name
insights.problem: Custom assistant_id provided to POST /runs was not injected into configurable['agent_name'], causing make_lead_agent to not load per-agent SOUL files.
insights.root_cause: build_run_config did not accept assistant_id and therefore never injected the mapping.
insights.solution: Added assistant_id parameter to build_run_config and inject normalized agent_name into configurable when assistant_id is non-default and no agent_name already present; validated normalization to prevent path traversal.
insights.lesson_learned: Control-plane fields (assistant_id) that determine runtime behavior must be propagated into the runnable config used by downstream components; validate and normalize values used as filesystem or agent identifiers.

insights.title: Extracted run lifecycle service layer and SSE formatting
insights.problem: Routers previously duplicated SSE formatting and run lifecycle logic leading to inconsistent wire formats and TOCTOU patterns.
insights.root_cause: Multiple router modules implemented similar SSE framing and run creation logic; wire format mismatches and race conditions existed.
insights.solution: Introduced services.py centralizing format_sse, normalize helpers, start_run, sse_consumer, _sync_thread_title_after_run, and upsert helpers. start_run delegates to RunManager.create_or_reject and run_agent to launch tasks and ensures store upsert + background title sync.
insights.lesson_learned: Centralize protocol-driven formatting and lifecycle orchestration to avoid inconsistent wire formats between endpoints and to consolidate error handling and store synchronization.

insights.title: Drain bridge queue on client disconnect when configured to continue
insights.problem: If on_disconnect=continue, bridge subscription could block worker if bridge queue filled and client disconnect happened.
insights.root_cause: Earlier implementation broke out without draining and worker could get blocked by backpressure.
insights.solution: sse_consumer changed to break on request.is_disconnected() but ensure run_mgr cancel semantics only when record.on_disconnect == cancel. RunManager now supports create_or_reject atomic operations.
insights.lesson_learned: When using in-memory queueing with optional background consumption, implement draining or non-blocking consumption to avoid backpressure deadlocks.

backend/tests/test_stream_bridge.py.short_description: Unit tests for the in-memory StreamBridge implementation and replay/trim/END semantics
backend/tests/test_stream_bridge.py.description:
A comprehensive test suite for the MemoryStreamBridge (in-memory StreamBridge implementation). These tests validate ordering, heartbeat emission, bounded history behavior, replay semantics with Last-Event-ID, behavior for slow subscribers after buffer trimming, END sentinel delivery in various scenarios (full history, empty history, preserving history), and concurrency where multiple runs are produced/consumed concurrently. The file simulates realistic production scenarios (slow consumers, reconnections) to assert that the in-memory bridge honors offsets and guarantees consistent delivery of the END sentinel.

The tests are written with pytest and anyio/asyncio; many tests use the bridge fixture which constructs a MemoryStreamBridge and rely on internals (e.g., inspecting bridge._streams and stream.events/start_offset) to assert trimming and offsets. The suite also includes a factory test validating make_stream_bridge returns MemoryStreamBridge when called without config.

key_constructs.name: bridge
key_constructs.purpose: pytest fixture returning a MemoryStreamBridge(queue_maxsize=256) used by multiple tests.
key_constructs.reasoning: Fixture centralizes bridge construction; tests assume default queue_maxsize unless a test constructs bridge locally with specific sizes.

key_constructs.name: test_publish_subscribe
key_constructs.purpose: Asserts that published events plus publish_end reach subscribers in order including END_SENTINEL.
key_constructs.reasoning: Verifies basic publish/subscribe path and ordering guarantees.

key_constructs.name: test_heartbeat
key_constructs.purpose: Asserts HEARTBEAT_SENTINEL yielded when no events for heartbeat interval.
key_constructs.reasoning: Tests heartbeat emission used by clients to keep SSE connections alive.

key_constructs.name: test_cleanup
key_constructs.purpose: Checks cleanup removes run stream and counters from bridge internals.
key_constructs.reasoning: Ensures resource cleanup removes internal data structures for run ids.

key_constructs.name: test_history_is_bounded
key_constructs.purpose: Verifies queue_maxsize enforces retained-history limit and older events are evicted.
key_constructs.reasoning: Confirms retention trimming semantics used for replay and memory bounds.

key_constructs.name: test_multiple_runs
key_constructs.purpose: Ensures multiple run_ids behave independently and do not interfere.
key_constructs.reasoning: Validates per-run isolation of streams.

key_constructs.name: test_event_id_format
key_constructs.purpose: Asserts event IDs follow timestamp-sequence format using regex.
key_constructs.reasoning: Clients rely on ID format (timestamp-seq) for replay and offset handling.

key_constructs.name: test_subscribe_replays_after_last_event_id
key_constructs.purpose: Simulates reconnect and asserts replay only for buffered events after Last-Event-ID.
key_constructs.reasoning: Validates replay logic when subscribers reconnect with Last-Event-ID header.

key_constructs.name: test_slow_subscriber_does_not_skip_after_buffer_trim
key_constructs.purpose: Ensures slow subscriber resumes from correct absolute offset even if first events were trimmed.
key_constructs.reasoning: Important for correctness when history buffer evicts older events.

key_constructs.name: test_publish_end_terminates_even_when_history_is_full
key_constructs.purpose: Asserts publish_end terminates subscribers and does not mutate retained history.
key_constructs.reasoning: END delivery must not be prevented by a full history buffer.

key_constructs.name: test_publish_end_without_history_yields_end_immediately
key_constructs.purpose: Asserts end is delivered even when no events were published.
key_constructs.reasoning: Subscribers connecting after end must still receive END sentinel.

key_constructs.name: test_publish_end_preserves_history_when_space_available
key_constructs.purpose: Checks publish_end preserves prior events when capacity exists.
key_constructs.reasoning: Validates expected behavior when history has spare capacity.

key_constructs.name: test_concurrent_tasks_end_sentinel
key_constructs.purpose: Runs multiple producer/consumer pairs concurrently and asserts each run receives its END sentinel.
key_constructs.reasoning: Stress test for concurrent usage of the bridge instance.

key_constructs.name: test_make_stream_bridge_defaults
key_constructs.purpose: Factory test to ensure make_stream_bridge returns MemoryStreamBridge by default.
key_constructs.reasoning: Ensures the factory default matches expectations (no config -> in-memory bridge).

key_behaviors: asserts event ordering and end-of-stream delivery
validates heartbeat emission when idle
verifies bounded history and replay behavior with Last-Event-ID
tests concurrent producer/consumer correctness

pitfalls.mistake: Relying on private internals (_streams, stream.events, start_offset) in tests without adapting when implementation changes.
pitfalls.consequence: If MemoryStreamBridge internals are refactored or renamed tests will break even if public contract remains intact.
pitfalls.prevention: When editing bridge implementation, update tests accordingly or add/maintain public inspection APIs for test assertions.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing event id format or event objects returned by the bridge.
pitfalls.consequence: Many tests assert the format/regex or structure of event.id and event.data; changes will break tests and may break client replay logic.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve timestamp-sequence event id format or coordinate changes across tests and client parsers.

pitfalls.mistake: Altering heartbeat timing or sentinel behavior without adjusting tests' heartbeat_interval/timeouts.
pitfalls.consequence: Tests that expect a heartbeat within a short timeout may intermittently fail.
pitfalls.prevention: Adjust test heartbeat_interval and timeouts when changing heartbeat semantics.

key_sections: test_publish_subscribe: basic publish/subscribe ordering and END delivery
test_subscribe_replays_after_last_event_id: replay and Last-Event-ID behavior
test_slow_subscriber_does_not_skip_after_buffer_trim: trimming and resume correctness
test_concurrent_tasks_end_sentinel: concurrency stress for END sentinel delivery
skip_unless_needed: test_history_is_bounded (self-contained, small queue-size variant)
test_make_stream_bridge_defaults (factory smoke test)

relevant_snippets.description: Fixture and basic publish/subscribe test that demonstrates order and END sentinel.
Tests for replay semantics after Last-Event-ID and slow subscriber resume after trimming.
Concurrent producer/consumer stress test ensuring each run gets its own END sentinel.

related_files.reason_to_check: Any changes to stream semantics, sentinel objects, or event ID formatting must be validated here.

insights.title: Expand tests for memory stream bridge replay, bounded history, and end delivery
insights.problem: Ensure new in-memory event log correctly replays from Last-Event-ID, preserves END semantics, and handles slow subscribers after buffer trims.
insights.root_cause: Queue-based tests were insufficient for replay and trimming semantics.
insights.solution: Added tests for replay after last_event_id, slow subscriber resume after trim, publish_end behavior with full history, and concurrency using anyio task groups.
insights.lesson: When changing stream semantics to support replay/offsets, update tests to cover offset resolution, trimming, replay correctness and end-of-stream guarantees.

backend/tests/test_sse_format.py.short_description: Unit tests verifying SSE frame formatting produced by format_sse
backend/tests/test_sse_format.py.description: Small focused tests that validate key aspects of format_sse: the JSON encoding of data (including explicit null for end events), presence of event header and optional id header, and that error event payloads preserve 'message' and 'name' fields. The tests exercise format_sse via a helper wrapper and parse the resulting frame strings to assert expected substrings and JSON payload content. These tests ensure the wire format matches client expectations and are tightly coupled to format_sse's exact output formatting.

key_constructs.purpose: Asserts that format_sse('end', None) serializes data: null.
key_constructs.reasoning: Clients relying on 'end' event expect data: null in the payload.

key_constructs.name: test_sse_metadata_event
key_constructs.purpose: Asserts metadata event includes run_id and an id header when provided.
key_constructs.reasoning: Checks both event header and id formatting in the SSE frame.

key_constructs.name: test_sse_error_format
key_constructs.purpose: Parses frame to assert error payload preserves message and name fields.
key_constructs.reasoning: Validates JSON payload structure for error events as consumed by clients.

key_behaviors: asserts 'data: null' for end events
asserts 'event:' header and optional 'id:' header
ensures JSON payload contains expected keys (message, name) for errors

pitfalls.mistake: Changing whitespace/newline ordering or field order in format_sse.
pitfalls.consequence: These tests will break and clients parsing SSE frames may fail; field order and trailing blank lines are significant.
pitfalls.prevention: Maintain exact ordering and use json.dumps with same parameters when altering format_sse.
