backend/packages/harness/deerflow/agents.short_description: Factory and middleware assembly for the lead LangChain agent (runtime model & middleware orchestration)
backend/packages/harness/deerflow/agents.description:
This module constructs the lead agent used by DeerFlow runs. It encapsulates runtime model resolution, summarization middleware creation, todo-list middleware creation (plan mode), middleware chain assembly (respecting order and feature flags), and the final make_lead_agent factory which wires model, tools, middleware, system prompt and per-run state. The file centralizes runtime decisions such as which model to use (request override, agent config, global default), whether to enable "thinking" mode, which middlewares to include, and how to tag run metadata for tracing.

Design decisions visible in this file: model resolution is centralized through _resolve_model_name and create_chat_model (to ensure alias resolution and consistent model instantiation), middleware assembly is explicit and ordered (several middleware ordering constraints are noted in comments and enforced by _build_middlewares), and make_lead_agent uses lazy imports for tools to avoid import cycles and to allow runtime feature toggles (bootstrap flow vs default flow). The file is an integration point between configuration (get_app_config, load_agent_config, get_summarization_config), model factory (create_chat_model), tool discovery (get_available_tools), and runtime middleware building (build_lead_runtime_middlewares and many custom middlewares).

key_constructs.name: _resolve_model_name
key_constructs.purpose: Return the runtime model name to use, preferring a valid requested name and falling back to the global default; raises if no models configured.
key_constructs.reasoning: Model selection must be deterministic and safe: reject empty global model list, accept request overrides only if present in app config, otherwise return default. Tests rely on exact fallback and warning behavior when requested model is missing.

key_constructs.name: _create_summarization_middleware
key_constructs.purpose: Instantiate a SummarizationMiddleware based on summarization config (or return None if disabled).
key_constructs.reasoning: This uses the central create_chat_model factory to instantiate the summarization model (important for alias resolution and consistent model options). It prepares trigger/keep parameters and optionally includes token trimming and custom prompts. Tests validate that when a model alias is configured the factory is called with name=config.model_name and thinking is disabled.

key_constructs.name: _create_todo_list_middleware
key_constructs.purpose: Create a TodoMiddleware configured for 'plan mode' with DeerFlow-specific system prompt and tool description (or return None if plan mode off).
key_constructs.reasoning: This encapsulates DeerFlow best-practices for todo/task management in a middleware; the long system_prompt and tool_description are intentionally detailed and form part of agent behavior when plan mode is enabled.

key_constructs.name: _build_middlewares
key_constructs.purpose: Assemble the ordered list of AgentMiddleware instances for the lead agent run, considering config flags, model capabilities (vision), and custom injections.
key_constructs.reasoning: Middleware order is semantically important (comments list ordering dependencies). This function also conditionally inserts TokenUsage, ViewImage, SubagentLimit, DeferredToolFilter, LoopDetection, and Clarification middleware depending on config and model properties. Tests exercise that resolved model_name (not stale config value) is used to decide whether to include vision middleware.

key_constructs.name: make_lead_agent
key_constructs.purpose: Factory that builds the lead LangChain agent with model, tools, middleware chain, system prompt and ThreadState state schema.
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the runtime entry point used by LangGraph/Gateway to create the agent for a run. It applies request-level overrides (thinking_enabled, model_name, plan mode, subagent settings), injects run metadata for tracing, supports a 'bootstrap' minimal flow, and uses lazy imports for tool discovery to avoid module cycles.

key_behaviors: resolve runtime model name with safe fallback
create summarization middleware using model factory
assemble ordered runtime middleware chain
build lead agent with proper model, tools, system prompt and state schema

pitfalls.mistake: Directly instantiate or pass raw model name strings instead of using create_chat_model
pitfalls.consequence: Model aliases, default options, or other normalization done in the central factory will be bypassed; tests (and runtime behavior) expecting resolved model objects will fail.
pitfalls.prevention: Always call create_chat_model when constructing model instances inside this module (as done in _create_summarization_middleware and make_lead_agent).

pitfalls.mistake: Reordering or removing middleware without respecting documented ordering constraints
pitfalls.consequence: Subtle behavioral regressions: e.g., ThreadDataMiddleware must be before SandboxMiddleware for thread_id availability; ClarificationMiddleware must be last; moving TokenUsage or ViewImage insertions can change runtime semantics.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the documented ordering in _build_middlewares and consider how config flags affect insertion positions.

pitfalls.mistake: Removing the lazy imports in make_lead_agent (importing get_available_tools at module top)
pitfalls.consequence: Potential circular import errors across agents, tools and middlewares.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep get_available_tools and setup_agent imports inside make_lead_agent.

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming model_config is always present without checking
pitfalls.consequence: If model resolution fails or the model name is invalid, subsequent code may throw cryptic exceptions; the function explicitly raises a ValueError when model_config is None which tests assert on.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect checks and error messages; when changing resolution logic, ensure equivalent checks and messaging are preserved.

key_sections: _resolve_model_name: how request/agent/global model selection works and how failures are handled
_create_summarization_middleware: how summarization is configured and why create_chat_model is used
_build_middlewares: ordering and conditional middleware insertion (feature flags & model capabilities)
skip_unless_needed: the long inline system_prompt/tool_description strings inside _create_todo_list_middleware (read only if modifying todo behavior or text)

relevant_snippets.description: Helpers to construct AppConfig and ModelConfig used by the tests
Tests for _resolve_model_name fallback and warning behavior
Tests that make_lead_agent disables thinking for models that don't support it and that middleware builder uses resolved model_name for vision middleware

related_files.reason_to_check: If middleware constructors or ordering rules change, update _build_middlewares accordingly.

related_files.relationship: ThreadState is the state_schema provided to create_agent, influencing per-run file directories and lifecycle
related_files.reason_to_check: If the ThreadState contract changes, ensure state_schema usage is compatible.

related_files.reason_to_check: Tool discovery and tool API changes affect what the agent is created with.

insights.title: Resolve model aliases for summarization middleware
insights.problem: Summarization middleware used the raw config.model_name string instead of resolving it via create_chat_model; alias names weren't resolved (e.g., model alias mapping), leading to wrong model objects passed to middleware.
insights.root_cause: Code passed config.model_name directly instead of delegating to the model factory which applies alias resolution and option population.
insights.solution: Call create_chat_model(name=config.model_name, thinking_enabled=False) when building summarization middleware so the resolved model object is used.
insights.lesson_learned: Always use central model factory functions to resolve aliases and instantiate models so all config patterns and defaults are consistently applied.

insights.title: Optional TokenUsageMiddleware insertion based on config
insights.problem: Token usage logging needed to be opt-in via config
insights.root_cause: No existing hook to add token-usage middleware conditionally
insights.solution: Import TokenUsageMiddleware and append it to middleware chain when get_app_config().token_usage.enabled is true
insights.lesson_learned: Feature flags in config should control middleware insertion to avoid noisy logs and provide predictable behavior for users.

backend/tests/test_lead_agent_model_reso.short_description: Unit tests for lead agent model resolution and middleware assembly behavior
backend/tests/test_lead_agent_model_reso.description: This test module validates critical runtime behaviors of the lead agent factory and middleware assembly: (1) model resolution fallback semantics and warning messages when a requested model name is missing, (2) behavior when no models are configured, (3) that make_lead_agent disables 'thinking' when the selected model doesn't support it, (4) that middleware assembly uses the resolved model name to decide vision capability insertion, and (5) that summarization middleware uses the configured model alias and create_chat_model to instantiate the summarization model. Tests use monkeypatching to replace get_app_config, create_chat_model, and other dependencies to isolate behavior.

key_constructs.reasoning: Tests the commit-level bugfix where summarization needs to use the model factory for alias resolution.

backend/packages/harness/deerflow/agents.short_description: Middleware that filters conversation and queues memory updates with correction/reinforcement signals.
backend/packages/harness/deerflow/agents.description:
This module implements a LangGraph/LangChain AgentMiddleware that prepares and queues conversation context for asynchronous long-term memory updates. It exists to prevent ephemeral data (notably file-upload bookkeeping) and intermediate tool call chatter from leaking into persisted memory, and to detect user feedback signals (corrections and positive reinforcement) so the MemoryUpdateQueue / MemoryUpdater downstream can adjust summarisation behavior. The middleware runs after each agent execution (after_agent) and, when enabled and when a valid thread_id is present, sends a filtered conversation plus metadata (agent name, correction/reinforcement flags) to the memory queue.

Design decisions: the middleware keeps only "human" messages and final "ai" responses (AI messages that do not have tool_calls). It strips an injected <uploaded_files> block from human messages and drops turns that consist solely of that block (together with their paired AI response). Correction and reinforcement detection are conservative: they inspect recent user messages (last 6 messages) using compiled regex patterns for English and Chinese signals, and treat correction as higher priority than reinforcement. Message content normalization is handled by _extract_message_text (supports plain strings and multimodal/list-style content). The module defers queuing and configuration to external components (get_memory_config, get_memory_queue) and guards runtime.context being None.

key_constructs.name: _UPLOAD_BLOCK_RE
key_constructs.purpose: Regex used to remove the <uploaded_files> block from human messages.
key_constructs.reasoning: Centralised pattern to reliably strip the ephemeral upload metadata inserted by uploads middleware before memory persists content. Editors must preserve the regex semantics to avoid leaking file paths or removing legitimate text.

key_constructs.name: _CORRECTION_PATTERNS
key_constructs.purpose: Tuple of compiled regex patterns that signal the user corrected the assistant (English and Chinese).
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by detect_correction to decide whether to mark a queued conversation as a correction event. Patterns are intentionally conservative and multilingual; altering them changes detection sensitivity and downstream memory behavior.

key_constructs.name: _REINFORCEMENT_PATTERNS
key_constructs.purpose: Tuple of compiled regex patterns that signal positive reinforcement/approval (English and Chinese).
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by detect_reinforcement to identify approval signals. Mutual exclusivity with corrections is enforced in after_agent; maintain this ordering to avoid conflicting signals.

key_constructs.name: MemoryMiddlewareState
key_constructs.purpose: State schema compatible with ThreadState (subclass of AgentState).
key_constructs.reasoning: Provides a typed state placeholder for middleware integration with LangChain/LangGraph; kept minimal to be schema-compatible. Changes here affect middleware state typing but not runtime behaviour directly unless fields are added/used.

key_constructs.name: _extract_message_text
key_constructs.purpose: Normalises message.content into a plain text string for pattern matching and filtering.
key_constructs.reasoning: Supports multiple content shapes: plain strings, lists of multimodal parts, and dict parts containing 'text'. This function is central to correct detection and upload-block stripping; modifications must preserve handling of list/dict content.

key_constructs.name: _filter_messages_for_memory
key_constructs.purpose: Filters a conversation to only user inputs and final assistant responses; strips upload block and drops upload-only turns.
key_constructs.reasoning: Encodes the rules for what is allowed into long-term memory: remove tool messages, exclude AI messages that are intermediate (have tool_calls), and remove ephemeral upload bookkeeping. Preserves the real user question when combined with uploads. It also implements pairing behavior (skipping the next AI response when human turn is upload-only).

key_constructs.name: detect_correction
key_constructs.purpose: Detects recent explicit user correction signals in a message list.
key_constructs.reasoning: Scans up to the last 6 messages and returns True when any recent human message matches correction patterns. The result is used to mark queued memory items as corrections; correctness here affects whether downstream memory updater treats the conversation as requiring correction-focused summarisation.

key_constructs.name: detect_reinforcement
key_constructs.purpose: Detects recent positive reinforcement signals in a message list.
key_constructs.reasoning: Similar to detect_correction, but detects approval signals. In after_agent, reinforcement is only considered when correction is not detected (reinforcement has lower priority).

key_constructs.name: MemoryMiddleware
key_constructs.purpose: Agent middleware that enqueues filtered conversation for asynchronous memory updates after each agent run.
key_constructs.reasoning: The primary integration point between agent execution and the memory subsystem. It consults memory_config.enabled, extracts thread_id (from runtime.context or a configurable fallback), filters messages, checks minimum requirements (at least one user and assistant message), computes correction/reinforcement signals, and calls get_memory_queue().add(...) with these values. The method is defensive about missing runtime.context and empty message lists.

key_behaviors: filters out ephemeral upload bookkeeping from messages before queueing memory
removes tool messages and intermediate tool-calling AI messages from memory
detects correction and reinforcement signals in recent user turns
queues filtered conversation to the memory queue with flags

pitfalls.mistake: Assume runtime.context always present and access runtime.context.get('thread_id') without a None guard
pitfalls.consequence: AttributeError in runtime environments where context is None (seen in past commits/tests)
pitfalls.prevention: Keep the existing defensive access (runtime.context.get(...) if runtime.context else None).

pitfalls.mistake: Modify the upload-block regex or removal logic without considering multimodal/list message content
pitfalls.consequence: Upload metadata or file paths may leak into memory summaries; or legitimate user text could be erroneously removed
pitfalls.prevention: When changing _UPLOAD_BLOCK_RE or how content is rebuilt, ensure _extract_message_text and tests that use list/dict contents still pass.

pitfalls.mistake: Treat reinforcement as higher priority than corrections or allow both flags to be set simultaneously
pitfalls.consequence: Memory updater may get conflicting signals and apply unsuitable summarisation (commits show corrections should override reinforcement)
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve after_agent logic: compute correction_detected first, then reinforcement_detected = not correction_detected and detect_reinforcement(...).

pitfalls.mistake: Include AI messages with tool_calls or ToolMessage instances into queued messages
pitfalls.consequence: Intermediate tool outputs could pollute long-term memory and mislead downstream summarisation
pitfalls.prevention: Retain the filter that drops AI messages with tool_calls and ToolMessage types.

key_sections: _filter_messages_for_memory: core rules for what to keep/drop (upload-only detection and pairing behavior)
_extract_message_text: multimodal/list content normalisation used by detectors
detect_correction and detect_reinforcement: regex-based signal detection and priority rules
skip_unless_needed: MemoryMiddlewareState: placeholder subclass of AgentState with no extra logic
logger setup and module-level constant declarations (read them only if tuning detection regexes)

relevant_snippets.description: Covers upload-block stripping, tool message filtering, multimodal content handling, and signal detection tests for both correction and reinforcement.

related_files.relationship: Consumer of queued conversation items (get_memory_queue(), MemoryUpdateQueue.add); the middleware passes messages and correction/reinforcement flags to it.
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to queue.add call signature or expected flags (correction_detected, reinforcement_detected) require corresponding updates here.

related_files.relationship: MemoryUpdater processes queued conversations and contains _strip_upload_mentions_from_memory used by tests; interacts with flagged correction/reinforcement to modify summarisation behavior.
related_files.reason_to_check: Editing detection logic or message filtering may require aligning updater behaviour that strips upload mentions and responds to correction/reinforcement flags.

related_files.relationship: Provides get_memory_config() consulted to decide whether to enable queueing behavior.
related_files.reason_to_check: If default enable/disable semantics or config keys change, after_agent must still consult the correct properties.

insights.title: Detect positive reinforcement signals and forward to memory queue
insights.problem: MemoryMiddleware detected corrections but not positive reinforcement (approval) signals; memory updater couldn't apply reinforcement hints.
insights.root_cause: No detector for positive reinforcement existed in middleware and queue/updater plumbing lacked reinforcement flag.
insights.solution: Add _REINFORCEMENT_PATTERNS and detect_reinforcement(messages) function, compute reinforcement_detected = not correction_detected and detect_reinforcement(filtered_messages), and pass reinforcement_detected into queue.add.
insights.lesson: Signal detection must consider mutually-exclusive signals (prefer correction over reinforcement) and pass explicit flags through queue/updater so downstream modules can adjust behavior.

insights.title: Detect correction signals and filter upload blocks before memory queueing
insights.problem: Upload metadata blocks were leaking into memory updates and explicit user correction signals were not detected to prioritize corrections in memory extraction.
insights.root_cause: Filtering didn't fully normalize content types and no correction detection existed. Also upload block regex duplicated across file previously.
insights.solution: Added _extract_message_text to normalize mixed list/dict content, reuse of UPLOAD_BLOCK_RE removal, added detect_correction() with English and Chinese patterns, and passed correction_detected flag to memory queue.add().
insights.lesson_learned: Signal detection should operate on normalized text and be conservative (look at recent user turns). Avoid leaking ephemeral upload blocks into long-term memory.

insights.title: Replace prints with debug logging in MemoryMiddleware
insights.problem: Prints emitted when skipping memory updates due to missing context or messages.
insights.root_cause: Informational messages were printed instead of logged.
insights.solution: Use logger.debug when skipping memory update paths.
insights.lesson_learned: Prefer debug-level logging for expected, routine non-error paths to avoid alarming users while preserving observability for debugging.

insights.title: Defensive null checks for runtime.context in MemoryMiddleware
insights.problem: MemoryMiddleware accessed runtime.context.get('thread_id') causing AttributeError when runtime.context was None
insights.root_cause: Assumed runtime.context always present
insights.solution: Guard runtime.context access: thread_id = runtime.context.get('thread_id') if runtime.context else None; log and skip if missing
insights.lesson_learned: Always treat runtime.context as potentially None in middlewares that may run in different runtime states (tests, partial setups).

backend/tests/test_memory_upload_filteri.short_description: Unit tests that verify upload-block filtering and correction/reinforcement signal detection for memory middleware/updater.
backend/tests/test_memory_upload_filteri.description: This test module verifies two related pieces of functionality that prevent ephemeral upload bookkeeping from being persisted and that detect user feedback signals: (1) the _filter_messages_for_memory function in memory_middleware and (2) the _strip_upload_mentions_from_memory helper in the memory updater. The tests exercise a range of cases: upload-only turns should be dropped (with their paired assistant response), uploads combined with questions should have the upload block stripped but question preserved, tool messages and AI tool-calls should be excluded, multimodal list content must be handled, and no upload file paths should survive filtering. Separate test classes verify correction detection and positive reinforcement detection (English and Chinese patterns), including windowing semantics (only recent turns checked) and precedence (correction takes priority over reinforcement).

key_constructs.name: _UPLOAD_BLOCK
key_constructs.purpose: Test fixture string representing an <uploaded_files> metadata block used in human messages.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used across many tests to simulate the ephemeral upload metadata injected by UploadsMiddleware; editing this constant will affect many assertions.

key_constructs.name: _human
key_constructs.purpose: Helper that builds a HumanMessage with given text content.
key_constructs.reasoning: Creates inputs to the filter/detection functions in a compact way; tests rely on HumanMessage.content semantics.

key_constructs.name: _ai
key_constructs.purpose: Helper that builds an AIMessage and optionally attaches tool_calls property.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to simulate AI messages that may or may not be final answers (tool_calls present marks intermediate responses).

key_constructs.name: TestFilterMessagesForMemory::test_upload_only_turn_is_excluded
key_constructs.purpose: Asserts upload-only human turns and paired AI replies are dropped.
key_constructs.reasoning: Validates the core protection against uploading metadata leaking into memory.

key_constructs.name: TestDetectCorrection::test_detects_english_correction_signal
key_constructs.purpose: Asserts detect_correction recognizes English correction phrasing.
key_constructs.reasoning: Confirms correction patterns are sensitive to common English phrasing and that the detector scans recent messages.

key_constructs.name: TestDetectReinforcement::test_detects_english_reinforcement_signal
key_constructs.purpose: Asserts detect_reinforcement recognizes English approval phrasing.
key_constructs.reasoning: Confirms reinforcement patterns work and are distinct from correction detection.

key_behaviors: verifies upload-only turns and their paired AI responses are removed
verifies upload blocks are stripped when user includes real question text
ensures tool messages and AI intermediate tool_calls are excluded
tests correction and reinforcement regex detectors across English and Chinese

pitfalls.mistake: Changing test fixture _UPLOAD_BLOCK content or structure without updating memory_middleware's UPLOAD block regex
pitfalls.consequence: Tests will fail because the regex no longer matches the test upload block.
pitfalls.prevention: When editing upload block structure, update both the regex in memory_middleware.py and this fixture in the tests.

pitfalls.mistake: Altering message helper semantics (_human/_ai) to mutate types or content shape
pitfalls.consequence: Many tests rely on message.content being a string or list; changing shape will break test assertions and the middleware's extraction logic.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep message helpers producing langchain_core.messages types with expected content forms.

reading_guide.start_here: _UPLOAD_BLOCK and helper functions at top of file
key_sections: TestFilterMessagesForMemory: tests for filtering behaviour and upload-block handling
TestDetectCorrection: tests for correction detection (English & Chinese and windowing)
TestStripUploadMentionsFromMemory: tests that the updater removes upload references from summaries and facts
TestDetectReinforcement: tests for reinforcement detection and priority over corrections
skip_unless_needed: Test helper _make_memory inside TestStripUploadMentionsFromMemory (only relevant when modifying updater)

related_files.relationship: Source under test (filtering and detection functions).
