backend/packages/harness/deerflow/agents.short_description: Agent middleware that detects repetitive tool-call loops and injects warnings or forces a hard stop.
backend/packages/harness/deerflow/agents.description:
This module implements a LangChain AgentMiddleware used to detect and mitigate infinite or repetitive tool-calling loops produced by LLM agents. Its primary safety goal is to prevent agents from repeatedly invoking the same set of tools with the same (or semantically equivalent) arguments until the run fails or times out. It does this by normalizing tool call arguments, deriving a stable key for each tool call (with tool-specific rules), hashing the multiset of tool calls in an order-independent way, and tracking recent call-hashes in a per-thread sliding window.

The middleware is per-thread-aware via a thread_id read from runtime.context and uses an OrderedDict as an LRU map to limit memory usage across many threads. When the same call-hash repeats a configurable number of times it injects a warning (as a HumanMessage) once per hash per thread; if repetition reaches a higher hard limit it strips tool_calls from the last AIMessage and appends a forced-stop message. The module also defensively handles differing shapes of AIMessage.content (str, list, None) when appending messages to avoid TypeError across providers (e.g., Anthropic thinking-mode where content may be a list of blocks). Thread-safety is enforced with a threading.Lock for all mutations of the tracking structures.

key_constructs.name: _DEFAULT_WARN_THRESHOLD
key_constructs.purpose: Default count at which a warning message will be injected for repeated call-hashes.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used as a default safety threshold; tests and callers may expect this default behavior when constructing the middleware without parameters.

key_constructs.name: _DEFAULT_HARD_LIMIT
key_constructs.purpose: Default count at which a hard stop is enforced by stripping tool_calls.
key_constructs.reasoning: This constant is an authoritative default used by the class constructor; edits must consider tests that assume these defaults or instances created without explicit overrides.

key_constructs.name: _DEFAULT_WINDOW_SIZE
key_constructs.purpose: Default sliding window size for per-thread history of recent call-hashes.
key_constructs.reasoning: Determines how many recent call-hashes are remembered before older ones are dropped; changing it affects loop sensitivity and test expectations.

key_constructs.name: _DEFAULT_MAX_TRACKED_THREADS
key_constructs.purpose: Maximum number of distinct threads tracked before LRU eviction.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to bound memory while still tracking per-thread histories; tests exercise eviction behavior.

key_constructs.name: _normalize_tool_call_args
key_constructs.purpose: Normalize a tool call's args into a dict plus an optional fallback key for non-dict or stringified payloads.
key_constructs.reasoning: Providers sometimes pass args as JSON strings or other types. This function defensively parses JSON strings and provides a stable fallback key for non-dict payloads, which is critical for stable hashing and avoiding crashes when unexpected arg types are seen.

key_constructs.name: _stable_tool_key
key_constructs.purpose: Produce a concise, stable key for a single tool call by selecting salient fields or hashing whole args for content-sensitive tools.
key_constructs.reasoning: Different tools require different sensitivity: read_file needs bucketing by line ranges, write_file and str_replace are content-sensitive (hash full args), while other tools can be represented by a selection of salient fields. This function encodes those heuristics; changes here directly alter loop equivalence classes.

key_constructs.name: _hash_tool_calls
key_constructs.purpose: Create an order-independent deterministic hash for a multiset of tool calls (name + stable key).
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the core of loop detection: identical multisets should map to identical hashes even if order differs. It sorts the normalized representations and returns a short MD5 digest. Tests assert order independence and sensitivity characteristics.

key_constructs.name: _WARNING_MSG
key_constructs.purpose: Text of the injected warning when repeated calls reach the warn threshold.
key_constructs.reasoning: The exact string is asserted in tests; it is injected as a HumanMessage to remain compatible with providers that restrict SystemMessage insertion mid-conversation.

key_constructs.name: _HARD_STOP_MSG
key_constructs.purpose: Text appended to AIMessage.content when a hard stop is triggered and tool_calls are stripped.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to mark forced termination; tests check presence of this substring and rely on text content to ensure the hard stop semantics.

key_constructs.name: _append_text
key_constructs.purpose: Append a text block to AIMessage.content handling str, list, and None without mutating list inputs.
key_constructs.reasoning: Providers may represent message content as structured block lists; this method ensures the hard-stop text can be appended safely for all supported content shapes and avoids in-place mutation of input lists (tests verify original lists aren't changed).

key_constructs.name: LoopDetectionMiddleware
key_constructs.purpose: AgentMiddleware that tracks recent tool-call hashes per thread and injects warnings or forces hard stops when repetition is detected.
key_constructs.reasoning: Encapsulates configuration (thresholds, window, max threads), synchronization, history storage (OrderedDict per-thread list), and the lifecycle hooks after_model/aafter_model. Its methods ( _get_thread_id, _evict_if_needed, _track_and_check, _apply, reset ) implement the tracking, eviction and enforcement behavior that tests exercise.

key_behaviors: detects repeated sets of tool calls after each model response
injects a HumanMessage warning when repetition reaches warn_threshold
strips tool_calls and appends forced-stop text when repetition reaches hard_limit
maintains per-thread sliding history with an LRU eviction policy
defensively normalizes tool-call arguments (stringified JSON, non-dict payloads)

pitfalls.mistake: Assume AIMessage.content is always a str and concatenate directly.
pitfalls.consequence: TypeError at runtime when providers produce list-structured content (Anthropic thinking-mode), leading to middleware crashes.
pitfalls.prevention: Use _append_text which handles str, list, and None and avoids in-place list mutation.

pitfalls.mistake: Hashing the raw args or depending on key order for equivalence.
pitfalls.consequence: Semantically identical tool calls may hash differently (false negatives), allowing loops to bypass detection.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect the normalization and stable key derivation implemented in _normalize_tool_call_args and _stable_tool_key and retain order-independence in _hash_tool_calls.

pitfalls.mistake: Mutating shared list objects used as AIMessage.content in-place.
pitfalls.consequence: Tests and runtime code relying on keeping original message objects intact will see unexpected changes; tests explicitly assert non-mutation.
pitfalls.prevention: Create new lists when appending and avoid mutating the incoming content lists; _append_text returns a new list.

pitfalls.mistake: Not using a lock for reading/mutating _history and _warned.
pitfalls.consequence: Race conditions when middleware is used concurrently across async contexts, causing corrupted tracking state or KeyErrors.
pitfalls.prevention: All mutations to _history and _warned are wrapped under self._lock in the current implementation; maintain this discipline when changing data structures or access patterns.

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming runtime.context is always present.
pitfalls.consequence: AttributeError when runtime.context is None in certain runtimes/tests.
pitfalls.prevention: Use the defensive runtime.context check as implemented in _get_thread_id (runtime.context may be None).

key_sections: _hash_tool_calls: contains the order-independent hashing approach and normalization pipeline — changing it changes loop equivalence.
_append_text: handles all content shapes and avoids mutating originals — important for hard-stop behavior.
_track_and_check: enforces thresholds and returns warning/hard_stop decisions.
skip_unless_needed: module-level default constant declarations (only relevant when tuning defaults)
_evict_if_needed internal LRU loop (read only unless changing eviction strategy)

relevant_snippets.description: TestHashToolCalls class — asserts hashing invariants, order independence, and normalization behavior.
TestLoopDetection class — verifies warn/hard-stop thresholds, thread isolation, window sliding, eviction and reset behavior.
TestAppendText and TestHardStopWithListContent — ensures _append_text handles str/list/None and hard-stop logic preserves list content shapes.

related_files.relationship: unit tests that exercise hashing, normalization, and middleware enforcement logic; must pass when modifying middleware behavior
related_files.reason_to_check: Any changes to hashing, thresholds, message types, or content-handling must be validated against these tests.

related_files.relationship: base class and lifecycle contract (after_model/aafter_model) that LoopDetectionMiddleware implements
related_files.reason_to_check: If LangChain API surface changes, hooks or expected return shapes must be reconciled with this middleware.

data_constants_to_check: warn_threshold, hard_limit, window_size, max_tracked_threads (module-level defaults and constructor arguments)

insights.title: Stable hashing for loop detection of tool calls
insights.problem: Loop detection hashed full tool-call arguments; noisy or non-salient changes (e.g., nearby read_file line ranges, stringified JSON args) caused semantically identical calls to appear different and evade detection.
insights.root_cause: Hashing used entire args payload and order-dependent serialization, which magnified non-salient differences and allowed loops to bypass detection.
insights.solution: Normalize args defensively (parse stringified JSON), derive a stable key from salient fields (path, url, query, command, pattern, glob, cmd). Special-case read_file by bucketing line ranges, treat write_file/str_replace as content-sensitive (hash full args), and build an order-independent sorted blob for hashing.
insights.lesson: Loop-detection should focus on stable, salient identifiers and bucket noisy numeric ranges. Defensive parsing of providers that pass stringified args avoids crashes and improves stability.

insights.title: Harden argument normalization and stable key derivation
insights.problem: Stringified and non-dict args could crash or produce inconsistent keys.
insights.root_cause: Providers sometimes pass args as JSON strings or other types.
insights.solution: Defensive parsing of string args into dict or stable fallback JSON string; added additional salient keys (pattern, glob, cmd) and normalization for read_file ranges including reversed ranges.
insights.lesson: Normalize external inputs early and provide a reasonable fallback key for non-dict payloads. Tests should cover these variants.

insights.title: Handle list-type AIMessage.content when appending hard-stop text
insights.problem: LoopDetectionMiddleware concatenated hard-stop messages using string concatenation assuming last_msg.content is a str; some provider modes produce AIMessage.content as a list of structured blocks which caused TypeError (list + str).
insights.root_cause: Code assumed AIMessage.content is str and used (last_msg.content or "") + text, which breaks for list content.
insights.solution: Introduce static method _append_text that handles None, str, and list content: returns text for None, appends a {'type':'text','text':...} block for list, and concatenates for str; coerce unexpected types to str. Update hard-stop branch to use _append_text. Add unit and integration tests for list content behavior.
insights.lesson_learned: LangChain/LLM message content may be structured (list of blocks); middleware and tools must treat both str and block-list forms consistently to avoid runtime TypeErrors.

insights.title: Use HumanMessage for mid-conversation warnings to support Anthropic
insights.problem: LoopDetectionMiddleware injected a SystemMessage mid-conversation which caused Anthropic (langchain_anthropic) to raise 'multiple non-consecutive system messages' errors
insights.root_cause: Anthropic's message formatter requires system messages only at the start of a conversation; injecting SystemMessage later is incompatible
insights.solution: Change middleware to inject HumanMessage(content=warning) instead of SystemMessage when warning is needed

test_functions: tests updated to expect HumanMessage injection

insights.lesson_learned: Injecting system-level messages mid-conversation can break some model providers; prefer using HumanMessage for in-conversation warnings to remain provider-agnostic.

insights.title: Null-safe access to runtime.context in middleware
insights.problem: Accessing runtime.context.get(...) when runtime.context is None caused AttributeError in some runtime scenarios
insights.root_cause: Assumption that runtime.context is always present
insights.solution: Add defensive checks: use runtime.context.get(...) if runtime.context else None
insights.lesson_learned: Middleware should defensively handle missing runtime.context; runtime may provide None in some test or integration flows.

backend/tests/test_loop_detection_middle.short_description: Unit tests covering hashing, normalization, and enforcement logic of LoopDetectionMiddleware.
backend/tests/test_loop_detection_middle.description:
This test module validates the loop-detection middleware's hashing, normalization, edge-case handling, and runtime enforcement behavior. It contains unit tests asserting that semantically equivalent tool calls produce identical hashes (e.g., dict vs stringified JSON, reversed read_file ranges), that content-sensitive tools (write_file, str_replace) produce different hashes when content changes, and that the middleware reacts with warnings and hard stops when repetition thresholds are hit.

The tests also cover runtime concerns: per-thread isolation via runtime.context thread_id, LRU eviction when max_tracked_threads is exceeded, reset semantics, and multi-shape AIMessage.content handling (str/list/None). Helper factories (_make_runtime, _make_state, _bash_call) are used to build minimal runtime and state representations and to avoid object aliasing (deep-copying list content when constructing states). The tests directly assert message types and content shapes (e.g., HumanMessage injection for warnings, AIMessage.tool_calls emptied on hard stop), so they are sensitive to message-format and string constants used by the middleware.

key_constructs.name: _make_runtime
key_constructs.purpose: Create a minimal MagicMock runtime object with a context containing thread_id.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to simulate distinct threads for per-thread tracking tests; tests rely on runtime.context being a dict with 'thread_id'.

key_constructs.name: _make_state
key_constructs.purpose: Build a minimal AgentState dict containing a single AIMessage with provided tool_calls and content.
key_constructs.reasoning: Ensures tests exercise middleware via its expected input shape and avoids shared mutable content (deep-copies lists).

key_constructs.name: _bash_call
key_constructs.purpose: Helper to produce a representative bash-style tool call dict used across tests.
key_constructs.reasoning: Keeps test code concise and consistent when creating repeated or varied tool calls.

key_constructs.name: TestHashToolCalls
key_constructs.purpose: Group of unit tests validating _hash_tool_calls invariants and normalization edge cases.
key_constructs.reasoning: Directly tests hashing and normalization utility functions which form the equivalence logic for loop detection.

key_constructs.name: TestLoopDetection
key_constructs.purpose: Tests middleware enforcement: warn injection, hard-stop behavior, per-thread tracking, LRU eviction, and reset.
key_constructs.reasoning: Confirms operational behavior of LoopDetectionMiddleware under diverse scenarios and threshold settings.

key_constructs.name: TestAppendText
key_constructs.purpose: Tests _append_text behavior for different content shapes, verifying no in-place mutation for list inputs.
key_constructs.reasoning: Regression coverage for prior TypeError and mutation bugs; ensures content handling is robust across providers.

key_constructs.name: TestHardStopWithListContent
key_constructs.purpose: Regression tests ensuring hard-stop appending works when AIMessage.content is a list, None, or str.
key_constructs.reasoning: Catches provider-specific shapes (Anthropic) and validates the middleware appends stop messages without crashing.

key_behaviors: asserts hashing invariants for tool calls (order independence, normalization)
verifies warning injection as HumanMessage at warn_threshold
verifies hard-stop behavior (strip tool_calls and append message) at hard_limit
validates per-thread isolation and LRU eviction
verifies content appending handles list/str/None shapes and avoids mutating inputs

pitfalls.mistake: Sharing the same mutable list instance across successive _make_state calls without deep-copying.
pitfalls.consequence: Tests would mutate the shared list and give false positives/negatives for non-mutation assertions.
pitfalls.prevention: Use _make_state which already deep-copies list content; preserve that pattern when adding new tests.

pitfalls.mistake: Relying on exact message types or substrings without updating tests when message type (e.g., SystemMessage -> HumanMessage) or the warning string changes.
pitfalls.consequence: Spurious test failures if middleware string constants or injected message types are modified.
pitfalls.prevention: When editing middleware text or message kind, update these tests to match new semantics.

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming runtime.context is always present or populated.
pitfalls.consequence: Some tests create MagicMock runtimes without context; ensure the helper _make_runtime or test-specific mocks set runtime.context appropriately.
pitfalls.prevention: Use _make_runtime or ensure runtime.context is an empty dict to exercise fallback thread id behavior.

reading_guide.start_here: TestHashToolCalls (first class) — confirms the hashing invariants that underpin loop detection.
key_sections: TestHashToolCalls: checks normalization and hashing equivalences
TestLoopDetection: enforcement behavior, thresholds, thread isolation, eviction
TestAppendText / TestHardStopWithListContent: content-shape handling and regression coverage
skip_unless_needed: helper functions at top once you understand their deep-copy behavior

relevant_snippets.description: Contains helper factories and TestHashToolCalls — key for hashing/normalization tests.
Contains TestLoopDetection which verifies warning/hard-stop behavior, thresholds, thread isolation and eviction.
Contains TestAppendText and TestHardStopWithListContent which validate content-handling and regression coverage for list content shapes.

related_files.relationship: The system under test — these tests assert its hashing, normalization, and enforcement behavior.
related_files.reason_to_check: Any edits to the middleware's hashing logic, message strings, or content handling require updating these tests.

related_files.relationship: Tests construct AIMessage/HumanMessage/SystemMessage objects from this package; changes in these message classes or their constructors could affect tests.
related_files.reason_to_check: If the message class constructors change shape or attribute names, tests must be adapted.

insights.title: Add tests for stable hashing and normalization
insights.problem: New loop detection behavior requires test coverage for stringified args, reversed ranges, and salient-field hashing.
insights.root_cause: Previous tests did not cover edge cases causing false negatives.
insights.solution: Added multiple tests asserting that logically equivalent calls produce identical hashes and that content-sensitive tools differ when content changes.
insights.lesson: When changing hashing/normalization logic, add unit tests that assert invariants about equivalence classes and sensitivity to content.

insights.title: Update test to expect HumanMessage injection
insights.problem: Existing test expected SystemMessage but code now injects HumanMessage for compatibility
insights.root_cause: Behavioral change to support Anthropic models
insights.solution: Change test assertions to expect HumanMessage
insights.lesson_learned: When changing provider-compatible behavior, update unit tests to assert new semantics to prevent regressions.

frontend/src/components/ai-elements/prom.short_description: React prompt input component with attachment, drag/drop, IME, and speech handling
frontend/src/components/ai-elements/prom.description:
This file implements a fully-featured PromptInput UI component and an optional surrounding provider for lifting its state. It exists to let users type messages to the AI, attach files (images or generic files), drag & drop, paste, and optionally use speech-to-text. The component is designed to operate in two modes: self-contained local state for attachments and input, or provider-backed state when wrapped by PromptInputProvider. The provider allows sharing attachments/text across multiple prompt instances.

Key responsibilities include: managing attachment lifecycle (create object URLs, revoke on removal/unmount), validating attachments (via splitUnsupportedUploadFiles and a PromptInputValidationContext), handling keyboard events including respecting IME composition so Enter doesn't submit while composing, providing a hidden <input> for native form sync if requested, and a microphone UI that integrates with SpeechRecognition (the component contains logic to avoid leaking recognition instances and avoid stale closures). The file integrates many small UI primitives (InputGroup, DropdownMenu, Command palette, HoverCard, Select) to provide an advanced UX (attachment previews, inline remove controls, add-file menu, alternate send controls).

Design decisions reflected here: separation of provider vs local mode for attachments, explicit lifetime management of object URLs via revokeObjectURL, use of refs for stable callbacks (important for SpeechRecognition lifetime), centralized validation via a PromptInputValidationContext (so validators can be injected), and careful IME-composition checks using the shared isIMEComposing helper (commit history indicates fixes around this). The component coordinates with external helper modules: core/uploads (splitUnsupportedUploadFiles), lib/ime (isIMEComposing), and relies on many shared UI components in the codebase. Because the file contains a lot of UI wiring, changes must respect attachment cleanup, provider/local duality, and side effects around browser APIs (Clipboard, Drag & Drop, SpeechRecognition).

key_constructs.name: PromptInputProvider
key_constructs.purpose: Context provider that lifts prompt text input and attachments into a shared controller
key_constructs.reasoning: When present, this provider centralizes files and text so multiple PromptInput instances can share state; editors must preserve reference logic (fileInputRef, openRef) and object URL cleanup behavior when changing the provider.

key_constructs.name: PromptInputController
key_constructs.purpose: React context storing controller APIs (textInput, attachments, __registerFileInput)
key_constructs.reasoning: Acts as the central API surface for provider mode; consumers call usePromptInputController() to access controller methods; context shape must remain stable for consumers.

key_constructs.name: ProviderAttachmentsContext
key_constructs.purpose: React context for attachments exposed by the provider
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to allow optional provider-less operation; provider / local dual-mode requires this context to be present or absent; changing semantics affects usePromptInputAttachments behavior.

key_constructs.name: usePromptInputAttachments
key_constructs.purpose: Hook that returns the active attachments context, preferring provider when available
key_constructs.reasoning: This dual-mode hook is used by multiple subcomponents (attachment renderers, add-menu). When editing, ensure it still prefers provider attachments and throws when absent for required usage.

key_constructs.name: PromptInputAttachment
key_constructs.purpose: UI component rendering a single attachment with hover preview and remove control
key_constructs.reasoning: Handles object URL preview rendering for images and provides a remove button that revokes the URL; modifications must maintain stopPropagation and revoke semantics.

key_constructs.name: PromptInputAttachments
key_constructs.purpose: Container component that maps attachments to renderable children
key_constructs.reasoning: Only renders if attachments exist; iterates attachments.files and expects unique id property; layout and truncation logic live here.

key_constructs.name: PromptInputActionAddAttachments
key_constructs.purpose: Dropdown menu item that triggers the openFileDialog from the attachments context
key_constructs.reasoning: UI glue that calls attachments.openFileDialog(); if file dialog registration changes, this wiring must be updated accordingly.

key_constructs.name: PromptInput
key_constructs.purpose: Main exported form component that accepts text, files, paste/drop, keyboard and speech interactions, and invokes onSubmit with a PromptInputMessage
key_constructs.reasoning: Central form component which coordinates validation (accept, maxFiles, maxFileSize), file ingestion (paste/drag/drop), IME-aware submission, and integrates provider/local attachments. Edits must respect props semantics and lifecycle (object URL cleanup, hidden input sync).

key_constructs.name: PromptInputValidationContext
key_constructs.purpose: React context used to provide a validation function for upload files
key_constructs.reasoning: Allows injection of custom file validation; modifying how validation is invoked affects file acceptance and error handling across the component.

key_constructs.name: LocalAttachmentsContext
key_constructs.purpose: Context used to hold local attachments when the provider is not used
key_constructs.reasoning: Supports the component's dual-mode; changes here change how local state is exposed to nested children.

key_behaviors: collects text input and attached files into a message and calls onSubmit
manages attachment lifecycle (create/revoke object URLs)
supports paste and drag-and-drop file ingestion (globalDrop optional)
prevents submit while IME composition is active
provides optional provider mode to share state across components
integrates browser SpeechRecognition for transcription with stable refs

pitfalls.mistake: Removing or altering the URL revocation logic (URL.revokeObjectURL) tied to remove/clear/unmount.
pitfalls.consequence: Leads to memory leaks from unreleased blob URLs, especially with many image attachments.
pitfalls.prevention: Ensure remove, clear, and unmount cleanup paths still call URL.revokeObjectURL for each attachment that has a url property.

pitfalls.mistake: Altering SpeechRecognition effect to recreate recognition on every render or to include changing refs in the effect dependency array.
pitfalls.consequence: Can create multiple concurrent recognition instances or stale closures that call outdated callbacks, causing memory leaks or incorrect behaviour.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep recognition effect stable and use mutable refs to access up-to-date callbacks/refs (as done historically in commits).

pitfalls.mistake: Ignoring IME composition state when handling keydown events (i.e., submitting on Enter while composing).
pitfalls.consequence: Users typing with IMEs (e.g., CJK) will have input prematurely submitted and composition interrupted.
pitfalls.prevention: Use the shared isIMEComposing helper consistently in key handlers; ensure nativeEvent/composition event nuances are respected.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing provider/local dual-mode behavior without keeping registration of the file input and open callback consistent.
pitfalls.consequence: Provider clients may not be able to open the file dialog or have attachments out-of-sync; fileInputRef may point at wrong element.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect __registerFileInput API in the provider and maintain fileInputRef/openRef semantics so attachments.openFileDialog works across modes.

pitfalls.mistake: Bypassing or re-implementing validation without calling splitUnsupportedUploadFiles or the PromptInputValidationContext.
pitfalls.consequence: Unsupported package files or disallowed types/sizes may be accepted, leading to unexpected failure upstream.
pitfalls.prevention: Use existing splitUnsupportedUploadFiles and allow optional validators through PromptInputValidationContext; preserve error codes and onError callbacks.

key_sections: PromptInputProvider: provider state, object URL creation and cleanup, file input registration
PromptInput: main form component that ties text, attachments, drag/drop, paste, IME handling, validation and submit logic
Attachment subcomponents (PromptInputAttachment, PromptInputAttachments): UI and revoke logic
Validation & helpers: where splitUnsupportedUploadFiles and PromptInputValidationContext are used
skip_unless_needed: Detailed JSX layout for UI primitives (Dropdown menus, Command palette sections) if only behavioral changes are required

tests.example_command: cd frontend && pnpm build && pnpm lint

related_files.relationship: Provides splitUnsupportedUploadFiles and PromptInputFilePart types used for file validation and splitting supported/unsupported files
related_files.reason_to_check: If upload validation semantics change (max size, allowed types), update call sites in PromptInput to maintain consistent user errors.

related_files.relationship: Defines isIMEComposing used to decide if Enter should submit the form
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to IME composition detection logic must be reflected in PromptInput key handlers to keep behavior consistent.

related_files.relationship: Provides InputGroup, InputGroupTextarea and other UI primitives used to render the main textarea and controls
related_files.reason_to_check: If the input primitives change APIs or composition events, PromptInput event wiring (onCompositionStart/onCompositionEnd/onKeyDown) must be adapted.

tests_to_run: frontend: pnpm format (to keep formatting CI happy)
project-wide: make check (ensure environment tools) if running full dev stack

insights.title: Respect IME composition in PromptInput enter handling
insights.problem: Enter key submission could fire during IME composition causing incorrect sends.
insights.root_cause: Handlers didn't check composition state consistently; previously used isComposing but not nativeEvent flags or keyCode heuristics.
insights.solution: Use shared isIMEComposing(event, isComposing) helper to prevent submit during composition.
insights.lesson_learned: Centralize IME composition checks across input components to avoid inconsistent behavior.

insights.title: Prevent SpeechRecognition instance leaks and stale closures
insights.problem: Speech recognition effect used component props/refs directly in effect dependencies leading to unstable dependencies and potential leaked/duplicate SpeechRecognition instances or stale closure callbacks updating the wrong refs.
insights.root_cause: Effect depended on textareaRef and onTranscriptionChange, causing re-runs and re-creation of recognition instances; handlers captured stale values.
insights.solution: Use a stable ref (callbacksRef) that holds the current textareaRef and onTranscriptionChange and remove those values from the effect dependency array so the effect runs once; reference the stable ref inside callbacks to avoid stale closures.
insights.lesson_learned: For browser APIs created once per component (SpeechRecognition), avoid re-creating on prop changes by keeping an effect stable and use mutable refs to access up-to-date callbacks/refs inside long-lived handlers.

frontend/src/components/ai-elements/sugg.short_description: React client components for rendering compact suggestion chips with staggered animation and optional icons
frontend/src/components/ai-elements/sugg.description:
This file provides two closely related client-side React components used by the UI to display small suggestion chips (quick prompt templates) and a container that lays them out with a staggered fade-in-up animation. It exists to offer a compact, reusable UI primitive for suggestions that can include an icon, custom children, and click handling. The components are intentionally lightweight and composed from existing UI primitives (Button and ScrollArea) so they integrate with the project's design system.

Suggestions is a wrapper around the project's ScrollArea component that lays out children in a wrapping horizontal flow and applies a per-child staggered animation by mapping children into span wrappers that set animationDelay using an index-based offset and step. Suggestion is a single chip implemented on top of the project's Button component, exposing a small API that accepts either a suggestion (ReactNode) or children, an optional Lucide icon, and an onClick callback. The code uses 'use client' so both components are client-rendered and interactive. Styling and animation rely on specific class names (e.g., animate-fade-in-up, text-muted-foreground) and a small set of constants that determine the animation timing.

Design decisions visible in the file: animation is index-based with a fixed offset and step (STAGGER_DELAY_MS_OFFSET and STAGGER_DELAY_MS) so multiple suggestions appear with a slight stagger; Suggestion deliberately omits Button's onClick type in its prop type to wrap onClick in a local handler and to allow optional onClick values. The components depend on shared UI primitives (Button, ScrollArea, ScrollBar) and a utility function cn for className composition, so changes must respect these contracts and the expected CSS class names present elsewhere in the repo.

key_constructs.name: STAGGER_DELAY_MS
key_constructs.purpose: Milliseconds added per item to stagger animation between suggestion chips
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by Suggestions to compute the animationDelay for each child; changing it changes visual timing across the entire suggestions list and must remain accessible to the rendering logic.

key_constructs.name: STAGGER_DELAY_MS_OFFSET
key_constructs.purpose: Base delay (ms) applied to the first suggestion before stagger steps
key_constructs.reasoning: Provides an initial delay before the stagger sequence; it must be preserved or adjusted carefully because CSS animation sequencing and perceived UX depend on it.

key_constructs.name: Suggestions
key_constructs.purpose: Container component that lays out suggestion chips and applies staggered animation to children
key_constructs.reasoning: Primary entry point for rendering a list of suggestion items. It wraps children in span elements with inline style animationDelay, uses the project's ScrollArea and ScrollBar components, and composes layout classes via cn. Agents editing the file should preserve the client-side behavior, the mapping over Children (index-based animation), and the ScrollArea props spread so calling code retains expected scrolling behavior.

key_constructs.name: SuggestionsProps
key_constructs.purpose: Type alias for props accepted by Suggestions (reuses ScrollArea's props)
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to keep Suggestions API consistent with ScrollArea; modifications to ScrollArea's public API could require updating this alias.

key_constructs.name: Suggestion
key_constructs.purpose: A single suggestion chip component that renders a Button with optional icon and click handler
key_constructs.reasoning: Encapsulates the UI and behavior for one suggestion. It composes Button, supports an optional Lucide icon prop, passes through Button props (except the onClick type is omitted and handled locally), and falls back to suggestion prop when children are not provided. The local handleClick calls onClick?.() so the Button's onClick prop is intentionally controlled and should not be overridden by props spread order.

key_constructs.name: SuggestionProps
key_constructs.purpose: Type alias describing Suggestion component props (derived from Button props but excludes onClick)
key_constructs.reasoning: Ensures Suggestion has the same props surface as Button except that it controls onClick typing/handling. When editing, ensure prop compatibility stays aligned with Button.

key_behaviors: renders children as suggestion chips inside a scrollable area
applies a per-item staggered fade-in-up animation using inline animationDelay
renders a Button-based chip with optional icon and click handling
passes through ScrollArea props to control scroll behavior

pitfalls.mistake: Removing or altering the 'use client' directive
pitfalls.consequence: Component would render as a server component, breaking client-only features like event handlers and Lucide icon rendering, leading to runtime errors
pitfalls.prevention: Keep the 'use client' directive at the top of the file when editing or moving the component

pitfalls.mistake: Changing the order of props spread on the Button (e.g., placing {...props} before explicit onClick or className)
pitfalls.consequence: An external onClick or className passed in props could be overridden or inadvertently ignored, changing behavior and styling of chips
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve current spread order (explicit props then {...props}) so local handlers and className composition take precedence

pitfalls.mistake: Removing the inline animationDelay or the animate-fade-in-up/opacity classes
pitfalls.consequence: Visual stagger and entrance animation will be lost; other components or tests may rely on these class names for visual cues
pitfalls.prevention: Retain animationDelay computation and CSS class names; if renaming, ensure corresponding CSS/animation definitions are updated

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming Children.map assigns stable keys automatically
pitfalls.consequence: React may warn about missing keys in dev mode; reordering children could produce unexpected reconciliation behavior
pitfalls.prevention: If adding or modifying key handling, maintain stable key values for children wrappers (e.g., don't remove index usage without replacing with unique keys)

key_sections: STAGGER_DELAY_MS and STAGGER_DELAY_MS_OFFSET: animation timing constants
Suggestions: how children are wrapped, how animationDelay is computed and applied, use of ScrollArea/ScrollBar
Suggestion: how it composes Button, handles optional Icon and onClick, and fallbacks between children and suggestion prop
skip_unless_needed: Type aliases (SuggestionsProps, SuggestionProps) if you only need runtime behavior
Imports at the top (unless changing dependencies or bundling)

tests.example_command: No component-specific unit tests provided; use frontend build and lint checks: cd frontend && pnpm build && pnpm lint

related_files.relationship: Button is the base primitive used by Suggestion; prop types and visual styles come from this file
related_files.reason_to_check: If Button's API or classNames change, SuggestionProps and how Suggestion composes Button must be updated

related_files.relationship: ScrollArea and ScrollBar are used to host the Suggestions container and control scrolling behavior
related_files.reason_to_check: If ScrollArea prop types or behavior change, SuggestionsProps or the scroll usage may need to be adapted

related_files.relationship: Provides the cn helper used for className composition
related_files.reason_to_check: If the cn utility's signature or behavior changes, className composition in both components could be affected

related_files.relationship: Contains the CSS classes referenced (animate-fade-in-up, text-muted-foreground, size-4, etc.)
related_files.reason_to_check: If CSS class names or animation definitions are altered, the visual behavior of Suggestions/Suggestion will change

insights.title: New Suggestions component and Suggestion button
insights.problem: No compact suggestion UI for quick prompt templates.
insights.root_cause: Missing small reusable UI for suggestions with icons and dropdown extras.
insights.solution: Add Suggestions container and Suggestion button components with icon support, compact styling, and dropdown to create additional templates.
insights.lesson_learned: Small reusable components improve consistency; provide icon prop and allow children to be ReactNode rather than plain string for flexibility.

backend/app/gateway/routers/skills.py.short_description: FastAPI router exposing skill management endpoints (list/install/custom edit/history/rollback/update).
backend/app/gateway/routers/skills.py.description:
This module implements a FastAPI APIRouter (/api prefix) that provides HTTP endpoints to manage DeerFlow skills. It centralizes operations for listing skills, installing .skill archives, and full lifecycle management for "custom" skills (read, edit, delete, history, rollback). The endpoints rely on lower-level skill manager utilities (atomic_write, append_history, read_custom_skill_content, get_custom_skill_dir/file/history), the security scanner (scan_skill_content), and the global prompt cache refresher (refresh_skills_system_prompt_cache_async) to keep the runtime prompt cache current after mutations.

The file exists to expose disk-backed skill operations over HTTP while enforcing validation and security checks. For custom-skill edits/rollback/delete it validates markdown content, runs the scanner (and can block operations if scanner returns decision == "block"), records human actions into a per-skill history, uses atomic writes when mutating SKILL.md, and invokes a non-blocking async cache refresh after successful mutations. The router also updates a central extensions_config.json when enabling/disabling skills and reloads in-memory config via reload_extensions_config. Error handling pattern: HTTPException instances are allowed to propagate; unexpected exceptions are logged and returned as 500 responses.

Design decisions visible here: (1) separation of concerns — this router delegates low-level file and history handling to deerflow.skills.manager and security checks to deerflow.skills.security_scanner; (2) asynchronous non-blocking cache refresh — functions await refresh_skills_system_prompt_cache_async() after writes so the HTTP request can return without long blocking; (3) history-first or history-after choices — update/delete/rollback append history entries describing human action and scanner results. Tests in the codebase monkeypatch many of the imported functions, which indicates these module-level imports are intended to be replaced in unit tests and by runtime dependency injection in the larger application.

key_constructs.name: _skill_to_response
key_constructs.purpose: Convert a Skill object into a SkillResponse Pydantic model used by responses.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by many endpoints to normalize Skill -> API payload; any change must preserve model field mapping and not drop fields clients rely on.

key_constructs.name: list_skills
key_constructs.purpose: GET /api/skills — returns all skills (public + custom).
key_constructs.reasoning: Wraps load_skills(enabled_only=False); exceptions are logged and converted to 500. Important to keep consistent behavior and shape of SkillsListResponse.

key_constructs.name: install_skill
key_constructs.purpose: POST /api/skills/install — install a skill from a .skill ZIP archive in a thread's virtual path.
key_constructs.reasoning: Resolves virtual path → host path with resolve_thread_virtual_path, calls install_skill_from_archive, then refreshes the system prompt cache async. Handles FileNotFoundError, SkillAlreadyExistsError, ValueError specifically and maps them to HTTP 404/409/400 respectively.

key_constructs.name: list_custom_skills
key_constructs.purpose: GET /api/skills/custom — list only custom skills.
key_constructs.reasoning: Filters load_skills output by skill.category == 'custom' and returns SkillsListResponse; simple wrapper relied upon by tests to detect created test artifacts.

key_constructs.name: get_custom_skill
key_constructs.purpose: GET /api/skills/custom/{skill_name} — return SKILL.md content and skill metadata for a custom skill.
key_constructs.reasoning: Finds the skill from load_skills and reads raw SKILL.md using read_custom_skill_content. Raises 404 if not found. Tests expect that returned content contains the markdown header (e.g., '# demo-skill').

key_constructs.name: update_custom_skill
key_constructs.purpose: PUT /api/skills/custom/{skill_name} — validate and apply content edits to SKILL.md for a custom skill.
key_constructs.reasoning: Performs editable check (ensure_custom_skill_is_editable), markdown validation, async security scan, and if allowed atomically writes SKILL.md, appends a history entry with scanner metadata, and triggers an async refresh of the skills prompt cache. Treats scanner 'block' as an immediate 400 response. Tests patch the scanner and expect description updates and history entries.

key_constructs.name: delete_custom_skill
key_constructs.purpose: DELETE /api/skills/custom/{skill_name} — remove a custom skill directory and record deletion in history.
key_constructs.reasoning: Run ensure_custom_skill_is_editable, read previous content for history, append a human_delete history entry, remove the skill directory via shutil.rmtree(), and trigger async refresh. Tests check that deletion removes SKILL.md but preserves history to allow rollback restore.

key_constructs.name: get_custom_skill_history
key_constructs.purpose: GET /api/skills/custom/{skill_name}/history — return a list of history entries for the custom skill.
key_constructs.reasoning: Uses read_history and get_skill_history_file to decide 404 vs returning history. Tests create history files manually and assert the content structure and scanner metadata.

key_constructs.name: rollback_custom_skill
key_constructs.purpose: POST /api/skills/custom/{skill_name}/rollback — restore an earlier SKILL.md state from history.
key_constructs.reasoning: Reads history, selects record by history_index (supports negative indices), validates target prev_content, runs scanner, appends a rollback history entry (includes rollback_from_ts), writes the target content atomically, triggers async refresh, and returns updated skill content. If scanner returns 'block' it still appends the history entry (with scanner decision) and then returns 400. IndexError is caught and translated to 400.

key_constructs.name: get_skill
key_constructs.purpose: GET /api/skills/{skill_name} — return metadata for any skill (public or custom).
key_constructs.reasoning: Simple wrapper around load_skills that searches by name and returns 404 if not found. Used by UI or API clients when showing skill details.

key_constructs.name: update_skill
key_constructs.purpose: PUT /api/skills/{skill_name} — toggle skill enabled state in extensions_config.json and reload config.
key_constructs.reasoning: Resolves/creates config_path (ExtensionsConfig.resolve_config_path or fallback), mutates in-memory extensions_config (get_extensions_config), writes JSON to file (mcpServers + skills), calls reload_extensions_config(), triggers async cache refresh, then reloads skills to return updated metadata. Tests patch load_skills and ExtensionsConfig.resolve_config_path to validate behavior and resulting config file content.

key_behaviors: lists skills for UI consumption
installs skill archives from thread virtual paths
validates and applies custom skill edits
records history for edits/deletes/rollbacks
runs security scan before accepting changes
refreshes skills system prompt cache after mutations
updates extensions_config.json when toggling enabled state

pitfalls.mistake: Removing or changing calls to refresh_skills_system_prompt_cache_async or making them blocking.
pitfalls.consequence: Clients/tests expect prompt cache refresh to run after mutation and may rely on load_skills after refresh — making refresh synchronous could block API responses or change ordering in tests.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep the async await call pattern and preserve invocation sites after successful mutations; respect how tests monkeypatch this symbol.

pitfalls.mistake: Catching and swallowing HTTPException or rewrapping it into a generic 500.
pitfalls.consequence: The router intentionally lets HTTPException propagate to preserve status codes and messages (e.g., 404/400). Changing this will break client-visible error semantics and tests that assert specific status codes.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the 'except HTTPException: raise' pattern and avoid converting known HTTP errors into 500s.

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming ExtensionsConfig.resolve_config_path always returns a path; writing config without checking fallback behavior.
pitfalls.consequence: The code currently falls back to Path.cwd().parent / 'extensions_config.json' when resolve_config_path returns None; removing the fallback or altering default path may break tests that patch resolve_config_path or expect config to be written to the provided tmp path.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect existing fallback logic and be careful when changing how config_path is resolved or written.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing history format or the content/keys of appended history entries.
pitfalls.consequence: Tests and other code that read history entries expect fields like action, author, file_path, prev_content, new_content, scanner (decision/reason), ts; changing keys or format will break history tests and rollback logic.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep the history entry structure the manager expects, and if changing it, update read_history/append_history and all tests accordingly.

key_sections: install_skill: resolves virtual path and delegates to installer then triggers async refresh (important for archive installs).
update_custom_skill: validation, scanner call, atomic_write, append_history, async refresh (central to edit flow).
rollback_custom_skill: history selection, scanner handling (block vs allow), atomic write, append_history, async refresh (complex error paths).
update_skill: writes extensions_config.json and reloads config; check resolve_config_path fallback and file content shape.
skip_unless_needed: _skill_to_response (boilerplate mapping)
simple endpoints like get_skill/list_custom_skills (read-only wrappers)

relevant_snippets.description: Main lifecycle test: list custom skills, get content, update skill (PUT), assert history and rollback behavior and that refresh was invoked twice (update + rollback).

Test that rollback is blocked by scanner: test writes a history file manually and monkeypatches scan_skill_content to return decision 'block', asserts API returns 400 and history shows scanner decision 'block'.
Test delete preserves history and rollback restores SKILL.md; verifies async refresh calls count.
Test update_skill writes extensions_config.json, calls refresh, and that returned skill enabled flag reflects the post-refresh state.

related_files.relationship: This router calls manager functions (atomic_write, append_history, read_custom_skill_content, get_custom_skill_dir/file, get_skill_history_file, read_history) for file IO, history management and validation.
related_files.reason_to_check: If changing history entry structure, file locations, atomic_write semantics, or validation rules, update router code that constructs/reads history entries and unit tests.

related_files.relationship: Router invokes scan_skill_content to decide whether edits/rollback are allowed and records scanner metadata in history entries.
related_files.reason_to_check: Any change to scanner API (ScanResult fields, sync/async signature) requires updating scan caller sites and tests which monkeypatch scan_skill_content.

related_files.relationship: install_skill endpoint delegates to install_skill_from_archive and handles SkillAlreadyExistsError.
related_files.reason_to_check: If installer exceptions change or return payload structure changes, update install_skill's error handling and response shaping.

related_files.relationship: Router awaits refresh_skills_system_prompt_cache_async() after mutations to ensure the lead agent prompt cache is refreshed.
related_files.reason_to_check: If refresh API signature or semantics change, router must adapt; tests monkeypatch this symbol directly.

related_files.relationship: update_skill uses ExtensionsConfig.resolve_config_path, get_extensions_config and reload_extensions_config to persist and reload skill enabled state.
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to config resolution, on-disk layout, or get_extensions_config model will affect update_skill's file-writing behavior and tests which patch resolve_config_path.

data_constants_to_check: history file format produced by append_history/read_history

insights.title: Add custom skill management API and integrate non-blocking prompt cache refresh
insights.problem: No HTTP endpoints for custom skill lifecycle (create/edit/patch/delete/history/rollback) and cache refresh blocking on these operations.
insights.root_cause: Skill management used ad-hoc file ops; prompt cache clearing was synchronous and duplicated across code paths.
insights.solution: Added REST endpoints for skills custom lifecycle, integrated security scanning, history append, atomic writes, and switched to calling refresh_skills_system_prompt_cache_async() to avoid blocking the request thread; wire scanner and manager utils for validation and sanitization.
insights.lesson: Management endpoints that mutate disk-backed resources should validate, append history, run security scans, and refresh in a non-blocking manner or via background workers; ensure API returns well-defined errors for scanner blocks.

backend/tests/test_skills_custom_router..short_description: Unit tests for custom skill management endpoints on the skills router.
backend/tests/test_skills_custom_router..description:
This test module exercises the skills router's custom skill lifecycle and the skill enable/disable update logic. Tests create temporary skill directories (tmp_path) that mimic the runtime layout the router expects and then use FastAPI's TestClient (with the router included) to invoke endpoints. The tests heavily rely on monkeypatching to replace global helpers imported by the router (get_app_config in multiple modules, scan_skill_content, refresh_skills_system_prompt_cache_async, load_skills, ExtensionsConfig.resolve_config_path) to create controlled conditions and to avoid touching the real application data or external services.

The test suite covers: listing custom skills, reading SKILL.md content, editing custom skills (and ensuring history entry is recorded), rolling back edits (including a blocked rollback scenario where the security scanner returns a block decision), deleting a custom skill while preserving history and then restoring it via rollback, and updating a skill's enabled state (verifies file write to extensions_config.json and that the prompt cache refresh occurs before the response reflects the change). The tests assert on HTTP status codes, response payload structure and content, history contents, presence/absence of files, and that refresh calls were invoked the expected number of times.

key_constructs.name: _skill_content
key_constructs.purpose: Generate a minimal SKILL.md content string for a given skill name and optional description.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to create initial SKILL.md files in tmp directories and to produce edited content for update tests.

key_constructs.name: _async_scan
key_constructs.purpose: Return a ScanResult-like object asynchronously for monkeypatching scan_skill_content.
key_constructs.reasoning: Tests patch scan_skill_content to this to simulate scanner allowing changes without calling the real scanner implementation.

key_constructs.name: _make_skill
key_constructs.purpose: Construct a Skill dataclass-like object for use in load_skills monkeypatching.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by test_update_skill_refreshes_prompt_cache_before_return to control what load_skills returns before/after refresh.

key_constructs.name: test_custom_skills_router_lifecycle
key_constructs.purpose: Integration-style unit test that covers list/get/update/history/rollback paths for custom skills.
key_constructs.reasoning: Validates core edit and rollback behavior including that refresh is called twice (update + rollback) in the happy path.

key_constructs.name: test_custom_skill_rollback_blocked_by_scanner
key_constructs.purpose: Tests rollback when scanner blocks the target content; asserts 400 and history entry with scanner 'block'.
key_constructs.reasoning: Simulates a pre-existing history file and a blocking scanner to validate router's behavior for unsafe rollbacks.

key_constructs.name: test_custom_skill_delete_preserves_history_and_allows_restore
key_constructs.purpose: Tests deletion of a custom skill preserves history and allows a subsequent rollback to restore SKILL.md.
key_constructs.reasoning: Validates that delete appends a 'human_delete' entry and that rollback can recreate SKILL.md from history.

key_constructs.name: test_update_skill_refreshes_prompt_cache_before_return
key_constructs.purpose: Tests that update_skill writes a config file, invokes refresh, and the returned skill enabled flag reflects the refreshed state.
key_constructs.reasoning: Patches load_skills and refresh to validate ordering: refresh updates state that load_skills later reflects.

key_behaviors: creates temp skill files for test scenarios
monkeypatches module-level imports used by the router
verifies HTTP responses and side-effects (file writes/history entries)
asserts specific scanner-related behaviors (allow vs block)

pitfalls.mistake: Relying on the real scanner or real app config instead of monkeypatching.
pitfalls.consequence: Tests will be flaky or slow and may interact with real user data.
pitfalls.prevention: Always ensure tests monkeypatch get_app_config and scan_skill_content as shown; when editing router imports, keep import paths stable to allow monkeypatching.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing history file format or path names.
pitfalls.consequence: Tests write/read history files directly (get_skill_history_file) and assert on keys like 'action' and 'scanner'. Format changes will break the tests.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep append_history/read_history and history format stable or update tests accordingly.

pitfalls.mistake: Modifying module-level import names used by monkeypatch.setattr calls.
pitfalls.consequence: Monkeypatch calls in tests (e.g., monkeypatch.setattr('app.gateway.routers.skills.scan_skill_content', ...)) will fail to find the attribute/path and tests will error.
pitfalls.prevention: If refactoring, ensure tests patch the new import paths or keep old symbols exported at the same path.

key_sections: test_custom_skills_router_lifecycle: full happy-path scenario (lines ~20-80) to understand expected behavior
test_custom_skill_rollback_blocked_by_scanner: how blocking scanner behavior is asserted (lines ~80-140)
test_update_skill_refreshes_prompt_cache_before_return: demonstrates how refresh ordering affects returned state (lines ~220-300)
skip_unless_needed: _make_skill (helper used only in one test)
_async_scan/_skill_content (small helpers)

relevant_snippets.description: Lifecycle test: GET /api/skills/custom then GET /api/skills/custom/demo-skill and assert SKILL.md content is returned.
PUT update flow and subsequent assertions about description and history entry 'human_edit'.

related_files.relationship: The router under test; tests exercise endpoints defined here.
related_files.reason_to_check: When changing endpoint behavior, routing, or import names, update these tests to match the new signatures/paths.

related_files.relationship: Tests interact with manager helpers (get_skill_history_file) and monkeypatch get_app_config used by manager.
related_files.reason_to_check: If manager API or file locations change, tests will need to be updated.
